advanced booking via shopee as the place can only be accessed by an authorized ranger. no public vehicle allowed plus the road going up is a bit narrow and quite high up. the weather is super cold thank goodness we read comments and decide to layer up. it s a ladder path going up and down not hiking inside the woods u can enjoy the scenery and be amazed by the mossy trees. safe for kids and elderly who are fit to climb the ladder. and there are watch tower too where u can see a sea of forest and mountains quite breathtaking i may say. you can contact 60 12 444 6690 for booking and the entrance fee is about rm10 pax and only accepts cash.

personally i don t find anything special about this place. it s more like a man made trail cater for tourist. however can give it a try if you have extra time in cameron highlands. driving up here and boh tea center can be quite challenging for bigger vehicles better to go with the local tour guide.

splendid views at the mossy forest tower! easy walk throughout as it is paved with wooden boardwalks. child friendly as well. air was lovely but remember to apply sunblock ! recommended to come here for the sunrise sunset but it is peak hour and might be crowded

beautiful and serene lord of the rings forest. best to avoid for now in may 2022 as it is being controlled by tour cartels which charge a minimum of rm25 per head which is poor value for money as the transportation is illegal and against transportation regulations. even hikers are not allowed to walk there.

amazing place with an already enjoyable trip up the hill. great atmosphere even if i had something different in mind. plenty of steps so if you have back hip or knee problems maybe inform your guide and or take it slowly

it s worth getting there. after turning left and not right to boh teh . turn left onto jln gunung brincang and follow this to the top. the road is pretty good. you ll arrive at the top. warning. the road is mostly 1.5 car width. you ll need to beep the horn a lot to make sure cars coming toward you slow down. if you re not familiar with this follow one of the landy s tour guide vehicles and they will do all the beeping for you. for those wanting more of a hike. i went straight ahead instead of turning down jln gunung brincang. when the road became a 4wd track i parked at an intersection at an altitude of 1600m and walked some 2km to the 2000m altitude. the walk was quite easy even with the thinner air and you would want to be reasonably fit. you walk through market gardens. you can pick wild chokos for making niouk yen or a stir fry. there are some interesting landscape features as seen in the photos. the forest boardwalk was amazing. the pictures and videos say it all. there are plenty of resting places along the walk. when you ve had enough tea golf and strawberries this forest adventure is definitely your cup of teh.

beautiful place. amazing to see the variety of plants. the stair walk is good and helps protect the forest from us but just be careful as it can get slippery. well worth the visit and entry fee. i recommend taking a 4x4 or get a local taxi to drive you up as the road can get a little narrow and there are some major potholes.

no entry for public transport and will immediately ask to turn around without coming down from the vehicle. no drop off as well. only able to hire guide with transport to come up. may contact 60 10 363 3316 for transport and little guide. guide and transport can fetch you up starting from 8am

one of our favourite favorite attraction is strangely beautiful enchanting mossey forest tucked in the highlands of mount brinchang with mysterious mist and shrouded in a magical aura at an elevated position of 2 032 metres above sea level.

an easy walk suitable for all ages as long as you re ok with climbing some steps. it s not walking on mud or soil but just wooden boardwalk. so in a way it s close to nature.. but not exactly. it s a very short walk to the top where there s an observation tower. maybe 30 45 minutes? it s rm 10 for locals and rm 30 for tourists. to continue walking to go to gunung irau you need a permit 2 weeks in advance. do take note!

one of the oldest nature forest in the world. its spreads around 914 hectares in green sceneries mosses at the summit of mount brinchang pahang. explore yourself you will feel like in other nature. the air is often covered by mist and clouds can be seen between the trees creating a breathtaking and glorious experience o nature.

mossy forest in cameron highlands is an absolutely magical place like stepping into another world. the air is cool and fresh and the mist covered trees create a mysterious fairytale like atmosphere. the wooden walkway makes it easy to explore without disturbing nature and every turn offers a perfect photo spot. it s a must visit for nature lovers photographers and anyone who enjoys peaceful moments surrounded by greenery. just make sure to go early in the morning before the crowd arrives and wear comfortable shoes as it can get slippery when wet.

my personal opinion besides boh tea plantation this is a must do sight seeing when coming to cameron highlands. i would recommend to hire a guided private tour who would show and explain the various plants in the area. there is a bit of walking on man made wooden steps so wear something comfortable to walk with and try to go in the morning. usually there us not much of waiting time but today we had to wait over 30 mins as the forestry dept were late to open the attraction.

it s a forrest where tree trunks and brunches are covered with mosses. on top there is boardwalk way which requires ticket at the entry. it s a bit expensive but worth going. along the walk there is a small 2 storied tower that ables to see both pahang and perak city on each side. the entire walkway is full of learning and beauty. most of the trekking trails are now sealed to preserve the extinctive forrest plants.. that s what our guide informed us.

an easy walk for someone who fits. the view is scenic. should go up to the tower for fresh clean air and it s windy. there is also information about the plants and trees along the boardwalk. careful with your steps because it is slippery.

we arrived by scooter and were able to drive to mountain strawberry farm. from there only agencies are allowed to continue by car. but you can walk to the entrance in about 45 minutes about 200 meters in altitude . entry is myr 30 for foreigners. the path in mossy forest is unfortunately quite short. we d say that you won t spend more than half an hour there even if you take a lot of pictures and enjoy the view. it s still a nice place to see though. we can t say what the agencies drivers cost but it would certainly have been too expensive for us. also it was a nice walk uphill and we already saw a pitcher plant and a cameron highland pit viper on the way which was our highlight. originally we wanted to walk to gunung irau. however this is not as easy as we thought. we were told that a permit min. 2 weeks processing time and a guide are required for this. unfortunately we hadn t found that information anywhere beforehand so we hadn t arranged those things in advance. but overall a great trip which can be combined with a visit to the boh tea plantations.
good but trails are closed so no access to the real forest. just walk on the man made small wooden stairs.

a guaranteed tour stop at cameron highlands. the mossy forest is eerily beautiful lush and green. beautifully preserved and one of the oldest forests in the world. near the peak of mount brinchang at 2032m above sea level this forest is frequently shrouded in mountain mist and can be quite cool. bring your jacket and cash for the entry fee 30 myr for tourists 10 myr for locals. the track runs across the state borders of perak and pahang.

book a tour to and they will take you up to mossy forest. my tour pick me up at hotel and bring me to boh tea sungai palas then go to mossy forest for rm50 per person. the entrance fee is rm10 for local and rm30 for international tourist. currently no private car allow to enter. so you must take 4wd via the tour agency. the mossy forest looks very cool. well maintained walk way.

a must place to visit if you are in cameron highlands. love it love it love it. the air up there is so fresh...the view hmmmm just splendid. anyway please wear a suitable attire because the surface is very wet and soft. my suggestion for you please book a tour guide because they will carry you up there by jeep and will provide you with all the info you need about this place. the road up there is very very narrow so better do not drive up there by your own car. this forest is older than amazon according to our nice and polite tour guide mr ravi. wow...really proud of malaysia...older than amazon. this place is really a gem.

wonderful experience but quite tricky to figuer out how to get there. you need to go to the boh tea plantation shop restaurant and look out for a toyota highlux near the parking area to take you up to the mossy forest area. no public allowed to go up by themselves. trip is 25 per person and ticket is 50 per person.

this is the worst road i ever experienced. then the noticed said it was maintenance until 25 september but today the forest is closed should just close the road before intersection. then we have to pay 10 ringgit per person for entering the forest is outrageous just because of this road. i don t recommend for the normal car driver to go there.
must see place in a life time .beautiful can t express in words. every human being must come and visit this wonderful heaven on earth

captured plenty of photo can t shared it all here. went up by rented jeep rm 35 each person. if you re a nature lover you will like it with lots of positive energy to breath in rm 10 per entry each person.

magnificent nature experience. there is a viewing tower with amazing panoramic scenery the best spot for pictures. you need to walk a bit to get there. overall it s one of the best activities in cameron highlands.

must visit. an unforgetable experience. the best spot in ch. it sits on the second highest peak in ch and over 200 million years old. it s a critical part of the ecosystem and water supply. it s so cool and the air is very clean and fresh. entrance fee is rm10 person for malaysian and rm30 per person for non malaysian. do not drive there using your own vehicle. the road is steep and narrow. according to our guide the road is ungazetted hence any damage will not be covered by your vehicle insurance and be prepared to pay exorbitant tow fee. go there with a tour guide using their 4x4 land rover. best to go on a weekday after 11.00 a.m. it tends to be crowded on a weekend or ph.

the mossy forest was a nice adventure. walk deep into the forest and experience the moss. see monkeys cups and other amazing plants. beautiful views from there also. warning the road is rough getting there and you will climb lots of stairs.

mossy forest is really beautiful the views are stunning and the trees and flowers are amazing. the walk is not very long but long enough to see a lot of beautiful views. i really recommend to go on a clear day if it s possible because you can see a lot more. i do not recommend to drive by yourself if you re a tourist because that could be dangerous book it with a tour or arrange a taxi instead.
it was great experience the place is magical however it wasn t fully open hence we spent long time going up on the road which was very difficult and end up with little to see
nice forest track well maintained walk way.

i love this place. it was so cooling and has a great view. they have nicely done up the trail to walk. the challenge is the road up is extremely nnarrow and steep. if you are not skillful driver please dont drive. also there are no road signage after getting in from main road. any time you meet a split road take the left one. there are tour excursions that bring you there.
amazing not for less able can be very slippery. the guide we had on our tour was so knowledgeable about all of the different plants and types of forrest.

indeed very mossy! we booked a tour with discovery! and mr. rajoo gave an impressive tour! he accommodated to all of the guest s questions and gave many details about the mossy forest! loved the small hiking session within the mossy forest albeit it being very muddy so make sure no pretty shoes during the tour!
beautiful place. there is a bit of walking and hiking but manageable!

an experience for nature lovers not to be missed. a great tour guide enhances your experience by adding information about plants flowers and there medicinal purposes. an easy walk with cooling temperatures. amazing views from the tower!

i took the half day tour package and the tour guide picked me up from copthorne reception. the road conditions were good but advised to take tour so you can enjoy the views. the weather was good fresh air and windy. a light jacket is recommended. entrance to boardwalk is rm10 for malaysian. the stairs are covered well and not difficult to walk. 20 to 30mins should be enough to finish the walk as they close the gate for gunung irau entrance. the tower is recommended to climb up because clearer mountain view. very good place to take pictures with the mountain background. a very free and easy walk suitable to bring kids. so far no mosquitoes attacks during the walk.

as they say this is one of oldest forest in the world however we didn t get a chance to see it completely as due to rain there were damp mud all over place and you are unable to walk further. but still we get a chance to see it small enough and it was good. all trees covered in green algae type thing and ferns were growing on stem it was pretty.
nice and cold route to top. it is beautiful view from the top about 2000m above sea level. have a good jacket and poncho as it gets wet here n there with rain.

i ve been here at least three times. it was very cold and foggy. it s a great place to come. tickets are not expensive either with an entrance fee of rm10 per person. there was no anti slip net. now there is. the rm10 ticket is worth adding facilities to them.

mossy forest in the cameron highlands of malaysia is one the oldest and and beautiful forest. trees and plants covered in moss from top to bottom it s branches floor roots everything looks like magical and its quietness birds whispering and blowing of wind make it much more peaceful. people believe that the existence of this forest is from the period of dinosaurs existance. this peaceful place is worth experiencing with beautiful sights of nature.

it was a mixed experience to mossy. firstly the transportation getting there was confusing as many rumours were spreading around. if you have a car yes you can self drive to the mossy it is a bit steep and narrow at a certain location but not an issue if a normal car can t climb up to mossy. those who feel uneasy or uncertain about driving up there can always engage a local agent who uses a high performance 4wheel drive car at a cost of 35rm person. once u reach the peak parking could be an issue as only 15 20 car lots are available so weekends could be crowded. entrance for locals is 10rm and 30rm if not mistaken for foreigners. the wooden walkway to see the moss is probably 150 200meters walking distance. it was built to avoid stepping on the moss and killing them. go to the lookout tower for those photo sessions. at the end of the walkway there is a gate entrance to the hiking trails which requires permits and guides. nothing much can be seen if the condition is foggy but in a clear sky some forest and mountain peak can be seen. enjoy.!

if you want to drive up beware of the narrow lane sharp corner muddy road and very steep slope. your vehicle might get stuck if you stop on a slope. there is a boh glass tea house on the way to mossy forest which you can enjoy a great tea farm scenery closed on monday . if you want to walk up it will take about 30 40min to reach car park from where the hiking starts.

we came here on a tour from tanah rata. really good walkway not slippery and takes around 20 minutes to walk along the boardwalk. stunning views and places to sit along the way. plenty of parking and a public toilet available.

warning scam at mossy forest we were scammed by our guide at mossy forest. he demanded 30 ringgit from everyone even those who didn t enter. the trail was closed after 100 meters and he never paid any entrance fee at all just kept the money. total rip off. be very careful and don t fall for this trick.

quite overpriced especially for foreigners for such small nature attraction. 10rm for local and 30 rm for non malaysian. compare to taman negara pahang with entry fee 1rm. just walk around it s free of charge. jungle area rainforest and mossy forest are everywhere not only inside gated area. the best place in mossy forest is view deck no tickets required . the best time to visit during ramadan month before afternoon. afternoon usually will be rain. can walk from main road. it will takes about 1.5 hour and around 6km. it s very convenient come there by motorcycle. by car can be quite jam. but if you go by car it s not necessary use 4x4. i saw a lot of sedans going there even taxi and perodua axia also. currently it s allowed to use your own car to come until mossy forest gate. but parking space is not too much spacious.

1000 years old forest at the summit of mt. brichang in cameron highlands pahang malaysia. the place is amazing. weather can be harsh sometimes. trees covered with moss creates figures and shapes. different kind of species and insects can be found and heard. what an experience we had and will continue be magical. you can check my full guide youtube video monggie q

as it is a tourist attraction the road to the mossy forest should be wider and proper. only 1 car can go at one time. don t wait for fatality to happen before the road is upgraded. you want tourist money but do not care about their safety.

one of the must visit during your time here at cameron highlands. went out for sunrise shoot over the valley this morning and then head up to mozzy forest. entrance fees are charge at rm10 for malaysian adults and rm30 for foreigners. there is a nice 40 minutes easy hike walk on a stretch of platform as you enjoy the serenity of the forest and fresh cool mountain air. you will see moss growings on trees am told one of the few locations in malaysia where one can witness this. only drawback is there is no sign of what time the place is opening up apparently it was 845am this morning. the road up is a bit bad but managable with cars.
throw back 2015 ride with fellow friends. didn t go up hike the mossy forest. we just took few photos at the entrance

i can now tick off mossy forest from my list. this popular tourist attraction is actually located in between pahang perak border i even took a picture of the batu sempadan or milestone with pah on it. it s a good exercise and nice experience to see the nature and the difference of plants at 1750 2000m above sea level. a lot of stairs going up and down with some gazebos for those who want to rest.. as well as a viewing tower where the word puncak dunia or at world s peak sign was placed. alright.. it might be a bit exaggerated to claim this but at that point of time i did feel like on top of the world. the road leading to mossy forest is not for the faint hearted as it was quite windy and narrow. a lot of land rovers were seen ferrying passengers up the hill. from there one can spend easily 1 3 hours admiring the scenery and enjoying the cool weather.

deserve for paying 200 rm for a taxi to the forest. you have to buy a group tour to the forest because not all vehicles can go up there. lucky a taxi driver promise to bring me a only can seat 2 people because if too heavy the car can t goes up . whole journey took about 1 30h.

the journey to the entrance was very bumpy and you cannot drive your car there. the view was spectacular. lots of greenary. just pleaseeeeeee... all visitors must play by the rules don t jump outside of the walking trail just to take photos. you damage all the moss. don t be selfish. protect our nature.

great humid forest on the top of mountains close to cameron highland. absolutely must see it. 45 minutes walk on the deck which sometimes can be a bit slippery. unique plants and and trees covered by moss. to drive there take the 4x4 car all travel companies are offering this point during sightseeing tour.

it s a place worth visiting. truly special at least during our visit as clouds covered higher parts of the highlands and the forest as well. however it was a long trek to the top and if not for a lovely couple that gave us a lift on the way up we would have spent hours walking up the road.

the place is really good amazing but the short route is only 500 meters and that s it after that stop with closed for repairs. it was not possible to fully enjoy the beautiful views moosy forest. by the way i found out about this only upon arrival at the place with a guide when booking these details are not discussed.

great experience going up on 4wd..really cool place..beautiful nature at its best...there s also a tower inside..on way down our driver spotted rare arisaema brinchangense...forestry dept should enforce only authorised 4wd allowed to bring in tourists

we wast rm30.00 at the entrance and it just stairs into the forest. they said that it takes 40me each way but actually just 10 or 20nd is maximum. suitable for kids and the older. the real trail is free and it is trail no.1 and it 50m away from the gate. it s tough all trail not well marked you may need a guide! the trail is really nice but there are a lot of mud really slippery. the trail takes more than 2 hour to finish.
majestic view of the mossy forest fresh air super easy trail for newbie knowledgeable friendly guide
it s a new experience to walk at a high peak to see the mossy trees. i was expecting more to walk and see. but overall was nice. including all the jeep riding to enjoy the atmosphere.

hundred thousand years of forest. must include in your activity when at cameron highland. need to book 4wd with driver to go there. the road quite dangerous if drive normal car. when you above there very breathtaking view....

magnificient... please use legal tour guide to reach the mossy forrest. rm10 fees is acceptable can enjoy about 1 hours experience the mossy forrest and coastline view is superb... really enjoying the trip... the most beautiful in the world is of course the world itself. wallace stevens

the less i can see forest closed because of the weather from yesterday i had a really good time with a guide which explains everything about the forest so that was such interesting! great weather so we were able to see all the mountains and some hills far away on pangkor island. such a good time

do encourage to have 4wd to drive up as the road condition is not that good at the beginning. there are people who would discourage you to drive up as you don t own a 4wd and offered to take you up for rm20 per person. so i walked up to mossy forest. it took about 40 minutes to reach the ticketing counter. malaysian has to pay rm10 for the entrance fee to get up to the platform to enjoy the beautiful scenery.

beautiful view of mount brinchang. go early in the morning if possible to avoid heavy traffic on a very steep terrain. best driven by people familiar with terrain as the route is very narrow and steep. additional hiking trails are currently closed till further notice.

one such gem is the enchanting mossy forest a strangely beautiful forest tucked in the highlands of mount brinchang the second highest peak in cameron highlands. blanketed with mysterious mist and shrouded in a magical aura the mossy forest is located at an elevated position of 2 032 metres above sea level.

please do not drive up to the mossy forest with sedan car as you will have a hard time going up. rent a jeep up would be safer and cheaper. the road it extremely bumpy and dangerous. seems like only one car can pass but it was a two way road. in many occasions it s very hard to have the opposite car to pass. really scary experience as afraid the car will break down from banging on the pot holes and also might slide down the hill especially rainy day. however scenery was good.
if you have never been to any mountain you can pay either rm10 for local rm30 for foreginer to have a look. you also need to drive very carefully as it is not a easy route to go up.....

although road is closed until feb 2018 and bit dodgy you can still park your car before closure sign and walk up 20 mins. renovations are happening but you can walk past builders anyway and look at first part of the boardwalk. still well worth it i would say but be prepared to ignore a lot of no entry signs.

so glad we booked this excursion! it was a lot of fun. do not take your own vehicle unless you have 4 wheel drive and are experienced with off roading. we saw several people trying to get up there but at the top there were only tour groups. there are also very small windows of time that the park is open and changes with the season. we saw an amazing rain forest ecosystem that i m sure my kids will remember forever. it was quite chilly on top of the mountain and i was glad to have my sweatshirt with a hoodie and good hiking shoes that have good traction. a light rain jacket would have been nice. fyi you will get quit muddy.
the place looked nothing like the pictures. everything was closed. we took the main path and it lead us along mediocre forest path until we reached fenced military ish place.

i drove for about 20 mins to the entrance from nova hotel. can bring your own car . no potholes like few years ago . check weather before going there if rains you will be shivering . ticket for adult rm10.
no private cars are allowed to enter to the area require to contact tour agency. i looked for let s go discovery from facebook their service is ok! the view from the tower is spectacular!

a nice hidden place at the top of brinchang mountain. beautiful mossy forest with great view. a must visit place in cameron highland. at one of the 3 stop over location there is a unique man pride shape display as well. i attached the photo here.

we booked a tour through a tour guide near the bus stop. everything was made easy and we had no issues getting confirmation or organizing our tour. we were told that we would be picked up by a certain time and to be ready. we were ready to go and excited to meet our tour guide. when he arrived we were the first ones picked up and shared a keep with 3 other people. once we picked everyone up we were off to the races ! first stop was the mossy forest. this forest is so cool the trees stand tall and are so large. the fog is thick and chilly it s almost as if you re walking through a mist. we climbed the stairs to the tallest point and explored around a bit but all you could see was thick fog it was still beautiful though. there were lots of people as it s a popular destination by many but we found it easy to walk around and explore the area with our group and away from our group. the drive up was cool as you get further up the hill the fog gets thicker and the road turns sharper.

on the way to mossy forest you will pass thru amazing boh tea plantation. you have to be careful if driving your own car because the road quite narrow. the mossy forest mt. irau is about 2.000 above sea level the weather is quite cool so bring jacket and water with you to keep hydrated no shop at all here . in the first 1 km they have stairs and wooden trails after that you will walk on the ground use sport shoes because it s slippery .

to visit this place you need to hire a tour guide and rent a 4wd no point to drive your own car! for those hand itches pls stop damaging the nature and mosses there! usually the tour guides will meet at cameron square 7 11 in the morning pls call to book them before reaching ch

visited the mossy forest around 10am. big mistake for not bringing any jackets it was so misty cold and windy we were shivering. we took a guided jeep tour from eco cameron it was well worth it the guide explained quite a lot of stuff which otherwise would have missed or not known. also be prepared to climb some steep steps during the boardwalk tour in the forest. overall a must visit place while in cameron highlands just bring your sport shoes and jackets if visiting in the morning.

great place for scenery. the temperature can be quite chilly so it would be recommended to put on a few layers before coming here. the visibility at the peak of the mossy forest can be obstructed during windy and wet conditions.

absolutely stunning! you are in the clouds up there. pro tip be very prepared for much cooler weather there and more rain. it could be a sunny hot day in town and a rainy cold day in this forest. i thought it was a welcome coolness if you ve been in hot weather for a while. also on mondays they open later than usual.

please do not throw rubbish at this place. we brought our kids 6 8 years old for the walk on the wooden walkway and spent about 1 hour here. recommend to come up here with a land rover via local tour or u gonna regret spoiling your tyres suspensions etc it is just not worth it becoz the roads are full of holes n rough. we overheard someone regretting bringing the camry up here there is a new viewing tower launched n officiated by the pemangku sultan this march 2018 visitor can climb up to the tower. its windy up there so bring a jacket to cover your ears. moss are dissapointing for me becoz i can see most of it destroyed i still give it a 5 star becoz i like this place.

one of the must see in cameron highlands. but don t go with your own car. take a tour. eco cameron offers great tours of the place with very passionate and knowledgeable guides. had a great time and learned a lot. was fantastic to see the amazing wildlife in that very special forest where you get to see plants that you don t get to see anywhere else in malaysia and for so.e anywhere else in the world

rm 10 for malaysians and rm 30 for foreigners. toilet out of service. road up is terrible and dangerous. place itself is interesting for nature lovers.. others might not find it as fascinating. good for first time experience.

closed for maintenance and yet there are tour guides scavenging for innocent tourists giving petty tours at the side. we were scammed by 4x4 wheels driver who over exploit this by bringing tourists up for a ridiculous fee. malaysians are supposed to pay rm10 for the permit and rm10 for the ride up by truck from nova hotel. and this driver charged us rm50 per person. all in all the biggest scam ever. a mossy forest which is meant to be closed but still open unofficially so that the tour cars can still have access to somehow. its a disappointment after all! a big tourist trap!!! don t bother going unless the walkway is officially open. you need to register online to buy an entry permit this costs rm10. other than that it s hell to drive up with a normal car. book a 4x4 wheel in advance and make sure you get a receipt which mentions what is included in the fee. taxi drivers charged insane price and they often twist their words especially if you are foreigners or with a foreigner!

the mossy forest in malaysia is a captivating natural wonder that immerses visitors in a world of enchanting greenery and mist laden landscapes. located in the cameron highlands this unique ecosystem stands at an elevation of over 1 500 meters creating a surreal atmosphere filled with cool temperatures and an abundance of moss covered trees. as you step into the mossy forest you are greeted by a carpet of lush moss that blankets the forest floor creating a soft and otherworldly terrain. the towering trees draped in moss and epiphytes cast an ethereal vibe as they disappear into the mist. the diverse flora including exotic orchids and pitcher plants adds to the forest s mystical charm. the trek through the mossy forest is an immersive experience with wooden boardwalks guiding you through the dense vegetation. the air is crisp and the symphony of bird calls and rustling leaves provides a serene soundtrack to your journey. the moss covered branches and trunks create a surreal ambiance making it a haven for nature enthusiasts and photographers seeking to capture the ethereal beauty of this unique ecosystem. the mossy forest is a testament to the delicate balance of nature and the importance of preserving such ecosystems. it offers a glimpse into the biodiversity of malaysia s highlands showcasing the resilience of life in this misty realm. visiting this natural masterpiece is not just a trek it s a journey into a dreamlike landscape that leaves an indelible mark on the hearts of those fortunate enough to explore its wonders.

couldn t see anything at the top because of the fog but it s a very pretty forest. overpriced though because it s a tiny walk. we were told we could not go up by moped but we did because no jeep would take us and no one said anything.

view from the newly opened watch tower was great. temperatures could drop to 17degrees so be prepared with warm clothing. joined cs tours for 60rm sunrise tour which included sunrise along the mountain mossy and boh tea. overall experience was great.

the unmissable destination for the hikers out there that you would definitely have mossy forest on top of the cameron highlands travel guide list let dive into a calm and peaceful hike through one of the oldest forest in malaysia that covered with mist and variety kinds of moss and flora. challenge yourself to go on top of the mount brinchang the second highest mountain of the country situated at 2 032 metres above sea level the forest is normally close on the rainy season due to the landslide and even if there is a path on the maps you re not allow to drive up there every car motorcycle rental will have a disclaimer on this best way to visit is to go with a guide tour that normally include boh tea centre by the end route
great little tail with a lot of stairs but well worth going. please don t touch the mod that you see. the forest it 200 million years old.

love this place. the new railings are nicely done. there is a new toilet near to the entrance free for use. i dunnmind to pay for the admission as long as they maintain this place well. i ll come again for sure.
lovely forest and very happy to see they re working to try protect the environment. a lot of steep hills to drive up to get there.

not worth it.. expect to walk a short wooden walkway behind many other people. it s crowded small and to be honest the forest is nothing special. you won t miss anything if you don t go. the views are okay but you have to wait in line to get up the observation tower. the tour operators will drop off many tourists already in the early hours.

amazing experience and can recommend to everyone! we had a guided tour incl. mossy forest tour company eco cameron guide hemee . our guide had detailed knowledge on mossy forest and explained us the fauna ecosystem and further details. for those looking for an easy walk or with family you enter into the area open for visit after buying the tickets foreigners 30 riggits locals 10 . there are all around wooden boardwalks which make the walk very easy. the whole trail is around 600m. for those wanting to have hiking adventure there is another 7 km trail for which you have to get a permit in advance. it must be guided hence you may want to do it with one of the guided tour companies they have to apply for that permit as well . we did not know this in advance so could not organise short notice. but i hope this info may help you

i enjoyed the lush greenery of the forest and the walk along the trail was relaxing and enjoyable as the route was along well maintained wooden paths with high railings. the mist that appeared frequently during my casual 1.5 hour walk added an eerie but exciting sense to the environment. we were told that due to the recent landslides along the route to the attraction private vehicles are not permitted to drive up the route and we hired a private operator to drive us up on his four wheel drive vehicle.
highly recommended! it s future into brinchang boh get into local tour package to skip the driving! air is super clean and it s pretty chill in there can spend around an hour there.

highly recommend to have a visit there. the price for malaysian is rm10 for adult rm5 senior citizen. the only thing that block tourist for going is the slope of the road if driver are not experienced it is pretty risky. i have saw some local guide driving up there but i am not sure what is their price.

the moment i came here got notice said it close from november till end of january due to monsoon season and maintenance routine. yeah i feel meh but since no one was here that day i just breaking the rule. im sorry guys since i already reach here. i enter with precaution on my surrounding for my own safety. love the view the wind blow so hard and its raining. be careful on your step during wet day. i not go too deep so i must come to mossy forest again when it open for public.

i wouldn t bother visiting mossy forest. i think the actual rainforest that tanah rata is surrounded with is much nicer and there is much more to see in terms of fauna and flora. the entrance fee was 30 ringgits but there is literally no facilities there. toilets are two holes in the ground there is not even a proper sink to wash your hands. the forest is tiny and the boardwalk is too narrow so you have to keep letting people pass by and the watchtower is also very small so not many people can fit in. to get up to the entrance it s a very steep hill and the road is also narrow only suitable for 4x4 types of cars. i would recommend to go up on a rented car.
paying 30 ringgit for a 10 15 min walk is just too much there are better views and trails available for lower price. don t drive up to the entrance with the scootes as you ll get a fine.
recommended place to visit . go for a walk in forest with green environment.

the road is bumpy and narrow. it is not easy to go up with car. we booked a tour with local agent and going up with the 4 wheels drive as shown. those in the car were all foreigners except us. for those interested to go up it is recommended to go up with 4 wheels drive. this place is beautiful with the green moss. the wooden walkway is under construction when we went in feb this year. it was free entry that time. if you like nature this is the place for you.
took a 4wd tour package to mossy forest and it is really worth it. the mossy forest observation deck is the main highlight of our trip. such a unique forest lush and green.

worth the drive up the narrow winding road to the entrance of mossy forest. if you have 4x4 that would be a big plus. otherwise drive carefully. if you intend to do jungle trail please apply your permit in advance.

the climb using a sedan or mpv is not advice although you still can reach the spot. better to rent taxi or van or 4wd. your car engine will have to work very very hard if its not 4wd the climb is very steep at some spot 50 60 . worth the effort.

mossy forest are very unique forest reserves found in very few countries it will being locations similar seen in lord of the rings movie.. you need have trek shoes and rain jacket to navigate through as most part of year it would be wet.. recommended seaosn to visit between october to january. this is part cameron highlands which located 2hr drive from kuala lampur.. you will have bus service from kl to cameron highlands quite frequently or else you can hire a taxi which is expensive option compared to bus trip
mossy forrest is a must go when you at cameron highlands for at least once. suggest to take 4x4 wd car to go in. worth for the price. trust me. rm25 per adult. may contact ah han tour guide.

i don t get all these 5 star reviews the mossy forest seems to be closed for now. we drove up there by scooter and were stopped by a landslide that destroyed the road. back at our hotel the staff confirmed that it was indeed closed. seems to be something shady going on with tour operators that do still get you there. but that s just speculation to explain all the recent positive reviews. either way not worth your time if you re going in solo with your own scooter car!
short 2.5km hike turned into a challenging hike when the rain start pouring down from beginning till the end in and out on a slippery muddy trail.

nice walk from slip road all the way up to mossy forest. can opt to drive up your self. encounter road closure . believe it s setup by private entity due to recent land slide. called and check with pahang forestry department was told no issue to access.

it was an awesome experience to reach the view point. the breeze was chilling and the view was every worth for the climb. though the road is very bad at some place i would recommend to keep patience and proceed. do keep a jacket. the view point is 2000m asl.

magnificent piece of nature. i took a 4x4 tour for rm 30 and paid another rm 10 to enjoy the mossy forest. well managed and maintained by the forest department. it will be closed for 4 month starting 10 august 2020.

i love this cool forest so much! foggy and windy staircase all the way easy to walk through this forest. however it s kinda slippery after raining watch out. the tree trunk here are just like sponge really cool! by the way it s the way leads you to the irau hill too.

warning! if you plan to drive there on your own please be forewarned. the road heading up winding and often single lane. as you head closer the road gets narrower and nasty. as i approached closer the road get almost undrivable. and signboard reads do not enter. the forest ranger drove past saying that we can actually drive in as they are now open for public but car must be robust. unless you drive a jeep or 4wd strongly recommend to take a tour. pay a bit more to get a piece of mind. alternative suggestion it s still ok to drive up to sungai palas boh tea farm. park your car there. negotiate with driver to pick you up. can get a good price at rm30 per person.
a feel of real dense forest with unique feature of moss on almost trunks of all trees. went there on week days hence very few peoples and was very peaceful.

hikers gear up... this is must visit place for nature lovers and adventurers in cameron highland. you have to take permission to do trekking here which would be given either by the cab drivers agents or by the local government. you have to apply in advance for the permission. this place is shared between two states pahang and perak. for people who don t like to trek you can walk on the wooden platform laid which is very safe. you also have place to sit and relax in between and shelters if it rains.

it s a bit of an adventurous drive. cars with wide body and low body beware as the road to mossy is small land full of potholes. but the forest itself is very nice. go in the morning when the mist is still about and the people are still asleep and you will feel the majestic of the forest.
the drive up is a little technical. the forest is foggy and chilly. please bring enough jackets and warm drinks
beautiful view needs an experienced driver to get up there no need for 4x4 . steep staircases up to the peak. free entry. don t go when it s raining. no toilets at the top.

i was very disappointed from the mossy forrest very dirty and overcrowded place. if you really want to see the real beauty of the malaysian rainforest you should take the jim thompson trail together with the guide madi

really worth a trip out to visit the most forest. not too crowded mostly tour groups. i would recommend the eco cameron tour group as it was good value for money and you stop at other places. it is not too strenuous but there are a few ups and downs.
be careful when going there. bumpy road. recommended to use 4x4 car or call a tour guide. the view up there is so spectacular. serious. have fun

the forest is very pretty but not worth the price for foreigners if you are a hiker. we paid 90 myr entrance each and would have had to pay 120 myr return taxi. we opted to take a taxi to the forest 80 myr one way after the taxi driver had to attempt the hills 3 times we managed to do the the 30 min trail inside the forest . we then opted to hike down to birchang via trial 1 which was just as pretty as the actual mossy forest. the taxi back from birchang to tanah rata was only 15 myr. on a sidenote taxi drivers will try to discourage you from hiking up or down from the mossy forest but the trail was very well marked and maintained. worth the visit if you are not a avid hiker or have time constraints.
nice place to explore. however the route is relatively too short as we have travel a long way through up the hill by car .

you can drive all the way up 4km in about 15 20 minutes road is however really steep many holes and one lane only. we did it with a simple sedan car but were thinking to turn back at few places. avoid in case of heavy traffic. once up at parking for about 20 cars you need to pay 30 myr per person in order to access a 500m mossy forest walk and a tower with an amazing view. all in a very good condition . you need a license though and a two week notice if you want to continue walking to mount gunung irau. they only let in 60 tourists a day shame! . otherwise you can hike up or down both road or trail 1 path no guide needed bring mosquite repellent water and good shoes. get ready to get dirty if its wet.

incredible. beware the road is tight and rutted. do not take heavy vehicles up here large vans buses . we did it in a toyota vios carefully. it s a long road and although beautiful i wouldn t walk it. the boardwalk is under construction but usable. the scenery is amazing. the cloud and moss makes everything wet and an unusual ecosystem i ve never seen before. there s a tower to climb above the canopy you can t see due to cloud but that s the point . it s short and worth it a must see if you re in the cameron highlands and enjoy scenery. watch out for cars and farmers on the way and take your rubbish with you.

not mossy enough nowadays perhaps due to uncontrolled visits and irresponsible behaviours of some visitors who stepped on the moss to get that instagram picture. access is now controlled and charged. rm30 adult for non malaysians and rm10 for malaysians. public toilet is available at the entrance but unfortunately no soap available. the readily made pathway is practical and easy enough for everyone to do some hiking. a watch tower has also been constructed enabling visitors to get a breathtaking view of the tropical forests. the pathway leads to a registration booth hut that controls access to the peak of gunung irau however access has been stopped because the gate was locked and no one was manning the registration booth hut. there are rest huts along the way to allow visitors to catch their breath. some stairs are quite steep with tall steps so caution is needed.

another scam! you need to pay 30rm for entering and only then you learn that the track is closed! no info whatsoever near the ticket counter. you can walk on the wooden platforms and get to the viewpoint. that s it! the track is closed till 31 jan 2020.
small but pretty walk through the mossy forest great views up top. some large spiders!
very good place for healing first time reached up here by my own car. no need to waste rm30 pax to pay 4x4 but make sure your car is fit. enjoy the view!
narrow roads going up some in bad conditions. not for small underpowered car. but you ll be rewarded by well designed pathways to mossy forest and observation tower.
never hurried yourselves once you re here. get yourselves indulge with the weather and serenity in mossy forest. of course you have to reach there early to avoid crowd. do visit if you re in cameron.

it s a beautiful misty mossy forest. there are information boards describing the plants and their features in english and malay. i personally think the trip will be more interesting with a tour guide during the trail journey. the trails are easy just that the staircase might be difficult for elderly. but as long as one do not have knee problem it shouldn t be a big problem. i saw many aunties aged around 50 . my bf s mum is 65 no regular exercise but she manage to walk all parts of the trails. entrance ticket rm10 u need tour agency to bring u up to the entrance by truck which costs rm25. if you drive your private car up your car will be blocked by a guard at the junction before u reach the entrance. i would advise not to drive up yourself even without the regulation because the road is terrible. kinda disappointed because i didn t spot any pitcher plant. perhaps the forest is not that natural anymore.
beautiful place. top of the cameron highlands. before going there please keep sports shoes. because there is damp and mud. you will have to hiking a bit. it is the border of phang and perak
nice scenary and well maintained hiking trail.. some stairs might be a bit slippery during rainy season... wear suitable shoes ro avoid slipping and injuries.

currently closed due to landslide. we didn t see any signs or check online so drove most of the way up only to find the road closed about 3 km from the entrance. the drive up was spectacular if a little crowded for a single track road. we were in a 2 wheel drive car and managed to get to the end of the road but in low gear. was still worth the drive for the lovely views and saw tea planters working hard on the way down

recently drove up to the mountaintop to visit mossy forest but unfortunately when we arrived around 5 6pm it was already closed. the operating hours were not clearly stated online and the contact number provided was unreachable. and there is now an entrance fee to enter the forest. on the same day there were other tourists who specifically made the trip to visit only to be told that the forest was closed. this experience was truly disappointing.
loved the mossy forest! definitely my favourite place in cameron highlands you can see different types of plants that you ve never seen before it really looks like a fairytale

wonderful experience but quite tricky to figuer out how to get there. you need to go to the boh tea plantation shop restaurant and look out for a toyota highlux near the parking area to take you up to the mossy forest area. no public allowed to go up by themselves.

go by touroperator! it s not a save road to drive yourselves. it s marked as off road so in case of any issues the insurance won t cover the damage. beautiful forest which you can view by following a boardwalk way. unfortunately due to the negative side of tourism all other walkways are closed which means there s only one entry left. it s cool up the hill approximately 2000 2200m. you ll have beautiful views and can find many trees flowers orchids mosses and other plants that you can t find anywhere else in malaysia. or at least not in this form. worth the visit!
cloud jungle. moss and lichens. view obscured by.... clouds. hard to get but we managed to do it with out own simple rented car. the views till and from there are spectacular.
most cooling place and a great bridge walk. never missed it.. my every year place to go up n relax and wait for the wet fog to come.
easy boardwalk through tiny patch of nondescript forest. some good views.

there is a few reasons why i give 1 star which i have stated below disclaimer i did not enter mossy forest but please finish reading my review to know the situation there now so for some context previously you could drive up or take a jeep provided by tour agencies to enter mossy forest but now they ban the former. there are now 2 blockages both with plastic road barricades blocking the road from normal traveller driving up. hence we tried to hike up from the 1st blockage to the ticketing area. the journey was about 15 20mins hike 1 way 1.3km . however when we arrived at the ticketing area they said that the ticket is only available for touring agencies and closed to general public. they claimed it is due to maintenance. so if you want to go in please go through a tour agency. so the reason for the 1 star is 1. there was no signs or information on tickets being available to tour agencies only anywhere or near the blockage areas. i don t see it in the internet too maybe i missed it 2. the entrance to mossy forest is sort of monopolised by tour agencies ? 3. staff attitude but i won t say it here because it s not the main point. 4. if the mossy forest is under maintenance why can tour agency go in? i failed to understand the ticketing office s reasoning. so do you still want to go into mossy forest? if yes there are a few points to take note 1. we asked a travel agency on the jeep pricing and they stated that it is rm70 80 pax for 1 round trip. 2. make sure to get the schedules how long you can stay at mossy forest from tour agency as some varies. 3. you can visit klook for a pricing on mossy forest boh tea plantation etc but i am not sure whether klook s ticket allows you to enter to mossy forest. also to check if the pricing includes the jeep to travel up to mossy forest because the road to mossy forest was hella steep and i wouldn t recommend you to walk up if you re not really active fit. so i would suggest you check and make sure on these 2 matter as well.
nice place to see the mossy in the forest in the month of july 2018. cool and wet. good place for study tropical jungles. a borderline place where perak and pahang share the beautiful mossy.

love this place remote from sound of traffic. so calm and relax feel. need permit if want to go further hiking in the jungle. rm10 per person for local. rm30 for foreigner. about 6km from main road so need transportation to reach this place. better if using 4x4 vehicle because the road not so good. but on my visit i ve seen perodua myvy and proton wira reach this place.

open 9am be sure online buy ticket first and arrive there before 9am. the road to here is narrow and challenging. private car with big cc 1.5 above can drive up here yourself. many hikers and 4x4 vehicle arrive around 9am parking space not big be sure to arrive before them no toilet open to use if no water. the environment is chill in the morning.

the mossy forest trail is a bit hard to find on your own. but you can join a tour or get a guide to take you there. the walk is not very far... and take only about 30.min to finish. personally i would skip this.

for those who like hiking you can come to mossy forest because this place is so amazing !! the weather here is cold . i suggest to not to drive up yourself even with regulations because the road is quite adventure . last but not least the view was so nice and beautiful !

trying a self drive up at about 60 there roads leading uphill says it s closed. on the left of this sign there s a mud water puddle which i m unsure of its depth. while on downhill journey few 4x4 on the way up..

very cold and nice place to chill. advice to get 4wd as the road condition is getting worst and worst when u driving up to mossy forest entrance. not advisable for beginner driver to drive up here as it is quiet dangerous to drive up here.

unique place and a must visit very nice walkway in the jungle at the peak of gunung brinchang. managed to drive up most of the road was ok except 1 or 2 areas . single lane only so if another vehicle is approaching you will have a problem. mostly pick up trucks or jeeps use the road. entry fee rm10 for malaysians.
hope this trail can preserved both by hikers and the authorities. its beautiful here and it feels like you are in a different world. i truly enjoy it very much

very mystical windy cold and wet. exciting place to visit up in the mountains. you can probably get more out of it if you get to go on a trip out into the jungle and not just along the stairs. but the environment and natures are fantastic. interesting when the guide tells you a little about the flora and nature.

do not come here. it s not worth it. it s 30myr for a 10mins hike on an average at best boardwalk. it s way more fun and better views to just do one of free jungle trails. the drive up is also quite inconvenient and dangerous. i wouldn t come here again.

we book 2h package via shopee mossy forest tour transfer package cameron highlight. adult rm25 and rm15 for children between 3 to 10yo. package inc pickup from nova hotel 1 hour free and easy walk at mossy forest. pls note that the package exclude entry tickets into mossy forest.

tbh as a tourist from europe i m not sure this forest and cameron highlands in general is worth the five hour drive from kuala lumpur. we spent three nights here without doing anything significant or remarkable. it was pleasant and cool yes but nothing special.

mossy forest is located at gunung brinchang in cameron highlands at a height of 2032m above sea levels. the moss provides a green thick coated layer to the branches and tree trunks. the moss was soft and moist. i have been to cameron highlands many times but mossy forest was my 1st visit and was fascinated by this unique natural environment.

mossy forest is one of main attraction included in either full day tour or half day tour package by land rover. we joined local tour and it s the first stop one local guided us in a group to hike to the peak view point with some explanation during the path. it was such fascinating ambience inside the forest cold temperature morning fog bird chirps huge trees mostly covered by moss few plants from particular varieties and be prepared of dirty shoes since the trails were muddy. overall it s amazing experience for everyone visiting cameron.

people also ask what is special about mossy forest? just like its name it is filled with trees and all sorts of flora covered with layers of moss. you ll also get the chance to encounter exotic plants such as ferns spices orchids and even medicinal plants that grow wildly in the moist environment.
for nature lover.cool area with average temperature of 19 c.unfortunately the road condition is suitable for 4x4
the view at the top of mossy forest is amazing! it s cold so layer up and also hire the appointed tour guides to reach the peak. it s definitely worth the view

really recommended. entrance fee rm10 per person for malaysian. got toilet. better use 4wd than normal car as the road is not in a good condition. the scenery is really breathtaking foggy and fantasy look like. charge for shooting is rm500 idk maybe for wedding photography

beautiful walk up the mountain. easily doable. most of the locals are willing to take you down if you are too tired to walk back down. on the way there are plenty of beautiful plants and animals to spot. the entrance to the actual forrest on top is 30myr which i found a bit high for what it is.

nice track along the designated pathway. however to get here it is advisable to hire guide or 4wd vehicles as the road condition is not suitable for those who love their car. messy bumpy and dangerous road towards the entrance.
view absolutely breathtaking! for those who love nature i truly recommend this place. a great place to heal yourself after a long day.
this place is very beautiful and i very excited to comes again in this places

very nice place for public to visit while in cameron highland. i advice to come as early 7am to see such thick cloud and very cold feel. i come during weekdays and arrived here at 8am. just very few people. when i start going down at 9am people going here start to increase and i drive down little bit slow cause need to excuse people drive up. better come early as you can to this place to prevent so many people especially during weekends.

nice place to visit. to get here it is basically going towards the sungai palas boh tea plantation until you reach a split on the road. obviously by then follow the sign that says mossy forest . the terrible thing is actually the drive towards the forest. the roads are broken as heck. potholes more than actual road i m exaggerating but it definitely is terrible a lot of the way . the mossy forest itself was actually very pleasant. 5 5 stars if the roads are fixed. do tell who to write to about the roads. i m more than happy to write in to the authorities to have it fixed
stunning views easy walk lots of little details to take in. 30 rm is a little pricey but not too bad.
great views and impressive forest and vegetation. the place is accessible by private car but the road is narrow steep bumpy and not paved.

it s a scam. i rented a motorbike and parked like 2 km before the forest because when i rented it they told me that o couldn t go near. so i walked like 40 minutes. when i returned the bike they gave the company a fee of 20 euros because o rented where it wasn t allowed. the most important thing is that there is no sign or anything explaining from which point you cant continue with tje bike. so it s just a scam for local guides to charge extra to tourist to take them there.

currently aug 18 you can only do a short 300m walk the rest is officially closed due to landslide. if you re adventurous though you can also hike all the way 5 km? despite warning signs. there were no checks when we were there. can a sedan make it to the entrance? it is a bumpy road but yes it can. actually sedan might even work better than a 4x4 as the road is also very narrow. no facilities on top bring all you need with you.
the place is temporarily closed till jan 2019. we still manage to go in by walking. glad to discover the hidden beauty!
that s place must go! if come to cameron highland. fresh air unique mossy no regret. place like stay
we booked through klook. mossy forest n half day tour at cameron highland. entry tickets is buy separately fyi. just sit back n relax the half day tour.

great cold place where you can feel the could as u r eating it. they have a canopy walk. it s cold slightly than the rest of cameron. roads are quite dangerous 1 lane both way road. potholes is at max level. broken roads. only pro drivers should dare to drive here.
if someone going to camron highland then this place is must to visit. one can go there with any car but expert driver require as there is very narrow road.

road condition is bad recommend to use jeep truck rental costed us rm120 per truck fitting 12 pax . rm10 entrance fee for malaysian rm30 non malaysian. easy walk on nice platforms. go before 9am or after 5pm and there may be no one collecting entrance fee.

i m not rating the place but the info on google. i don t understand why it still says it open when it s not. the place is closed indefinitely probably due to the monsoon season but the notice is right at the top and not at the bottom so you don t have to make the difficult drive to the top. plus the phone number displayed in google is not contactable
such a mystical experience! we loved admiring the forest especially the pitcher plants!

mount brinchang and also mount irau is currently closed. for 30mr you ll get just a short walk through rainforest boardwalk! . can be done in 10 mins. there is a viewing platform so you can at least take a picture if you re lucky and not completely covered by clouds. for someone who never seen rainforest it might be good experience. for me it simply wasn t worth it. i have seen way more impressive forests on vancouver island. this should be for free.
need to upgrade the trailwalk. some parts a bit dangerous as parts of the metal net cover on the stairs has tear off and the railing on the side starts to fall off.
go with your car to the forest and do the walk. its really nice to do.
a real great preserve area. highly recommend get permit to experience trekking in mossy forest.

there s unfortunately a dual price policy on the entrance ticket for foreigners and malaysians people. this is regarded as unfair in international standards. aren t we all equal when we visit this place? by the way talking about the boardwalk there s a 600 metres long way that wanders around the forest. if you are into the high altitude equatorial flora this is a good place to visit. amazing shaped trees wild orchids ferns and moss. i wouldn t recommend you go on a cloudy day because there s literally nothing to see beyond a few meters. but on a sunny day it s worth it. the road uphill is an absolute enchantment sunrays through the foliage makes the colors magical. i gave only 4 stars because of the dual pricing.

hiking through the mossy forest is a unique experience unlike other typical hiking trails in malaysia. hiking to mount irau is not advisable for beginners with a somewhat moderate level of difficulty. at a modest pace it takes around 3 hours to the summit and 3 hours return. gloves are recommended as the terrain requires you to often use tree branches to support yourself.

this is an unmissable attraction when visiting cameron highlands! if you re a nature lover you ll fall in love with this place completely. the scenery is absolutely stunning and the view from 2 000 meters above sea level is truly breathtaking. the entrance fee is rm30 for adults and rm15 for children and it s well worth the memorable experience. however be aware that driving to this location can be quite challenging if you re using your own car it s best suited for experienced drivers. personally i enjoyed the drive and the adventure of reaching the top.

the mossy forest in cameron highlands consistently receives overwhelmingly positive reviews with visitors highlighting its unique and enchanting atmosphere. here are some of the key positive aspects frequently mentioned mystical and ethereal ambiance reviewers often describe the mossy forest as a truly magical and otherworldly place. the dense mist low hanging clouds and the way sunlight filters through the thick canopy create a mystical and ethereal atmosphere that feels like stepping into a fantasy novel. words like enchanting surreal magical and dreamlike are commonly used to describe the experience. unique biodiversity and lush vegetation the forest is praised for its incredible biodiversity and the abundance of unique flora including various species of moss ferns orchids pitcher plants and medicinal herbs. nature lovers and botany enthusiasts are particularly captivated by the dense and varied vegetation that thrives in the cool moist environment. excellent for hiking and nature walks many reviewers enjoy the hiking trails within the mossy forest particularly the boardwalk path on gunung brinchang which allows for easy exploration of this unique ecosystem. the trails offer opportunities for gentle strolls as well as more challenging hikes catering to different fitness levels. the experience of walking amidst the moss covered trees and listening to the sounds of nature is highly valued. cool and refreshing climate the cool and often misty climate of the mossy forest provides a refreshing escape from the heat and humidity of lower altitudes. this adds to the overall pleasantness of the visit. breathtaking scenery and photography opportunities the unique landscape of the mossy forest offers stunning and dramatic scenery making it a paradise for photographers. the interplay of light and mist the textures of the moss covered trees and the overall atmosphere provide countless captivating shots. educational value visitors appreciate the opportunity to learn about the unique ecosystem of a cloud forest and the adaptations of the plants and animals that live there. guided tours often enhance this educational aspect. relatively accessible while located at a higher altitude and sometimes requiring a 4x4 vehicle to reach the very top of gunung brinchang the boardwalk section of the mossy forest is generally considered accessible to most visitors. overall unforgettable experience many reviewers conclude that visiting the mossy forest is an unforgettable and highly recommended experience when in cameron highlands offering a unique and captivating encounter with nature. in summary the positive reviews of the mossy forest consistently highlight its magical atmosphere unique biodiversity excellent hiking opportunities refreshing climate stunning scenery and overall unforgettable experience for nature lovers and adventurers alike.

sipadan island is a renowned diving destination located in the celebes sea off the east coast of sabah malaysia. known for its breathtaking natural beauty vibrant marine life and pristine coral reefs sipadan has gained international recognition as one of the top diving spots in the world. one of the most notable features of sipadan island is its incredible biodiversity. the waters surrounding the island are teeming with a wide variety of marine species including turtles reef sharks barracudas and an abundance of colorful fish. divers can witness large schools of fish stunning coral formations and even the occasional encounter with larger marine creatures like hammerhead sharks or manta rays. the diving conditions at sipadan are generally excellent with excellent visibility and warm waters throughout the year. the island itself is relatively small offering limited accommodations which helps to maintain its pristine environment. the dive sites around sipadan such as barracuda point turtle cavern and south point offer diverse and unforgettable underwater experiences. in addition to diving sipadan island offers other activities for visitors. snorkeling is a popular option for those who prefer to stay closer to the surface providing ample opportunities to observe the colorful marine life and coral reefs. the island s picturesque beaches and crystal clear waters also make it an excellent location for relaxation and sunbathing. it s worth noting that access to sipadan island is restricted to protect its delicate ecosystem. the number of daily permits is limited and visitors are required to obtain a permit in advance. to secure a permit it is advisable to book with a licensed dive operator or resort well in advance. overall sipadan island offers a unique and unforgettable diving experience for enthusiasts from around the world. with its stunning underwater landscapes rich marine biodiversity and commitment to conservation it continues to be a must visit destination for divers and nature lovers alike.

sipadan island is a small island located in the sulawesi sea near the east coast of borneo. administratively the island is part of the malaysian state of sabah. sipadan island is widely recognized as one of the world s best diving locations. the island s uniqueness lies in its extraordinary underwater biodiversity with steep coral cliffs that drop as far as 600 meters from the seabed. its underwater beauty includes diverse marine life various species of large fish such as barracuda reef sharks and snappers. green turtles the island is an important habitat for green turtles where divers can often encounter dozens of them in a single dive. healthy coral reefs clusters of colorful and beautiful coral reefs. sipadan island was the subject of a territorial dispute between indonesia and malaysia for decades until in 2002 the international court of justice ruled that sovereignty over sipadan island and the nearby island of ligitan belonged to malaysia. this decision was based on the principle of effective management where malaysia was deemed to have already implemented concrete administrative and utilization activities on the two islands. in an effort to preserve its ecosystem the malaysian government imposed strict limits on the number of visitors allowed to dive or snorkel each day. furthermore accommodation is prohibited on the island. visitors wishing to enjoy the beauty of sipadan typically stay overnight on nearby islands such as mabul or kapalai and travel to sipadan by speedboat.
a beautiful marine reserve with intense animal activity.

god s aquarium photographer leonko0219 scuba guide john9810 world renowned oceanographer jacques cousteau once hailed sipadan island as an unspoiled work of art. divers have also voted it one of the world s top five dive sites. sipadan offers year round diving but the ideal time is from april to december when the waters are calmest and clearest. the water temperature remains comfortable year round ranging from 26 c to 30 c. to protect this beautiful and pristine sipadan the national park has a limited number of diving spots with only 120 allowed per day. malaysia semporna sipadan divetrip scuba islandhopping island divingpeople islandhoppingtravel aodoulife malaysia sipadan seprona diving scubadiving divingtrip sipadanisland

a dream island for scuba divers located at the westernmost tip of malaysia. currently overnight stays are not permitted on this small island. it appears that the malaysian military is permanently stationed there. currently there seems to be a daily limit on the number of visitors due to nature conservation efforts. you arrive here by boat from nearby accommodations and what s surprising upon arrival is the color of the sea. up to the pier the water is shallow and calm but beyond the pier it s a deep blue ocean. and beyond the pier there s a drop off of over 600 meters. in other words it suddenly becomes a bottomless cliff. as you drift leisurely along the edge of the drop off manta rays and other marine life pass by far below. you can make a detour to the entrance of a cave that seems to be a sea turtle graveyard and if you re lucky you might encounter a barracuda tornado. the shallow waters of the island are a paradise for fish and you can encounter schools of various large fish. it s a fantastic diving spot.
you ll feel so excited if you go diving!

we lost the sipadan and ligitan islands because british malaya once built a lighthouse and a turtle conservation area and after malaysia was freed from british control they began to care for the islands while the dutch who colonized indonesia only stopped by the islands and then abandoned them. so now the one who contributes to the development of the two islands is malaysia. it is a bitter reality but that is what happened.

i wanted to try diving in the beautiful ocean so i asked a local if sipadan island or mabul island would be better for diving and headed to sipadan island. there s a 600m navy drop off and you can encounter sea turtles. those without a licence can only snorkel. a malaysian tourist also warned me to be careful of abu sayams.

it s a long time ago but i went there twice. the first time was in march 2000 and the next time was during golden week in 2001. it s a fantastic diving spot. it s such an attractive tourist destination that i would have returned every year if i had the money and time. i stayed there for about six days both times. the second time there were reports of pirates and soldiers with machine guns were guarding the island so i knew i wouldn t be going back. the first time the guests were all gentlemanly and normal but the second time after the new year i was disappointed to find several japanese guests with poor manners. some were accustomed to resorts and were arrogant toward the local staff while others threw cigarette butts directly on the beach. maybe they were overindulging since they were off work and overseas. the staff are a little more open minded and willing to talk to you if you re a repeat visitor. the local staff told me at the time that they had a hard time finding a good job due to what seemed to be religious discrimination. were the attitudes and behavior of some japanese guests justified? staff and guests are all human beings but hotel staff are not slaves. this resort diving experience left a bad taste in my mouth and made me think about human rights issues wondering if it was just adding fuel to the fire of already simmering discontent over the gap between rich and poor. the natural beauty of the island and the ocean is truly magnificent but how do the people compare? so perhaps it s no surprise that the island was subsequently closed.

sipadan island is an oceanic island near semporna a town on the east coast of sabah malaysia. sipadan island is the only oceanic island in malaysia made of limestone with an elevation of 600 meters above sea level. 1 sipadan island is known as one of the best scuba diving destinations in the world. according to the results of a public vote issued by the sole organization for the new 7 wonders of nature voting committee sipadan island was listed among the 77 most magical and best landforms in the world in the year ending july 2009

aquarium of god a pearl hidden in the world once voted the world s premier diving destination sipadan island in malaysia is a unesco marine environment reserve. fishing and hunting are prohibited and the protected ecosystem fosters a diverse array of tropical fish abundant marine life and coral reefs making it a place worth visiting again and again. here you can see sea turtles larger than a person schools of bumphead parrotfish barracuda storms battling the current swarms of hundreds of jackfish and whitetip reef sharks patrolling the island. daily limits on the number of divers are in place and currently only the military is stationed on the island. for a diving trip consider staying at nearby mabul island for a five day four night itinerary a truly worthwhile experience.

from the moment we arrived the staff were very kind and helpful. staying for two nights we wished we could have stayed longer. the room was great extremely comfortable and felt part of the ambiance of the building. the food was great having tried resort breakfasts prior the breakfast here was the best quality. pool access was great just wished we had more time to try the pool. walking around the grounds and learning about it s history the mansion has so many things to discover. i ve never felt so comfortable in a new place so quickly and i must highlight the staffs hospitality to us and other guests. would highly recommend anyone staying here not just because it s unesco status but because it s actually an amazing stay for anyone visiting georgetown.

a great experience to stay in a heritage hotel as blue mansion. the room is spacious and bathroom well renovated. for those who seek to try out a hotel that is unusual and unique book yourself in one of 18 rooms available. the only

this is a review regarding the guided tour not the hotel. i made a reservation for the guided tour online fuss free experience. gather at the entrance 15 mins before the start of the tour and present the qr code to the staff they will allow you into the property. the group gathered at the hall area before the start of the tour and we can look around at our own pace while waiting. the hall looks very rich in history with elements of chinese and western culture. the tour guide was very humorous cracked jokes throughout the tour. he is very knowledgeable as well. the pace of the tour was just nice around one hour. i came in without expectations but was very surprised by the rich history of the property and the family behind it. highly recommend to join the tour will try to book a room when i come for holiday next time.

we stayed for a few days in both the qing suites and the blue mansion. both accommodations impress with their style design and the incredible attention paid by the staff. special thanks to anna and vincent! we enjoyed all the amenities the bar the delicious michelin starred dinner at indigo and the classic treatments at the traditional chinese medicine spa. you couldn t ask for a more pampering experience. we felt very comfortable and enjoyed every moment. the hotel is simply breathtaking.
