do not go if you re planning on walking in. the experience is only worth it if you get a guided tour. please do not go if you re expecting something like the peranakan museum this is as far removed from that as humanly possible for walk in guests. if you come in from the restaurant side they will tell you that you need to eat the equivalent of rm25 per person to exchange your receipt for a ticket to the museum there s no indication of this and we only found it out from their flustered staff who impatiently asked us to spend money at the restaurant. we found a discarded receipt from another customer and brought it to the reception on the entrance side only to find out that you can purchase a walk in ticket for rm25 too. no need to dine at the restaurant. already this felt like a cheap con. inside there are only 4 rooms to actually view historical artifacts and the rest of the location is used for the hotel bar gift shop and toilet. perhaps if you were staying here as a foreigner it would be a novel experience. as a local being hustled into spending money at the restaurant only to find a pretty location to take photographs and nothing more left a sour taste in the mouth. i guess spending rm25 and getting at least food for your troubles makes up for the barebones experience but i think there are more customer friendly ways to limit visitation. like simply denying entry and explaining that this is a guided tour only attraction clearly with big letters. i can tell the staff were quite tired of explaining to tourist about the t cs of entering the location and tourists from all over were clamoring into the restaurant to simply spend rm25 to meet the minimum spend.

we enjoyed staying in the heart of george town close to dining and attractions. we loved the renovation of the heritage building and the sofa bed in the living room separated by doors meant that 2 adults and 2 teenagers could each have their own space. beds were very comfortable towels fluffy and aircon very effective. the buffet breakfast at the mansion was generous and delicious and we also ordered the continental breakfast delivery twice for days where we were getting up early. the only reason not to give it 10 out of 10 is because the open courtyard design means that mosquitoes could get in easily. we did our best with mosquito coils and insect repellent. we did not find the ambient street noise or the call to prayer times problematic though they provided ear plugs . highly recommend the tijong townhouse for a unique stay in george town.

we loved this hotel its character and its staff. the restoration is stunning keeping so much of its history alive. the peeling paint and original furniture and fittings adds so much to the experience of staying here it felt more like being in someone s home than being in a hotel. the staff were all friendly and very helpful from booking taxis to opening the gift shop so we could buy a book before we left. getting free access to the tour was great and added to our understanding of its history.

we had the pleasure of staying at the qing suites a charming boutique hotel beautifully curated with a seamless balance of old world heritage and subtle modernity throughout. these suites once served as the servants quarters in earlier times for the famed residence located just opposite. the suites are generous in size featuring separate lounge areas and balconies or gardens that ooze comfort and a luxurious ambiance. the serene central courtyard is the heart of the hotel offering guests a peaceful escape from the hustle and bustle of the surrounding streets. the bar serves unique and delicious asian inspired cocktails and doubles as a lovely setting for dining. qing suites also houses an excellent traditional chinese medicine wellness centre which truly completed our stay. a big shout out to anna and mani whose attentiveness and warm hospitality made our experience unforgettable. a definite must try for anyone visiting penang it truly stands out from the usual big chain hotels check out my instagram photos wahwaheats

we had a wonderful room and excellent service. we stayed in the old kitchen room which had a jacuzzi and we were also able to relax in the space just outside our room. however our level of satisfaction might vary depending on the room type. the location was convenient and we really enjoyed the atmosphere at the bar. every member of the staff was kind and helpful. one downside was that the room felt a bit too dark. in addition the pool did not appear to be well maintained and felt dirty overall giving it a somewhat neglected and unclean impression so we chose not to use it. if the pool environment were improved this would be an even better place to stay. we would likely choose to stay here again when visiting penang.

we really loved this hotel. not only the place itself is full of history and located in the heart of georgetown old town but the rooms in qing suite were wonderfully refurbished with the finest taste. it was quiet and very comfortable and the staff was perfect as well as the food.

the blue mansion was one of the nicest small boutique hotels we ve ever had the good fortune to stay in. we were in the old kitchen room for 3 nights. the room was huge great air con very quiet and plenty of places to put everything. comfortable bed and great linens. there is a soaking tub that heats up at 4pm which is a lovely perk after a day of walking around in the heat. very friendly and helpful staff. perfect location near everything in georgetown but on a quiet street.

visiting and staying at the cheong fatt tze mansion was an absolute highlight of my short vacation in penang. the iconic indigo blue architecture is breathtaking and every corner of the mansion reflects a rich blend of chinese and european influences steeped in history. the staff were warm attentive and genuinely passionate in sharing the story of the house which made the experience feel personal rather than touristy. dining at indigo was a delight and wandering the mansion during quieter moments felt like stepping back in time. while it is a heritage property rather than a modern luxury hotel its charm character and sense of place make it truly special and well worth the visit. did i mention it has its own indoor bar as well as live jazz music at cafe mangga too?

this hotel was originally built in the 1890s was home to chinese tycoon cheong fatt tse. it blends traditional chinese architecture with british colonial elements featuring distinctive indigo blue walls made from natural dye and featuring elaborate courtyards. it has extensively renovated to highlight the historical craftsmanship through visitor tours and stays. unlike seven terraces hotel nearby you are not allowed in unless you purchase the expensive rm25 tours or spend rm 25 pp at their cafe. the hotel is exclusive accommodation and caters for a niche market.

for my birthday my husband booked us a terrace suite in the newly renovated qing suites. everything inside and out was tastefully decorated modern minimal yet full of traditional charms. we enjoyed the lovely garden terrace just outside our door for morning coffee and evening drinks exclusive breakfast in suite and the colonial styled courtyard with a bar. everything was quiet luxury and felt exclusive. every staff was pleasant attentive and made sure we got everything we needed. the manager anna handpicked a beautiful rose bouquet together with delicious cakes to surprise me upon arrival a personal touch that made us feel welcomed and pampered. well done!

we had a 2 night stay at the hotel and we are very happy about the whole experience. the location is at the edge of the historical george town which is quiet but close to everything. the guest room is comfortable and full of character. the complimentary guided tour is insightful and fun. all of the staff are very friendly and helpful. especially the lady who seems to be the manger sorry i didn t catch her name . she squeezed us in the indigo restaurant when it was fully booked which was also a nice experience with great food and wine paring. overall would recommend to everyone who s visiting penang george town.

spent my birthday at the blue mansion and i d stay here again in a heartbeat. the experience was truly lovely every member of the team was attentive and friendly. special shout out to aseem and jalal for making my birthday extra special we booked a room with a jacuzzi and it was stunning huge space soaring ceilings and constant wow moments. at night the mansion feels like stepping back in time. there is a small pool in the property it s also lovely.

we loved staying at the blue mansion. it felt so much more special than the many big modern hotels in the same area. the staff were lovely and the food at breakfast was outstanding. just a couple of small things it wasn t explained well to us how to use our jacuzzi and there seemed to be a lot of mosquitos in our room even though we had all doors closed all the time. but still one of our favourite hotels on a long trip around asia

we absolutely loved our stay at the blue mansion even though it was a short one. we stayed in the beautiful han suite mandarin and it was everything we could have asked for elegant comfortable and full of character. traveling with an infant we were truly touched by the care and attention the staff showed to ensure we had a seamless experience. every request was handled with warmth and efficiency. the food was simply delicious i d highly recommend dining at their in house restaurant which perfectly complements the charm of the property. every corner of the blue mansion is picture perfect from the architecture to the courtyards it s a photographer s delight. if you re visiting penang this place is an absolute must stay for its heritage hospitality and heart.

can not fault the beautifully kept historical building the impeccable quality of the service and food. attention is paid to every aspect of the hotel. the original materials unfortunately do not prevent noise travelling from upstairs rooms to the ground floor bedrooms late at night early in the morning. perfect in all other respects.

visited with a self guided ticket. well worth the 25myr. self guided tickets seem to be mu h more readily available than thr group tour tickets. lovely mansion in terrific state of repair. find out about the seventh wife and the deathbed change of will in her and her baby son s favour! see where they filmed crazy rich asians and indochine.

a terrible experience one of the worst in penang. highly recommend to avoid. yes it s a luxurious and huge mansion steeped in history but it felt so commercialised as they converted at least half of the space into hotels. the other half was a mix of tourist space and dining spaces. frankly they shouldn t have opened it up to tourists as there was almost nothing to see very small and very boring. the penang peranakan museum was much more insightful and interesting. the available space for tourists to browse at the blue mansion is very limited and frankly boring. i get that the hotel residents got to have their privacy but a steep entry fee of 25rm did not warrant the many limited to hotel guests signs all around. as a visitor it seemed very hostile towards me. my photos included the menu for the restaurant indigo on level 2. please avoid coming here if you are a tourist and want to see more the available space to browse is honestly pathetic. felt like i wasted my time and money here. the self guided tour also needed you to download an app. good water cooler wifi at the mansion. bad expensive tickets small space to view.

stayed here for 3 nights on a mini tour.of malaysia. upon arriving you are blown away by the stunning building however the hotel lobby courtyard was full.of tourists waiting to go on the museum tour. this made the checkin process very distracted and not very special. our room taipu was very large but quite sparse with the adjoining bathroom being a little dated but thats part of the charm of the building. breakfast in the courtyard was stunning although the spread was limited but sufficient. reason for 3 5 is the very loud live music from next door s food market whoch only stopped at midnight. you could literally hear every word. the hotel provides ear plugs but they help. also there is limited natural light in the rooms as they fall back from the centre courtyard. recommend visiting for pictures of the house and story but consider the potential noise f you stay.

we loved our stay. the room was beautiful and the decor of the hotel so tranquil. we loved the attached cafe mangga with the live jazz too. the breakfast was good and all the staff excellent. the location was perfect i loved that we could easily walk everywhere. we also enjoyed using the pool.

our stay at cheong fatt tze mansion was nothing short of exceptional. as a well travelled family accustomed to staying in distinguished five star hotels across the world we can confidently say this property ranks among our absolute favourites. every detail was impeccably considered reflecting a rare level of care discretion and refinement. the service was gracious and intuitive with heartfelt thanks to ms anna and her team for their outstanding hospitality and attentiveness. the room itself was exquisitely appointed featuring sumptuous linens plush duvets and thoughtful welcome touches from bath tea to carefully selected amenities for both adults and children along with a well curated in room refreshment offering. a truly luxurious and memorable stay in an iconic heritage setting.

if you are still deciding where to stay in penang stop and book this place. ive just checked in and not even checked out yet but i just feel very connected with this place already. the history the people the architecture. amazing. the guided tours are offered at 11am and 3 30pm. it is a must. gives a good intro to the history of this mansion and the chinese man that is named rockerfller of the east . fascinating and partly what put penang on the map for so many. the painstaking restoration took place only in the early 90s and took over 6 years. wow. this is only a 18 room property and from what i gather they tend to sell out a lot. during the day it is accessible to public but if you are into architecture and history you could spend hours here. stop by the bar and get some unique chinese brandy and wines by the man established the chinese winery. i ve also stayed at the edison across the street. while it is charming and offers some unique amenities all day lounge but this one gets my vote.

very unique experience as you feel that this house is full of history the walls have seen a lot. i give 4 stars for rooms as i did not like that the room did not have windows 4 stars for service as i would ecpect people working in hotel be little bit more nicer would smile as well and the breakfast could use some upgrade but in overal solid ok or 4 from my side. also there is a nice cafe just around corner where live musoc performances happen every other day.

we drove more than 800km to visit the museum on a weekday morning only to be turned away because tickets were sold out. 11am sold out on a weekday? and we had to walk far under the sun to reach the destination. disappointing and we don t need a guided tour because i am well versed in peranakan history and collect their antiques for past 3 decades so can educate my guests.

the building itself is beautiful and historically significant and i understand why it attracts so much attention. however during our stay in a suite the jacuzzi was unusable due to a boiler issue. maintenance staff entered the room frequently which significantly affected privacy and comfort. we skipped breakfast and the tour and waited at the hotel for the situation to be addressed but communication between staff was inconsistent. even the next morning the issue did not seem to have been properly handed over. compensation such as afternoon tea was offered but it did not address the core problem. this is a property with great potential but hospitality is most evident when problems arise. in our case the response fell short. after posting this review i noticed another guest reported very similar issues in the same room just a few months earlier. this suggests the problem may not be isolated.

we went to do the audio tour and to have high tea in the courtyard area. this is a wonderful restoration and it has faithfully been restored to near its original grandeur from a nearly crumbling ruin. hats off to them. the audio tour was interesting although there are not too many rooms to see as the place is now a hotel. it was very touristy with so many people posing for those perfect instagram pics. pretty ridiculous. we were so glad we didn t book a stay here. we also forfeited our high tea deposit and left early as it was just a disappointing experience overall.

we spent 4 nights at the blue mansion. the rooms are extremely comfortable and the whole building has been beautifully restored. the staff were very helpful and the restaurant crew could not do enough for me in catering to my gluten free requirements. the high tea and breakfasts were great.

i enjoyed my stay at the blue mansion. this hotel a designated unesco site is steeped in history and timeless charm. after changing hands in 1989 the new owners carefully restored the once run down mansion preserving its original features and character. the rooms are generously sized and rich with a nostalgic old world atmosphere. every ground floor room opens into a private backyard with a small pond filled with goldfish leading directly to the pool area. the bar is fantastic too

the qing suites were breathtaking. from the moment you entered the ambiance was radiant but also calming. the check in process is smooth and efficient. the rooms are modern and very well thought out. each detail has been thought out carefully. the access to facilities is easy and the dining options are plentiful and convenient. the location is outstanding.

we didn t stay at the hotel but joined a private tour around the blue mansion and it was a fantastic experience. we were very lucky to have sally as our guide. she was knowledgeable engaging and truly captivating throughout the tour. sally brought the history to life explained everything clearly and made the experience both informative and enjoyable. highly recommended!

we had a great stay in this historic hotel. if you have the chance enjoy the property tour on friday morning the only one by the owner. the staff is super friendly and efficient. we also enjoyed very much the chef s dinner. a must place to visit and possibly stay when visiting georgetown.

when looking at coming to penang we looked at staying here and chose elsewhere. having then come for the tour and then lunch i think we will stay here next time. the food at indigo was truly amazing and you don t find waiters like rubel in many places anymore. what a truly wonderful experience. he catered to our every need on both a personal and professional level. a big shout out to the tour guide who catered for all nationalities with humour and interest that far surpassed other tours we have experienced. he actually made it worth listening to. we were dubious after our visit to the green mansion but our fears were soon allayed as he started and held the room with interest. yeap is an exceptional example of what a tour guide should be and rubel is the one off waiters you can only wish for.

i only did the tour and for me it was rather disappointing because you don t have access to nearly half of the rooms only hotel guests . the free audio guide is quite informative but it repeatedly contains advertisements. unfortunately the blue mansion was not a highlight for me.

staying at the blue mansion was an unforgettable experience. from the moment we arrived the staff were warm professional and incredibly welcoming. jalal and rubel in particular went above and beyond to make our stay comfortable and memorable truly exemplary in their service. the mansion itself is stunning a beautifully preserved piece of penang s history with so much character and charm. it was a privilege to stay in such a famous and iconic heritage property. every detail from the architecture to the atmosphere made our visit feel special. we would highly recommend the blue mansion to anyone visiting penang. it is more than just a hotel it s a chance to step into history and be cared for by a wonderful team.

the whole townhome at tjiong was just excellent. literally everything that we needed was provided for. we stopped to get laundry detergent on the way back one day only to realize that was already included too. the beds were comfortable and the historic old home was updated and renovated to be as comfortable as possible for modern travelers. i only wish there was more ways to prevent mosquitos as we did get bit a few times but i suspect that is not possible with how the home is designed. it was excellent having the blue mansion available to us as a brief walk or drive to tour or have breakfast in.

cheong fatt tze the blue mansion is a gem that needs loving restoration. our two rooms were dated with leaking pipes and the interior of the room was dark which led to poor housekeeping. it s a georgetown historical gem attracting visitors on their daily tours. hotel staff try their best to deliver with limited tools. on the very first day we got charged twice for drinks at the bar and went to reception the next day to the advice of the error. a snapshot was taken of my son s bank transaction to clarify the error. waited 24 hrs for a response. no explanation was offered. decided not to use the bar or restaurants for the rest of our stay as we had lost the trust of their guest s accounting system. the jacuzzi worked in our room with a leaking water pipe that trickled the entire length of the bathroom. my son s jacuzzi was faulty from day one and was switched on and off at the discretion of the hotel admin. please restore your rooms for a memorable stay at a historic location in penang. take responsibility and ownership from the top and the staff will follow and deliver an outstanding service.

this review is only for the self guided audio tour. the mansion itself is beautiful and i am glad it was restored. however the audio tour was 100 not worth it. first it was difficult to get tickets. they are very limited and the website makes a big deal out of limiting the number of people touring the mansion at one time. i thought that at least it would be nice on the tour and not crowded. boy was i wrong. there seem to be constant huge private tours going through which make it hard to see stuff though i did pick up a couple interesting tidbits from listening in on them. i m honestly not sure why they bother with the public tours at all as there is very little of the mansion that you actually get to see...just the main courtyard and a couple of rooms off of it that are set up like mini museums with some artifacts they found during the restoration and examples of materiels used during the restoration. they don t even have a lot of information about some of them. as for the audio you have to bring your own phone and headphones and download an app to get the audio. they couldn t even be bothered to have a real person record it. it was done in text to speech by a super robotic sounding voice. plus half of it just seems to be advertising for the restaurants and hotel. the staff at the tour check in were super rude as well. skip the tour. maybe just go for a bite at the bar then you will be able to see everything anyway or maybe even stay. some rooms were actually really reasonably priced.

jan 2026 went for the self guided tour and learnt about the history and background of the patriarch as well as the mansion. as a good portion of the mansion was accessible to only hotel guests the self guided tour was limited to only certain areas. it s a huge property with 5 courtyards and the largest main courtyard was the set of crazy rich asians.

the most beautiful mansion in all penang. amazingly well preserved. the virtual guide is precise and well historically supported! worth the time and investment. if your budget allows you stay in this hotel!

the building is gorgeous but the self guided tour could benefit from some improvements 1. using a narrator who has at least some basic knowledge of chinese pronunciation! 2. numbering the locations so they match the audio clips 3. recommending that visitors download the audio app before they arrive if they order tickets online as we did

went for the tour. the guide was very knowledgeable and humorous. it is a must see to learn about the house itself but also the way it fits into the unesco designation of the entire georgetown! book you tour in advance as only 20 people are allowed at a time!

the rooms are all filled with historical perspectives and memories. beautifully and carefully reinstated with modern amenities. the staff are extremely supportive and helpful. the tour of the blue mansion is a must for every resident to really appreciate the immense historical significance of the building.

we did the tour of blue mansion and it was 25rm for self guided we had to wait in the cafe for 45min for our slot. most part of the mansion is converted into rooms and private areas. we just get to see lower courtyard upper floor with one room and that s it. the tour took hardly 15 min and we clicked pictures which took another 15 min. just the blue color looks attractive other than that not worth the money and it s a rip off for 25rm. pinang peranakan museum is worth 30rm not this. the owners of this should either provide a proper tour or reduce the cost. wouldn t recommend.

i came here for the guided tour. our guide was very great explaining the history on how the blue mansion was preserved. the place itself was full of character and is in a very well conditioned. it also appears on the hollywood film crazy rich asians mahjong scene. i recommend everyone to visit whenever you re in penang. me myself almost book this place as my stay but got better deal on other property instead next time will definitely stay here..

the mansion is a beautiful example of peranakan culture and i loved learning about the history and traditions behind it. the decor is stunning with intricate carvings and beautiful furniture. overall i d definitely recommend visiting the blue mansion if you re in penang. it s a unique and unforgettable experience!

it is currently a hotel. because it is a historical site it offers scheduled self audio guided tours daily for a fee allowing visitors who don t live there to see its splendor. however the left and right courtyards for residents remain closed to visitors for hotel guests. i recommend you check the scheduled tour opening hours before your visit or you may only view the mansion from the outside.

my husband and i stayed five nights at the blue mansion purely as we wanted to enjoy the cultural experience. as one would expect the rooms are not 5 star but comfortable. ours had access to a rear courtyard where one could sit and relax watching the fish in the large fish pond or enjoy a swim in the nearby swimming pool. the shortcomings are a lack of places to enjoy a light lunch or a snack in a relaxed environment. the indigo restaurant semi formal is open for dinner and accessed by a stair case there are no lifts and cafe mangga situated near the entry to the car park open for lunch and dinner has an ok menu nothing outstanding. there is entertainment on the stage in the outdoor seating area. the hotel bar does not provide any complimentary peanuts or crisps to enjoy with a drink. the gift shop has a basic selection of local items and books. it s not very inspiring. the staff overall were polite and helpful. javeed serving breakfast was outstanding and penny at mangga also very attentive and helpful especially with dietary assistance. i have coeliac disease and gf bread was provided for breakfast. there is no separate toaster so bring your own byo toastie bags. overall we were happy with our stay but five nights just a little too long.

beautifully decorated hotel with lots of history. a few good food options for vegetarians for both breakfast and dinner in the courtyard cafe. service was also excellent with very friendly staff who were keen to help us with our bags and taxi

visited the blue mansion as part of the self guided audio tour. this hotel is a must visit in georgetown it s absolutely stunning. when you see the state the building was in prior to renovation you will be shocked the attention to detail is inspiring. we booked the self guided tour as the tour guide led ones had sold out. regardless of which tour you want to do you must pre book we were turned away one day and also witnessed people turned away on the day we booked. the self guided tour was a slight let down as many of the areas discussed are not accessible to non hotel guests i m not sure if this would be the case for the tour guide led ones. the tour is accessed through an app on your phone so i recommend taking your own headphones.

an amazing and unique place to stay in penang! while many people visit the blue mansion just for sightseeing we had the chance to stay overnight and it was a truly special experience. the rooms are beautiful full of character and the staff are incredibly friendly and helpful. highly recommend both the stay and the atmosphere of this historic gem!

everything was perfect . very unique location and experience . the staff was extremely helpful careful and friendly . live music every evening . one of the most beautiful unique boutique hotels ever . unforgettable experience .

my sister and i stayed from friday through sunday. the room was lovely clean and the mattress was comfortable. the staff were helpful. sally the tour guide on friday afternoon was knowledgeable and friendly. the whole experience was positive. highly recommend.

this mansion is actually a hotel located in an authentic house like a few others in downtown penang. it originally belonged to a rich family but it was gradually dilapidated. the story is quite poignant and well presented in the public part of the mansion. this house was the set for many popular films such as crazy rich asians . beautifully restored but with less things to discover than in the nearby peranakan mansion which takes the cake. we didn t stay at this hotel so we can t judge the rooms and service provided. be careful the number of visitors is limited so booking a tour in advance is a must.

extremely disorganised experience. we paid for the guided tour in advance and there was literally no tour despite arriving ahead of time. a friend recommended this tour and claimed it was comprehensive so i m not sure if things have changed post covid or if this was an unusual bout of incompetence. the actual place seemed... okay. prior to coming to penang we d been deciding between staying here and at the e o and after having visited are so glad we selected the latter. this hotel seems disorganised at best.

we arrived at a time when the venue was full but somehow managed get a place in because the ticketing staff kyra tried her best to see if there s any opportunity for us to slot in. the mansion is really worth the visit with wonderful artifacts and architecture.

huge shout out to the very friendly helpful and welcoming security officer who was on duty at 6pm 29 12 2025. he was outstanding and deserves an appreciation by the management for the assistance he rendered us. also the indigo mansion is truly a timeless architectural masterpiece. looking forward to seeing its sister mansion in medan indonesia.
we did the 11 00am walking tour here which was absolutely fantastic. out guide really bought the place alive with his story telling. unforgettable and an absolute must if in george town.

didn t stay at the hotel. just visited and went for the self guided tour. tickets sell out fast so better to buy online the night before. not much to see though since most parts were closed off for visitors but the place was well maintained.

my cousin and i did the walking tour with sally our tour guide . she was very professional and we were learning a lot of things about the house his owner and his life at that period. we 100 recommend to go and visit the mansion specially with tour guide as sally. i was tour guide in the past and she does the visit very entertaining.

beautiful mansion entirely renovated and now used as a hotel. it is possible to visit for 25rm which includes a guided tour in english if you arrive at the right time 11am or 3 pm . our guide was sally and she was great very knowledgeable about the building perfect english and very nice.

this hotel truly delivers the feeling of history comfort and desire to return again. we had a pleasure staying here and completely satisfied with the service and location. it s a walking distance to major city points shops and restaurants. there is a complimentary house tour session that takes place in the afternoon and it s must visit for everyone who wants to know more about the story behind this beautiful mansion. highly recommend to take a room with the breakfast as it truly suits all types of dietary preferences. it was a great surprise for me to discover the organic salad options as part of menu breakfast as it s rare even for a high priced international 5 star hotels. credits to chef and the whole service team! definitely a place to come back!

the guided tour was nice. the guide was very knowledgeable and friendly. the bar inside was also nice even though it s a hotel bar. the gift shop needs some improvement because i don t feel relevant to buy anything.

they closed the mansion oct 4th 2025 for a private event. they did this after many people ourselves included already made tour reservations. we received no email and they have not responded to our follow up email asking for a refund. completely unacceptable. they know who reserved for oct 4th. they can process the refunds proactively. unprofessional and unacceptable business practice. they should require private events to be booked a certain amount of time in advance so they will not sell any individual tour tickets. this would avoid this problem. the reality is that money talks and they know you ll come regardless of how poorly they treat you. if you think the mansion is some immaculate structure take a look at the outside of the building that doesn t face the entry. it s in desperate need of paint and renovation.

absolutely shameful for penang and for malaysia nothing to see but the courtyard. everything is in poor state some cheongsam are displayed with holes.on the first floor there are mostly just posters of the movies filmed on location pixelated on top of it.paint peeling off the walls. the place looks tremendously unkept.

we went for a drink in the evening. the house is full of character and charm. it is really like stepping back in time in a wonderful location. staff were friendly and prepared very good cocktails. a must visit when in penang.
a stunning old beautifully run hotel in old george town. we had 5 fabulous nights there. highly recommended. if you re noise sensitive book an upstairs room.
i did not stay here. but the uniqueness and history of this place is one of it s kind. and centrally located within george town. worth considering for my next trip.
ww had dinner in indigo restaurant a part of the blue mansion. perfect!! the atmosphere the food the waiter rubel about all we were very satisfied. for sure we will come back overhere.

did not stay in the hotel but visited and joined a tour of this beautifully restored mansion. lin hotel owner was so friendly and has so much interesting information. resident cat ginger is adorable and friendly

the blue mansion is absolutely stunning! its vibrant indigo walls are eye catching while the intricate chinese carvings and colonial style windows create a unique charm. it feels like stepping into a elegant and timeless masterpiece. a must see in penang!
wonderful restoration and peaceful space. tour is worth doing sells out at times over weekends busy periods but is free for guests of either of their hotels .

i am so glad we stayed here! they originally were fully booked so we booked at e o and then last minute a room opened up here and we snagged it and i am so glad we did!!! it was truly a highlight of our trip! the hotel staff is so lovely and so helpful! the breakfast was delicious!!!! the tour was one of our highlights our tour guide was so funny and it was so informative a great way to learn about penang and malaysian history. i loved the little treats they would leave in our room the pool while we didn t use looked very relaxing! the property is just stunning could roam around it for ages! would definitely stay here again!

the building architecture is really beautiful and well preserved. would love to see more artifacts from the family to add more to the building story. fyi to those who are planning to visit please reserve your ticket a week in advance to avoid any disappointment. unesco has placed a limit of 250 visitors in a day. in addition to that some tour guides purchased tickets in bulk.

it was a lovely stay. our room used to be a kitchen and the hot stove is now used as a table. i like how they preserve and restore the place. unlike going to a museum you do not need to see things behind the glass but can live with the old things we are cat lovers. the hotel s cat ginger came to us and lay down quietly next to our breakfast table in the restaurant area on the last day of our stay and this added extra joy to our time there
we visited for a cocktail in the evening and it was magical! a pianist played in the courtyard and the drinks were delicious. i was in heaven! it s stunning!

i love this hotel it really felt like i was staying as a guest in a mansion and not a hotel. the people that run the hotel are the best they are so kind and friendly while being ever so attentive. i truly felt at home here and part of a family. and yes the hotel itself is also a unique and beautiful grand historic mansion as you likely have read about.
the tour was really interesting. sally was a fantastic tour guide. you need to book a few days in advance otherwise the face to face tour sells out

this is my third time living in blue mansion for my travels in penang. the culture taste of the owner service and the story behind always attract me going back and again and again. special thanks to the staff kenny for his warm welcome and help during our stay.

we stayed 4 nights and from our arrival following a long haul flight to our departure we had the best time and 100 would return again. we were looking for something different and staying at this heritage site certainly is a great experience. we enjoyed every aspect of the home of cheong fatt tze and especially the staff who provided excellent hospitality. special thanks to jalal and team for wonderful customer service throughout our stay. the breakfasts are good and offer a daily buffet dish the nasi lemak was tasty and the making of noodle soup too and breakfast includes set menu dishes to choose from and also a buffet fruit selection. rooms and bathrooms are comfortable clean and tidy and spacious. separate entranceway so guests can easily access outside courtyard and pleasant pool area. informative and well delivered tour included in stay is recommended early on in your stay to better understand the background stories about the people and building. staying at cheong fatt tze is great example of a historic building still functioning in today s world. recommend if you re looking for a unique experience.

it s located heart of penang i stayed with my girlfriend 30 days amazing place to stay very clean cozy place for vacation friendly stuff and the caf was so nice food spacial the cofee my girlfriend love the cofee
the guided tour at 15.30 is amazing the guide explain alot about the history of the mansion the owner and chinese culture too if you are bored with the tour you can escape to the bar to get some beer

had a fantastic experience visiting cheong fatt tze s blue mansion! i booked my ticket online for the guided tour which i highly recommend it was definitely worth it. the guide provided fascinating insights into the mansion s unique architecture and the life of cheong fatt tze making the history come alive. the tour covered the beautiful reception hall the iconic courtyard and the museum exhibition all within about 45 minutes. although only certain areas are accessible since the mansion is also a hotel the parts we saw were stunning and full of character. booking online in advance was convenient and ensured i got a spot as tours often sell out. overall a memorable and enriching visit worth every cent and highly recommended for anyone interested in penang s heritage!
everything was wonderful the staff the room the facilities couldn t fault anything would definitely look to return

got the self guided audio tour. not very obvious where the tour started or how to access and took us a walk around before. beautifully restored mansion with inner courtyards as it is now a hotel only one is accessible to public doing the tour. upstairs areas with rooms and some displays

did the guided tour at the blue mansion! the guide was incredibly knowledgeable about the history of this beautiful establishment and it was clear they had a deep passion for sharing its story. however one downside was that not everyone in the group seemed interested in the tour itself which became a bit distracting and took away from the experience for those of us who genuinely wanted to learn. tip if you re planning to stay at the blue mansion make sure to book well in advance they re often fully booked!

update following the initial review we were contacted directly by the hotel. the manager called to say she had read our post and was deeply distressed by the conduct that had occurred on our arrival. she was very apologetic and offered us a full refund for our stay airport transfer and dinner at the blue mansion as well as assuring us that her staff would be retrained. this went a long way to correcting our original complaint. we have now received a full refund for our stay and accepted a free airport transfer. we feel that this original post shouldn t be used to measure the overall quality of service at the hotel. were we to pass through georgetown again we might consider rebooking the blue mansion. very disappointed with the experience with this hotel. we were really looking forward to our stay at the blue mansion having booked to stay here months in advance for the 5 facilities and reviews. however despite receiving confirmations of our stay online check in and marketing about additional add on experiences up to the night before we arrived in georgetown we were informed by the receptionist when we arrived to check in that there was a leak in our room. we then waited 10 minutes for the manager i believe his name was fazli? to come and speak to us who informed us that our credit card had failed and so we could not stay in the hotel. upon checking the confirmation the hotel policy is to take payment 7 days prior to the stay but at no point had they attempted to contact us to let us know of a payment issue. the manager refused to take payment there and then and continued to insist it was our fault so i believe that they had never actually attempted to charge our card as they knew in advance of the issue with their rooms. when challenged on this the manager did admit that it was because they had a leak in the room. they did call up and take us to another local hotel on our behalf which is fantastic however when we asked what compensation we could be offered for the inconvenience he laughed in our faces. after some time we were offered a complimentary tour and later offered use of facilities but at that point it felt borderline insulting having been lied to and laughed at it s understandable to a degree that things go wrong especially in old buildings but the communication was beyond poor and to lie and blame the customer is appalling for any hotel let alone one of this prestige and price. after reading other reviews on here from recent months it seems that there have been other issues with leaky rooms and poor customer service which have not been adequately addressed so i would strongly advise against booking a stay here.
guided tour was very well conducted. had sally as a guide she was very knowledgeable about the history of the man and the house do request for her if you can!
not worth to buy tickets to visit. it all rooms are used as hotel. for self guided tour it takes less than 5min to finish visiting

my wife and i had an excellent experience staying here. i wanted to go visit for it s history and she wanted to because it was a filming location in crazy rich asians. i am happy to say that the hotel did not disappoint! as a historian i could really see and appreciate the effort they put into restoring and maintaining the building as a heritage site and anyone can appreciate how beautiful it is! they even have regular tours free for guests with a knowledgeable and charismatic local guide that are worth your time while staying even if he seemed to be self censoring a little to cater to the sensibilities of some malay guests and those from the prc... . on top of all this the staff were amazingly kind and helpful and the included breakfast was also delicious every day. my only real complaint was when i ordered a negroni at the bar they used very small ice cubes that began melting quickly and ruined the taste one large cube is ideal for a boozy drink like that and i expected better from a bar at a premium hotel so maybe head over to farquhars at the eastern and oriental hotel five minutes away instead if you fancy a good drink. other than that it was an excellent experience and a must do in penang if you can manage it at least take the tour! .
beautiful exterior. could not go in today tickets sold out but still very nice to see this piece of history and the intricate details on the exterior.

highly recommended tour what an extravagant mansion full of stunning architecture and rich history! i didn t stay here but i did join the awesome tour led by sally. she s a perfect mix of knowledgeable cheerful and funny. so many fascinating stories behind this place and its award winning restoration project! big shout out to kenny one of the lovely staff members who really looked after me and even helped taking some great photos of me when i m travelling alone. massive thanks to all the amazing staff for the world class service in penang!

a mixed experience with the home tour incredible guide underwhelming tour content i want to start by highlighting the true star of our visit the tour guide. she was exceptional fluent in english incredibly knowledgeable and genuinely passionate about the history of cheong fatt tze mansion. her storytelling brought the place to life and for her alone i d give 5 stars. that said the actual sightseeing aspect of the tour left much to be desired. the accessible areas are quite limited mainly a small inner courtyard a few halls and two rooms with scattered exhibits some traditional clothing old typewriters vases and posters featuring cheong fatt tze s wives and famous past visitors. there s no access to bedrooms the kitchen or more personal areas that could offer deeper insight into the home s architecture and design which was disappointing. compared to other heritage sites like casa rocca piccola in malta or the achilleion palace in greece this felt more like a brief glimpse than an immersive experience. the tour feels caught between being a hotel and a museum and unfortunately doesn t fully succeed as either. while the history is rich and well told the limited physical access makes it hard to connect with the place meaningfully. in short come for the guide but manage your expectations about what you ll actually see.

this hotel sells tickets to outsiders to visit the public area most of whom are not hotel guests and the management is poor. visitors get angry because they cannot visit some areas at ground level only for hotel guests. a hotel should not sell ticket to visitors!

one of the most memorable experiences in our lives. we were lucky enough to stay in the escoy room featured on the website and it s even more wow in real life. room food facilities were all excellent as were the staff especially mr fazli who couldn t do enough to make our stay special. ever woken up in a hotel room and thought you could be anywhere in the world? that s not the experience we wanted and the blue mansion delivered good different in spades. we loved it.

this house was once ranked among the world s top ten mansions and is over 120 years old. if you love history and appreciate historical mansions with a blend of eastern and western influences this is a great place to visit and take photos. you ll need to purchase an entry ticket at the entrance which costs 25 per person. guided tours are available every one to two hours where they explain the history of the house and its past residents. if the main gate is closed it means you ll need to wait for the next guided tour session before they open it. after the tour you re free to explore and take photos on your own.

beautiful place. the blue mansion is without a doubt an incredible place it looks amazing from the outside and the inside. it has a lot of history and you can feel it during the visit. self guided tour it s a complete tour it starts outside in the garden and then it takes you around the house. it explains you on detail about each room. however they don t provide headphones it costs you as the normal tour and it mentions some areas which you can t see properly because is exclusively reserved for hotel guests. so in reality you just see the hotel from the outside main lobby and common areas.

unbelievable experience of staying here. rooms are incredible and unlike anywhere else in the world. service is very good we were shown to our room by nikia who was absolutely lovely. would come back and recommend to all who are due to stay in penang
did the tour and thoroughly enjoyed the information by the owner of the house who discussed the incredible restoration that occurred through the 1990s.

a very historic place that allow you experience how s like to live in the past as a rich person. where you can feel the feeling of living in the town area everywhere is access by feet bicycle that s provided by the hotel. high recommend

beautiful mansion with some history to learn about. the only down point is the guide tour needs to be booked while the other nyonya museum they have guide arrange all day. and the place is so small basically are reserved area for hotel and restaurant even you bought ticket to visit many places you can t go. otherwise it is beautiful and worth to take a look.

very disappointing stay. room lock was faulty at check in and despite leaving early our room was still not cleaned by 2 15pm. front desk told us to wait and then they went to serve other guests. poor service for such a well known hotel. you can get much better elsewhere.

a stay at the blue mansion is for sure the highlight of our trip in penang! it s a very unique experience staying at this gorgeous mansion with huge historical and cultural values. our room is very spacious with a heritage vibes and aesthetics which goes beyond our expectations! also love the guided tour to be able to learn about the history and story of this beautiful mansion highly recommend!

we had a 6 night stay at the blue mansion and it was an amazing experience. the mansion is a cultural heritage so the atmosphere is very special and the room was very nice with vintage style. the part that make any hotel move from good to great is the service attitude and attention to details of the staff and how they adress any slight problem that might occur. we would especially like to mention and thank the operations manager mr mohd noor fazil who swiftly and professionally handled and adressed some small issues we had during our stay. his kindness and customer attitude was nothing but exceptional and excellent. the same goes for mr jalal who served us at breakfast and assisted us whenever we had any asks during our stay. the ladies in the reception are always helpful kind and smiling. we can also highly recommend the cafe the mango tree they have excellent coffee and fresh cakes as well as nice food. we truly recommend staying at the mansion and we are already looking forward for our next time at cheong fatt tze.

my love from the first sight. a house full of penang history. kept and preserved with love and care. vibrant colors add to its vibe. perfect sleeping arrangement. guests comfort is well respected against crowds of tourists taking a tour around the place.

if you are a history and art lover then this place will be included. beautiful buildings with fantastic cutlery designs and so much more are there. entry charges at 25rm which is a bit higher. avoid during holidays and weekends unless you can t witness the beautiful place completely. only 2 guided trips are in a day so plan accordingly .

today we had the pleasure of dining at indigo and from the moment we were seated it was clear why this restaurant has such a stellar reputation. the food was nothing short of exceptional creative refined and full of flavour. each dish on the menu was beautifully presented and thoughtfully curated. the service staff were equally impressive warm knowledgeable and attentive without being intrusive. however our experience was significantly marred by the appalling conduct of the security guard at the entrance on duty at approx 1pm dressed in black . despite having a confirmed reservation we were stopped and spoken to with extreme rudeness. he insisted we had no booking and demanded to know who we had spoken to on the phone then proceeded to call multiple people to verify all while we were made to stand outside uncomfortably as if we were trying to sneak in. he even made derogatory comments about us during his phone calls which we could clearly hear. this encounter was humiliating and completely out of step with the high standard of hospitality the restaurant prides itself on. for a place of this calibre the experience at the door should be just as polished and professional as what s offered inside. we sincerely hope management addresses this issue it s a real shame to see such a glaring contrast between the exceptional dining experience and the unpleasant welcome at the

we absolutely loved every minute of staying in a museum! the history of george town was so cool and the blue mansion was awesome! the food and cool street art was definitely a highlight of our trip! would definitely be back!

in my opinion the tour of the mansion comes highly recommend. i thoroughly enjoy the 45 minute rundown of the mansion s history and the many artifacts on display. it is amazing to see how well the mansion was restored and maintain to this day and with novice interest in architecture i m blown away by the historical aesthetic of the place. special mention to the tourguide sally who provided a very informative educational and most importantly entertaining guide of the mansion. you can tell she has a lot of passion for this beautiful building and its history. highly recommend any history buff to conduct the tour.

excellent location and beautiful surroundings. great choice for breakfast. it is a little noisy at night but there are ear plugs provided for sleeping and the ambience during the day is very peaceful. guest comfort is well taken care of. it feels good to stay in such a historically important building and surrounded by great craftsmanship. very clean and has a turn down service.
did not stay but was a great experience around the museum at a good price. the staff at the entrance were very friendly and kind with us.

an old style boutique hotel with wonderful atmosphere. relax on a daybed in the inner courtyard or by the pool. we greatly enjoyed relaxing after a busy 2024. little touches like hand delivered sweet treats to our room each afternoon enhanced the experience. staff addressing us by our names from the first made us feel very welcome. in a great location close to lots of food options public transport and an easy walk into the unesco zones of georgetown. the staff deserve recognition for always being on hand if needed but quiet calm and discreet. the cafe mangaa served a fabulous range of savoury dishes and was one of the few places we found where decent wine by the glass was offered. thank you for a wonderful stay at the blue mansion.

the iconic blue mansion is truly a sight to behold stunning architecture that tells a story at every corner! inside they have a cozy restaurant where we took a refreshing break with some delicious iced tea just what we needed after wandering around under the blazing sun. it s the perfect spot to cool down soak in the history and recharge for more exploring. definitely a must visit when you re in penang!

don t go if you have limited time. go to green mansion instead. i paid rm75 full price for 3pax but the courtyard was still under restoration. they should have done it years ago given the time they had. over rated attraction. the main hall the exterior was the only impressive section of the mansion. the exhibitions were also poorly done. we were not allowed to roam most of the mansion. staff was rude as well. compared to green mansion blue mansion is not worth the visit. the management was only taking advantage of the status and history of the mansion not providing actual value to cultural tourist. felt conned
did a tour here. really enjoyed it. sally was a fantastic guide! thank you
wonderful historical hotel. great amenities and close to major attractions.

we loved our stay here. this unesco world heritage site is beautiful and the staff were fantastic. a special shout out to hana on reception who was extremely helpful. every evening we came back to our room to a lovely message she had hand written on a banana leaf. neelan on the bar was one of the nicest guys i ve ever come across. moni was a character and my brother dave the trainee on the gate always welcomed us warmly. our room was fantastic and the staff really couldn t do enough for us.

came for the walking tour. the architecture of the building and the conservation efforts are notable and the reason for the tour and popularity of the mansion. the tour guide was funny and made the experience really enjoyable. the story of the family was fun. there are only two tours a day at 11 and 3.30 and tickets can only be purchased 10mins before each tour which is a system i really like.
our dinner was excellent and the service provided by rubel made the experience so much more pleasurable. it was lovely dining at this establishment

it was an amazing experience to join the guided tour led by ms. alexis. she is highly knowledgeable about the history and patiently assisted us throughout the tour. fyi you need to book the guided tour in advance through their website.

paid rm 25 self guide tour but since didn t bring earphone along so i didn t listen to it. love the building and architecture which is well maintain. i feel that they could ve add on more displays such as the kitchen equipments what are some specialties dishes embroidery and shoes as well.. rest room was really nice adding a bowl of pandan leaf is such a great touch. there s water provided too. good place to just sit and chill. there s restaurant and bar inside.

we did the self guided tour 45 minutes bring your own headphones airpods to listen to narration . the mansion is beautiful but the self guided tour is more limited than the guided tours that are offered at 11 30am and 3 30pm so i would recommend booking one of those prior to your visit

i have somewhat mixed feelings about this. the place is stunning but with just the entrance ticket you can only see a few rooms. those rooms are indeed magnificent. the details in them showcase the finest craftsmanship and are some of the best examples of their time. it s a shame that most of the manor is not accessible to regular visitors. i suppose one should stay overnight at the blue manor or dine at the restaurant. so the area that is accessible is not worth the price but it s still a wonderful place. if money is not an issue i definitely recommend visiting.

i did the english guided tour at 11am with other tourists. entry fee rm25.00 which can be purchased from the guard house on the left of the mansion. you can stay back after the guided tour to take photos enjoy the magnificent surroundings and visit the gift shop. the guided tour is a must. enjoy!
the staff was really kind and helpful! the location is lovely and definitely worth visiting if you come to penang

was there for a self tour it s 25rm for each person. and you need to book online which there is only 2 tours per day. it was very pretty outside and you can feel its history also from the inside. but the place is very small nothing much to see. there is only very little display and a lot of places is off limit because it s where their guest stays. they also have a souvenir shop but nothing much to buy tho. it is quite pricey for such a short tour which we didn t know the place was that small. also i would recommend that they could let us redeem a cold drink? maybe people will think it s more worth going? because it s quite hot inside.

i attended a guided tour today the guide was friendly and knowledgeable and the restoration details and conservation efforts were impressive. however i was disappointed that the tour only takes you to the lobby atrium and a museum display upstairs. it was also crowded with 40 50 attendees. this option would suit most mainstream tourists but wasn t enough for design and architecture lovers like myself. i wish that there was an option in between doing this tour and paying more to stay at the hotel overnight.

the blue mansion is more than just a historic house it s a journey into the opulent past of penang s heritage. located in the heart of george town this unesco awarded site is one of the best preserved examples of chinese courtyard style architecture in southeast asia.

i was supposed to stay for four nights but checked out before the first night due to a leaking roof that dripped directly onto the bed and woke me up. when i called the front desk to report the issue they came to my room with two towels and suggested shifting the bed. they said there was nothing else they could do speaking in an extremely nonchalant tone as though i was responsible for solving the problem myself. to make matters worse they added that they couldn t move us to another room because the hotel was fully booked. the manager mentioned they can shift me to another room tomorrow but my trust in the hotel and their service is completely lost. as the manager continued to plead that they ve never received complaints about their roofs before which is an obvious lie. then they suggested to shift us to a room on the first floor so we won t be affected by the leaks. which is another way of saying they ll just push the problem to some other guest. the manager asked me to be more understanding of a heritage hotel over a century old. however when i m paying nearly 200 usd per night well above the average in the area i think the least i can expect is a proper roof over my head. what s even more baffling is the staff s nonchalant attitude which makes it clear that leaks are a common issue in the mansion. this is completely unacceptable especially considering the exorbitant prices they charge. it seems the hotel has grown arrogant due to its high occupancy rates. if the building requires maintenance to fix the roof then close it for a month and address the issue properly. leaving it as is is not only unacceptable but also a safety hazard. the magic of staying in a historic mansion fades very quickly when you can t meet basic expectations like shelter from the rain. while i understand the challenges of maintaining an old mansion i cannot justify the price or the lack of basic standards. i would not recommend this hotel to anyone. with this price you can find a way better room.

they have 2 tours a day at 11 30 and 3 30 better to buy tickets the day before. super interesting tour with lots of insights on the man s life and his gorgeous blue mansion. so beautiful it appears in many movies like indochine or crazy rich asians.

very interesting tour to discover history. 11.am and 3.30 daily. try to fit it into your schedule if you are interested in history. you won t be disappointed. well stocked bar for a quick gin and tonic

took a tour here and the tour guide was very engaging. she explained about the mansion s history and how it was built. update i have finally managed to stay here for a weekend when i visited penang recently. my room was huge and airy and i love all the wooden antiques there. i love the tub in my bathroom and the linen and sheets were white crisp and clean. the service was amazing. i felt very welcome and everyone was warm friendly and engaging especially the manager fazli who was incredibly courteous and made my stay a memorable one.

wonderful! definitely stay here even if for just a night. instagram perfect. unique and very special for an authentic stay in penang. excellent service beautiful well appointed rooms instagram perfect mansion hotel and impeccable service.

the self audio tour requires you to download a separate app. poor accessibility. the app also has ads as well. just host the audio on a web browser or provide separate dedicated audio devices like at other attractions. bring your headphones too.

didn t stay in the hotel but took a guided tour. fascinating and beautiful place tour guide was very good and amusing. explained the history decor and people who built and owned it. most of the wings with bedroom areas were out of bounds to the tour which is only right and there was more than enough with the public areas and the set aside rooms with history clothing etc. only slight criticism is the guide used a cheek mic with mini speaker which was distorted so it was a little hard to make out what was said at times.

our lunch experience at here coincided with a guided tour of the historic residence making it truly memorable. the ambiance was welcoming and the service was impeccable. we were fortunate to have a knowledgeable guide who provided fascinating insights into the history of the place while we enjoyed our meal. the food itself was delicious with a variety of options to choose from. each dish was prepared with care and attention to detail and we especially enjoyed. overall our visit left a lasting impression. combining a delightful dining experience with a glimpse into the past offers a unique and enriching outing for locals and tourists alike. highly recommend it!

the building and our room was incredible. however the staff and service were not up to scratch. i was expecting high levels of service and hospitality but it was poor. particularly the staff at breakfast they didn t clear plates should have provided instructions guidance introduction to how it worked they would have to be asked to take our orders breakfast was not out and ready at breakfast start time. the bell staff who brought are luggage was very dramatic and acting about how tough carrying our bags the short distance was to get a tip. we had an ant problem in our room and our mini fridge exploded after staff put it on too cold when i reported it to reception i got no apology. concierge service was good. i d stay one night for the experience not your whole stay

beautiful historic chinese mansion. good breakfast. restaurants are on site but we didn t try them. fairly easy walk into town to look around. free mansion tour for guests was very interesting particularly learning about feng shui.

we were excited to see the mansion and learn about the history. we asked for the private tour booked on and came back an hour later. we were told to sit and wait only to find out we had been booked onto the audio guide that we didn t want. after explaining our concerns we were told they couldn t accommodate. this was just fustrating as we were misled. the tour itself is dismal. wifi keeps dropping in and out and half the guide focusses more on the cafe and gift shop. supposedly to upsell. personally i think this was not a good experience

i m kind of conflicted on how to rate this place because if you re just visiting it s worth a visit. however if you miss one of the tours there s not much to see. they have daily guided tours at 11 am and 3 30 pm. cost is 35 rm for 45 mins. if you miss those times then they have the self guided audio tours. the cost of that is 25 rm. the audio part is very short. there are two floors you can explore. there is some reading. outside of those two floors that s it. you can only see everything if you book a room. the guided portion you can see a bit more. meh overall this is a 3 out of 5. it only became popular among tourists because of the mahjong scene at the end of crazy rich asians.

truly the most amazing stay during our vacation. awesome service nice and friendly staff we love you! the breakfast was the best concept i ever saw local fresh traditional but modern with a creative touch. we did not miss anything.

don t bother with the audio self guided tour it s basically just marketing for the hotel. starts off with some interesting information but seems like they really don t know much about the man the tour is supposed to be about. the guided tour is probably better but can be booked up several days in advance. mid.
beautiful hotel. everything is so beautiful. downside is you may not want to leave the premises to explore because it is so lovely.

we didn t stay here. we just did a day visit. it s beautiful and well worth it. also gets you out of the super hot daytime sun for a couple of hours. not expensive to enter. and not too busy. lots of people dressing in period clothing and having photos taken. it s actually quite nice to watch and see people enjoying themselves.

a disappointment. not so ornate. furniture are donated odd period pieces to fill the space. only shown a few rooms. the guide was good with his presentation history of the building it s owner though. perhaps the hotel portion of the building could be more impressive.

we visited the blue mansion as tourists not residents. got there fairly late around 5ish and we had to wait until 5 30 for reception to be available to get tickets and was still able to go in and look around. so check times. the surroundings inside are incredibly serene photos came out amazing. the restoration of that building really gives us an idea of how beautiful these buildings were. we didn t take the guided tour but can hear the guide talking to the group with a lot of in depth detail and stories.
we loved staying here for three nights beautiful spacious rooms great individual touches and a real historic feel. would definitely recommend booking months in advance.

be a guest in your own museum when you walk into cheong fatt tze aka the blue mansion it honestly feels like you ve stepped back in time a hundred years. the building has been beautifully restored and has this rich exotic vibe that hits you right away. the staff totally match that atmosphere they re super helpful and incredibly friendly. one of them even brought us by car over to armenian street when we asked where to find the best street art. they hold doors open for you move ropes out of the way when they can and there s a warm smile wherever you go. and the hotel itself? they don t use room numbers each room has a name. ours was called fragrant poem and we had to use this old fashioned 100 year old key to open the door. inside the room had all the modern comforts but with antique touches in the furniture and on the walls. it was honestly very nice. breakfast was another highlight super tasty really well done and a little different each day. just to show how special the building is they do guided tours twice a day don t worry not through the guest rooms and as a hotel guest you can join for free. the funny thing is during the tour you might catch a little wink from one of the staff who recognizes you it s a nice touch . long story short cheong fatt tze is one of the most unique and authentic places we ve ever stayed. and while the price per night is relatively high at least by malaysian standards if this place were in the netherlands where we live it would easily cost five times as much. totally worth it.

my entire family experience at this heritage house was deeply disappointing. much of the areas are restricted exclusively to hotel guests only. the few areas accessible to visitors have a handful of antique furniture pieces and some ancient clothing .if to compare the entry fee against limited access make it a poor value for money. the entire visit took us not.more than 10 minutes compared to 30 minutes to reach this place.

great stay at the blue mansion for nyn. great surprise with the jazz bands at the indigo resturant and the bar. good location and walkable within the city. next door to the red house for local food and entertainment. only concern was some

we had a fantastic time at the cheong fatt tze blue mansion. the hotel is beautiful with a lovely history. they do a nice afternoon tea and have great breakfast options and a lovely bar for the evenings with live guzheng performances. the staff were extremely supportive and caring even when i made a mistake with my booking they went out of their way to fix it for me including giving me their direct whatsapp number and regular updates. the room was fantastic and comfortable. if ever back in penang i wouldn t stay anywhere else.

don t go to the museum on the self guided tour! limited access and the information available is fairly superficial. maybe better with a guide. also the toilets were gross because of bad water pressure for flushing

visited the mansion as tourist not as guest so unable to write a proper review about the place and service. only selected areas are permissible for non guest and they are quite limited. a very short tour which is a shame because entrance ticket is quite expensive for such quick walkaround. we were there when they host the jazz event if i recall correctly. we would like to explore more but alas.

visited the blue mansion doing the self guided tour. you need to bring your phone and own headphones. the wi fi is really poor so you only could listen to the tour in certain spots. overpriced at around 5 euros for less than 30 minutes as it basically is just the lobby courtyard and a few rooms as the rest is restricted for guests of the hotel only.

nice place to visit. it has a long and interesting family history passed down to the people visiting this age old house. this is the place worthwhile to visit. of course the witty explanation of the guide gives the tour more flavor. also i would like to thank one of the staff for kindly helping me a lot in taking pictures and everything. he has to be recognized for sure. forgot his name unfortunately his name starts with t and ends with y . i enjoyed it a lot there.

we didn t stay at this hotel but just did a quick tour while in malaysia. it s a nice cute spot for some nice photos. we got ours taken by selva his skills are amazing and his enthusiastic in customer satisfaction lit up the entire tour.

this is the most special place i have ever stayed in. we started the visit with the tour. the chinese lady conducting the tour was the best guide i have ever seen so knowledgeable to a very detailed level. the rooms and inside of the mansion are all beautifully done out and restored so that you really feel you are stepping out in time. best of all was breakfast in the courtyard looking at the ornate door and cocktails in the blue bar. our family of four will never forget this experience.

the blue mansion is definitely a must when in penang . you can do a guided tour or a self guided tour. we opted for the self tour which suited our needs as we didn t want to be locked in with a guided tour where they offer at certain times. there is a bar there if you want to order some drinks and spend a little extra time in the mansion. definitely wouldn t pass it up if you re in penang due to its history.

unforgettable stay at the blue mansion. due to limited availability we experienced two rooms the han triple suite blue room first floor and a more modern one on ground floor. the han triple suite was amazing and i would recommend it if you have the choice. we did 5 nights which was more than enough for penang. the pool is very basic and the breakfast was excellent.

the blue mansion is the best accommodation in penang. only 18 bedrooms so you must be lucky to sleep there. the place is actually like a museum people pay tickets to visit. the rooms are nothing short of magnificent. xixth century character in the architecture and furniture in a traditional chinese style. amenities are definitely xxth century with strong wifi and air conditioning service is top notched. upon arrival the lock to the room was jammed. but the staff quickly upgraded me to another room. special mention for the breakfast leaning towards chinese food served in the courtyard. most unique experience you get to cut your salad before eating eat. certainly the freshest salad i ve ever had. only regret no gym but there s a small swimming pool and if you re in luck you can get one of the rooms with direct pool access through the garden.

this is a beautifully restored building with a rich and fascinating history. the attention to detail the care taken to enhance the environment and the staff respect for the space was wonderful. breakfast provided an interesting variety. the massage services arranged were well worth the time and expense. rooms are spacious with plenty of outdoor nooks and crannies for quiet contemplation. entertainment show casing local talent was soothing demonstrating a tangible interaction between the hotel and the community. commendable!! the tour of the mansion was engaging and informative. i found most of the staff friendly respectful and eager to accomodate a visitor. they are happy to provide information to enhance a visit to penang and take pride in their city. it was important to me as a regular traveler that not one member of staff complained about their job or showed any signs of discontent in their employment. it makes the energy and atmosphere of the blue mansion so much more!!! the gift shop is a haven of unique delights especially if one does not want to buy the global brands available in every mall and duty free shopping site. using the local grab system of transport was easy. the hotel is extremely accessible with various sites of interest within walking distance. as a woman traveling alone in malaysia i felt safe and comfortable. i sincerely hope it remains this way and continues to be respectful of women. i also found it to be extremely child and family friendly. my only complaint is a minor one... the complimentary ironing made suitcase creases worse... garments folded instead of on hangars. i highly recommend a stay here... and will most assuredly be returning.
