staff are friendly and helpful. the guided tour is professional and arouses ones s interest on the history of the mansion. the room is well kept and clean. the courtyard is beautifully decorated in a serene atmosphere. it s a nice place to stay outdoor if the temperature is not so hot. breakfast is of high quality with excellent service. the buffet bar of fruit and hot dishes is different everyday.

it is a building imbued with charm and historical allure not merely a house but a crystallized epic of art and culture. with its deep indigo exterior walls exquisitely carved wooden windows an artfully arranged chinese style courtyard and western decorative details it showcases a distinctive architectural style much like an isolated blue gem silently recounting a century old tale. i am especially grateful to these two staff members danial and izham for their help which has given me a deeper understanding of this place.

not as good as expected. it s called a former residence but it s more like a small courtyard where anyone can take pictures. there s nothing particularly worth visiting but the number of people entering each hour is limited and most people take pictures in a few places. if you come here specifically it s totally unnecessary. the staff at the door have a bad attitude but the staff at the ticket office have a good attitude. it should be noted that even if the official website shows that the tickets are sold out you can still wait at the small iron gate for the release once an hour. it s not a worthwhile experience. maybe it s better for young people who like taking pictures.

cheong fatt tze s blue mansion is a striking heritage landmark in george town penang offering a glimpse into the life of one of the most influential chinese merchants of the 19th century often referred to as the rockefeller of the east. this prized residence definitely went through years of meticulous restoration to shine again in its former glory holding a slice of history unknown to many even local malaysians today the mansion operates as a boutique hotel and museum whereby most areas are restricted to in house guests only. tickets costed rm25 via walk in on the left side entrance with self guided audio tour downloadable on your phone headphones recommended to fully indulge whilst on the go. follow through the experience by dropping by the outdoor cafe at mangga their pancakes are delicious! great to hop in on the crazy rich asians and catch a glimpse of the set in the movie too!

we stayed in the blue mansion for one night and had such a wonderful time. it s such a beautiful place where you feel instantly relaxed as soon as you enter. we used the pool which was lovely as well and did the guided tour of the mansion which was very interesting. i would definitely recommend a stay here to any friends or family visiting penang.

it s hotel. i review only for the looking from outside. it seems like historical hotel. and they also provide the tour but it seems like it s very popular even i checked 5 days ago then i could not find any available ticket may be because of the holiday season... anyway it looks nice in blue.

really not worth it.save your time and visit other places this place is just a simple blue building in the middle of the city. of course there s some history of a chinese merchant who created the first winery in malaysia and it s now the biggest in china and so on but the villa itself is really not that special. the operating hours limit the visitors to amount of 16. just save your time drink a singapore sling and have a nice day at the beach instead

just fabulous. beautifully restored mansion with wonderful attention to detail and great integrity reflecting its history. internal courtyard is perfect for breakfast or a pre dinner g t. the staff are just terrific all very well trained friendly helpful and very professional. great value given the overall quality of the experience. we can t recommend highly enough.

from the moment we arrived we felt cared for by the staff at the blue mansion! they brought us tea assisted with luggage had our room prepared with some lovely surprises for our anniversary and gave us a drink ticket to use at the bar to celebrate. the property is beautiful and wonderfully kept. we enjoyed the museum aspects of the house and the fact that each room feels like a museum. the staff were so helpful with recommendations of where to go around penang and were always warm and welcoming. in the evening on our first night at the mansion they brought us some lovely desserts. they really made our stay feel special and it is something we will remember for many years to come!

beautiful mansion with its rich history. unfortunately it s fully booked for the hotel room and it has passed the afternoon tea time so we purchased a ticket for rm25 each person to visit the place. this place is also featured in crazy rich asians movie as well. nice place to visit however there are only few rooms.
it is a very enjoyable and unique stay i can t stop taking pictures since almost every corner every furniture or plant tree has soul. in summary it is a must stay in george town.

came for the walking tour a few years back and returned as a live in guest recently. it was a worthwhile experience as we got access to the entire mansion with lots of interesting areas to explore. the collection of antique furnishing is impressive and the classical straits chinese decorated rooms are really nice to stay in. book a suite for maximum comfort and enjoy looking at the fine details such as architectural blueprints of the mansion reframed as drawings to decorate the walls. also book a table at the adjacent mangga cafe for a jazzy entertainment to accompany your evening meal.

not sure if malaysia s unesco places have staff who are somewhat rude? this is the second unesco place i visited and had a very bad welcome experience. upon arrival by grab the person at the main entrance s first question was what you want? in an unfriendly tone. i thought it should have been how can i help you? the guided tour was entertaining and i learned more about some history. could spend about 45mins or so with the guided tour.

absolutely not worth the hype. the tour contents were underwhelming to say the least barely 15 minutes long but worth 59 rm. the pinang peranakan museum is a much more worthy option for international tourists.
great place to visit . tickets confusing . they said tickets are sold out but we bought 50 rm worth at cafe next door and we re then allowed in. interesting history and worth the visit.
we stayed in the pool rooms which open on to the beautiful gardens and pool.

staying at the blue mansion in penang felt like stepping into a living storybook rich with history romance and intricate beauty. this iconic indigo hued heritage home is more than just a place to rest it s an experience that immerses you in the legacy of cheong fatt tze with every corner whispering tales of the past. the architecture is stunning with detailed woodwork stained glass and an elegant mix of eastern and western design. rooms are spacious and thoughtfully preserved offering modern comfort wrapped in old world charm. one of the highlights was the charming bar tucked within the mansion s atmospheric halls. it s a moody intimate space where time seems to slow down. the bar offers a well curated list of cocktails with creative nods to penang s cultural melting pot. i sipped on a house special infused with local spices which was both surprising and delightful. the bartenders were warm knowledgeable and happy to share stories about the mansion s past. whether you re a history lover an architecture enthusiast or simply someone seeking a memorable stay the blue mansion delivers a unique blend of elegance and heritage. the bar alone is worth a visit perfect for a quiet enchanting evening.
gorgeous mansion and enjoyed their guided tour. pity we could not see any hotel rooms. ticket box employee kiruna deserves some compliments as well very cheerful and friendly!

the parts of the house that can be seen on the tour are very nice. however most of the mansion is closed to the public. the tour could be reduced to 15 minutes. i wouldn t recommend it for international tourists.

v friendly hotel. while hotel is small it also means guests would rcv dedicated services and full attention. hotel is rich in history. so never miss the guided tours which are in high demand free for hotel guests .close to many of the popular local foods. and just 20min from the airport.

i wanted to eat here but they serve western items. but i still decided to do the tour. the tour guide was friendly and enthusiastic about the history of the blue mansion. it looks absolutely gorgeous. and restoration is very well done. could not hear fully of the tour guide that spoke in english since our group was quite big decided to walk around and take some selfies instead. i could do without the tour if it s too crowded.

stunning hotel for the price. bedrooms are large well air conditioned good sized bathrooms too. the hotel itself is beautiful with plenty of spots to sit and relax with a drink and take in the surroundings. it s a short walk 5mins or so to get into the heart of georgetown where there are plenty of cafes shops bars restaurants and convenience stores too. thoroughly recommend it and would definitely go back.

i went for a guided tour which was very informative and entertaining. the tour guide was great and it was fun learning about the history of the mansion and how it was maintained. there are also self guided tours available. the guided ones should be booked online in advance to secure a spot. it costs rm 25 and lasts about an hour. would definitely recommend.

i went on the guided tour and the tour guide was amazing. i wasn t able to catch her name but she explained the details of the blue mansion. the house itself showed many beautiful historical artifacts and the facts were interesting too.

excellent place to go if you love history. rags to riches story of a man who traveled the world and returned to heavily influence the development of penang. the architecture of the mansion is also impressive.

a must visit in penang! the blue mansion is a stunning blend of chinese architecture and colonial influences. rich with history every room and corridor tells the story of cheong fatt tze s legacy. the guided tour is informative and well paced offering insights into the mansion s construction symbolism and restoration. it s beautifully preserved and makes you feel like you ve stepped back in time. great for history lovers architecture buffs or anyone looking for a unique cultural experience. don t miss the iconic blue walls and intricate tilework absolutely instagram worthy!

i didn t stay at the mansion but joined the tour last friday. it was one of the best tours i had. the guide a conservator was a wonderful storyteller as she played a huge role in the mansion s conservation. she shared the ups and downs the history of the mansion and the family. it was definitely more than 45 minutes and worth even more than rm26 ticket. please book online prior to the visit. i definitely value unesco sites more than before. i would love to stay there next time!
amazing hotel the architecture is really stunning staff also super friendly and helpful.
skip the visit. disappointing. not much to see. basically you get to visit the lobby of a hotel.
cool building but the tour is not worth it. 25 ringgit for constant advertisements to stay at the hotel. 3 out of the 12 parts of the tour are focussed on selling you things.

an amazing hotel the most beautiful place. oozes character and history. stylish comfortable rooms. divine architecture and styling. nice bar good wine list. pool area quiet and pretty though next to main road . one of our favourite ever places to stay.

if you like to live in historical mansion and learn the story of a chinese businessman and how he came about with the mansion this is the place you should go too. the person who conducted the mansion tour tells the story so lively that it takes your mind back to those days and make you imagine how life was in that mansion back in that century. rooms are equipped with bathroom attached and many designs available furnitures are mostly antiques furniture. don t miss trying their restaurant indigo for example fine dining in the mansion serving good food

we recently had the pleasure of staying at cheong fatt tze the blue mansion and it was an exceptional experience. the location is ideal perfectly situated for exploring gtown. the hotel s decor is truly stunning showcasing a remarkable preservation of the building s rich heritage. every corner of the mansion reflects its historical charm with beautifully restored features that transport you back in time. the service staff were outstanding providing warm and attentive service that made my stay all the more enjoyable. overall our stay at the blue mansion was delightful. the combination of its unique heritage excellent location and exceptional service made it a memorable visit. will make this a yearly thing

i regret to say that our visit to the cheong fatt tze mansion was marred by unprofessional and unwelcoming behavior from the reception staff. given its reputation as a unesco heritage site we expected warmth and professionalism but instead encountered rude reception where the staff member who greeted us was dismissive impatient and seemed disinterested in assisting visitors. no smile no courtesy just a transactional attitude.
very stupid regulation. come so far away with cab and not even allowed to come and visit the restaurant.

i made a reservation for lunch at 1 30 when i got there at 1 05 i was stopped by a bald security guard who did not let me enter the property even though i had a reservation. he said that indigo was full and doesn t take walk ins for the day. i was made to call the restaurant and confirm that i had indeed made a reservation. the security guard insisted to speak to the person working in indigo . i understand the whole process seeing as it is a hotel after all and security of guests should be respected and protected however the issue i had was that his tone and attitude was extremely rude ! and when we got to the restaurant it was half empty !!! however our server jalal at indigo was extremely polite and attentive . he made sure to explain every dish that was served offered to help take photos for us topped up our water without being asked . jalal was the saving grace of the cheong fatt tse experience.

a place not to miss if you are keen to learn its founder interesting history. the display become more lively with the excellent guide who speak so well. the hotel section is closed to general visitors.

the only way to visit the blue mansion is to join a guided tour three times per day. however you see pretty little of the mansion just the inner courtyard on the ground and on the first floor . the tour is pretty boring you stay at least 20 minutes in the main hall with explanations from the guide. lots of crowds on the tour ....

you must make a booking to visit the museum. there are two types of tour guided and self guided. both are 25 ringgit each and the the tickets can be bought online. you should buy the tickets early as there is a limited number of slots per session.
i didn t realize the tours were limited in number so i missed touring the building. best to make online reservations in advance.

it s my second and last visit of this place. we went on 4th august morning around 11am and realized all the guided tour were booked and earliest next available was 1 30pm. many of the issue pissed us off and decided we will never recommend this place to anyone anymore. first we were approached by an unfriendly indian staff at premises while we were trying to take photo under a tree. was told photo taking is not allowed if we are not guest of the hotel. then was told to go near the gate to scan qr quote to book guided tour by simply pointing his finger and totally not helpful and friendly at all. obviously our mood of the day were almost ruins. we went into the cafe instead hoping to cool ourselves down and were really delighted by the staffs especially the indian lady staff . overall i think this hardware premises and the building is nice but the software the way they run this place is suck. no proper staff attending to visitor at all. we left and ended up at straits and oriental museum which totally save our day which ruined by this blue mansion. good bye will not visit this place and for sure will not recommend at all.

i came here only to visit the building and didn t stay in the hotel. we had a self guided audio tour as the tour guide was already booked out. i recommend to book a tour in advance scan qr code below to get a spot the tour lasts around 45 min and you can only enter the building with a guided tour or a ticket for the self guided tour with the audio guide via app on mobile phone for a certain time slot. the building is quite impressive and has beautiful elements the history is interesting. unfortunately almost all the original collection was sold a few decades ago so there is not a lot of the original stuff left.

building was interesting to see. would echo some of the other comments people wrote about the tour. only a small portion of the building is opened up for the tour and there s not much displayed. i was a bit disappointed to see some of the items displayed weren t being properly cared for. one of the dresses visibly needs attention.

went on a tour and the owner delivered it. such a passionate woman caring deeply about penang s heritage and sharing very personal stories regarding the restoration of the mansion. we need to support local stuff like this as travellers and not big soul less multinational hotels. highly recommend this tour.

the place has converted from historical house to hotel. it closes most areas to the public and have a few posters and cheap antique items for showing the history of the place. the guided tour is only offer to visitors at certain hours and close off to visitors in the house if they come in earlier despite paying the same price. it has totally lost its public education function. yet it is still charging the same price as the pinang peranakan mansion which has proper collection and runs good educational tour. not worth of the visit. you can learn everything they have to say by downloading the audio guide app.

we booked a tour also reconfirmed the day before to make sure it was open only to turn up be told it was closed for renovation. pls be aware that the official booking site redirects you to their agent who takes your booking irregardless!!! the management of the blue mansion know this do nothing about it . shocking!

wonderful boutique hotel with beautiful rooms and courtyards good breakfast with lots of choice and very friendly staff. a small pool allows you to cool off after a hot day of sightseeing. perfect place for our three day stay.

beautiful historically significant mansion with pleasant rooms and a top notch breakfast. even though our afternoon tea reservation was little delayed it wasn t a big deal because the tea itself was delicious. beautiful architecture and pleasant stay to stay here is a bit like staying in a museum. high ceilings and antique furniture. i recommend indigo as the perfect venue to host private events. the food is delicious the atmosphere is relaxing the interior deco is lovely the staff are friendly helpful.

it s a quaint place at night security is tight security didn t get my name even though i booked it early on with management . however the place can be a little hot although it s at night. the restaurant is upstairs but there is no clear signage. the bar is also very small and brightly lit. there is a food court nearby that you can walk to and it s convenient to get to town with this prime location.
we were passing by and found that there was a limit on the number of tourists so we didn t go in and out. it looked ok.

this is an amazing heritage site. it is beautiful inside and out. the staff are really lovely friendly and chatty. the rooms are big comfortable beds drinking water and juices provided free of charge a coffee machine kettle in the room but it is only replenished every other day. every afternoon around 4.30pm room service comes with a small steamer basket with two traditional malaysian desserts. one star deducted due to the mass crowds who come to visit at 11am and 2pm. if you are in the guest area relaxing outside your room you really do feel like a zoo animal on show as visitors have no qualms about climbing over the ropes or shoving their phones to snap pictures of the beautiful spaces and sadly you! otherwise go out at these times. the hotel needs to find a balance as it struggles to be either an exclusive hotel or a public museum.

the most amazing experience if only i could stay here everyday. the service and detail in this place is phenomenal. i stayed in one of the liang rooms it was huge super comfy... and just had all those extra little details and gestures that go above and beyond better than i could have ever imagined. the pool was lovely and relaxing the courtyard is beautiful and the complimentary guided tour was interesting. i went for the 5 course taster menu in the indigo restaurant which was also beyond all expectations. recommend to anyone visiting the area.

amazing property and staff. my husband and i recently stayed at the blue mansion for three wonderful nights. it is a little oasis offering luxury and comfort in the middle of a dynamic bustling city. just entering the courtyard transports you to another time and era. upon arrival we were greeted by staff and given nutmeg juice while we registered. delicious! we were then taken to our room which was huge and decorated to respect the grandeur and history of the building where everything was explained to us. during our stay we took the tour of the building to learn its history enjoyed cocktails at the bar listened to live music while we dined in the courtyard cafe mangga and enjoyed dinner at indigo. all of it was fabulous. and yet these physical elements of our stay paled in comparison to the level of service we experienced. everyone was friendly extremely attentive and conscious of needs often before we knew what we needed. jalal in particular served us many meals and ensured all wants were met. the blue mansion and their staff are an experience not to be missed! thank you for a wonderful visit.

we were given a wonderful experience in the wonderful lotus room which had a rear door that led out to the pool. the room had plenty of space. the people working the check in desk were not only very efficient but also very informed about the surrounding area. i had a wonderful time eating breakfast in the courtyard of the hotel. we made the decision to arrange a guided tour which if you stayed in the hotel was completely free.
uniquely penang. delicious tasting menu at indigo. a special place for memorable dinners. mansion parts and restaurantsused as movie set for high budget movies

did a guided tour and it was fantastic! cumalie was the perfect level of engaging very informative and also shared interesting facts! we also had the chance to have a drink at the bar and it was a nice piece of history!

ever wanted to spend the night at a museum. staying here is pretty close to that. a historic building with the restoration staying true to the original with the addition of the necessary modern accessories. the staff and service were top notch. the included breakfast was also very good. we really enjoyed our stay here.
only visited the public ruins we did not stay at the hotel. if you are interested in staying there book you rooms far in advance. mansion itself is gorgeous and worth the trip!

i didn t stay at the mansion but joined the tour this morning. it was one of the best tours i had. the guide a conservator was a wonderful storyteller as she played a huge role in the mansion s conservation. she shared the ups and downs the history of the mansion and the family. it was definitely more than 45 minutes and worth even more than rm25 ticket. highly recommended!

we didn t stay here just a visit. this is a must visit if you re into history and or heritage conservation architecture. tickets are required and the arrangement is great as crowd control is managed well. the tour included is a nice touch as it brings meaning to the place one can t help but have respect for the past and current owners of this mansion. if you make this visit you could skip the other peranakan mansions.

this is our second visit to cheong fatt tze famously known as the blue mansion. this beautifully restored mansion comes with an interesting and fascinating history. we purchased six tickets 25 per person and spend some time here enjoying the view and learning about the history. the first time we visited we managed to see more rooms and artifacts. they have converted part of the mansion into a hotel and restaurant. we were too full from lunch so we gave teatime a miss. it will be an awesome experience to stay here so maybe we will keep that plan for our next trip back to malaysia. it s so cool to know that they filmed the movie crazy rich asians at this mansion simply love the mahjong scene in the movie!

dec 31 2024 i went there for tea time it was a great experience! i never expect bout the taste all of the desserts but it s so tasty! and what makes me happier is i had a really good service from the staff not only with me but to all the guests! thank you mr. ali for your service!

lovely mansion full of history rooms were comfortable breakfast was excellent. there was a slight delay with our afternoon tea that we had booked earlier but was not a big issue afternoon tea itself was delightful. we liked that we could park our car right at the entrance and the location was perfect for sightseeing in georgetown. we enjoyed the delicious kuih that was provided in the rooms and loved that we can refill our water at the water machine such a sustainable way of keeping guests hydrated. the included tour of the house was great. we thought that the bar could be kept open for a bit longer 10pm seems a bit too early... but not an issue overall.

we have stayed at blue mansion for 2 nights during our honeymoon trip. it was the most special place so we highly recommend it! it is a beautiful unesco heritage place. rooms are luxurious and well maintained authentic yet clean. breakfast is delicious with plenty of options. attentive stuff we have got a complementary cocktails to celebrate our honeymoon. minor things to consider you will hear sounds front the hallway and from the music from the food court nearby but the hotel offer earplugs. laundry service is seriously overpriced we paid 60 euros for 1 laundry bag.

a nice hotel in georgetown. it is well located near to little india in a quiet street. the hotel is a heritage monument and has regular tours passing through daily. these can be a bit intrusive especially as the ropes to keep the tourists out of the private seas are easily and frequently stepped over. the room was large and had access right onto the pool area. the bed was comfortable and the shower was excellent! breakfast was provided and was ok. the choices were fairly limited and did not change from day to days

i recently visited cheong fatt tze s blue mansion in penang and opted for the audio self guided tour which i highly recommend for its flexibility compared to the scheduled guided tours. this stunning mansion once home to one of asia s wealthiest men has been beautifully restored and now serves as a museum hotel and restaurant. as i explored the mansion i was captivated by its exquisite furnishings crafted from the finest materials of its time. the design reflects the rich heritage and opulence associated with cheong fatt tze. one fascinating aspect was the feng shui principles applied in the mansion particularly the four points of gold. these four iron cast columns in the main courtyard are said to conceal buried gold symbolizing the prosperity cheong aimed to secure for his family. the mansion s striking indigo hue derived from indigoferra arecta a highly prized dye in the 19th century adds to its allure and prestige. while the courtyards are lovely it s worth noting that some areas are reserved exclusively for hotel guests limiting access for visitors. overall my visit to cheong fatt tze s blue mansion was a delightful journey into the past showcasing the grandeur and intricate details of a bygone era. don t miss this gem when in penang!

liked the hotel itself is just gorgeous. such a unique historical boutique hotel. incredible breakfasts amazing areas to sit and a delightful pool. we stayed in leanhwa lotus flower room which had pool access. sooooo good. we loved the hotel bar cocktails and kept going back for more. fully recommend the caiprinhia the rooms are stunning. great temp control huge ceilings that just give it an amazing airy feel. disliked very very minor grumbling about the tours the hotel itself is an attraction some people on the tour manage to sneak into the hotel area and then pester you to take photos for them or get out of frame . it s rare though! there is a nearby hawker centre that does late night music some nights. it sounds like karoake but isn t they are just terrible singers and very very loud. on a plus side though right next to the hotel is a cafe maanga which has music nights and they sound wonderful. the hotel mentions noise a lot on it s pages but we didnt have any problems sleeping whatsoever if anyone is concerned the music is shut off by midnight.
love the peranakan heritage history and beautiful restoration of this hotel. breakfast spread is amazing. afternoon kuih is a hit! staff are all friendly and professional. love it!

disappointed ! bought ticket to visit the heritage mansion. lady supervisor is rude n only gave us 30 minutes to tour around for we dun want to follow the guide tour. she gave a reason that they need to control the number of visitors so not to disturb the hotel residents puzzled why they open to the public if they intend to keep the place quiet for hotel clients. thus many areas are cordon off and only open for hotel residents. nothing much to see and we completed the tour in 15 minutes not worthwhile if u are not staying in the hotel
we only visited with a tour. you only get to see one atrium and the hotel lobby and need to reserve time slot because only 30 people can go in each time.

the heritage site is definitely being preserved really well maintaining the originality of the building added with few new extensions such as bar and mini museum. the service provided by the staffs are really nice. they are very friendly especially the one named selva. he s a passionate person offered us to take some pictures and he did take very nice shots. overall it s an enjoyable tour.

if you just want to take pictures of the exterior you can do so just from the main gate. you can t go past the gate and get closer to the building. they do have tours that you can pay for to get inside.

i didn t stay overnight in this beautiful mansion but i was lucky enough to join a guided tour of the mansion with mr. yap. he was a fantastic host knowledgeable friendly and always ready with an interesting fact or story. his engaging manner ensured that everyone felt part of the experience. his detailed explanations of the mansion s history and artefacts were fascinating. a highly recommended tour for anyone interested in stepping back in time!

the place is definitely overrated! it s beautiful to visit for a couple of minutes or for a drink and photos but not suitable for a stay lasting more than one night. we re glad thatwe experienced it but we wouldn t choose to stay again. during our two night stay on the ground floor the noise from upstairs was unbearable. every single move from the guests above could be heard. the courtyard lights stayed on all night shining into our windows and directly into our eyes. we didn t have good nights sleep at all. the breakfast was also very disappointing. better breakfast options can be found at nearby cafes. while the room itself is pretty it only has tiny single beds and the bathroom is very small as well without a bath tub. if you are expecting a chinese style heritage hotel this might be the place for you. however if you re looking for a comfortable night s stay it may not be suitable.

my stay at cheong fatt tze was incredible. the location is close to everything i wanted to see in penang the management made sure we understood there is noise from nearby food court with live music nightly beautiful historic building that was renovated with love and care the small touches of a boutique hotel kueh every evening delivered to your door and most of all the staff were amazing. everyone i came across was so nice and wonderful from ali and nasreen in the bar to the staff at breakfast and shiva at reception and the malay gentleman who was always smiling and happy such positive vibes. and i can t leave out the elderly gentleman always a pleasant good morning every morning i saw him. i wanted to book the rooms with the the reflection pools but chose the other side instead which had direct access to the pool and small patio area in the back. make sure you check when you book. i had two room types and both are spacious. i could have used more light in the rooms but found it was the same in another property in kl perhaps it s a malaysia thing? the tours gave so much context to the mansion and brought it s history alive. i had booked it because of it s unesco designation but during the tour i learned it was the setting for indochine and crazy rich asian! i appreciate the rooms displayed with the clothing and items found during renovation that was open for visit anytime. the live music jazz at mannga and guzheng in the courtyard were a nice touch. breakfast in the courtyard with it s rotating menu was a great way to start the day and the blue bar was such a nice a quiet oasis for a quick drink. i d definitely recommend it to friends and family and would certainly return at some point.

i stayed here with my family over christmas 2024 and we were very impressed by the building and the attention to detail the staff make to ensure your stay is as pleasant as possible. i was thoroughly recommend staying here if visiting georgetown as this building is fairly unique and steeps in history.
i don t think it is worth 25rm for a tour. however there is guided tour at 11 am and 3.30 pm. also there is a restaurant called indigo and a bar inside. you can see the mansion once you go to indigo.

this structure had an extremely distinctive design that resembled a chinese palace. it was a highly famous historic hotel. in addition the staff was really kind and welcoming. the services provided were exceptional and first rate. it has a large room great space and a sweet pool area. i had a great time during breakfast and the food was very amazing. definitely worth it for the price.

tours are horribly organized. they let you wait outside for 1h without any explanation and then tell you they can just let 10 peole in. horrible communication and organization. the front sign does not specify the size limit. i ve been there trying to get in twice and saw soooo many people furious about it. pre order online if you really want to get in.

wonderful 3 night stay at the blue mansion. rooms are spacious well equipped. helpful friendly staff. lovely cocktails in the courtyard bar. mangga restaurant serves good food and has nightly music. blue mansion had been wonderfully restored. one of my favourite hotels worldwide.

we loved our guided tour! such a beautiful building with rich history. our tour guide was insightful and lovely. it was however frustrating that she was rudely interrupted by an outsider tour group multiple times... they shouldn t be allowed to operate while genuine tours are in progress. also a shout out to the member of staff who offered to take photos of my family! he really went above and beyond to get the perfect shot!

our hotel in penang was once more the blue mansion also known as cheong fatt tze s mansion. this beautiful house was built at the end of the 19th century and is today a heritage monument of penang and a museum. but you can also stay there as a guest in one of the awesome rooms. it was our second stay there. lots of history to see the main lobby is beautiful. the rooms are full of historical objects but modern comfort is also there in a tasteful way. this year our room had a terrace which was great in the morning with a koi pond one small monitor lizard and birds singing. if you re looking for a very special place to stay in the heart of historical georgetown this is amazing.

this beautiful mansion was built by cheong fatt tze a wealthy businessman. we took a self guided audio tour of this mansion by logging on an app on your smartphone phone. this is beneficial as you can go at your own pace. it is recommended that you book the audio tour tickets online. we didn t purchase tickets ahead so we returned again later. note they only accept at maximum of 12 people per time slot. you only get to tour the main lobby area courtyard ground level and upstairs as there s a hotel within the mansion. it s a beautiful mansion that took six years to restore and repair. we saw some beautiful costumes worn by women musical instruments and asian decor. on the main floor there s a gift store. however other areas such as the restaurant the bar and cafe were available to hotel guests. if you love history and architecture this is a beautiful place to visit.

the blue color of the mansion stood out among the buildings on the street and i was amazed by the majestic architecture of this ancient place. they have a guided tour at certain times of the day and you have to pay for it which kind of forcing visitors to pay just to enter even though i didn t want the tour and just wanted to have a look and take photos of the place. i guess this is another way the place wants to generate more profits even though the place itself is a hotel. i was impressed by how they detailed the history of the owner of the place and the interior decor. i furthered learned that this place has been a popular place for movie shootings as well the known one was crazy rich asians. i d love to stay here for a few nights one day just to enjoy and experience a stay at an ancient mansion.

we were given a wonderful experience in the wonderful lotus room which had a rear door that led out to the pool. the room had plenty of space. the people working the check in desk were not only very efficient but also very informed about the surrounding area. i had a wonderful time eating breakfast in the courtyard of the hotel. we made the decision to arrange a guided tour which if you stayed in the hotel was completely free. the guide was outstanding and responded to questions with great expertise. what a delightful visit we look forward to returning soon.

staying at cheong fatt tze the blue mansion is a one of a kind experience not only in penang but the world. given the history of the man behind the original building of the mansion the uniqueness of staying there in the present is magical. it s the result of the individuals who decided to transform the building after it suffered from decades of neglect back into a state of magnificence. their marvelous achievement allows you to experience the distant past in the most comfortable of settings with present day luxurious conveniences. we stayed in the 50s room for 5 nights and the experience remains in my daily thoughts weeks later. having your private jacuzzi ready and swirling especially after a trip to penang hill and then hiking down to the botanical gardens is just one luxurious example of staying in this particular room at the blue mansion. and writing of immersions this room has direct access to a serene swimming pool and its garden like grounds. along with the incredible place are the friendly and helpful people who work there making our stay even more unforgettable. from the moment we arrived whether getting help with our luggage checking in or ordering the first of many delicious meals the kindness we were greeted with added greatly to the experience of staying in such an impressive place. a special shout out to the welcoming waiters at indigo restaurant the courtyard breakfast crew including jalal and resty and the best barman bar none ali. looking forward to our next opportunity of staying at cheong fatt tze s mansion in penang.

visited the blue mansion in penang expecting something impressive but it was a complete letdown. the place is extremely overrated. the paint is fading and it s clear this is just an old neglected house. the indian guard at the entrance was incredibly rude treating visitors like they re beneath him. they wouldn t even let us near the premises unless we had tickets which are always fully booked for no good reason. inside there s nothing special just old rooms with worn out furniture. total waste of money! even the welcome arch looks like a child s sloppy spray paint job. save yourself the trip!

beautiful mansion full of history rooms were comfortable breakfast was excellent. staffs are good and and very helpful. we enjoyed the delicious kuih that was provided in the rooms and loved that we can refill our water at the water machine such a sustainable way of keeping guests hydrated. overall satisfied good value for money.

managed to join the guided tour today and it was an amazing eye opening experience! honestly i only expected to see a very blue building but left with so much more than that! catch the 11am friday tours if you can. our guide sorry i forgot her name! said she only does this slot weekly. she and her husband are responsible for the restoration of the blue mansion and you could feel her passion coming through for this project. it really helped make the tour so much more enriching

its a beautiful traditional house. the tour was informative although the guide was at times difficult to hear due to the large group and the bad microphone she had. you can only see a limited part of the house as they renovated it to a hotel. not a must in my opinion.

beautiful mansion. the location is central to the city. the place is nicely renovated and charming. i stayed there for 2 nights with my colleagues. the triple bedded rooms were huge ceilings were high and the bathroom had its own huge ceiling fan. the only thing was the hot water was not continuous for bathtub soaking but was not a major issue for me. the breakfast buffet was also good. the sitting was sort of inside the courtyard so it was very cool. the whole vibe is tranquil and throws you back into the last. there is a guided house tour in the morning for a few but free for the hotel guests. couldn t join due to back to back plans in vibrant penang.

this is a review of the tour not the hotel. the tour was boring too long and too crowded and there s very little to see in any case. the pinang peranakan museum is much more interesting even though the building is not as impressive.

my friend and i joined the guided tour and totally enjoyed it. the explanation from the guide was very informative and clear. the mansion is beautifully restored. the experience was further enhanced by one of the staff selva. he knows that visitors are there to enjoy the mansion and will want to take beautiful pictures in the mansion. he readily helped my friend and i to take beautiful pictures and i also saw him helping other visitors to do it too. he has very good photography skills and i can see that he is passionate doing it. selva is definitely an asset to the mansion. well done selva!

we had our wedding here last week and we were so impressed by how exceptional all the staff were. they were friendly attentive hospitable and many of our wedding guests were amazed. in the midst of the busyness they noticed and immediately attended to any of our guests who needed anything. jafifah the hotel manager was everything we could ever ask for and more. on top of displaying superior professionalism she went out of her way to make this wedding happen for us and reassured us that we were in good hands every step of the way. on the day of the wedding everything was so well coordinated from decor setup to the timing of food service of the amazing dinner spread chef jack had prepared. she and the staff members of the blue mansion worked so harmoniously with all the other vendors we really could not have asked for more. we highly recommend the blue mansion to anyone who s considering having their wedding here.

we have long been fans of the blue mansion. it is beautiful distinctive and richly filled with history. this history includes our own we chose to have our wedding dinner here about a decade ago lovely! . our most recent visit for high tea was brightened by one of their excellent staff members selva who took a number of skillful photos of us with our iphone. we will cherish those photos and look forward to our next visit to the blue mansion.

staying at cheong fatt tze the blue mansion is truly like stepping back in time yet with all the modern luxuries you could want. i was fortunate enough to join a tour led by the owner herself on a friday and her passion for the mansion s rich history was evident in every word. her knowledge made the experience not only informative but deeply engaging allowing us to appreciate the mansion s cultural significance in a whole new light. the rooms are immaculate with a perfect blend of old world charm and contemporary comfort. it s rare to find a place where history and luxury harmonize so beautifully. if you stay here do yourself a favor and book the deliciously extensive breakfast it s worth every bite. the indigo restaurant and bar are also must visits offering fantastic service and a diverse menu that leaves you wanting for nothing. the wide selection of wines and cocktails is another highlight perfect for those who enjoy a good drink. entertainment is abundant too. the bar just outside often hosts jazz performances while inside the mansion you re treated to the soothing sounds of traditional chinese instruments from the courtyard creating a serene and unique atmosphere. overall cheong fatt tze the blue mansion is not just a hotel but an unforgettable experience.

the mansion is now a hotel bar and restaurant.. and most of the building is blocked off unless you stay here. rm25 to enter and view a hall and few rooms is not worth it at all. they should price it down to rm10 max

the rooms were spacious and preserved the old charm of the mansion. rooms had complimentary filtered water and drinks in the fridge. complimentary nonya kueh as provided every evening as a snack. location is convenient the only drawback are the crowds of visitors during the tour hours that may crowd the main hall when you are there.

booked this awesome hotel to celebrate a special occasion. the hotel was very accommodating and made special arrangement to decorate the room and offered a free afternoon tea. the staffs speak good english and are very friendly and helpful. the place is just filled with history. the inclusion of a free guided tour is a bonus and allowed us to know the history of the building and the owner. i would suggest that you book a guided tour even if you are not staying in this beautiful hotel.

reviewing the tour only as i was not a guest. i d advise you to save your money and skip this spot. the tour doesn t show you much. you only get to see the main courtyard and surrounding main rooms which have been converted into museum exhibits rather than preserved in their original state. as it is now a boutique hotel the other rooms are rented to guests so you can t see the rest of the mansion until you are staying there. the whole tour felt like an advertisement trying to convince you to book a room and spend money at the restaurant or gift shop there.

i booked a ticket online in advance for the 15 30 guided tour in english. the tour starts punctually so arrive on time. check the website for tour times or you can purchase at the door. stunning mansion. the architecture. elaborate. very organised. the tour is advertised as 45 minutes but allow an hour or slightly longer. my negatives were the guide and the group size was way too big. i couldn t understand the guide well unless i stood close to her. some of the information she gave wasn t really great. she made a comment about wearing black and white meaning sadness in chinese culture and she didn t understand why people would wear this combination. there were a few people in our group wearing black and white and i think it was very offensive of her to say that. all she needed to do was state the facts. she was also constantly trying to promote and sell things like staying at the hotel having a drink or food and then suprisingly leading us to the gift shop to end the tour. i started to get bored towards the end and so did others. i think they need to reduce the group size of the tour and limit it to 45 minutes.

beautiful historically significant mansion with pleasant rooms and a top notch breakfast. even though our afternoon tea reservation was little delayed it wasn t a big deal because the tea itself was delicious. the location was excellent for exploring georgetown and we appreciated that we this is such a green way to keep visitors hydrated. the house tour that was provided was excellent.

the mansion itself is unique when you re there it takes you back in time. as guests staying over at the hotel we had the pleasure of participating in the guided tour without charge. we enjoyed the tour the history and stories shared were interesting although one could argue the guide can do with less sexist remarks perhaps he wanted to emphasize the reality of what it was like in the early 1900s. our room was tastefully decorated well equipped and comfortable. breakfast was decent but service was extremely slow. i m leaving 4 stars as we a group of four so two rooms felt that the standards of cleanliness could be improved it is obvious that the cupboards and closets haven t been dusted in a long time the marks on the coffee machines and cups were not properly cleaned the glass shelves in the bathroom were similarly left un wiped.

if you are looking for a beautiful historical place to stay this is it. nicely done conservation and near to the city centre making it easy for you to go shopping and sightseeing. although it has a beautiful architecture with a prestige museum ambiance the couryard is very nice and relaxing place to be. the staff is friendly and accommodating. with spacious room for guest to stay it is a recommended place to host private events. please do try their breakfast it s top notch.

we had a great visit to the blue mansion. the room was large and very comfortable. we stayed in the old kitchen which had a spa bath to soak after our long days of exploring. all the staff were so welcoming and helpful during our stay. the blue mansion is such a beautiful building with a great story behind it that we learned about during a tour. would definitely recommend staying here. great breakfast choices and within walking distance of many of georgetowns must visit areas.

fantastic experience and sally is one of the best tour guide that we have seen in malaysia. great coordination from jafifah as well. well done. we love everything about here and the history is very inspiring

we arrived around 3 pm. possibly due to the heavy rain that day there weren t many tourists. according to online information it s best to book admission tickets in advance. this is the former residence of cheong fatt tze. i really liked the interior design which is relatively simple. interestingly right after the area for the tour is a part that s used as a hotel. i particularly liked the courtyard design. personally i found the second floor restroom the most interesting. there s a black and white photo of a woman and a black and white photo of a man hanging separately but there s no sign indicating it s a restroom. i hesitantly pushed open the door only to realize it was indeed a restroom the third floor has a restaurant. if you have time it s worth a visit. you can feel the homeowner s design intention for the courtyard and the place offers a great setting for photography.

easily the best hotel we have stayed at during our trip through malaysia. not just the unique feel of the blue mansion but certainly also the service was impeccable everyone was just so friendly and helpful for our 10 months old baby. in particular enjoyed the live music evening at mangga and the friendly and fast service by jalal always with a smile!

booked the old kitchen room for our anniversary and this place absolutely delivered. with beautiful traditional decor and amazing amenities everywhere in the mansion my only regret is only staying for a night! will definitely return when i m in penang again!

we took a tour of the mansion which was conducted by a man who claimed to be a descendant of the illustrious owner. he did a wonderful job of explaining the rich history of the mansion as well as taking us back to the time period. the opulence of the house and family heirlooms reflected their wealth. the architecture of the house was also fascinating. it is a place worthwhile visiting.

joined the tour here. totally enjoyed the tour. price of tour was rm25 for locals. do book session in advance. it was a fully book group in my session. you get to listen about stories of the families that stayed there original owner and the many film that uses it as filming locations. one of the recent one is crazy rich asian. don t miss this when you are penang.

when we travel choosing a hotel is the most important thing. when we came to indigo for lunch we decided to stay here next time. it lived up to our expectations. the staff were considerate and always made sure that tour guides could not access the hotel area so we were able to stay quietly. we thank the staff for preparing a nice room. we have decided to travel to penang to stay here again. and we would like to recommend your hotel to my friends.

we booked a couple of nights at the tang suite here and while the mansion was absolutely beautiful we encountered a few major issues so it s safe to say we won t be returning here again. if you are determined to stay here you should be aware of the shortcomings. the good good location. it was nowhere as noisy as expected and we were light sleepers beautiful historic mansion the breakfast at the courtyard was good sitting around the atrium at night and having a drink was memorable staff were helpful and mostly courteous the bad we booked the airport pickup and prepaid for it only the get a few subsequent emails asking us to confirm the details again and again with a payment link they sent a really old nissan sedan with a stinky smell and there was a used mask on the back seats. we managed to cancel the return leg of the transfer and they refunded the unused leg in the form of hotel credit which i suppose is as good as one could expect the hotel could be quite crowded with lots of tourists which some of them were not the most well mannered and could trespass into hotel guest only areas we book the suite but there was no living room and no sofa only two small uncomfortable chairs so there was no place to lounge around. it should really be named a slightly larger bedroom the jacuzzi in the room had cold water and we didn t use it at all. more of a gimmick. the ugly given they have a lot of visitors the restroom at the bar was very dirty and stinky. we had to check out at 12 and returned to pick up our luggage obviously one couldn t expect a spa for a shower so we had to use the bar s restroom and we noped out of it the aircon was very cold at 18c when we checked in and we had to turn the aircon down throughout the night to get to a comfortable temperature. the next night we had the aircon set at 23c and the second night we couldn t sleep as me and my wife suffered 5 mozzy bites each and that kept us up. having been to tanzania for a safari trip recently we already tried out best to minimise the time of the door being open and maintained light discipline but to no avail. it seems that the place was just swamped with mosquitoes generally. all in all by the time we checked out and got to the airport we were extremely knackered. was it a nice and beautiful mansion with lots of character? absolutely. was it a nice hotel to stay at where one can get a good sleep? not at all. if you don t mind the negatives then it could be a place for you. but equally if you could come here for dinner then i don t really see the point of having to stay here overnight.

we stayed at the blue mansion for four nights in feb 2024 and had the most magical time. we really enjoyed the historical energy around us loved the staff and thought the food at indigo cafe mangga afternoon tea and the bar were phenomenal. this was our first time to penang and we already can t wait to come back! there were bicycles for guests to rent and we were able to take the mansion tour for free to learn more about the mansion. there are private courtyards just for guests so even when the mansion is busy with guests touring there is a quiet and relaxing space to spend time in. my husband and i will definitely make this a regular vacation spot when we are in asia.

we got a self guided tour which we downloaded on our phone and used with headphones. very clever and modern way to view the mansion museum. there is also a bar and restaurant in the mansion alongside the hotel. worth a visit if interested in history.

the amount of work they ve put into restoring this place just wow. mom stayed here for a night and said the beds were a little hard but overall the stay was fantastic. she absolutely loved the staff and made friends with a few folks pictures below who she said went above and beyond to make her time incredible. we went on a tour the next morning and learning about the history of the place makes it even more interesting. beautiful grounds and in a great location if you want a unique experience highly recommend!
from the late night check to the 5 night stay end it was historic luxury!

visited this beautiful mansion for the excellent guided tour in english . well worth it. restaurant upstairs does a great value fine dining lunch menu in a beautiful setting. would have loved to stay for lunch but was out voted. the bar area is pretty dull compared to the rest of the place.

beautiful historically significant mansion with pleasant rooms and a top notch breakfast. even though our afternoon tea reservation was little delayed it wasn t a big deal because the tea itself was delicious. the location was excellent for exploring georgetown and we appreciated that we could park our car right at the front door. we relished the delectable kuih that was provided in the rooms and appreciated being able to refill our water bottles at the water machine. this is such a green way to keep visitors hydrated. the house tour that was provided was excellent.

we visited this famous restored mansion and joined the tour. there are lots of interesting architecture elements being a mix of chinese and western styles. the tour guide provided lots of information although not all areas of the place were open to visit. the tour cost rm 25 and the only way to get to visit the mansion is either being on the tour or being a room or dining guest.

delightful period building . it s like staying in a living museum. definitely worth a few nights. we stayed 3 nights. you can follow a tour around the hotel on a smart phone or a guided tour to get to its history. swimming pool a little small but a nice cooling dip after sightseeing.
room is ok hotel is right next to the beach love the little balcony which can hang dry my swimming suit. walking distance to main area.

was pleasantly surprised by this mansion tour. our guide alexa was very enthusiastic and detailed in her explanations. we learnt so much about the history of the mansion the life of the owner and occupants. there s also a really nice bar you can enjoy a cocktail at after the tour. well worth our money!
amazing world heritage site friendly and hospitable staff would recommend to anyone!

we did a tour of the heritage blue mansion. it was a very nice cultural and enriching experience. after the tour we went around taking photos and a very friendly staff by the name of selva actually helped and gave us advice on taking nice photos around the place. we were very impressed by his initiative and his photography skills. very nice experience!

beautiful building with many lovingly restored details. nice for a short visit. however when staying the night beware that the noise from the neighbours at night is extremely loud and makes any sleep before midnight impossible. most staff was polite but not as friendly as elsewhere. breakfast was ok.

this is a unique and fascinating hotel. we stayed four nights and every day revealed more details and history. unexpected pleasures included a jazz trio for sunday tea an excellent meal at indigo and attentive and friendly staff. great location in georgetown.

my wife and i stayed at this historical landmark mansion for 3 nights as part of our first anniversary trip! we surely enjoyed a unique experience staying in this mansion which takes you back in time! the staff was very friendly welcoming and helpful. i would surely recommend staying here for anyone looking for a unique experience to remember.

my wife and i stayed at this historical landmark mansion for 3 nights as part of our first anniversary trip! we surely enjoyed a unique experience staying in this mansion which takes you back in time! the staff was very friendly welcoming and helpful. i would surely recommend staying here for anyone looking for a unique experience to remember. we were welcomed by ginger a spoiled cute cat! in our room which was quite a fund memory!

the staff went above and beyond to arrange for a taxi for us and prepare breakfast for our early trip. the mansion is fantastic our room was large and furnished with all the luxuries you could want for a happy stay. we had a great time throughout our visit and would most likely return.

i recently visited the exhibition 22 may 2024 with my family and had a disappointing encounter with two female staff members at the service counter. despite being paying customers we were not provided with adequate assistance when we asked for directions to the nearest toilet. instead of directing us to the nearby facility within the building we were instructed to go to the cafe outside which was a considerable distance away. unfortunately these staff members did not demonstrate a helpful or courteous attitude which fell short of our expectations. however i would like to commend two other staff members who provided exceptional service. the first was a gentleman of malay descent who resembled a seasoned martial arts expert and kindly showed us to the nearest toilet. he also addressed the unhelpful behavior of his colleagues. the second staff member who had a physical disability offered us seats and helped us take a wonderful family photo all without expecting anything in return. his enthusiasm and dedication to his work were truly inspiring. i believe these two staff members deserve recognition for their outstanding service. on the other hand the two female staff members could benefit from additional training to improve their customer service skills.

what a stunning mansion and hotel! this exquisite location is famously featured in the mahjong scene of crazy rich asians. i joined the guided tour and was captivated by the rich history and beautiful architecture of the mansion. it was a wonderful opportunity to learn about peranakan culture and heritage. however the tour was quite crowded making it difficult to hear the guide clearly at times. it would be much more enjoyable if they limited the number of participants to ensure everyone can fully appreciate the experience.

i only attended a guided tour. no any instructions from the unfriendly security where we can cannot go and how long the guided tour takes . i had high expectation so felt dissappointed. the mansion is beautiful but there are very limited places to see. i am glad i chose another hotel to stay.

this is no doubt a must visit place when you are in penang. its beautiful architecture will please your eyes. but unfortunately that was not as much as you can explore since half of the mansion had became hotel rooms and fancy restaurant. do you think 25 ringgit worth the entrance fee? i dont think so. since that was not so many to explore. compared to tjong a fie mansion in medan where you can enjoy and explore every single room.

what an amazing experience to stay in such a beautiful restored heritage house. incredible! we absolutely loved our stay in penang and the blue mansion was the most perfect place to stay. i was even given a little cake for my birthday. we wouldn t hesitate staying here again next time we re in penang.

a really interesting tour of the blue mansion. well worth the money. our english tour guide was superb an excellent an informative communicator. there is a charming history of the building which is explained in detail by the guide. the mansion has been impressively well preserved. floor tiles from stoke on trent. who would of thought it? its great that there is british influence in the architectural style of the mansion. a special mention to mr selva who was a very friendly staff member working during our tour. he is very gracious and helped with a few queries we had whilst in the building.

it was our 40th wedding anniversary and we loved every moment at cheong fatt tze mansion. the mansion is old worldly and gorgeous. faizah and her team were all attentive and caring. our thanks to everyone. jalal made sure we started each day with a perfect breakfast the security guard at the gate by manga was ever so helpful with information when we needed it. and our room was exactly the one we had hoped we d get. it looked and smelled beautiful. thank you our stay was everything we could have hoped for.

this should be one of the better off island off kuala terengganu due to the limited resort operators hence limited number of tourists. however it is very sad that the entire beach is litter with rubbish and plastics are the main culprit. the resort operators the people who are working on these resorts and the tourists visiting these resorts need to do better and care more of the environment. it would be great for resort operators to educate tourists upon their arrival as part of their welcome orientation and entirely ban plastic bottles cups and etc. the government of malaysia should do better to ban plastic bottles on marine park areas. the dead corals is a sad sight to see thought the problem only plague the perhentian and redang islands due to its sheer numbers of visitors didn t expect the same happened to this island. please save our nature our islands and the ocean.
come here with my family. the scenery very stunned. very calm suitable for healing short break from busy town. love it.

to all island and diving lovers ! you guys have to go to this place . this place is suitable for a couple vacation honeymoon family day and holiday . this island is really beautiful and the sea is very clear . fantastic !

best island to visit for who loves to diving and snorkeling.

as part of our langkawi island hopping. just a stop by for maybe five minutes and we viewing whatever we could from the boat. of course the central attraction is helang or eagles flying up and about and closer to the water and the surrounding. my my! lang tengah island. as part of langkawi and its pride.

pulau redang is one of the best island in malaysia with beautiful view and scenery alongside pulau perhentian and others famous islands in terengganu. not to forget the gorgeous aquatic living and the white sandy beach make it as a main attraction point for people to come here. it s a spot for nature lover honeymoon and to bring family. there are numerous packages that you can choose starting from day trip to budget stay to a luxurious stay. it s all available. there are a lot of chalet and resort available and the departure is not from one particular jetty instead there are few points you can reach here. make sure to alert and be prepared. here you can see various type of fish turtle and even shark if you are lucky. it s a heavy tourist spots especially during holiday season so make sure to plan your journey well.

beautiful. quite accessible about one hour by boat. i would say the boat ride in bad weather is not pleasant. we stayed at the taraas resort which i cannot really recommend depends what you like. i would rather stay at a seafront hotel and have local food options to explore.
beautiful sea crystal clear water. little shark thousands of coral reefs and big turtles that are worth a visit this time. it is an island that offers privacy.

joined a day trip to redang with family and booked a private boat from haa tiey boat service. great experience! we went to several beautiful snorkeling spots with coral colorful fish nemo and baby sharks. the views were amazing. everything was well organized. long beach for water confident gua batu kongkang teluk mak delah for snorkelling turtle point pantai teluk dalam beting pasir pulau kerengga little maldives for awesome view. go and enjoy

last visit was 27 yrs ago. many changes happening. many resort building up. love the beaches. water is crystal clear. beaches are clean. properties maintain the cleanliness around them. i will definitely come back again.

beautiful white sand paradise on east coast of malaysia. must travel between march to october upon that is monsoon season. nature is beautiful but still have some pollution with old boats spilling diesel on the water. easily can see black tip shark and turtles.

we visited in mid march at the end of the monsoon season. there were three adults and two kids 6 and 12 in our group and we had the boat to ourselves. the ride from merang jetty took about an hour and while it was fun and exciting for the first 30 minutes it got pretty tiring after that. but seeing the turtles at the first stop made it worth it! the next stop had lots of fish and the sandbar with turquoise waters was perfect for photos. some of the corals were a bit bleached but the snorkeling was great especially for the kids since it was their first time. the return ride was rougher with bigger waves but luckily we had motion sickness meds or it would ve been tough. overall it was a great experience but i d recommend going when the sea is calmer.

came in july beautiful and blue water but the tide was quite high my journey going back from the island to the mainland were quite challenging all people in boat are soaking wet due to the high tide and boat speeding through it. the boatman said the best time to go here were in february to june but if came too early you may not find any turtle as it is not their season yet april and may are much better time with lots of turtle and better tide during that month. beautiful place but lots of their coral are bleached during my visits. the sandbank are beautiful but the sand are quite coarse as it has a lot of broken seashells so make sure wear a slipper or swimshoe to protect your feet bcs some boatman cant park their boat at the sandbank due to high tide so in order to reach the sand you need to swim a bit.

quoted by alessia. redang is a paradise. i prefer this island over perhentian due to the private and serene nature of the island. having said that turtle at here love people. they are big and happy to greet us. having my first snorkelling trip here. it was scary and fantastic at one point. the coral the parrotfish the big garoupa and the cute nemo are colourful. the water is turqoise in colour. wow cool awesome you name it. i will definitely come again for me time. meets few friends from italy belgium and albania. the said redang is one the most beautiful island in the world. terengganu and malaysia should be proud. come here and exprience it by yourself.
among the best beaches in malaysia.. please keep the island clean

lovely island with soft sand and clear blue water. love that this is not a party island or activity focused place as those places generally you cannot enjoy the beach due to many boats going in and out banana boat rides and jetskis. beaches here are generally clean but there are some people leaving trash behind. pls do not be that person.

absolutely beautiful with snorkeling diving exp. bring your gears gopro so you won t miss the beautiful scenes. a bit far from kl i took a bus to get there arrived early morning 6am but well worth the trip. they have 1 day tour for a quick visit but can absolutely stay longer 3d2n if you love the beaches marine life. set no expectation mother nature will revert to you pls bring your own bottles protect the environment

please take note best date to go to redang island is on mid of march 10th and later until end of september .redang island is close from october until february because of northeast monsoon winds. redang island for me is in top 10 list best beautiful must visit island in malaysia. it takes around 1 hour 30minutes maybe from kuala besut jetty to redang island. from marang jetty to redang island only takes 30 minutes. don t forget to bring ice box storage 10l or bigger with ice cubes inside with drinks if you are about to do snorkeling swimming diving journey to some places with boat captain. place your food and baverages on the boat. you may get hungry and dehydration because of water activities. bring your own snorkeling goggles for best experience. boat captain s provided snorkeling goggles but i think their goggles is little dirty and not be clean up properly. the most important thing is wear your safety life jacket correctly and properly!! enjoy your holidays . visit malaysia cuti cuti malaysia terengganu kita ganu kite

redang marine park not only offers crystal clear water but an amazing view of the sea and the hills. the island is located just 15 minutes away from laguna redang island resort by boat and it is highly recommended to visit. redang marine park is an ideal place for snorkeling with many colorful fishes and corals to explore. there will be snorkeling equipment available for rent and visitors are allowed to feed the bread to the fish. the speedboat ride to the marine park was very relaxing and enjoyable as the surroundings offers beautiful views.

pulau redang is situated off the coast of terengganu in the south china sea malaysia. one of the largest island in the east coast measuring 7km 6km. it is about 18nautical mile of merang jetty. merang jetty is about 40km from kuala terengganu.there is also boat service from shahbandar jetty kuala terengganu.the distance from shahbandar jetty to p.redang is 32 nautical mile.there is also domestic flight from kuala lumpur to this island. this island has been a popular destination for diving snorkelling and other beach activities in the sandy beaches and crystel clear waters. pulau redang is also gazetted and protected as marine parks. there are more than 10 dive centers and more than 20 dive site here. 2 ship wreck the sea here offers you with diversity of fishes like bumphead parrotfish barracudas jacks snappers napolean wrasse fusilier turtles and black tip shark. you can also find soft and hard coral sponges whips sea fans and nudibranch. some of the beaches here taaras beach best beach on island long beach teluk dalam beach besar and kecil mutiara beach this island has been too commercialised and always crowded during weekend and public holiday. the coral reefs in pulau redang are rapidly declining due to climate change and local human activities. came here for scuba diving.

having visited all 3 famous islands of terengganu i would rank redang as the 3rd island after the 2nd place kapas island perhentian island wins the 1st spot in terms of cleanliness seclusive well preserved marine reserve park. basically before visiting any island one needs to know what one wants from the visit before making a blind booking. in other words kapas redang islands are good for a quick family getaway considering the the amenities convenience available. while perhentian island demands more time patience willpower to explore its well preserved beauty well protected marine park. which of the 3 islands i would repeat? perhentian a big yes!! the marine park its beautifully alive. kapas maybe if i have a limited time budget at hand. redang not within the next 2 years. the marine park sucks bleached corals dirty beaches.

this used to be an amazing island maaaany years ago. nowadays coral is all dead. dont even think in snorkeling there is nothing to see. we did not had water for 3 days so no showers. no toilets working. if possible try not to come here unless you want to make diving. diving still has some good places to visit
