redang island is made for beach lovers with its white sand beaches and crystal clear ocean water making it popular with snorkelers great snorkeling is the island s main attraction. scuba divers love it here too. though it s one of the largest islands off the eastern malay peninsula it s small enough that visitors can get around on foot either on roads or trekking through the jungle. redang island is one of nine islands in a marine nature reserve. accommodations are mostly resort style. the island boasts some of the best beaches in south east asia. water is crystal clear and warm the sand is white and mostly clean and the island itself offers great places to snorkel or dive. the beaches itself make the island worth visiting. however there are two major drawbacks. first its quite a hassle to get to and around on the island. either you take a 1.5h public ferry or get a speedboat pickup from your resort costly not sure about the time saving . on the island itself there is no public transportation. as the island is very small it lacks proper infrastructure in general so you are pretty much stuck with the resort you are staying at except for a few restaurants as a cheaper alternative to the expensive on site options . second drawback is the lackluster quality of the resorts given the prices that are being charged. if you are there primarily for beach and water activities and do not care too much about your accommodation then this island is highly recommended. we visited for our honeymoon and it was marvelous. we swan with turtles saw coral and walked across beautiful powder white beaches. before booking an activity with your resort try checking with any of the local restaurants. pretty much all of them offer the same activity for a lower price.

too much touristy. chinese tourists ruined the serenity of the place. the water is amazing. don t book packages. be careful while swimming. wear life jacket. the reading beach resort is worst. snorkeling is good.

weather was beautiful beach was as clean as it can get with some litter here and there washed up by the waves. sand was super fine and soft. redang reef resort was basic but clean hygienic ok food but fantastic staff. all very friendly and nice but keeps to themselves.

i love this island. it s ridiculously crystal clear water i could see group of fishes under the water even in a long distance. to dive into a deep level of the sea is always on my bucket list and pulau redang is always been my preferred target to diving. it s the most lovely moment to swim with a lot of fishes around. frankly speaking it s very worth to spend my money at.

pulau redang is an island in terengganu malaysia. famous for its clear waters and white sandy beaches. it is one of nine islands forming a marine sanctuary that offers snorkelling and diving experience for tourists. the beach is covered with soft white sand. coral and fish can be seen just a few metres from the beach. the beach resorts are located in teluk dalam on the north and teluk kalong to the south.

the crystal clear beach is the best thing because i can see the fish and tuttle s just like that. i feeling like blessed seeing those for the first time. it s like a natural swimming pool. the best experience i had.

redang terengganu this east coast island is an absolute dream come true for marine wildlife lovers as it is a known nesting ground and sanctuary for sea turtles. this beautifully preserved island getaway is also popular for snorkeling and scuba diving and a perfect spot for a romantic honeymoon.

it is a beautiful island in malaysia. water is so clean and it is a good place for snorkeling. i went for a jungle trekking. it is a very good place for relax and one of my favorite places in southeast asia.

great place to relax but most of the coral at the marine park are dead. can t believe that they didn t take good care and it s disappointing and saddening to see all the dead corals are along the whole beach of the marine park.
very nice! we were swimming with turtles

a picturesque tropical island. the most popular beach of redang island is the long beach. it s located on the east side of the island. there are many resorts located right on this beach. it s a long stretch of white sandy beach with deep blue water. though it tends to be a bit crowded at times during the super peak season. my favorite beach is turtle s beach. it s located on the north of the island is not overcrowded like long beach even at it s peek i haven t seen more than a several dozens of people vast majority of whom were locals on the about 400m long stretch of the beach. as you would guess based on it s name you can see turtles over here. small boats in 2 5 batches are coming from all over the resorts located around the island to lure turtles by small fishes and squids from the bottom of the sea all day long. it s a good opportunity to swim only about 50 100m off the shore to join those people swimming with turtles. depending on your swimming skills or lack of that you may consider renting a snorkeling mask life jacket or even fins right on the beach for about rm25 30 per day per whole set. there are small bamboo stalls selling fresh coconut and canned beverages in the evening you may also purchase fresh grilled corn for only rm3 a piece. if you would be up to something other then spending time on the beautiful beaches there are some guided and unguided activities also available. you may go for a scuba diving session or even enroll for a course to obtain your diving license in a few days there s massage and atw and snorkling tours offered too. should you be up for some hiking or rather walking through the jungle there s a footpath available between long beach and turtle s beach. it s about 2.5km long and can be comfortable taken in about 30 minutes without rushing. should you just want rest on the island and do nothing you will appreciate the peaceful nature of this place though in this case you had better stay near the kampung aka village in the middle of the island like redang de rimba resort.
a peaceful relaxing island gear snorkelling

a clean island with good facilities for those who are looking for a calm stay far from crowded cities. the beach is clean with f fishes and coral. at one of the beaches one can swim with huge turtles as big as humans just like documentary films. the airport is rarely used and get is the most common way of getting there

1. you can buy a day trip package at the merang jetty. 2. buy a package with lunch. 3. there are many places to park at the jetty. 4. the boat will depart at 9 am from the merang jetty. 5. the trip takes 30 minutes to 40 minutes depending on the weather conditions. 6. the boat will take you to 6 7 places for snorkeling. 7. bring some bread to feed the fish. 8. hold the bread and the fish will stop to eat. 9. you can swim with turtles. 10. avoid touching the turtles because if you get bitten you may break your fingers. 11. there are many fish by the beach. 12. the boat will take you back and arrive at the merang jetty at 4.30 pm.

kek lok si temple is one of the most impressive places to visit in penang. the temple complex is large beautiful and full of cultural details that make every corner worth seeing. the architecture statues and prayer halls are well preserved and you can really feel the peaceful and spiritual atmosphere while walking around. one of the highlights is the towering statue of the goddess of mercy and the panoramic view from the higher levels of the temple. the view overlooking penang is stunning especially on a clear day. there are also souvenir stalls and small shops along the way which makes the visit more convenient if you want snacks or keepsakes. be ready for a bit of walking and stairs but there are lift options for easier access. overall it s not just a religious site but also a cultural and scenic attraction that gives you a deeper appreciation of penang s heritage. definitely worth visiting at least once.

view kek lok si temple. . also known as the temple of supreme bliss this is malaysia s largest buddhist temple and one of southeast asia s most important pilgrimage sites. . built between the late 19th and early 20th centuries it blends chinese thai and burmese architectural influences into a sprawling hillside complex with an inclined cable car lift. . highlights include the 7 storey ten thousand buddhas pagoda serene temple gardens zen prayer halls a 36.5 metre tall bronze statue of guanyin and a tortoise pond. . fortunate to visit during chinese new year when thousands of lanterns illuminate the complex.

we went from butterworth took the ferry to georgetown from there we got onto the public bus that dropped us off at the temple. we admired the architecture and all the work that went into building these layers and layers of structures. very impressive. to get to the top level we had to take the funicular as walking was apparently not an option. walking between the tiers was not as stringent as we expected it to be. it s very doable. we enjoyed the feeding the turtles in the very impressive turtle pond.

visiting kek lok si felt like stepping into a completely different world above busy penang. the walk up through the small souvenir stalls and steep steps already sets the mood before you even reach the main temple grounds. the mix of colors incense in the air and quiet prayers made the whole place feel deeply spiritual but still welcoming to visitors. climbing up to the pagoda was one of my favorite moments especially for the views over george town. seeing the huge guan yin statue up close was impressive and surprisingly peaceful despite other tourists around. if you take your time explore slowly and just sit for a few minutes you really feel the soul of the place. follow me for more roxana.iamandi.

well worth a visit we got a taxi from our hotel to the temple and spent a couple of hours wandering around. we enjoyed our tim here. nice view s from the top across the area. you can book tram tickets to the various levels and back again if you don t want a lot of climbing.

kek lok si is hands down one of the most impressive temples in southeast asia. the scale of the place is breathtaking especially when you reach the top to see the towering guanyin statue. i was particularly amazed by the intricate details from the walls lined with thousands of small buddha statues to the vibrant red and yellow lanterns that create a perfect frame for photos. the grounds are well kept and the view of georgetown air itam near air itam dam is worth the climb. it can get quite hot so bring water and wear comfortable shoes as there are many stairs though there is an inclinator lift available for a small fee. highly recommended for both the spiritual atmosphere and the photography opportunities.

this is the biggest coolest temple we visited in southeast asia! it is a gorgeous buddhist temple open to visitors with a lot of different views buildings and history! there is a great city view but it feels very separate from it in a good way!

absolutely stunning! this place is well worth a visit. of all temples in south east asia i ve seen this would be in my top 3. 6 ringgit to get to the top although when i was there we couldn t get right up the very top but i suspect this was due to set up for chinese new year.

apart from the famous buddhist statues and building it would be great if the management team can enhance the tourist experience by providing easy pricing structure to avoid many tourists have to spend time asking questions at the ticket counter. a clear pathway leading to the next transit stop is preferred as the stalls selling souvenirs or drinks tends to confuse tourists. other than that it s a must visit if you like temples and scenery which you can view the city from above.

it s a must visit destination when you re in penang. this place plays an important role for the local chinese community and many locals come here to pray. you can walk up to the second level or pay rm3 for a ticket. the temple usually closes at 5 p.m. and stays open later only during lunar new year so make sure to check the time carefully.

me and my family went there on the first day of chinese new year holiday so it was very crowded. the temple itself is very beautiful we went there at night on purpose so we can see how the temple lights up at night. it was a little bit overwhelming with all the lights and people but nevertheless it is pretty impressive. i have to say though that i think the funicular management can be better it was a little bit confusing and chaotic. when i went there the lady told us that the queue time for the ride to the guanyin statue was 45 60 minutes. so you can imagine how long the queue was and we just decided to walk more like hike to the top which only take 10 15 minutes so yay. you also need to queue for the ride down as well my advice if you want to visit this temple is to not waste too much time at one place because it is a very big temple and there are a lot of section that you can explore

majestic temple perched on a hill with a shuttle system consisting of lifts and buggies that brings you to the peak at 16rm round trip hikers could also opt to take a walk up the hill or do a hybrid method and hop onto a lift or buggy along the way. the peak was definitely scenic with the bronze guanyin statue in full view. the joss sticks do costs 5rm for 3 with candles going for various prices if you would like to write your name for well wishes. definitely recommended to come by earlier in the morning to skip the crowd as we saw tour groups and huge crowds when we headed down around 11.

not a lot of people here today. it s a tuesday. we took the trolly roundtrip and got off at each level. great pace but a lot of walking so wear proper shoes. if this is the only temple you go to in penang it will be worth is. this temple is breath taking. pay attention to the signs as you walk in and you will see the lift stand to buy tickets. it s 32myr total for 2 roundtrip tickets for all 3 levels. also the temple is made so as you walk through the stairs in tje temple the higher you go or lower you go. some decided to walk on the side streets the trolly took and i am not sure why. dont be that person.

great place to visit. it should definitely be on your list if visiting penang. beautiful architecture and culture throughout the site. the detail and workmanship is truly impressive. it isn t as calm as other temples i ve visited but is unique in it s own way with shops and vendors dotted around. my only criticisms would be the lack of signage helping you to the top if on foot and the piles of rubbish lying around. despite that highly recommend this place.

highly recommended to visit. its biggest and excellent temple at penang.on the hill you can see view of penang can t describe its so beautiful area with excellent architecture. can learn some knowledge and add sour experience. for others religion they can pray inside. free and clean toilet and parking.you can drive direct on top the hill or can parking at ground level entrance and take paid train to the top can enjoy the scenery inside the train coz its clear glassess. buy 1 tickets trips up and down more cheaper than you buy 1 ticket up and buy ticket go down.its also free if you want take outside normal way but confirm you will sweating but along the way you can enjoy the garden full of the flowers its greeny its well maintenence.if chinese new years decorating so beautiful.souvenir shop inside. big budha s inside the tample very nice to see no any restricted staff very humble and friendly.

can t recommend this place enough. we paid 8myr each for one way funicular tickets to the top station and then walked our way back down. lots of amazing architecture to see and plenty of souvenir drinks shops which was useful as it was a very hot day.

a beautiful temple complex with much to see. great views of the city from the top too. you can i did while away many hours here. many souvenirs available. drinks and snacks too. no particular dress requirements but of course no shoes in the temples themselves. cash needed.
truly enjoy thousands of lights lit 1 month in conjunction with chinese new year 2026. it s more beautiful and cooler to stroll this temple complex. good workout 10000 steps lol.

you can go to the top either by the parallel train or by hiking up. we chose to go by the train and it costs 16rm. the train has three stops and you get to see some of the temples. there is however a portion where you need to pay 1 ringgit to enter. the temple also has wishing ribbons with different colors that you can hang and on the top you will be able to view a big statue of the goddess of mercy.

what an amazing place i must do for any tourist visiting penang. very peaceful and quiet and beautiful. must have a peak fitness to climb stairs not really a place to visit if you are disabled because at the moment the train up the hill is under maintenance at the moment. i highly recommend this place to families couples and individuals a like. even if you re not religious it has a very profound feeling of peace and harmony.

kek lok si temple in penang is one of the most breathtaking attractions in george town and a must visit for travelers seeking culture history and stunning views. built on a hillside this buddhist temple complex is famous for its colorful architecture intricate carvings and the impressive 30 meter tall goddess of mercy statue. walking through the temple grounds you ll discover beautiful pagodas prayer halls and thousands of buddha statues. during chinese new year the temple lights up with lanterns creating a magical atmosphere perfect for photos. accessible by car or grab it s a peaceful escape where you can enjoy panoramic views of penang island. whether you re here for spirituality photography or sightseeing kek lok si temple is truly one of the top things to do in penang and an unforgettable highlight of any trip.

the kek lok si temple is one of the largest and most beautiful buddhist temple complexes in southeast asia offering a breathtaking view over george town from the hillside. it is impressive during the day but in the evening it becomes truly magical as thousands of colorful lights illuminate the entire temple creating a stunning almost fairytale like atmosphere.

i visited kek lok si temple with the expectation that it would feel like a deeply historical and well preserved heritage site but unfortunately from the entrance to the end it felt very commercialised. at first i thought this was because of chinese new year but after speaking to locals i found out it s usually like this. there are many stalls inside the temple complex selling souvenirs which really takes away from the ancient and spiritual atmosphere of the place. on top of that the ongoing reconstruction has also reduced the overall attraction and experience of the site. hopefully in the future i can visit again once the whole place is properly completed and restored. tip please go early in the morning to avoid the extreme heat as it can be exhausting and you risk heat exhaustion. if you don t want to walk all the way you can hire a car to drive up to the top which costs around aud 20.

kek lok si feels much more like a large religious supermarket than a place of reflection. everywhere you turn there are buddhist statues trinkets incense and endless items for sale. to reach the upper levels you need to buy a fairly pricey shuttle ticket. on the first ride a buddhist monk actually drives which already sets a confusing tone. then to get to the inclined elevator you walk through a long corridor lined with shelves of buddha themed merchandise gadget after gadget. at the top there is yet another shop before you finally reach the terrace and the huge bronze guan yin statue. the base of the statue is not open to the public which adds to the feeling of anticlimax. the garden above is pleasant with benches a pond and a pavilion and clearly cared for. however the temple area itself feels strangely hollow. one entire side of the main hall is filled with porcelain vases for sale. inside only a few sticks of incense burn. there is no real sense of devotion or spiritual atmosphere. i understand that a complex of this size requires funding but the commercialisation here feels excessive and overwhelms any sense of sacredness. overall kek lok si is visually impressive from a distance but the experience on site is disappointing.

kek lok si temple is the largest buddhist temple in malaysia located in air itam penang. the temple was founded in the late 19th century and has become an important pilgrimage site for buddhists in southeast asia. the temple s architecture combines chinese thai and burmese styles with an iconic seven story pagoda and a 36 meter tall statue of the goddess kuan yin. the temple is open daily from 8 30 a.m. to 5 30 p.m. visitors can enjoy beautiful views of penang city from the top of the temple as well as explore the gardens turtle pond and beautiful buddha statues. tickets can also be purchased for the tram ride to the top round trip .

largest buddhist temple in malaysia this has 7 levels. the steps can be challenging to those not prepared. you can take your time to cover each level. some areas are small and narrow. others are more spacious. the architecture and some relief with intricate details devoted to the buddhist legends are marvelous. it is free to visit donations are encouraged but not mandatory. shoes are off inside the temple for praying. incense are burnt and placed large holders inside or outside each temple. the scenery is pacific peaceful and respectful. you can view the city below from some points higher up. there is a parking area on the ground level. i m not sure if it s free or paid parking. it is a worthwhile trip although can be tough for some on a hot day which is typical in this region. it was 31c and humid it felt like 38c on the day of my visit in mid november.

stunning temple with beautiful view of the city when you get up there. lady guan yin statue is very impressive. the cable cars are easy and efficient to use. highly recommended for visitors to penang. one area for the venue to improve is the cleanliness of their toilets at the entrance.

this is a large temple complex in penang. because it is built along a hillside visiting on a hot day can be quite physically demanding. the path leading up to the highest guanyin statue is especially complicated so using the paid lift can be a good option to save time and energy. it is well worth a visit when traveling in penang.

kek lok si temple is a beautiful and iconic place to visit in penang. the architecture statues and view from the top are absolutely worth the trip! if you re able to walk up the temple i d highly recommend doing so it s a nice experience and you ll get to explore the surroundings at your own pace. be aware there are quite a few drivers touting their service around rm33 to bring visitors up to the guan yin temple level. personally i find it not necessary unless you have mobility issues. the tram service stop also isn t really worth it. walking up is definitely the better way to enjoy the place!

walked up with my 80 yo parents. it is a short disrance only the elevation requires negotiation but nothing is really challenging. some parts are under renovation repair. 2rm entry ticket to explore the ten thousand buddhas pagoda area including climbing up 7 storeies. you may be encouraged to donate towards the building work. going down is nothing. but insisted on my parents to take 2 legs of cable car buggy and we walked the last part they only take cash and i had only 10rm left. people are all very friendly there. shops at each level and bathrooms are easy to find. must visit in penang

it was a very colorful and beautiful temple. the most important tip is don t spend your money on transportation at this temple. this is because it isn t difficult to walk to the top of this temple. at first we met a pond where turtles lived in we bought a strange reaf to feed them. second when i passed throught the circle door. there is a secret place. it has a nice view. we sat on a bench and just saw breathtaking view for a while. finally. at the top of this temple we bought several ribbons to pray for our health success and wealth. although i don t trust the buddha i think the huge buddha may bring me good luck this year.

i thought this place was amazing and would say it s a must stop if you re in the area. entry can be a bit confusing with options to walk or take a combination of van cart and funicular for a small fee. if not walking all the way up be sure to stop and stake a look at each stop on your way up. i think some are confused and just go straight to the top without seeing what s at each level. the temple has some really interesting shrines and is constantly being maintained. the main prayer building is pretty amazing as is the top with the huge statues. it also provides a nice view of the city below.

amazing beautiful temple so vibrant with all the different colour of led lights. good to walk around it is free but have to pay rm2 if you want to see the goddess of mercy. however everything there need to pay with cash cannot tng debit or qr.

one of the oldest large buddhist temples in penang. kek lok si has several buildings and each building has different statues and functions. there is a large hall that contains five buddha statues. it also has the iconic 36 meter avalokitesvara kuan yin statue. there is heavy traffic when going to and returning from kek lok si so it is better to go earlier. the cable car opens at 8 30 am.

a must recommend place to visit in penang. the temple is a beautiful architectural monastery. there are many stairs and not suitable for aged and kids. however a cable car is there one can purchase tickets after reaching the main gate of the temple. many food stalls are also situated near this temple.

beware the opening hours! hours posted online differ from public signs which differ from when the security kicks you out. lucy was randomly closed at 4 30 despite a sign at the ticket counter saying it was open until 5 30. do not arrive after 3p if you want to explore! temple itself is very impressive but much is under construction as of jan 2026.

majestic and peaceful temple complex kek lok si temple is absolutely breathtaking a true symbol of penang s spiritual and cultural beauty. the intricate architecture towering pagoda and giant goddess of mercy statue make this one of the most impressive buddhist temples in southeast asia. the atmosphere is calm and serene with stunning panoramic views of penang from the top. it s best to visit early morning or late afternoon to avoid the heat and enjoy the beautiful lighting for photos. don t miss the turtle pond and the peaceful prayer halls. a must visit landmark for anyone coming to penang!

amazing vast site on many levels sometimes a bit difficult to work out which way to go but there are always kind people there to ask and they are very willing to help. so much to see and take pictures of i d advise 3 4 hours if you take lots of beautiful pictures. 2 3 hours if you don t. i took a taxi there but found via google the local bus 204 took me to 200 yds of my hotel and cost just 2 r didn t take much longer than a taxi and was a c .love a bargain

over commercialised lovely complex beautiful temples but as people have mentioned it feels like walking through a supermarket shops everywhere selling everything from religious statues to spiderman masks. also rang a bell with no sign next to it and a lady ran after me saying that i needed to donate money now. donation needed for this ticket needed for that etc...

a complex temple with plenty of buddha statues. amazing architecture with beautiful fountains and gardens. views from the top are breathtaking. when you want to go to the top part you either need to take a lift or take a walk along the street

a beautiful and serene temple with stunning architecture and panoramic views. the pagoda and guan yin statue are especially impressive. a peaceful place for reflection and a must visit landmark in penang.

i went there after many years and it was an interesting experience. the stone sculptures on the walls were hand crafted and beautiful to behold. walking in the prayer halls was calming. overall a worthwhile trip.
an experience not to be missed amazing temples a truly artistic and beautiful place very commercialised which can distract but if you can see past that it is definitely worth a visit.

it is beautiful a lot to see sometimes it was a bit hard to get to the upper levels. and yes there are shops everywhere. unfortunately not enough western style toilets and definitely no toilet paper so be prepared

recommended but with some negatives. pros still plenty of beauty in the buildings and grounds. nice viewpoints. staff are friendly and helpful. cons it s chaotic. the lower tram isn t working so they put you in a mini van to get to the next level up then a golf cart to the upper tram. there s no map or guide to help you and signage is confusing. there s a walk up and through gardens that would have been better than the golf cart. there s no way for first time visitors to understand the layout. they want you to go through the stores and you ll miss the best parts on the middle levels if you aren t careful. take your time to explore all the directions. it s quite commercialized. selling stuff i the temples seems a little weird.

certainly worth visiting. i would give at least 1.5 hours to explore. what i would avoid is coming later than 15.30 16.00. we arrived at 16.30 and couldn t see much due to funicular and stores closing. the workers in general were rude and not helpful. if you re going to rush people who come 2 hours before closing and not guide them then don t accept visitors at that point. the funicular was already closed at 17.20. they give no map or no arrows to guide you where to talk so once you reach the top you have no idea where you re walking. the route we took was not a nice walk either full of construction and cars.

well... if you really have a time then visit it otherwise don t fomo. the architecture of buildings are pretty impressive big buddha statue is pretty big there is a nice city view but all the buildings are not sacred but... gift shops as for the cable car there is no priority lane for disabled or infants so you have to wait in the warm gift shop in a close proximity to other people. to be honest skip the cable car cab option and take a walk from the parking to the top. it s very easy and short. if you have a baby trolley like me you can go around the stairs.
i visited on monday and didn t take the cable car to go to the top. i walked on my own pace to the top. it is well maintained as you can see constant constructions. the temple is impressive.

the negative reviews here are really spot on. the temple itself could be a really nice place if not for all the tourist souvenirs shops set up even inside the same hall where the buddha sits. this lack of reverence was appalling! i don t mind the payment for lifts etc. donations in form of incense purchases are fine i don t mind the dismissive attitude of the staff as sometimes that could be a cultural norm but ultimately there is no serenity here .under construction under kept no clear direction of how to get around even the turtle pond doesn t appear to be a healthy environment for the turtles. such a disappointing visit we were very eager to leave.

we arrived to kek lok si temple and took our line for the lift. after waiting for about an hour they said they accept only local cash and there is no other option. that s weird!!! they have to write it everywhere as it is xxi century and people pay online with cards etc but this place gets cash and most probably does it to avoid taxes. i will never come back here again and will not see this temple so they have to do their best to keep people happy who come to this country as tourists and leave money here. yet we had a local travel agent it s my mistake as i chose the wrong guy he failed to inform us as all 3 sights we were supposed to visit today we missed due to long lines and tickets unavailability despite purchasing the tour package 2 months before. saying i am angry is nothing the day was ruined..

this is a massivetemple. unfortunately some of it was under construction i couldn t see the whole thing but very impressive architect all around. it needs about 2 hours to explore the whole thing. definitely worth the visit.
stunning chinese temple set in the moutains with cable car to top nice scenic pics with lots intricate colorful ornaments several shops selling tourist nicnacs for takehome memorabilila

kek lok si temple is not just a temple it s a temple with ambition. it s huge colorful and spread out like it decided to be a small city on its own. every corner has something to wow you pagodas halls statues and then just when you think you ve seen it all you look up the hill and surprise! there s the goddess of mercy guanyin standing tall watching over everything. getting to the top feels like a mini adventure but once you re there the view is incredible and the statue is breathtaking. honestly it feels like the temple said let s put the cherry on top of the cake and then built an entire goddess instead. if you re in penang you can t miss it. wear comfy shoes bring your camera and prepare to say wow at least 27 times.
the big issue is they accept cash only. we have to walk up the slope instead of taking tram because of no cash. still the temples and statues are magnificent. the staff is also nice.

i m not buddhist and neither is my wife but if you are in penang malaysia or anywhere near it don t miss the statues and architecture of kek lok si temple! the funicular is worth every penny even though it doesn t go far and sometimes you must wait in the queue to get on it. take your time and see everything! your welcome!

spectacular panoramic view from the top. amaxing craftsmanship of the main hall which is simply unforgettable. standing serenely at the heart of it all is the magnificent statue of guan yin a truly awe inspiring masterpiece

if you re planning a trip to penang the kek lok si temple isn t just a stop on the itinerary it s a genuine experience that stays with you long after you descend the hill. known as the temple of supreme bliss it truly lives up to its name from the moment you catch sight of it nestled against the lush greenery of air itam. a feast for the senses walking through the temple grounds feels like stepping into a vibrant living piece of history. the architecture is a fascinating blend of chinese thai and burmese styles which is perfectly showcased in the pagoda of ten thousand buddhas. climbing to the top might be a bit of a workout but the panoramic view of penang island is absolutely worth every step. highlights you can t miss the bronze kuan yin statue standing at over 30 meters tall under a majestic pavilion this statue is awe inspiring. taking the inclined lift up to the statue is a fun mini adventure in itself! the liberation pond it s a peaceful spot where you can watch the turtles which are symbols of longevity in chinese culture. the intricate artwork everywhere you look there are detailed carvings bright lanterns and vibrant murals that tell stories of devotion. the best time to visit while it s beautiful year round if you happen to visit during chinese new year you re in for a treat. the entire complex is draped in thousands of glowing lanterns turning the hillside into a sea of light that looks like something out of a fairytale. even if you aren t particularly religious there is a profound sense of peace and craftsmanship here that is hard to find elsewhere. it s a place that celebrates culture art and spirituality all at once. pro tip make sure to grab some famous air itam laksa at the foot of the hill after your visit to round out the perfect morning!
it s actually quite a nice place to walk. views is pretty good n tranquil. lots of steps tho if you climb the pagodas etc.
beautiful and affordable. the shops are a little much. they sell stuff in every other room. not just temple lore but flip flops and baby wipes
large buddhist temple on a hill. worth taking tram up to the top the walking back down stopping at various points of interest.
an amazing buddhist temple in penang. be aware that in october of 2025 they are doing some major reconstruction and thus some restrictions are in place. parking is very tight.

it s an absolutely magnificent structure and must see when in penang. as a penang malaysian heritage it s too bad it is not maintained well. i wish they would offer tours with educational explanations for tourist whom are not familiar with the buddhism. it s best to visit early morning for less heat and crowd. buy your tram ticket for all sections together mr. 8 if you want to pay less. it ll be mr. 11 if you pay each segment separately.

we visited the kek lok si temple on a monday afternoon. it is a breath taking place and we were lucky enough to see the monks pray. it took us around 1 hour to see the most important landmarks of the temple.

disney temple. they say its a working temple but its a money grab. its a big ol tourist temple. sure there are monks here but they too look mostly like cashiers. the pagoda entrance did not mention its under renovation when you pay the entrance fee. then the nun makes you pay a donation.she also told me a buddhist of lifetimes how to pray and insisted i do it her way. what kind of follower of buddha is that? nice to look at the garden and sculpture. all spirituality has been downgraded to a capitalist endeavor. gift shops every corner selling anything related to bhuddism. over run with tourist. teruwan saranai
a short car ride from the lower station of penang hill. the temple is peaceful with beautiful views of penang. you can either buy the cable car ticket or walk. there is also a turtle pond.

so take a grab and walk around to the top quite nice with amazing stops you will spot.. no taxi needed to the top sure lil bit hot but that s like everywhere when you want to explore more definitely thought about if it is worth concerning the construction and the references here but it was amazing and we are also a couple which travels for month around asia we were too later so we was at the statue under construction but don t know if you could even go higher

as there are not many places to visit in penang so this location seems to be a must go one. the place is old and not maintained well every the lower cable car is broken for months. everywhere selling merchandise i don t think it is not dignify enough as a temple. if you are run out of time will recommend you go penang hill instead of this.

wonderful buddhist temple situated on the penang hillside. there are 3 floors to it each offering a unique view of the city and a different temple plus architecture to see. there are small trains you can take to reach each level and also buggies all available for a cost.

not knowing where else to go we headed to the kek lok si temple. those who prefer walking are welcome here. we opted to use the vehicle and elevator provided for a fee. we were first taken by minibus then by electric golf cart and then by train. the view from the top of kek lok si hill is beautiful with various statues and prayer areas inside.
the view of penang city from the mountain was amazing. huge kwan im statue temple. etc. 2 ringgit for rides and entrance fee. certainly they need the money for maintenance.

trash all around the grounds. disorganized and everything in state of repair. take some time to clean up the property. apparently have to walk on the road to get from top level to middle level as we did. i m sure there must be a passage so one wouldn t have to walk all the way down to middle level parking area and then back up. open it!
joey tan was an enthusiatic tour guide throughout penang and was particularly well informed about ke lok si temple as it included a great temple of his guardian diety
free of charge. the bus fare from around the armenian street is 2 myr. nothing special though. can take some nice photos. not many eateries in the vicinity.
we visited kek lok si temple during late dec 2025. the place was under heavy renovation and maintenance. but it still gave my wife and i a great memory.

kek lok si temple is worth a visit for the sheer scale and artistry of its shrine rooms each of which is beautifully detailed and genuinely awe inspiring. the craftsmanship and atmosphere inside them are truly memorable. however beyond these highlights much of the temple grounds feel neglected and badly in need of renovation with crumbling paths and tired structures. what also lessened the experience for me was the overwhelming focus on selling souvenirs and trinkets which felt more commercial than spiritual and was surprising for a religious site. overall it s a mix of remarkable beauty and disappointing upkeep including piles of rubbish which makes it a 3 star experience rather than the world class site it could be.

an amazing temple overlooking penang! it is absolutely huge you could easily take a couple of hours wandering. there are a couple of funiculars to aid with the hill but one of these was out of order when we arrived which was a shame for accessibility. each trip is 3myrs. there are multiple rooms to visit and explore. there is a bronze statue at the top which is absolutely huge and magnificent to see!! there are shops within the temple that you can buy cold drinks from and the main toilets were very clean. we took a grab here and it was very easy to do. the view is amazing and we didn t feel the need to go up penang hill as a result
very beautiful we didn t take cable car we walked up around 4 30 5 and it was lovely. greeted by a dog on the way out too

we visited on 31 march 2025. a public holiday. very bad traffic jam towards the temple. we parked at the 1st car park at base level. we didn t know that we could drive up to the different levels and pay parking there too. at base level you can take the lift funicular at rm 3 per person to go up to mid level. rm 3 for the down ride. total rm 6 person. the 15 minute walk up to the mid level was not nice as there was no proper walking path or signage the hot weather made it worse. you can drive up and park at mid level. from mid level same thing applies. lift funicular rm 3 per person to go up top level to view the large goddess of mercy. rm 3 to come down. unfortunately the goddess of mercy area was closed for upgrading works. at the top level you get a nice view and there s a temple too. you can also drive up to top level and it s paid parking. the pagoda area is worth a visit too. rm 2 per person. it s at the mid level and a short walk only. it is a beautiful temple and a must visit place. we enjoyed ourselves and you can spend up to 2hrs there. wear slip on walking shoes is best. enjoy

it has been decades since i last visited kek lok si and was looking forward to visiting kek lok si. it is one of the largest buddhist temples in malaysia i have been to. there are some significant differences since my last visit such as having independent stallholders for the stairs leading to the top of the hill. highlights the interior of the buildings were well maintained and were beautiful. this included any statues carvings or paintings. i liked that at the top and last station there is the huge kuan yin statue and also the 12 zodiac animals in the garden nearby. the volunteers staff whom i interacted with were helpful and friendly. the temple has one of the best views of penang. there is no admission fee and donations are optional. though transport up the hill via each station has a small fee of rm8 one way. coming down it is recommended tourist take the cable car from station 4 to 3 as this requires quite a fair amount of walking. after that it is a fair condition to walk all the way down the foot of the temple. i also recommend coming to the temple when the temperature is cooler like the mornings as afternoons can be very hot. the drawbacks that i experienced or saw it was not very clear that the cable car was not working for most of the stations except for station 3 to 4. they do wait till there is sufficient number of people to fill up before allowing people to board. if there are a lot of people it can be quite cramped and quite stuffy. although alternate transport was provided e.g. a connecting buggy ride from 1 station to another it was very confusing as we were rushed through to go to the next mode of transport to get to the next station. we were not sure what we were meant to do or where were we meant to go. there were a few buildings or outdoor statues that needed a fresh lick of paint or replacement roof tiles. each station had many areas selling buddhist trinkets statues and other cultural items. all proceeds go to the temple however it felt very commercialised due to the size and number of locations for these areas. for tourist who are not familiar with mahayana buddhism and even taoism would be helpful to have a description to help provide a guide. all in all it is still worth a visit to this place that can be considered a heritage site. hopefully the temple can get the cable cars fixed for the whole journey next time.

kek lok si is a must visit landmark in penang and one of the most beautiful temples in malaysia. the complex is huge filled with stunning architecture colorful lanterns intricate statues and peaceful gardens. the highlight is the impressive goddess of mercy statue and the tower where you can enjoy panoramic views of the hills and city. it can get busy especially during weekends and festivals but the atmosphere is still serene and spiritual. there are also small shops and stalls along the way so be prepared for a mix of sightseeing and local market vibes. overall a place full of culture history and beauty definitely worth the trip.

a must in penang. this vast temple boasts stunning architecture. i think it s the most beautiful chinese temple in penang. don t miss the pagoda and the large guan yin statue. there are three levels. i recommend walking up level 1 and taking the elevator up levels 2 and 3. it s definitely worth a visit.
big temple monastery the view on top was very pretty. recommended to start from the top take the cable train to bronze guanyin statue and walk downhill.

it s a big temple complex built along a hill with a giant statue of the buddhist godess of mercy at the top. it s got great sites and it makes you work for them a little. it s a great place to stop by for an hour and conveniently close to penang hill

always a glorious experience visiting here. the calm the nature the serene environment the exquisite architecture the place is just dripping with culture. you can take the lifts all the way up to the top. best time is usually 8.30am 10.00am when it s quiet and not crowded. bring lots of change as well so you can try out the wishing ribbons and purchase lamps for each shrine.

whole place is very unorganised and dirty. no signs of which way to go very confusing. construction materials everywhere. wherever they can fit in a souvenir shop they have. the cable car seller tried to scam me by giving the wrong change. toilets are so bad and smelly. dangerous electrics all over the place.
you know what? we can enjoy viewing overall kls light from sunshine central multistorey carpark level 5. nice scenery totally avoid from traffic jam with rm1 parking fee.

so many layers to this temple. we spent 3 hours exploring. stunning architecture. we loved feeding the tortoises at the bottom of the temple. it was fun taking the cable car from midway to the top. the statue at the top was stunning we really couldn t estimate how big the statue was from down below. the shopkeepers down below were so kind and helpful. they loaned us an umbrella when there was sudden rain and took time to explain and translate for us when we were exploring the shop. even in the rain we had a fabulous time. mostly covered between the tiers of the temple. definitely bring your umbrella in case of sudden rainfall like we had.

scruffy old temple which clearly isn t cared for very much if i compare it with those i ve seen in thailand or vietnam for example. it really needs a lot of attention. poorly maintained crap everywhere. it s sad that a place of worship would be so grotty. it shows a lack of respect and care. i just don t understand it. the tram wasn t working no surprise looking at the state of it so i decided to refuse the offer to join a driving party to the top and decided to walk up instead. that was a mistake. i m still nursing basically broken feet 7 hours later
biggest temple in southern asia! very beautiful architecture! beautiful night view!
nice big tempel with many hidden small places you can spend there many hours. turtles and nice over georgetown
pros high view points less smoke not too crowded quiet. cons hot at noon many slopes many uncompleted constructions.

i don t know how people can be reviewing this place negatively. for this to a predominantly free place paying for the funicular and pagoda part is very reasonable it was a very cool place to see. i didn t feel for one minute that the staff were pushy ok some could have been friendlier at the last part of the funicular but it didn t impact our trip at all . we only got the funicular on the last part up and wandered up at a nice pace. we spent about 2.5 hours. we enjoyed our morning very much we arrived around 10.15am on a sunday and it was fairly quiet. it was 20 myr in a grab from georgetown. the temple is stunning very tranquil and amazing views. some construction going on but doesn t impact too much. only thing i would say some of the walk up there needs to be more attention with the trash. it can be very ugly and for such a beautiful temple i d expect better attention. the turtles were so cute!!! highly recommend!
cool temple with a very impressive turtle pond by far the most turtles i have seen at once

a must see monument in penang with a few caveats kek lok si temple is undeniably one of the top landmarks to visit in penang. its sheer size is awe inspiring the complex sprawls across a hillside offering panoramic views intricate architecture and a deep sense of cultural heritage. the full tour takes about one hour and it s absolutely worth it if you re exploring the city. that said the experience isn t flawless. some areas are quite dirty and in certain spots there s a strong unpleasant smell that makes it hard to enjoy the surroundings. there are far too many souvenir shops to the point where parts of the temple feel more like a street market than a sacred site. you ll even notice some temple elements dismantled and stacked aside almost as if they re being prepared for sale which really detracts from the spiritual atmosphere. there s only one paid entry point 2 rm per person which gives you access to the final section of the temple where you can ring the bell and take in the view from the top. it s a small fee for a memorable moment. in summary kek lok si is a monumental and iconic place that you must see if you re in penang but go in with realistic expectations and a bit of patience to fully appreciate it.
kek loksi temple is a must see when you visit penang. it does get busy during the day time and we had to wait for almost an hour to take the lift to the top of the temple but it was worth it.

a must visit in penang stunning temple complex with beautiful architecture peaceful gardens and panoramic views. don t miss the pagoda and the huge guanyin statue. you can walk or take the lift up rm6 return . it gets hot and busy so come early. some parts feel a bit commercial but overall it s an impressive and meaningful place. definitely worth the visit.

visiting kek lok si temple is like stepping into a living storybook of chinese buddhist culture with pagodas prayer halls and statues around every corner. the architecture is stunning weaving chinese thai and burmese styles into one serene hillside complex. highlights include the majestic 30 m tall pagoda with multi tiered styles the scenic views of penang from the upper terraces and the towering goddess of mercy guanyin statue that stands like a guardian over the temple. what i loved beautiful and intricate design perfect for photos peaceful spiritual atmosphere with lots of space to explore great viewpoints at sunrise or sunset lanterns and lights make the temple magical during chinese new year if you re there then . know before you go it s huge you ll need 2 3 hours to really explore. some areas have confusing signage so it s easy to miss parts of the site if you re not careful. a lot of stairs and uneven walking paths bring comfy shoes. shops and souvenir stalls make it feel a bit commercial in spots but they don t take away from the cultural experience. tips visit early in the morning to avoid the heat and crowd. bring water and cash for small fees like the pagoda entrance . a walk around the nearby air itam market afterwards is a tasty bonus . verdict a must visit if you re in penang it s iconic breathtaking and offers a rich cultural experience you won t forget.
i have been in so many temples around the world this one is in the top 5 for sure amazing experience!! i recommend you to visit it during lunar new year.

every time i come to penang i visit ken lok si temple. it is an architectural masterpiece and an absolutely beautiful place. it is quite well maintained it is free of charge. just need to have some cash for funicular and passing to the higher level of the temple structure. but it s nothing compare to the experience you ll get. don t treat it as a sightseeing try to feel the energy of the place and spend some time sitting in their amazing temples. praying or not try to just be present and feel the space

this temple is truly worth visiting! it s full of interesting spots and the walk around is very manageable. i recommend taking the elevator to reach the top part of the temple it s inexpensive and definitely worth it!

if you are going by walk try to take a tram for 4 stations costs 8rm for adult n foreigners. while coming you can climb down easily. try to go by evenings to avoid extreme heat. the temple has multiple layers first cover the big statues and temple on the top and lower one while coming you can cover the rest . old and beautiful temple
very beautiful temple! was really quiet even though it was midday.

amazing place to visit especially on a sunny day all the temples and buildings are nicely decorated and vibrant. unfortunately the pagoda was under renovation when we visited to it wasn t up to its full beauty but this place is definitely worth de detour

a wonderful temple to visit when it s not crowded. which means avoiding the weekdays and not during school holidays or religious days such as new year or wesak. i discovered there are multiple ways to go up to kuan yin statue instead of taking the fernicular train. there are stairs across the room with merchandise. the guides at the counter didn t offer the cheaper alternative even when asked. they lack integrity as one would expect. a third option which is free is to walk up by the road. otherwise you can visit the temple for free. the temple seems to be undergoing perpetual renovation this time is the pagoda. otherwise it would have been a picture perfect shot. the photos are taken around 5pm. the weather is not sweltering hot in the late afternoon. best to up to kuan yin earlier as they close at 5.30pm.
one of the largest cave temples i have visited. level 3 to level 4 requires a ticket 3rm per pax one way. 6rm for two way up and down . quite nice views
i think it s better to take a taxi and go straight up to the top rather than take 3 super short trips with different vehicles. first would be a van than a golf buggy than a lift not cable car .

visited at 4pm about an hour before closing. they provide transport to the top in 3 stages the tram from the lower level is not working. so they shuttle bus us up to the second stage and then golf carted us to the last tram. view from top is great. at top there are variety of smaller temple to see and some prayer candles and other items to purchase.

one of the coolest temples i have seen. very big and very beautiful. for those wondering it is free to go in and you don t have to pay for anything. you can take a trolley up to the top i guess which cost a few ringgit. pretty easy to find scooter parking near the bottom for free. by far most turtles actually seen in one place at once.

kek lok si temple is located on a hill but it s very easy to get to. once you arrive you can either walk up or use the shuttle services provided by the temple including vans golf carts and a cable car very convenient. at the top you ll find the impressive giant statue of the goddess of mercy kuan yin along with a place to pay respects. from this viewpoint you ll also get a breathtaking panoramic view of penang. if you re visiting penang i highly recommend making a trip to kek lok si temple.
pay for the tram funicular all the way to the top then walk down. its not that bad of a walk down.

this was a short ride from the penang hill entrance. it s free admission. after you park or arrive by grab you can ride the shuttle to the second level where there s a gift shop. after that you can ride the golf cart up or just walk up. it s a very short walk and that ll take you up to the cable car area. they have a lot of items to sell to tourists. it s rm 16 for an adult round trip for the furnicular ride. i believe it s cash only. there was some construction going on back in may. we were able to see the giant statue prayer area and other buddha statues. take a look at my pictures and make sure you see everything.

very impressive pagoda complex. this can be the largest religion complex i have ever been. despite that there are not that many places to explore kek lok si is big because because of many building are built together but the culture vibe you get here is very unique. and take the lift to see the guan ying statue is a must because it looks impressive and the walking road may take you 30minutes to get there

just got back from here and still amazed by its scale! kek lok si is not just a temple but an incredibly vast art complex. as the largest buddhist temple in malaysia the atmosphere is truly grand and calming. what i think is unique and not to be missed is the 7 story pagoda ban po thar . its design is truly unique because it combines three architectural styles chinese at the base thai in the middle and burmese at the top. you can climb to the top to see the view of penang from a different angle. then there is the giant kuan yin statue. to get here we need to take an inclined lift. the bronze statue is huge and protected by a beautiful high roofed pavilion. a tip for other tourists save your energy because the complex is very large and has many stairs. if you don t feel like walking far use the inclined lift it costs money but is very helpful . the perfect time to visit is just before chinese new year when the temple is decorated with thousands of lanterns that light up at night the view is magical! but even on regular days coming in the morning is the best option to avoid the heat. although not as strict as temples in thailand it is still recommended to dress modestly as a sign of respect.

we visited kek lok si temple once but to be honest we left feeling a little disappointed with the overall experience. the environment wasn t well maintained and the staff we encountered mostly elderly seemed rather unfriendly. when we arrived at the tram station we found out the tram service was unavailable. unfortunately there was no clear signage or announcements so we had to ask around. only after checking with an older staff member were we told the tram was down and advised to take a rm16 car ride up. we decided to walk instead but the walk up was quite unpleasant. the path was lined with rubbish and had a strong smell which was disheartening especially for such a renowned tourist attraction in penang. we sincerely hope the penang government can step in to offer more support. it seems the temple may be short on manpower and most of the people working there are elderly. when we finally reached the temple we noticed that most of the payment counters only accepted cash. no touch n go or other cashless options. for a tourist hotspot this could definitely be improved for better convenience. we spent about two hours there. the temple itself especially the large guan yin statue accessible by the mid way tram is truly a sight to behold. it s a meaningful place to visit but we feel the surrounding facilities and upkeep need significant attention to win back the hearts of visitors

if you are already in the town and most probably visiting the hills then this temple is not far away and worth to visit. the main prayer hall as well as the big bronze statue are nice to watch. you can should use the inside railway to reach the top. costs an adult 8 rm one way 16 both ways . why only 3 stars? because there were too many renovations when we were there and because you have to use the same road like the cars do if you want to walk the way down . i was hoping for a nice walk inside the temple compound.....

interesting temple with many beautiful corners and an impressive pagoda with several floors which are accessible from a narrow staircase entrance rm 2 . unfortunately the temple was not very clean there was garbage here and there. i hope it is not usually the case and it was due to the large numbers of people who were visiting those days for the celebrations of the new year.

temple entry is free. but if you wanted to visit the buddha statue from the temple you need to take an elevator for that you need to pay 6 rm. but if you have a vehicle you can go for free with your own vehicle without waiting. because in weekends you have wait for that elevator for about 10 minutes. i guess parking charges will be there better check.

the ticketing system is very confusing and inconvenient. even though they claim all profits proceed will be contributed to temple building fund. still don t feel comfortable with the experience plus i visited the temple on rainy day made my visit even not worth it. won t visit this place again!

best time to go is early morning or maybe in the evening or night time if its open. place was peaceful and clean for a temple coz they dont allow burning jostick. so even the floors are clean. the lift had some issues after we bought the tix but we got refunded. ended up needed to walk up from main temple to the guan yin shrine. wasnt hard but it was steep
massive temple with an impressive kuan yin statue at the top. beautiful birds flying around at the top as well.

i visited the kek lok si temple when i was in penang. the temple was very beautiful and peaceful with colorful buildings and amazing architecture. i also enjoyed seeing the large statue and the view of the surrounding area. it was a memorable experience and a great place to learn about culture and take photos.
beautiful place and free to bisit other than the pagoda with was 2 ringgit which i definitely think it was worth it!

one of the most visited places of interest in penang. this buddhist temple can be seen from a distance as you make your way to the town of ayer itam. located on a hill slope the temple is prominent with the gigantic roofed statue of the goddess of mercy kuan yin and the bright orange rooftop of the temple complex. it is best to skip the elevators provided as queues can get very long. however if you like to immerse yourself in the whole atmosphere of innumerable visitors souvenir shops and prayer items along the path to the top you may do so at your own pace. i drove my car to the second level and was charged rm3. reasonable. parking was a little chaotic. once on top the views towards the city can be breathtaking if the weather permits. if you are satisfied with the photos of the city from the hill you can focus on the beautiful temple itself. as mentioned i arrived at the second level. there are 2 main levels. you can walk up to the top level or take the elevator. the statue of kuan yin is located on the top level as is the main temple. the temple has several buildings that are accessible to the public. lots of small places for prayers to different deities. in summary kek lok si is definitely worth a visit. however it is best to visit during a weekday. avoid weekends and school holidays. it can get very crowded.
beautiful place worth a visit especially for chinese new year. plan petty cash for the carpark.
probably the most beautiful temple in malaysia. large temple allow at least two hours. now works on pagoda so partly not accessible.

kek lok si temple in penang is one of malaysia s most iconic and majestic buddhist landmarks offering a serene escape and rich cultural experience. established in 1891 it stands as the largest temple complex in penang and is home to what is believed to be one of southeast asia s tallest guan yin goddess of mercy statues an awe inspiring 36.5 meter bronze figure sheltered under an elaborate pavilion atop the hillside. the temple complex is a beautiful blend of chinese thai and burmese architectural styles featuring intricately decorated halls sacred altars and the stunning pagoda of ten thousand buddhas. the entire site is infused with a peaceful spiritual ambiance making it an ideal destination for quiet reflection or photography. a half day visit is sufficient to explore the main attractions including the turtle liberation pond the prayer halls and the impressive panoramic views of george town and the surrounding hills. you can take the inclined lift up to the guan yin statue and enjoy the tranquility of the upper temple grounds. don t miss the on site vegetarian restaurant which serves wholesome buddhist cuisine. kek lok si remains a timeless treasure and a must visit for anyone traveling to penang.
amazing chinese bouddhist temples. don t be surprised going up with the cable car this is a bit random and funny. i would recommend to pay to go to level 4 and walk down !

it s a nice temple with good views once you get to the top which is a bit of a faff as you have to up by stages on a tram lift. it s not far to walk and saves you a lot of time depending on your fitness levels. nice temple which is meant to be incredible during chinese new year. didn t see any monks. take lots of water though you can buy snacks and drinks at the various shops along the way.

a buddhist temple with stunning architecture well maintained premises and good views of the city there are three main levels of the temples. the topmost being a gigantic statue of kuan. unfortunately it was under renovation and not accessible the other temples within the premises have statues of different versions of the buddha and information about the same with both the original sanskrit names alongside chinese versions the place is calm and serene and well maintained too. however multiple access points are paid entries. there is no single ticket that covers all levels including transport. it gets confusing and expensive too even the directions to go between multiple levels are not clear and obvious

very beautiful staggering hundreds over years temple. really worth a visit when you re in air itam or penang island. you may either hike up in around 15to30mins or take the incline cable car up for rm3 adult kids 1.50 highly recommended place.

kek lok si temple in penang is absolutely breathtaking a must visit for anyone in malaysia! the blend of chinese thai and burmese architectural styles is stunning with intricate carvings and vibrant colors that catch the eye from every angle. the massive bronze kuan yin statue is awe inspiring and climbing up the tiers offers beautiful views of the surrounding area and the sea beyond. even during busy periods the peaceful atmosphere makes it easy to feel connected to the spiritual essence of the place. the grounds are well maintained and there are also lovely gardens to explore. definitely worth the trip one of malaysia s most magnificent cultural landmarks!
beautiful and spiritual experience from this temple visit. namo buddhaya

a beautiful temple complex on the outskirts of georgetown. try and go as early as possible as the site will involve some climbing and it can get very hot past 10am. we arrived just after opening at 8.30am on a tuesday and the area was fairly quiet. the first cable lift was not operating so we walked up to the middle level which took around 10 minutes. at the middle level the cable car to the top was working so we paid 6rm for a return ticket to the upper level. some people have complained that there are too many souvenir shops in the complex but remember entrance is free so they have to make money somehow.

kek lok si temple is one of the largest and most magnificent buddhist temples in southeast asia. the temple complex is beautifully designed with detailed carvings colourful prayer halls and a towering statue of the goddess of mercy. the atmosphere is calm and spiritual making it a meaningful place to visit. during festive seasons the temple becomes even more magical with thousands of lights decorating the area. it is not only a religious site but also an architectural masterpiece that reflects rich cultural heritage.
very interesting temple many deities and wonderful things to see

the temple is nice but the maintenance of the walkways up to the temple is sad. i expected much better. also the man at the lower entrance to the cable car was rude. told me to go back to the bus...but i didn t come by bus. i came by grab. he told me i can t ride the cable car in a rude manner. he shouldn t work in this job if he can t be nice to people.

visited during public holiday 7 7 25 and totally worth the hype good walk exercise paid parking for rm 3 for two hours need to walk and took stairs to go up got buggy ride and cable ride but need to pay fees a lot of merchandise inside nice ambiance and cool spot for tourists around the world to visit and learn would love to go again one day

when you go to this temple you need to buy the ticket. 2 way ticket for adult cost 16 rm children to 12 cost 8rm there will be some station perhaps because no cable car when i came. first they will take you using the van to the first stop then you will use buggy ride to the next station when we arrive you can walk around to the fewest temple and the highest one is kwan im temple you gonna use small lift like cable car to bring you to the top. it was a nice weather when we came there not too hot and it was nice. toilet was dirty.

a huge temple on the hill. it s very beautiful and takes about 2 hours to see everything. it has amazing views from the top of penang city. the entry is free but you have to pay for the lift ride up to the top. one if the biggest chinese temples in southeast asia.

the view from kek lok si temple is just breathtaking. from the top you can see the whole of penang spread out below you. between the pagoda the giant statue and the lantern filled pathways it s both a spiritual and scenic experience.

been here for many times. a great place to be away from the bustling city. feel the nature and smell the good incense everywhere. if you love to walk can hikw to the top tower. there is a giant statue of guan yin. the tram to the top tower need to pay some extra cash.

this stunning temple is an absolute must visit whilst in penang. it s incredibly ornate and very large with multiple parts spread. we spent a few hours wandering around during the week and it was pretty quiet. it does get very hot but there are multiple shops selling drinks on the various levels. you can walk up to the statue on the top although we took the lift up the last section due to the heat.

beautiful temple complex. what i find amazing is how this has been built by the community over decades. no ticket required but donations are appreciated so have cash to do that. lots of constant renovations taking place.any areas must remove shoes so wear ones that are easy to take off.

kek lok si temple in air itam penang is without a doubt one of the most breathtaking and culturally rich temples in malaysia. whether you re spiritually inclined or just a curious traveler this place is worth the visit.

the quest for spiritual calmness and enlightenment. focus on your holy objectives and ignore the rest. kuan yin the goddess of mercy or bodhisattva of compassion stands as tallest made by copper in the world.

visited kek lok si temple and it was truly an amazing experience. the temple complex is huge beautifully maintained and full of intricate architecture and statues that are very impressive. walking around you can feel the peaceful and spiritual atmosphere especially with the beautiful gardens pagodas and the giant statue that overlooks the area such a majestic sight. it can get a bit crowded during peak hours but it doesn t take away from the overall experience. the temple is also very photogenic with lots of spots to take memorable pictures. staff and volunteers around are friendly and helpful if you have questions. whether you re visiting for sightseeing photography or just to enjoy the calm environment kek lok si temple is definitely worth a visit. highly recommended for anyone traveling to penang

a stunning and expansive buddhist complex offering visitors a serene atmosphere with its beautifully ornate architecture and peaceful gardens. the temple s towering seven story pagoda and impressive statues including the massive statue of the goddess of mercy make it a must visit for those interested in cultural and spiritual landmarks. the panoramic views of the surrounding city and the intricate design of the temple s halls provide a memorable experience for anyone visiting this historic site.

large buddhist temple which spans over multiple sections. there s a tram that you can take to go up to reach the guan yin statue section. the toilets are badly maintained though and there are a lot of repairs scaffolding in many sections. nonetheless it is still a beautiful temple worth visiting.

good place to visit. nice views at the top. it s very hot and smokey so be prepared to change clothes after the visit. bring drinking water. staff might look serious but they are friendly. the queuing and transport system is abit confusing as there are not many signs so feel free to just ask around. best to buy their 16 ticket for transport between the 4 levels. level 1 2 van level 2 3 buggy level 3 4 lift

absolutely a must see while visiting penang georgetown. beautiful views everywhere and so many places to explore and wander around. go in the morning before it gets too hot and crowded. it was a little confusing to navigate and the inclined lift was broken but they provided shuttles. not the most friendly workers and several big souvenir shops there focused on tourists.
good place for pray and also see nice view from the hill. parking is 3rm at the temple. lift to statute is 6rm for round ticket.

guan yin temple. many of tourists come from many country. good scenery with the view of old city and penang hills. this temple is located on the penang hills. also need to take a car to the place. there are not allow for grab to parking and waiting to this area. this place suitable for guan yin prayer and the donation be accepted in the temple.
