kek lok si temple is a stunning place to visit rich in culture history and intricate architecture. the views from the top are incredible and the massive statue of the goddess of mercy is truly impressive. there s quite a bit of walking and there should be more directions going up and down but overall it s a peaceful and inspiring experience. a must see when in penang.

i got there right as it opened. i highly recommend getting there at 8 30. buy your tickets there at the counter explore for ten minutes that the bottom then go up. there will be hardly anyone at the time so it s quiet and peaceful. i explored every where and took so many photos. was able to relax as well. it took me till 12 to get done. so it s a bit of an half day. come in the early hours though. it s good light for photos as well.
was here for chinese new year! amazing place go visit and admire the arts!!!
beautiful temples showcasing various architectural styles. highly recommended. the funicular ride to the top felt a bit unnecessary it s only a short walk.

if i could turn back time i would skip this part of penang. free entry but pay for transport and incline lift . nothing special upstairs except for overpriced souvenirs. view is better from penang hill.
night view of temple kek lok si was amazing... the fireworks so colourful

today i had the incredible opportunity to visit kek lok si temple the largest buddhist temple in malaysia. located in air itam this majestic temple complex sits on a hill offering breathtaking views of penang. as i arrived i was immediately struck by the grandeur of the temple s architecture. the blend of chinese thai and burmese influences was evident in the intricate carvings colorful pagodas and beautifully decorated pavilions. the temple was bustling with visitors some offering prayers others admiring the serene atmosphere. i began my exploration at the pagoda of ten thousand buddhas an impressive seven tiered structure that combines different architectural styles. climbing up the pagoda s steps was a bit tiring but the panoramic view of the city from the top made it all worth it. next i made my way to the massive bronze statue of kuan yin the goddess of mercy. standing at 36.5 meters this towering figure was truly awe inspiring. the surrounding garden with its peaceful koi ponds and lush greenery made the area feel like a sanctuary. one of the highlights of my visit was lighting an incense stick and making a wish at the temple s prayer hall. the walls were adorned with intricate murals and golden buddha statues creating a tranquil space for reflection. before leaving i visited the souvenir shops where i found buddhist amulets lucky charms and beautiful chinese calligraphy scrolls. i also treated myself to a delicious bowl of penang laksa from a nearby stall which was the perfect way to end my visit. kek lok si temple was truly a remarkable experience both spiritually uplifting and visually stunning. the peaceful ambiance stunning architecture and rich cultural heritage made this visit one of the most memorable parts of my trip to penang.
the big temple is on a high mountain. you should pay attention to the opening hours to visit avoid waiting for the gate to open. it usually takes about one to two hours to visit this place.
the temple have magnificent scenery. great place to visit for photography

travel by taxi in penang is recommended for a one day trip around town. we have four days and we spent half a day seeing this history temple. but we can t see inside the main kwan yin buddha hall because they closed and did not post on the website. the temple is so pretty but keeps your energy and water because there is a lot of walking and a high hill. i make a wish and pray for all to come true

this is a stunning temple located near to penang hill well worth a visit. the massive statue of guanyin is a sight to behold. came across it by chance as i had a bike and was headed to penang hill. bought a tile there as well as a donation to help maintain the temple as it s free to visit.
must visit place in penang. temple is huge and to appreciate it properly one need hour. i visited in day and evening and each time discovered new beauty to appreciate.

very big temple with three levels indian thaise and chinese which all got there own beautifull parts. best to combine with a visit to penang hill because they re close together.

beautiful temple easy to get to via bus from penang center just 2ring. the temple is mostly free. the pagoda is 2 and for a ride up to the statue you pay 3 you can walk up for free using the main road on your right at the very beginning so it s walking up twice i think to see everything. the market before the temple 700m outside of it is also really nice!!
worth the visit nice temple but not alot we could see due to construction at the time we visited.
wonderful template right beside mainstream penang hill. saw monks in action as well as folks making a dance video a few temples away.

kek lok si temple is one of the largest and finest temple complexes in southeast asia with spectacular images of buddha and hundreds of beautiful carvings sculptures and murals both inside and outside of the temple halls pagodas and archways. the place is huge and amazing! could have more signs to help finding all the spots. you need to pay 2rm one way to see the main pagoda but it is worth it. outside next to the entrance there is a lake full of turtles . definitely a distinctive heritage treasure!

famous tourist spot in penang. should visit at least once. u take a series of small trams up to the peak where they are working on the new additions. place is ginormous and quite breathtaking the first time you visit

a must visit if you come to penang. the ticket for all rides included to the top most part of the temple costs rm16 for adult and rm8 for a child age 7 12 roundtrip. a really great view from the top!! recommended for every tourist! thumbs up.

the temple is huge and wherever you look is beautiful. definitely a must go in penang. the entrance is for free you just have to pay 2 myr to enter the pagoda sector which i totally recommend the garden is beautiful . we didn t use the elevator cause it was closed but you can walk to get to the giant statue in the highest point just about 15 minutes but a steep walk

kek lok si is the largest buddhist temple in malaysia featuring stunning architecture. highlights include its iconic pagoda giant goddess of mercy statue and beautiful gardens. peaceful ambiance perfect for prayer or enjoying penang s views from above.

if someone told you to board cable car dont!! the faculty is old and dangerous we encounter emergency break several times in a row in the end someone came rescue us and we had to walk stairs the staff is rude and wont speak to us. in my recommendation just straight ride car until top dont ever use cable car. the scenery up here is nice tho. edit another tourist just told us the cable car before us alrdy have issues but they still let us board without notice. if you know u know.

free entrance to the temple you can walk up and don t need to take a cable car for the first part. be mindful the temple closes very strictly at 5.30pm! the funicular going from the main prayer hall up to the statue of guanyin costs 3rm one way last ride at 5pm. to visit the pagoda you ll spend another 2rm. the views are really amazing and definitely worth it. i would actually suggest visiting the temple instead of penang hill!

kek lok si temple is truly one of the most beautiful and culturally rich landmarks in penang. spread across a vast hillside the temple complex is a stunning blend of intricate architecture vibrant colours and serene spiritual energy. the moment you enter you re surrounded by beautifully crafted idols detailed carvings and ornate shrines that showcase the depth of buddhist art and devotion. every hall has its own charm and the towering pagoda stands out as a masterpiece of design. one of the highlights is the tortoise pond a calm and unique spot where hundreads of tortoises swim and sunbathe. it adds a peaceful almost meditative atmosphere to the temple grounds and is especially enjoyable for families and kids. the sprawling layout of the temple means you ll get plenty of scenic viewpoints overlooking penang. however one thing to be prepared for is the hike to the top. during my visit the elevator that usually assists visitors was not working so climbing up the pathways and steps turned into a bit of a workout. it s manageable but good to know in advance. a small but useful tip if you re taking a grab make sure to set your drop off location to the temple level itself not the lower parking area. if you re dropped at the bottom you ll have an unnecessarily long uphill walk before you even reach the main entrance. overall kek lok si is absolutely worth the visit rich in culture visually stunning and spiritually uplifting. just plan your route and be ready for a bit of walking and you ll have a memorable experience.

frankly there is no buddhist temple in the world quite like it. it s worth the 25 minutes car ride to ayer itam just outside george town. it s massive and you need to be able to climb a bit to enjoy and admire the temple complex. don t miss the 6 ringgit elevator ride to see a spectacular guan yin statue!! there is no entrance fee and you can donate money if you wish. remember this is a place of worship. be respectful! an absolute must if you are in pulang pinang

an interesting and historical place to see buddhism. the landscape is terraced and at each level there is always something interesting to see. so many carvings on the walls are made in great detail. at the very top you can see the statue of kwan im and can pray and do charity.

kek lok si temple is without doubt one of the most beautiful temples i have had the chance to visit. the complex has three entrances each with its own parking. i recommend starting from the lower entrance so that you are gradually more and more impressed as you ascend. at the base you are welcomed by turtles in small ponds and you can either take the charming footpaths or the funicular to begin your climb. you will first reach the impressive pagoda rising about 30 metres high the pagoda of ten thousand buddhas. just to the right a path leads to a peaceful zen area perfect for a quiet moment. from there you can continue with the upper funicular to the very top where a breathtaking view awaits dominated by the statue of kuan yin the goddess of mercy which stands around 36.5 metres tall.
a lot of things were under maintenance or construction best to come here early. tickets to use the lift were 6rm per person.

this is a really cool set of temples. i believe it s two separate ones of the same name but are in extremely close proximity to each other. you can take a tram to the second one which has the giant buddha but when we went the line was really long so we just walked. it was very steep and hot but it saved us a lot of time and waiting. i d say it s a must visit.

so beatiful! i really enjoyed this place. this one was so pretty. many buildings gift shops amazing views. i don t really get the bad comments? yes they are renovating some buildings now but it didn t bother me. then some people are complaining about the dirtiness rustiness etc? okay well now they are cleaning and renovating and that s a bad thing too?also there are many gift shops and not that many telling about the history. but you don t have to go to the gift shops and you can read the history elsewhere or ask the staff. if you like visiting temples you should like this one too.

beautifully kept grounds and the temple was huge. glad an elevator was installed as an alternative to climbing up the many steps. update i came by again recently with my family and was happy to see the grounds and gardens are still as beautiful as i remembered. we arrived on a weekday hence it was a lot quieter than i expected. we took our time to explore the temple grounds and admire the intricate carving and the beautiful flowers. the temple s interior was cool even though it was warm and balmy outside. there are some construction going on at the grounds behind the statute of the goddess of mercy and it looks like they plan to beautify that spot. i am sure it will look amazing once it is done.

the structure and the scenery here is soo beautiful went here also to pray. sadly its kinda dirty there are trash scaterred around. dont really recommend going here when its sunny its so so hot i feel like im melting dont forget to use your sunscreen..
scenic temple. inclined lift was one of the main reasons to go there. unfortunately it was not available the time i visited roughly around after 3pm

beautiful architecture. weirdly peaceful despite the large number of tourists milling around. be prepared to exert yourself. take plenty of water to drink sunscreen and a hat or umbrella for shade. pace yourself. warning if you are not physically fit and strong the steep hills and hundreds of uneven steps without rails may be disheartening. the electric buggy is reserved for worshippers not curious tourists.
went during a normal weekday not many cars it was smooth entering and parking. must visit when you come to penang beautiful scenery surrounding the temple.

elderly friendly place as there is a paid elevator service to get to the top of the temple. not particularly crowded no need for prior ticket reservations. there are a few other ticketed spots within the huge temple itself that comes at a low cost fee to support the care needed for the temple. do not miss not the fantastic view from the multi storey pagoda atop. umbrella would be recommended as the sun is pretty intense when you are exploring around. there is no restaurant within the temple however there is an air conditioned cafe heh cafe situated at the start of the temple entrance. a little costly but both food and drinks are worth paying for.

few places were under construction so we couldn t see. a lot of walking make sure to bring some water. don t forget to see the turtles at the entrance and feed them greenery man selling it on the spot . wish there was more accessibility around the temple.

a very interesting place. i loved walking around. definitely recommend to visit while in penang. the entrance is mostly free there is a fee 2 3 rm though to take the lift up and down as well as to enter pagoda. overall a joyful experience
beautiful and well maintained temple. great city view from top of the temple.

one of the biggest buddhist temple especially in malaysia with so many beautiful viewspot. entrance fee is free except if you wanna see the big kwan im statue upstairs via mini cable car it costs 6 rm up and down stairs .
even in the scorching hot penang sun the kek lok si temple experience was fulfilling. i love how things are structured and clean. it is easy to navigate through the temple.

this location is specifically for this higher area of the kek lok si temple where the giant guan yin statue is. it s a very impressive and large modern statue with a nice temple compound nearby as well.

cable car broke down since 1 month ago need another 6 12 months for repair. they provide van yo go up to temple area climb a few steps and take 2nd cable car to the top. waiting time for cable car are at least 30 minutes. very hot no fan
the lift was down and the walk up is pretty brutal when hot but manageable. worth it for the views
big buddhist temple with a huge statue standing on top really impressive pagoda praying halls with beautifully crafted statues many souvenir shops turtle pond

kek lok si temple it is located in air itam penang is a breathtaking and culturally rich destination that stands as one of the largest buddhist temple complexes in southeast asia. the temple offers stunning views of the surrounding area and is a beacon of mahayana buddhism. the temple s architecture is a harmonious blend of chinese thai and burmese influences showcasing malaysia s multicultural heritage. highlights include the pagoda of ten thousand buddhas which features a unique combination of architectural styles across its levels and the impressive 36 meter bronze statue of bodhisattva kuan yin is another awe inspiring feature that draws visitors from around the world. visitors can also explore prayer halls bell towers and beautifully landscaped gardens. just be prepared for a bit of walking as the temple complex is vast and situated on a hilltop. comfortable shoes and a camera are highly recommended! kek lok si is particularly enchanting during chinese new year celebrations when the temple is illuminated with vibrant lights and lanterns creating a magical atmosphere.

very kitchy looks more like a bazar than temple. at very level selling hundreds of gaudy looking souvenirs. only gardens were beautiful. all pieces of modern temple were made in china and shipped to malaysia to assemble. 2 statues were nice in the whole place

very nice place to visit. buddhist temple with frescoes sculptures magnificent architecture monks coming and going. a beautiful example of multiculturalism in malaysia. plan to have cash access to the temple is paid. we start with a cable car then a golf cart takes you to the temple. with a little luck at the top of the temple with a sublime view you will have the chance to hear the athan of the mosques resounding in the city.

during the day a very nice temple complex full of scent of the sticks nice gardens and a beautiful views of the city. almost all buildings were passed around with a wires and it was clear what will happen in the evening. at night the temple turned into a circus with all the colors. little bit tacky.

kek lok si temple is definitely worth a visit but it has its downsides. the temple itself is impressive with beautiful architecture and stunning views over penang. however it feels quite neglected in some areas and very chaotic. there are souvenir stalls everywhere. maybe it s nicer at night.

a grand buddhist temple in southeast asia with lots of history. a must visit place in penang. if you plan to take a cable car from bottom to top it will cost you rm 6 1 way . great view from the top of the temple.

the place is located over a hilltop with a beautiful view. the statue of buddha is really huge a magnificent sight. the temple nearby also is peaceful. there are lifts that carry you to the top for rm16 both ways else walking can also be considered. the surroundings are all beautifully maintained and the fish and tortoise ponds fountain are really nice. better to come when the sun is low else the heat can be unbearable. the view of the penang city can be seen from above

the temple and the premises are good the location excellent but probably due to the construction activities happening or due to the way the lift trains are positioned the serene and peaceful nature one expects at a temple was missing. it was more like a tourist commercial place. request the authorities to address this.
beautiful buddhist temple atop a hill. grand lady buddha statue is mesmerizing. must visit when in penang. there are lifts elevators available with a fee to reach the top.
one of the biggest bouddhiste temple in malaysia. a must visit when in penang

we visited kek lok si temple in penang and were amazed by its beauty and cultural significance. visiting the pagoda required a small fee of 2 myr but the rest of the temple was free to explore. the temple is a vibrant and colorful place filled with intricate architecture and serene surroundings. it also features a turtle pond adding to its charm. kek lok si is one of the largest buddhist temples in southeast asia and serves as an important center for chinese buddhist culture. the temple symbolizes harmony prosperity and peace making it a meaningful destination for both worshippers and visitors. it s a must visit for those seeking to experience penang s cultural and spiritual heritage.
the place is nice overall. the big buddha statue is a must see if you are in penang. get the lift to go up there otherwise it s quite a walk in the sun. the architecture is unique.

a stunning buddhist temple located at hill top that gives a full panoramic view of penang city. very expansive complex with cable car service and connecting tuk tuk ride for connectivity to and from the temple top. as it goes by the buddhist temples are quiet and serene providing utmost peace of mind. visited in the month of may. its suggested to start early as afternoon sun can be harsh at that altitude. there are lot of souvenir shops in and around the temple complex with reasonable prices. must visit.

historical site with almost 36 meters high kwan yin goddess bronze statue. can see penang from the hill. we rode the inclined lift charged rm 16 per adult rm 8 per child max 12 years old. some waiting time but managed ok on the moving up and down. we spent about 3 hours including the queueing.

wonderful temple building with a lot of interesting statues and architectures and many gift shops for those interested . one can take the lift for 8 ringgit 3 ringgit stop or walk all the way up which takes 25 minutes of slow walk.

we were first not sure if the temple would be worth the car ride. but it is a whole complex awaits plus a giant buddha. we were very impressed. ca 30 min by grab from downtown. the street at the bottom has a great food court street food style with exceptional dishes including seafood and a famous chamomile tea booth which has been served here warm and cold for decades. very much worth a visit.
this place is huge so make sure to eat on your way and bring a bottle of water at least! it s also a fun experience having to see its landscape and penang from up here.

this place remains one of my most favourite place to visit in penang although i have visited this place numerous times and started to get bored of it but whenever i see the beautiful temples and buildings i fall in love with it. and i got to pray there as well. it is a must visit place for tourists especially the first timer. you will spend around 2 hours there. you will get to see the whole penang when you reached the top there.

one of the most stunning temples in all the world and certainly one of the largest in sea. the grandeur and scale is hard to describe. the complex is built on a hill with the mountains of the island in the backdrop. the temple faces the city skyline below and even has the andaman sea in view. there is a 7 story pagoda that is beautiful and worth making the climb and explore to each level displaying unique and ornate design.
peaceful and holly place. when we went there i thought to myself oh too many people it will not be pleasant but the temple is so big you still feel the zen in the entire place. really nice surprise.

the temple is rather steeped in history and the architecture is intriguing. i went to the temple without doing any prior research and was rather surprised to see that giant kuanyin statue at the top as well as the fact that they have a little funicular lift

this temple has stunning view esp for this cny at night. i visit this temple when the firework fest and i enjoyed it this temple has many statue and buildings as we should take the stairs to the top. i was dissapointed by rapid penang for not stick to schedule when we were going home. we got stuck by the rain and wait for 2 hours. desperately to find grab also.

majestic temple with many statues and deep history. bronze guanyin statue was spectacular too. spend almost 2 hours exploring the compund including climbing up the pagoda some levels are under renovation repairs . view at the top was magnificent. remember to prepare your sunblock and water it can be very hot during the day.

beautiful temple with it s own history and culture. really love the statue and the architecture. just a quick tips. instead paying rm16 to use the cable car round trip you can actually drive tell your driver to go straightly to the top of the temple. also it s hot on afternoon so bringing umbrella will be beneficial to you. all revenues will go to the temple funds
lovely temple on the hill overlooking georgetown. the views are wonderful. the architecture is amazing to look at. incredible detail in every building.

definitely the biggest temple i ve visited so far in malaysia! requires a car or tram ride to visit the other areas alternatively you may choose to walk too. entrance to the temple is free except for certain parts! quite a lot to see around here! makes for a good trip or thing to do in penang!

the temple design is beautiful worker all friendly and helpful. but is not friendly for wheelchair users. i experience before if you trying to bring wheelchair users take cable car need to transfer to barging vehicle . is no convenient for wheelchair users. better way is using car .

the temple complex included several temples on the hillside. you can reach the top temple using an elevator or by climbing the stairs. it may be a bit tiring but it s definitely worth it because the view is breathtaking. it was one of the most stunning places i visited in malaysia. the entrance to the temple complex is free but there is a small fee of 2 rm to enter the final temple which is a 7 floor building.

one of the most beautiful temple i ve ever seen! i visited this temple last may and it wasn t crowded so i enjoyed my time exploring this place. and i swear they have a beautiful garden with many colourful flower! and also you can buy some souvenirs such as keychain shirt chopstick etc. and if you wanna see the kuan yin big statue you can use a lift but it s not free. absolutely kek lok si must be in the itinerary if wanna visit penang.

12 yrs ago i made a promise to return to pray for all the blessings i have. happy to be here and did my prayers at goddess of mercy top of hill. spent all my cash at donation boxes. did not have any cash to pay for the buggy back. thought cashless was available. special shout out to the lady at buggy station at main prayer hall. she saw i was having trouble with my knee actually allowed me a free buggy ride and gave me rm3 to take the next ride back to parking lot. feeling blessed and grateful blessings comes in many forms.

beautiful design for this huge temple. i just past by because the road is the same if you want to go hiking at air hitam dam. don t know anything about the facilities or anything but will come again for more information.

kek lok si temple is a stunning expansive buddhist temple with beautiful architecture intricate details and breathtaking views of penang from the top. the climb can be tiring but an incline lift requires payment helps save time. the temple features an impressive multi floor pagoda decorative lanterns and scenic gardens though some areas feel slightly neglected. visiting during chinese new year is highly recommended for a more vibrant experience. while there are many souvenir stalls and shops the walking path down can be confusing exit via the parking lot staircase. the temple is free to enter. nearby the ayer itam market offers delicious street food including famous laksa and chamomile tea. best visited in the evening when the temple lights up beautifully. a must see in penang but can get crowded.

this place is a proud of malaysians especially penang lang. lights on about 1 month during chinese new year but it closes after 6pm after the 15th day of cny. suggest to visit during cny but be prepared for the long queue can avoid if you go before 4pm. every year the lights will change. i gave it 4 stars because this year i went the time they closed at 6pm and were not able to go to the top to see kuan im.

this is a historic temple with some beautiful architecture and paintings on the main tower with the statue of buddha. inside the temple is another beautiful shine and some amazing artefacts that are for sale but quite expensive.. the only downside is there s a lot of building work going on and in some places there were some beautiful artwork on the wooden doors but there were cement bags stacked up in front of it such a shame just a few moments to think about putting it somewhere else would ve helped us see and photograph those beautiful carvings. in the garden surrounding the temple were some beautiful garden furniture items tables and chairs that seem to have been created from pottery with chinese prints on them. i thought they were beautiful.

we really wanted to see the big statue of the goddess of mercy. however the area is turned into a parking lot and you can t visit the statue from up close yet . there was apparently a big fire a couple of years ago so they are trying to collect money for the repairs which i understand and we have bought many items for support but the place feels like a chinese gift shop rather than a temple at the moment. i would recommend a visit if you have time to spare but it is not one of the highlights of george town.
beautiful chinese temple with large idol of bodhisatwa avalokitrshwar

probably the largest temple in penang if not malaysia with giant structures and a massive bronze statue of the goddess of mercy. lots of renovations are taking place on this visit especially around the main statue but the results are spectacular. the street stalls that used to line the walk up are now gone which is a shame. instead these have been replaced by controlled shops. the final lift to the upper concourse needs renovating as does several smaller items but based on the level of site works i would guess that these are going to be refurbished in due course. be aware that there are lots of steps and it s quite tiring. the train up from the bottom significantly reduces the climb but you miss lots of detail. the old path to the top still exists but is not sign posted. definitely add this place to your penang bucket list. a great day out with terrific views of penang from the top concourse and not expensive.

1. overview kek lok si temple is one of southeast asia s largest buddhist temples located in air itam penang. known as the temple of supreme bliss it s a must visit for its stunning architecture serene environment and breathtaking views of penang. 2. how to get there by car taxi around 20 30 minutes from george town. by public transport take bus 203 or 204 from komtar which stops near the temple. 3. key highlights pagoda of ten thousand buddhas a blend of chinese thai and burmese architectural styles. climb to the top for panoramic views. bronze statue of kuan yin a towering 30.2 meter statue of the goddess of mercy surrounded by intricate pavilions. prayer halls peaceful areas filled with intricate carvings statues and buddhist scriptures. turtle pond known as the pond of longevity where visitors feed turtles for good luck. 4. best time to visit morning cooler weather and fewer crowds. during chinese new year the temple is illuminated with thousands of lights creating a magical atmosphere. 5. tips for visitors wear modest clothing as it s a place of worship. bring comfortable shoes for walking and climbing. keep some cash for donations souvenirs and snacks. don t miss the temple s vegetarian restaurant for a wholesome meal. 6. admission fee entry to the temple is free but there are small fees for the pagoda and the inclined lift to the kuan yin statue. 7. final thoughts kek lok si temple is not just a religious site but a cultural and architectural marvel. take your time to explore and enjoy the tranquility of this iconic penang landmark!

i didn t go to the main entrance as i was too late i took the road to the giant statue. and strolled around the river and the rocks at the entrance. there are many rubbishes along the road. volunteers could clean it up.

well worth a visit especially during chinese new year. however the infrastructure is heavily car centric leading to congestion both on the way to the temple and within the property. cars are allowed to drive all the way up forcing pedestrians to yield along much of the route.

one of the most impressive temple complexes i ve ever seen. this was one of our highlights of george town. plan on spending a few hours here at least. the complex seems to continue on without end from temple to temple.
huge temple complex with impressive statues and architecture. the peaceful vibe stands out even with many visitors. great place for photos and quiet reflection

beautiful temple worth visiting and looking around. there are multiple levels and you can hike up i think or your can pay at each stage for a round trip ticket. you pay at each station. you can t buy everything in advance. i suggest going to the top as fast as possible then working your way down. there s a lot to see and a lot of extra stuff to do for blessings and merit driven offerings. just go. it s beautiful.

one word beautiful. i thought i d spend no more than an hour here but there were just so many rooms and sections and i had to see every one! it was not crowded at all when i went early afternoon i forget if it was a weekday or weekend and the staff was incredibly helpful. however there seems to be a deliberate effort to funnel visitors through the numerous souvenir shops which all sell more or less the same thing.
the largest buddhist temple in malaysia featuring a magnificent giant statue of kuan yin
we came on and the vibe is so good! all the lightings are really pretty too! worth the visit at night although it was really crowded! would recommend buying the cable lift up and walk down by feets
the huge guan yin statue is very impressive. you can also go up the pagoda. many prayer halls to visit.

kek lok si temple is an absolute must see in penang malaysia. the most famous and iconic buddhist temple in malaysia it s also the largest. massive bronze statue of the goddess of mercy kuan yin. welcoming to tourists even if not religious very clean.
huge place of worship. pilgrimage site of buddist religeous.

it is a picturesque place with refreshing cool air that left a lasting impression on me. the best part was that there was no entrance fee making it a delightful surprise. the only cost was a parking fee of rm3 valid until your departure. despite the nominal fee the captivating scenery more than made up for it. this place houses an enormous temple that took my breath away with its grandeur. the temple exudes a sense of tranquility and solemnity providing a serene atmosphere for inner peace. spending time within the temple allowed me to experience a profound sense of tranquility and spiritual purification. in addition to the temple i took a cable car ride to a higher elevation. standing there i was rewarded with an awe inspiring panorama as if being on top of the world. the breathtaking views from above were unlike anything i had seen before leaving me enchanted and reluctant to leave. however there is one drawback the place is currently undergoing expansion which restricts access to certain areas. despite this minor inconvenience considering the natural beauty and majestic temple i believe that once the expansion is completed it will attract even more visitors and offer more captivating spots to explore. overall this place is an enchanting destination that will captivate your heart. the stunning scenery serene temple and breathtaking views from higher grounds make it a remarkable tourist attraction. although there are some areas off limits due to ongoing expansion it does not diminish the beauty and allure of this place. if you appreciate nature s wonders and cultural magnificence i highly recommend you to personally experience this mesmerizing place.
great view and the temple is very lovely. however it is very commercialised and there is lots of restoring happening. i ve spend over 2h just wondering around. in the garden and couple temples.

nice temple easy to access you have the choice to get their by taxi or bus. to get to the temple you may ask for the taxi driver to leave you on the top. to use the cable car you need to pay 16 rm one way. there is shops free toilet. recommended.

this temple is all about selling you whatever they can. there s so much stuff for sale inside that there s hardly room for buddha. you have to queue in three long lines for the three funicular just to get to the top which took us over an hour. once you re there the lady buddha is in accessible due to construction so there s just the little bullet temple with everything for sale. don t waste your time!
great place! best explored earlier in the morning with less people
very pretty and interesting too see. it s a very big temple so make sure you get there in time to see it all before it closes.

wonderfull place to visit. ticket price depend on which service you want to take. i took the train to send until top which cost rm8 pertrip. same price for all. eventually all the money will used for the temple good things. pros 1. worth the price you pay. the scenery. 2. so many things to see and they have hanging garden which next to pagoda. 3.the shuttle service is fast and oriented 4. calm and clean place cons 1. damn hot during sunny time. it would be great if they provide water dispenser to top up water bottle 2. main huge statue is closed. u cant to near it. overall must visit place if come to penang. prepare to walk alot as this place is huge. better come early morning for less crowd and less temp. this place have huge parking area.

no tickets are required. but maybe because it was a festival there was a lot of traffic. the cable car was gone around 10 30. there were many small vendors selling things and there were offerings on every floor from the first to the third floor. there were toilets on every floor which was very convenient.

the place is huge and amazing! could have more signs to help finding all the spots i went straight to the top by lift and almost lost the main temple! so take your time. my advice is to go up first taking the lifts and then go down walking to explore all the rest of the temple. you need to pay 2rm to see the main pagoda but it is worth it. outside next to the entrance there is a lake full of turtles .
surrounded by serenity and blossoms of faith. every statue every flower reminds me to slow down breathe and embrace the present moment. grateful for this peaceful escape at kek lok si temple.

kek lok si temple is a truly breathtaking place. the architecture is stunning with intricate details and vibrant colors reflect a rich cultural and spiritual heritage. the atmosphere is peaceful and calming making it perfect for reflection and sightseeing. the views from the upper levels are beautiful and the temple is well maintained. a must visit destination for anyone traveling to penang.
colourful beautiful relaxing place with many idols of buddha!

info if you want to reach the statue take the elevator up and down. otherwise you have to follow a steep road with no pedestrian path walking out and around the whole temple area. 6 rm 03 24 is a fair price for the short cable car ride. otherwise i recommend going to the pagoda with its 7 levels. beautiful view and nice garden around. admission is 2 rm. the whole temple is really worth seeing and was a big highlight of our 2 weeks in malaysia.

the temple keeps on expanding having a lot of buildings now. it s worthy to visit. however there are many steps to climb if you want to see the various buildings. alternatively you can take the tram up to the top. free admission. the laksa on the roadside at the foothill is the best. must eat.

beautiful place.. if you taking a grab. the pickup place is all the down . you will need to take a buggy cart rm2.00 to the second level and then cable cart rm3.00 to the bottom. there are various charges to get into different sections of the temple. overall it s great experience. highly recommended
the places are nice. temples are beautiful. even it is temple but be aware of the staff. maybe because a lot of people are tourists so you know it
i was fortunate to be here on lunar new year the temple is covered by thousands of colorful led lights which create a fantastic exprience.

beautiful place to visit especially during chinese new year and at night when the lights are all lit up. the lights will remain switched on until about 10.30pm. during non cny season it is only open during the day time but even then it is still as beautiful.

my first visit to this temple in day and night on the same day. it is so astonishing looking at the lights that lit up the whole temple. it is such a beauty in the night. i am a visitor and was told that the temple was lit up during this lunar new year 2024. i am so lucky to catch the view of this beauty. my holiday here is fruitful so far. would recommend people to pay a visit during the day though you may not have the opportunity to see the night lights.

incredible place. highly recommended to visit it during chinese new year when it gets decorated and fully covered with lights. there are several levels which can be acessed by a lift car or walking so it might be a bit confusing.
great lay out means you don t rush and truly observe the beautiful designs throughout. great view at the top and a very impressive statue.

whether you re a history enthusiast a culture seeker or just someone looking to enjoy the beauty of penang kek lok si temple offers something for everyone. it s a place where spirituality and nature beautifully come together creating an experience that s both enriching and memorable.
nice lighting when chinese new year and beautiful scenery! good place for pre wedding or couple photos as well. for reference flame restaurant has a great view of kek lok si.

the most beautiful and colorful buddhist temple i have ever seen in malaysia. it is located on a hill so the views from there are spectacular. it is huge inside with so many golden statues that will leave you breathless. there s a giant buddha on top of the hill that you need to take a funicular for it s not necessary in my own opinion since the temple has already much to offer. must visit if you are in penang you can combine it in one trip with the hill

kek lok si is a perfect place to visit during chinese new year. many families come to receive blessings for the new year. the lights and decorations are stunning especially at night. however the journey up can take some time. i recommend walking up from the bottom and taking the cable car if you re able. if you re traveling with elderly family members it s better to avoid the walk as there are many stairs.

you can reach there by bus from komtar 2rm inside if you dont want to hike you can take van buggy elevator for 8 rm can be bought individually also. to see one of the pagodas after van you need to pay 2 rm.
good hike and view in a serene setting above one of the highest peaks in the area. long wait for the cable car to ride from the foot to the top recommend walking if physically able.

large structure of temples on the mountains. great views from the top. beautiful pagodas buddha halls. the entrance to the tram service is not very clean and it is confusing if the service is available. they should have clear signs. one you go up to station 2 to go up further have to pay at each step super annoying.

hop on the cable car for a scenic ride up to the majestic guan yin statue it s an absolute must see! standing at an impressive 67 meters tall this serene goddess of mercy left me completely awestruck. there s just something about her peaceful presence and the incredible attention to detail that gave me chills! the journey itself is half the fun with panoramic views that will have you reaching for your camera. if you re in the area don t miss this experience!

1200 steps but at this time and age lift is privided. the lift is in the form ground cable car that fits up to 10 pax per trip. it s equipped with aircond inside. a mixture of taoism and buddhism temple.
worth going. must visit place at the island

beautiful buddhist temple. a must visit place in penang. the vibrant colours and beautiful buddha sculpture with serene environment. you can also buy souvenirs on shops at different levels. also you will get stunning views of the penang from different levels of the temple. we were lucky to vist the temple during kek lok si temple. you can either opt to trek the stairs to reach different stages of the temple or else choose the elevated train and buggy service for going up the temple.

not as bustling as 20 to 30 years ago ... the bustling steps we walk up with stalls on both sides of walkway now remains only before the tortoise pond walkway upward is closed now people drive up halfway and take the cable car ...

10 7 25 here again. very quiet thursday. walked up from kingston hotel. cloudy day but very hot still. 5 2 25 snake year cny day 8 come here by bus no.204 rm2 from komtar. back to komtar rm2 bus no.203 . walked all the way up to temple from ayer itam market. hot weather.

its a really amazing area the scale number of paintings and carvings are cool to see. 6rm to use the lift car to the main statue and 2rm to enter the pergoda. these are worth going in for the price. 2 stars as thre are too many rooms with trinkets for sale and poorly tacked on lighting cabling.

the temple quiet crowded so it s better to visit in the morning as it gets quite hot in the afternoon. there are many photo spots and the temple is so beautiful and clean. entrance is free but some areas may have an additional charge around 2 5rm

2 different tiers in malaysia s largest buddhist temple with impressive and intricate architecture including a large guan yin statue at the top tier. you can also get a superb view of georgetown as well

we went to a short trip to gua niah. so the entrance fees are rm10 for local while foreigners they charge rm20. they not allow to wear slippers due to the path is quite slippery especially after rain. so please do wear cover shoes with good grip. or else is easily felt down. if u not wear any cover shoes they do rent kampung adidas which cost rm5. unfortunately we only able to visit the traders cave as other caves are still under maintenance. we enter the national park by a short ride boat which cost rm1 per one way.

niah national park is one of sarawak s most fascinating natural attractions famous for its huge limestone caves and ancient history. the park is peaceful and surrounded by lush rainforest making it a great place for nature walks and adventure. the highlight is the niah cave where you can see 40 000 year old archaeological sites and stunning rock formations. the wooden walkways make the journey enjoyable and safe even for beginners. overall niah national park is a must visit for anyone who loves nature history and a bit of exploration.

amazing experience! the 10km journey through time walk was peaceful and full of beauty. leading a stunning caves rich in history and mystery. well maintained trails friendly staff and breathtaking views a true sarawak gem worth visiting! tips for visitors bring a torchlight good non slip shoes and plenty of water for the 10km journey hand cloves optional and a small tower also help. the trail can be wet during raining season but it s worth every step!

it was an unplanned trip to niah cave so we weren t fully prepared no shoes or torchlights but it s not really compulsory just good to have for precaution.. the walk was easy about 6km back and forth and even my 9 year old son managed it without trouble.. totally worth it to see the nature and explore the cave! it s amazing to imagine how ancient people once lived there.. the only downside was the boat ride across the river.. we were waiting on the other side before 5pm for the boat to pick us up but when we got across the boatman asked for extra payment saying the rate increases after 5pm.. honestly that felt like a rip off..

a must visit if you are natural lover. this cave is easy to access with all the platforms build for you. if you coming to miri or bintulu take the pan borneo highway to reach within 1 1 2 hour. a highly recommended place to go and spend half of the day there

the directions given by the google was misleading. we ended up going through gravel road palm oil area and reached a village. villagers said yes we can walked through the side which was the back side to reach niah caves. with no tickets counter and very scary environment. whatever happened after we entered will be totally out of record. so we turned back and drive back to the main road and lucky to meet a helpful young guy who helped us search and pinned the location on our map. yes we wasted couple of hours drove in and out from the area. the niah caves was amazing we enjoyed ourselves exploring the area. please amend the directions those people like us driving a non 4wd car was totally felt helpless entering an unfavourable path to this amazing place.

visiting niah national park was truly unforgettable! the cave is huge with such a unique shape something i never imagined to see. we were so lucky to witness a man climbing inside to collect the bird s nest an absolutely amazing experience!

entrance fee is rm10 for malaysian adult. interesting place. this is where you wonder how older native can explore this place. very smart and brave. not suitable for those have knee pain and unfit pensioners. u have to walk on wooden platform which is a bit tricky and slippery on rainy day.

we had a great time at the niah caves. the journey involves a 2 hour round trip from the park entrance to the caves and another 2 hours exploring the caves. there is no need for a ranger or guide as there are well maintained walkways throughout. some parts of the caves are dark and require stair climbing but bringing a torchlight and pacing yourself should make it manageable.

important bring torchlight! not recommended on rainy seasons as the trail gets slippery. def need some exercise before going if you re not active in keeping healthy lifestyle. legs got wobbly in the great caves. didnt even go to painting s cave and bukit kasut yet. need to go earlier if want to finish before dark. also they have stall near the cave if you want to buy drinks and souvenirs. boat is only rm2 for going and coming back.

if you are driving here please do not plot this as the location for navigation. search for niah national park and plot the one that appears on the left. if you plot here you will have to drive through a very muddy and unpaved path that might damaged you vehicle! i plotted this as the destination on google maps and drove through a few km of mud that took 30 mins to a village. but luckily we were able to walk from the village to the caves. the villagers were very kind to tell us the way via simple english the path to the trail is filled with algae which makes it slightly slippery if you came early as the ground is slightly wet as well. at the junction that connects to the main trail there is a shop that sells snacks and cold drinks there is also a toilet right beside the shop. there were a few caves along the way and some parts is pitch black. make sure your phone as charged to use it as flashlight or bring a headlight. in the caves there are old structures that are abandoned by people who worked here previously and also shacks for hikers to rest. the is almost no signal in the entire trail or the road to the village. however signal is available at the village as there is a signal tower just outside the village. overall it was a fun experience although when we reach the painted cave we did not find any paintings at the cave. however there are some signs of faded paint on the wall probably the paint faded due to time. visited on 18 may 2025

an amazing visit to walk along the wooden boardwalk and then the series of caves. the walkways are well built but basic with no lighting etc. and unguided. caves are beautiful and huges but not well cared for like the caves in mulu. it s definitely worth visiting if you re in miri.

awesome cave system. a bit of a hike though please bring plenty of water and be moderately fit the journey includes long walks on boardwalks and climbing steep stairs. also need torchlight headlamp for certain sections. best to be at the great cave middle chamber with the overhead openings i.e. holes in the cave ceiling by noon so you can see beautiful rays coming down from the openings. so start your journey from miri as early as possible. the cave paintings in the painted cave are fenced off. would be great if sarawak forestry corp and the sarawak museum department changed the fence to a more suitable one that maintains the security of the paintings as well as allowing visitors to see the paintings clearly. at the moment the fence looks quite terrible a visual eyesore. please do something about it. apart from the bad fence i enjoyed the niah caves and highly recommend it.

there is no proper public washroom for visitors upon arrival at carpark. there is no interpretation boards along to walk ways. no park rangers on duty at the caves. this was july 19 2025 the caves are silent. no sound if swiftlets unlike 15 years ago the great cave entrance was silent. obvious over harvesting of bird s nest.

crazy adventure. we had 16km walking distance. we start walking around 8am only both of us were there at that time. use a boat to cross small river cost you rm 2 return tickets. walking about 3.7km to cave intersection. there are small stall but when we were there the stall not even open yet. keep walking 2 km more to trader cave and great cave. somehow decided to return since walkaway to painted cave is so dark we are not bringing the torch light and phone flashlight is not bright enough. we walking back to jetty passing thru the stall which they just start put al their stuff to sale like canned drink drinking water handicrstt things. we just past by since we decide to go back to jetty not dare to go dark cave since only 2 of us. after walking more for about 1.5 km from the cave we met with singaporean tourist. father and son asked them if they ever going inside the cave before they said no. tell them our situation and asked if they are ok if we joined them together into the cave. lucky they said yes. they bring proper touchlight. we were walking back to the cave and pass through the stall again. one of the staff recognise us since he was the one inside the boat at the morning. he asked u walking back we told the reason he is laughing said there was rental in stall. so dumb of us not asking. we were rented the headlamp torchlight with aunty and continue the journey. the staff did tell us the trick and fast route. however we could not understand properly end up we make wrong direction. and has to make few return to intersection. at entrance of great cave there is archelogical site beside the pagar there is road but we did not notice since there is not pathway. we learnt the hardway. the path cost us to walk more than 2km to go back the right path to painted cave. so total 10.5km walking distance from jetty to painted cave. then the return about 6km. we had 16.5km walking distance with more than 18k steps. what a experience!
slow walk and able to explore 3 caves even for elderly people . must bring touch light when explore moon cave.
niah cave is a marvelous place to visit. must visit if you re in miri or bintulu. the trail from car park to the cave about 3 km 45 min walking in the jungle

we had a great experience here. quite tough if you re not fit enough because there s a lot of stairs! i dont mind the walk but the stairs really challenging for me. dont forget to bring cash because you can buy drinks from local stores in the main entrance of gua niah support local shops!
it s just serene... a bit disappointed with the painted caves as it s really faded and not much of it.. however everything else makes up for it though. nice workout to go for the trail.

whatever it is. driving here. just direct to the museum. dont use all the xhs or tt way of using the rocky and muddy road. its actually just straight from miri. and exit niah flyover going over the town and you will reach. i will echo others. the initial 2km walk should be done asap. and your energy spent on the great cave. which spans 1.2km in the dark. we forgot our torch but hp light works just fine. the next 500m to the painted cave is a short climb. overall its 3hr exclude rest break. and to and fro of 10km . wouldn t say its tough but we did a moderate pace with average fitness. great experience
worth a trip to go a unesco world heritage site. the caves are truly beautiful and magnificent especially the great cave. just a tiring 4.2km walk from entrance to the caves.
visited on 7th march 2026. only trader s cave was open everything else is still closed. the hike to trader s cave took about an hour one way it rained and the path was quite slippery.
a destination to explore wonderful experience and not too bad walking trail! 3 caves to explore once you arrive.

couldn t say you ve been to miri without going niah. truly a unesco site. come early with good grip shoes torch and gloves. also bring extra cash for the boat ride back and forth. great for beginner hike. takes me 4 hours. might not be suitable for those with weak stamina and those with sensitive nose to oozy smells. the cave is truly amazing and breathtaking the gallery offers enough information and the personnel provides good service. all in all it s a once in a lifetime event that is highly recommended.

my trip to niah cave was an amazing experience! the place is full of history and beautiful nature. the walk to the cave was a bit challenging but it was really worth it. inside the cave the view was so unique and stunning like entering another world. it s a great place for nature and history lovers. i truly recommend visiting at least once in your life!
noah national park was amazing roughly a 7 8 km walk to painting cave. drinks pit stop prior to the start. torch is definitely required if exploring the cave further.

if you re in miri don t miss this experience! the cave adventure is unforgettable. we didn t have a torchlight but our phone lights worked just fine. that s how my husband and i went in. there are basic amenities available before the cave journey begins like water some snacks and toilets. totally worth it!

amazing place. visitors need to take their own torch lights or headlamps with them. might be not suitable for those who are sensitive to smell and weak on stamina. the whole to and fro trip from the jetty till the end of the painted cave could take up to 4 hours so start your trip early in the morning. trails could be slippery after the rain. recommended to travel with friends and bring with you plenty of water.

walaurecommwnded while in miri. fyi the boat ride is just opposite side. 1min only .. even can glide by rope if have good outdoor activity and must see place while here .. if have better maintaince will be perfect .. like more toilets facility better pit stops and maintainence of the park .... it will be big tourist puller .
niah is truly a natural wonder! the caves are incredible and the greenery along the trail is breathtaking. everyone should experience niah at least once in their lifetime.

the must visit place if you are at bintulu or miri 1.5hr from bintulu . entrance is rm10 for malaysian. parking available at entrance after register take a boat ride rm1 to cross the river will arrive at musium first free then start walking along the pathway to the caves. it take 40 min to reach the first cave trader cave another 20 min to reach the second cave great cave from great cave another 1 hour to reach the painted cave. trader cave is the easiest doesn t involved a lot of staircase straight path suitable for children and elderly great cave require hike up staircase and the most difficult and need light support if going up to painted cave. recommend to bring enough of water change of clothes after exploration of the cave good shoe as you will be walking around 10km more than 10000 steps toilet are available at the entrance and also inside. it takes about 4 5 hour to explore all the caves. return boat to entrance is rm1 and the last boat leave at 4pm.

be prepared to walk a good 10 km return journey if you want to get from the jetty to the painted cave but the experience is woww. the cave paintings are 2000 years old which shows sarawak wasn t exactly a advanced community for that time considering how the rest of the world already progressed. enjoyed learning about the 40000 year old deep skull. one of the reasons niah is a world heritage site is because there are 7 types of burials at the hell trench where the deep skull was found. need to bring a torchlight and good walking shoes. can also bring gloves to hold on to the railings as there is a lot of bat poop. there are a few vendors selling snacks and drinks inside the park and one the second or third stall from the left tried to short change my purchase so please be alert
this time we going to explored a bit in details which took about 3 hours to view verify photo taking reading every signage s the history the painting and story of the cave bird nest and guono.

this malaysia day 16 sept 2024 was especially unique. i m glad to travel to miri visited niah cave. total time spent was about 4.30 hours. starting with a 30sec boat ride to cross the river. then took abt 40min walk on wooden plank through the jungle to reach the entrance of the cave. not a flora person so it s a jungle to me. exhausted but the real adventure starts! at the vast entrance you ll notice many poles. questions fr e one how they build the poles attached them on the ceiling abt 60m high ? ascending descending flight of stairs to explore various areas within the cave. most of the area are pitch black. luckily i brought my powerful led torch. the strenuous walk with a little fear excitement curiosity made the walk interesting. the highlight of the trip was to be able to catch the sun rays at diff locations. so angelic majestic. ah the malaysia flag. sponsored by koji. glad that i brought it along. most ppl would say wah nice happy malaysia day! including the kids! needless to say pinjam boleh? met many ppl few locals from miri bintulu and some from semenanjung. a european and a taiwanese couple. drenched tired but elated completed one of my bucket list!

this is the number 5 in maylasia world culture heritage and not many people knows yet. there are three cave to visit. one is traders cave the second is the great cave and third is painted cave. pls remember to bring fresh light this is so important because so dark inside the cave. visiting time around 3 6 hours depends on how far away you want to explore.

must visit when you re in miri. highlight of the whole trip! came with my family husband me our 7yo boy. it was tiring for me with all the steps especially on the way out. super impressed that my son still had enough stamina. there are some pitch black areas about 1km long in the great cave. painted cave was unfortunately locked so we didn t get to see the drawings. bat poo slippery walkway definitely need good shoes with traction torchlight plenty of water. lots of rest areas good signage. 1.5hrs drive from city centre 4 hrs in the caves 1.5hrs back. it s a good almost full day excursion. need to leave the caves by 4pm so start early plan for snacks in lieu of lunch. mostly enjoy the untouched nature with no mobile reception.

great for beginner hike. about 5 to 6 hours in total. hike untill painter s cave tips each person must register first rm10 ticket for local malaysian. if you are planning to hike untill painter s cave you might need to start your hike at 08 00 a.m. wear gloves bring a torch light if you are not from around miri or bintulu you can pre book hotel accommodation here. they also have canteen. niah town is also located nearby. prepare rm2 for the boat ride before the hike. as of september 2024

very good place cause not so many people to come. in my whole trip 4 5 hrs not so crowded in the walking path. round trip to painting cave should be around 7 km. it is not very hard journey for most of people. although the monument is not in the cave should be in museum i still find the really cool ancient paintings thousands years ago inside the cave. i also meet people who are collecting the bird nest. some trails are really dark. i use cell phone light is just enough for walking. if you want to see more in the dark cave need bring powerful flashlight. base on the introduction the ancient people may from taiwan so cool that they can travel so far.

why do we need pay for the boat ride rm1 person ride as we have paid rm10 entrance fee to sarawak forest department? if they can build a hanging bridge to cross the river will be a better option instead of relying on the boat that has no license and guarantee. the park facility is under maintenance such as smelly toilet and broken pathway..... the cave itself is really magnificent and stunning the gallery provide sufficient info staff service is good as well just unsatisfied on the boat.

amazing!!!! first time coming to this national park and it was brilliant. a historic cave with early evidence of human activity and burial of human remains. with the perfect environment and green nature before entering the cave i enjoy the surrounding nature since i haven t get along with nature for years. i ve been amazed when i first entered the cave since it s been years i ve been looking at pictures of niah cave before i get the opportunity to make my first trip to niah cave. unfortunately i wasn t able to explore painted cave due to slippery ground. but i still enjoy the experience around the niah cave. will come back again in the future.

a distanced walk calculated from phone is about 14km walk spent approximately 5 hours here with slow paced walk. explore until the deepest with proper trail known as painted cave. towards the end part of the trail is a bit dangerous as it is obvious that less people walk until here lack of maintenance as well fresh air surrounded by flora. cave is reeked of bat s and bird s poop. suggest anyone that are coming here to prepare lots of water mask some oat bars and chocolates for light munching a spare shirt torchlight can be rent at the counter for rm5.00 ea a pair of good shoes mosquito repellent to be secure and lastly a hat. mid way through near to the only toilet you ll see rm0.50 per use there s one lady selling drinks and souvenirs feel free to help the local economy though it s pricey that s what we ve to pay for something middle of nowhere. there s online guide to master this niah caves be sure to pay it a visit. anyway carpark is free and rm1.00 for the 1 minute boat ride. stay safe and remember there s a very dark place inside phone s flash light will help a lot but it ll draw the insects attention just ignore it and walk cautiously. good exploration and experience. look out for bats and birds bombarding poop. peace out. stay hydrated.
easy 4 hours walk. flat wooden bridge all the way to the cave. plenty of stairs in the cave to facilitate your climbing . must bring torch light along if you intend to go all the way in the cave.

advisable to arrive before 9 am as the humidity can be really high in the late morning. this is my 6th walk into niah cave. suggest that you wear a hat a face mask due to the acrid smell in the cave a hand glove is handy if you don t want to accidently touch the handrail which might be wet from the swallows droppings a pair of shoes which is good on wet wooden planks a headlight for me a handheld torch and water or snacks. always go with a buddy. floor can be wet but it s really nice inside the cave. it s about 3 4 hrs depending on your speed. bring along small change of rm 1 or 5 for boatride. happy trails and enjoy ur walk.

time needed 1 2 hour to buy a ticket and visit the museum. 1 hour on the rainforest boardwalk to reach the 1st cave. 1 1 2 to 2 hours to explore the 3 caves on the guided inner boardwalks without stopping. 1 1 2 hour to go back out on the rainforest boardwalk slower because you ll be tired by then . total 5 hours minimum. add 1 hr drive to and from your hotel at miri town. so 7 hrs total. if you keep pace and don t rest. 9 hours is you b take pics and rest. wear good shoes get ready to climb lots of stairs bring a strong torch each it s all dark in the main caves wear a hat and yes gloves or bring a cloth to hold the railings in the caves where bats and swiflets poo freely wear a hat!! do bring at least 1.5l of water per person. you need to be mentally prepared and have the time to be at niah caves. i did it in the morning of the day i had to catch an evening flight and regret cos i was stressed about not making it back to the admission counter you need to report in and when you re out and its a good hour each way to and from miri town where i was staying by car. also i got on the flight sweaty and stinky. sorry fellow passengers! i heard good things about the canteen at the admissions counter but didn t get to eat there because of the rush. highly recommend visiting niah caves. it s fascinating from geological and paleoanthropological points. but give yourself a whole day and try to start by 9am.

good hike here to bukit kasut about 4 5 hours required for return hike 14km total distance starting from the park entrance. not recommended in rainy seasons as the path may be flooded muddy. very easy path so you can venture on your own as long as you follow the trail. the hike might take longer if you re in a big group and take lots of photos along the way. brilliant views from the top as you can see around niah area. be careful of the black ants at the stairs they re especially painful when they sting!

the view is splendid definitely a must visit destination in sarawak. the only downside is the souvenir shop and rest stop were not opened that day not sure if it s closed long term or not. the track and route are well maintained rubbish bins are prepared sufficiently. overall it s a very memorable experience.

visiting niah cave is like stepping into a living museum of southeast asian history and natural wonder. located in niah national park about 90 minutes from miri this cave complex is one of malaysia s most significant archaeological sites and a must see for nature lovers and history buffs alike.

came here on a relatively quiet day weekday non ph and had a great time! walk 3km to reach trader s cave great cave painted cave. at the soveiner shops junction theres a left turn to go to a nearby rumah panjang homestay available there too . advisable to bring insect repellents some water to hydrate and for sure a good torch light esp if you want to go through the whole course. admission fee is rm10 for malaysian adult and rm1 for boat ride per trip. well worth the fee the view is absolutely breathtaking.

another great unesco site to visit really enjoy the trip and the place is amazing. highly recommend to visit here when come to sarawak. the place is well maintained and clean thanks to the staff to work hard and done their job. attached are the photos to review and contribute here at my best. note remember to bring a torch light when visit and keep the place clean for everyone

a great place to bring your family and friends for short vacation. you need to bring your own head lamp and wear sport shoe s because the cave surface is slippery. head to nearby local restaurants to enjoy the delicious prawn noodle.

niah national park located within miri division sarawak malaysia is the site of the niah caves limestone cave and archeological site. known for the 40 000 year old human remains discovered niah national park offers a 2 hour walk of jungle walk and spelunking experiences. a two hour drive from miri niah national park can be explored through a plank walk within the lowland forest with a fantastic flora and fauna. tiring trek but all super worth it! more about my niah cave adventure at enjayneer.com.

very nice park with a good accommodation. the hostel rooms are clean and equipped with a fan for a good price 50 rm per night may 2023 . check website for booking. canteen is available for limited time there. very nice staff speaking english well entrance fee to the park is 20 rm pax. there is paved trail till great cave and inside also slippery inside better to have proper shoes need a headlamp there. a lot of bats inside and a long way throught the hill in the dark paintings in painted cave are nice. very silent place only sounds of the forrest around the cottage. you can get there from miri bus terminal to batu niah by bus 15 20 rm pax . then ask people around a lot of buffets there someone will drop you there by car for 30 40 rm. the same for returning take the number of the taxi guy or ask in the office. someone takes you back to the bus people are very nice and kind.

an hour s drive away from central miri on slightly windy roads takes you to niah national park. you can explore nature in a one hour 4km paved trail to see the beautiful caves in this barely touched landscape. the scenery is just stunning. facilities are pretty well maintained and considerably clean. do come with good shoes though pathway can get slippery and bring along a flashlight as the caves get pitched black along the trail. definitely a must go it s worth the trip.

my family 2 adults 2 11 yr old kids went to niah soon after the restricted movement order was eased in late july. not all the caves were open but the traders cave and main cave were open. about a 3.9km trek each way but a wonderous sight awaits those who make the journey. tips bring plenty of water there is a clean public toilet along the way and do wear some insect repellent. best get an early start as the last boat to return to base is at 3.30pm. the accommodation at base camp was under renovation at this time a story for the next trip perhaps.

easy wooden bridge trail but quite slippery since the brigde are always wet and covered with bats stool. you will need a good grip shoe. wear glove so you will not have to touch the dirty handrails directly. and wear headlamp or torchlight because there will be dark area at certain inside the cave. highly recomended to come here.

great place to visit! i have some recommendations take torchlight with you better to fix it on your head don t forget repellents don t forget cap to protect yourself from birds surprises take gloves with your to hold hand rails properly wear relevant clothes boots long sleeve trousers better to have water tea snacks for breaks entry fee is 10 rm for malaysian visitors 20 rm for non malaysian visitors fee to cross the river is 2 rm per person 1 rm on a way to the cave and 1 rm on a way back have some cash to get refreshing drinks on a way to caves

enjoyed to explore this historic place. amaze by the beauty of nature and of the historical left. walkway to the cave is slippery and inside the cave also plus super dark. u need to bring torchlight and gloves.
if u depart from miri town must leave 6am can have breakfast near by niah town it take about 2 to 3 hour to explore the whole place include the rumah patrick libau can get some water from the village

niah caves may be the best place to visit if you want a unique experience in an area with few tourists and that is not difficult to get to. i cannot recommend visiting the cave enough and the cave system is amazing to explore. you may want to bring a powerful flashlight if you want to see all the cave has to offer. i did the cave tour by myself with just my phone s flashlight and i was fine. the park offers basic facilities for visitors with a bathroom close to the entrance of traders cave one of the entrances of the cave system it left much to be desired in cleanliness. the walk to the entrance of the cave system is easy as it is on a raised platform but then turns into a climb up stairs and once in the cave it is a combination of stairs walking on uneven ground or some slippery ramps. people with a physical disability or people with a health condition may struggle or be unable to complete the full hike.

a great day out at the fascinating caves. we stayed in the hostel dorm room on the night previous night to enable us access to the national park as early as possible. due to the boat staff being late we were granted access to the np at 08 30am. we were the first people to enter the national parks. we completed the hikes to all three caves whilst stopping to observe nature with time spent in the museum by 13 30 pm. the dorm room was hot and stuffy which we expected were happy to put up with it for a night. no toiletries or towels are supplied so bring your own. no cooking facilities either. the park canteen is open from 07 00am 18 00. it serves a variety of food at fair prices. didn t try the food as we came supplied. can purchase a good meal from the bus terminal and bring with you. bottled water can be purchased for 2.5 ringet. bring your own tea coffee hot water is provided for 0.50 per cup. there is no transport available from batu niah bus station which is frustrating. no grab or maxim drivers in the area meant we had to hitchhike to and from. if the national park wants to attract more western tourists backpackers it should provide a shuttle mini bus service at least twice a day. not all of us can afford to hire a car or be ripped off by locals at the bus station. staff at the national park were friendly and accommodating. check in for accommodation opens at 08 00 and closes at 17 00. if travelling from sibu bus terminal leave as early as possible to get to the area well in time.

it quite a journey. as i remember it took 5 hours journey from entering the woods to the niah caves at exit from it. it was such a memorable and quality time spent with my college friends as this is our first trip together on the mid sem break! eventhough we faced such a frightening moment as we saw a snake during our trip inside the cave. it even worst at the moment because it was totally darkk!! at first we thought that it was a stick until one of my friends realized that it wasn t what she thought at the first moment. even it was like gonna attack us we took a few steps down from the wood track but alhamdulillah the ground is quite flat as we are off track. but to sum up the whole journey it was hella fun that we finally entered one of the world largest limestone cave. and the ticket price was so affordable which is rm 10 for malaysian adult.

great cave that you should come when you visit miri town. we rent a car kancil rm60 day and just fill in rm20 can drive all day to gua niah and back to town. kancil really save petrol. drive distance about 1.45 hour when reach the entrance we need to go to office for register and buy ticket rm10 person and the staff will show the map to the caves. taking boat rm1 person to cross the river. walking distance about 4.2km with a proper staircase and tracking road. about 3.8km there is a few small shop selling some drink. 100 plus rm4 its tourist price..... can take a rest before start climb up the caves. wash room also ready. if you want to go into the caves better prepare hand glove and cap together with flashlight..... because we dont know what thing along the caves that we need to touch. its was amazing place because the caves are really huge big. its worth to take trip to here. after that we rewards us with kfc after we drive out around 5 km from the entrance.
