not that easy to reach but definitely worth it! how to get there from miri grab a grab for around 75 ringgit it s a drive about 1 5 hours on a very bumpy road and not every driver is happy about the long drive... best would be to find a driver for the whole trip who is waiting for you and takes you back to miri also you can stop at nearby beaches ! at the nationalpark headquarter at nationalpark is right next to the entrance and the fee is 20 ringgit per foreigner just cash! . to get to the caves you have to walk about 3 km through beautiful rainforest on a wooden walkway so easy access... the caves are beautiful there s a wooden boardwalk through all of them but watch out a lot of smelly guano is on the ground you should take a strong head torch with you or you can borrow it for 5 ringgit at the jetty entrance because the moon cave is really really dark! it takes all in all around 4 hours to reach the caves and walk through them. how to get back the nice lady at the headquarters calls a local driver or one is already waiting at the parking lot and he brings you to the bus stop junction in 17 km for 25 ringgit. there you can take the bus kuching miri for 10 ringgit person it stops every hour and takes around 1 5 hours to miri. the niah caves are huge there are just a few tourists and you feel a little bit like indiana jones discovering them! even if it s not so easy to get there it s definitely worth it!

1 a super quiet niah national park would be nice for those dislike touristy spots 2 super friendly park office personnel 3 straightforward park direction 4 some walking about an hour or more to reach the main cave 5 could buy some light snacks drinks before reaching the caves 6 kind of lack of any information when one reaches the caves 7 no local guide available to hire to explore the darker parts of the cave 8 the darker part of the cave kind of creepy
fantastic park was the first one to go in so i was completetly alone for the entire walk thrugh thecaves. special feeling and an incredible experience. highly recommended when in sarawak
remember to bring torch light ur handphone flashlight is not enough not just showing the way in the dark cave but also enable u enjoy the majesty of the stalagmites.

one of the oldest and biggest cave in the world on 1 way to go on to this place 2hrs drive from miri along the way you will have the feeling lost but trust your gps. avoid going by driving with small car the estate palm trees road are not good after or during rain you can be stuck in mud mid way. upon arrival take 5mints toilet break the public toilet dont have water no maintenance ..i borrowed one of the rumah panjang residents toilets which they happily let you use them. there are some creepy people here too the teenagers boys just ignore them they want to talk but couldn t understand a word they are saying and looks like some addicts on the loose. anyway they wont bother just ealk your way to the cave. there is ticketing office but its close not operating...means entrances is freeee! bring your own food snacks drinks most importantly mosquito repellent. wear a good shoes because most of the wood pathway stairs and inside the cave floor are slippery. bring handkerchiefs towels or extra shirts to change because you will sweat like a pig. cave is chilly but no wind to blow our sweat away. not to forget bring torch light too. head torch is better . dont really need a guide the pathway is clear and safe hard to get lost unless you purposely wonder off the pathway stairs. if you like nature you will love this cave hike. 3 4hrs depands on your walking speed .
beautiful place. it need strength good legs workout to visit the place. also bring energy bar food or lot of drinking water with touchlight to help navigate the place

the reception staff are friendly nice and kind people. the worst service is the boat ride service. do not have scheduled time for boat service. we waited from 8.15am to 9.00am for boatman and his son to arrive. on arrival he still need time to prepare. it s the boat service that affected my trip ended unhappy ones. niah cave management need to look into it to improve
so big and beautiful cave!! a must go spot ived only heard during my school days about its wonders but now became a reality for me after ived visited it!!
revisited this after 20 year. nice place. it is scenic and makes u feel closer to the nature. more preservation could be done to this historical place

well maintained national park.thumb up for the staff here.keep it up. we are lucky today is a good weather.we drive frm miri around 7.30 am and reach here around 9.30am. we reach the painting cave at 12noon. the path till grand cave still ok but the way to painting cave quite challenging if u brings elderly. the cave still remains it s natural.not many or not at all artificial sport light.good to c the can remain the nature.this is an eye opener for me how does gua niah really looks like.and is the best nearest attraction frm miri town

really enjoyed our day here. easy walk on path and boardwalk to traders and the great cave. undulating stairs for the last 2 caves. allow 4 hrs for a leisurely extensive tour. really like that sarawak forestry has employed paths to protect the nature here. they really need to do the same at other sarawak national parks especially bako np to protect the forest floor from degradation.

stay in park hq here very good with aircon. not many people stay here. there is a cafe that opens at 7am providing breakfast lunch and dinner. please do inform park hq if people are stealing birds nest in non harvest period. it is sad to see so many eggs on the floor. must visit cave in sarawak but hopefully more can be done for conservation. ancient cave paintings maybe related to australian aboriginal art dating back 40000 years.
beautiful back to nature experience

best place for a couch potato like me to have a workout . there is an area inside the great cave that have array of lights enters from an opening in the cave roof. the view is stunning during a sunny day. this is a historical sites too. highly recommended.
adult pass rm10. cant stop taking pictures. main entrance called gua dagang or trader s cave. inside the cave is amazing. need torchlight in the cave its dark

great challenges exploring the darkness of this unique borneo earliest civilisation cave and coming out of the beautiful openings. would appreciate malaysian authorities focus on the improvement of safety and user friendly walk ways to minimise injuries.

stayed here 1night and the room was not bad although don t have aircon a bit hot as no mosquito net at window. however quiet clean also big enough. not bad for staying 1night. however you better to prepare food in advance if you don t have car as the restaurant a bit costly and may don t have dinner.
very good experience but it is quite exhausting
splendid..worth visiting bring along torch light and trekking shoe it s dark slippery inside
beautiful 60 000 year old caves the trail to reach is around 4 km one way and full of stairs and humid weather but eventually is worth the pain
it was great been there couple of times n i love the place.
by far the most beautiful cave we ve visited.its worth the hike.the paintings on the cave wall was well preserved a must see.

to get here please avoid using google map. they will mislead you to the wrong side where you are in a quarry behind the park entrance. you will be separated by a river and you can never get across. wasz is the map that will bring you to the right entrance of the national park.

one of the must visit tourist spots. the journey from the ticket counter to the jetty is 200 meters. taking a boat across the river takes only 3 5 minutes. from the jetty across the river to niah cave walk on a comfortable track. you only need shoes and suitable clothing to walk for about 3 km.

known for the site where the oldest human remains were discovered in southeast asia amaze cave structure cant imagine how birdnest harvester work at this height.... overall is a 7.5km back and forth walk to visit a few caves u may go here by taking bus at miri bus terminal or self drive get a buddy with you if you afraid of dark but it is totally fine to visit alone cause human is way more scary than ghost

yes if you come to miri must visit this place by renting a car. public transport direct to national park is not available unless you join a tour group. entrance fee is rm 10 for malaysian. foreigner is higher. boat trip go across the river is rm 1 per pax return. bring some cash with you.

we rented a car from miri at around rm 150 day and drove for 1 hr to reached taman negara batu niah. the place is easily accessible by car. entrance fee is rm10 person. to go to the caves we need to cross the river using boat at cost rm1 person ride. then hiked for 1hr to reach the 1st cave traders cave. quite a big cave inhabited by bats and swiftlets. due to the heavy rain we couldnt make to the great cave and painter caves which were 5 and 15 minutes walking distance from the 1st cave. highly recommended for natural green lovers and be prepared to walk. cheers
clean nice path and big natural place to visit for short hike from miri.
the niah caves are a wonderful place to explore. the park is beautiful.
amazing place! curious how the human make it
it s really amazing cave and best ever i ve been ...

the cave is dark inside without any lights so remember to bring your own torchlight. the road is slippery and full of guano which made walking difficult. we even saw snakes crawling past us which scared us to death. we didn t manage to get to the painted cave because it was too dark inside we couldn t even see the signs and ended up going the wrong way. it would be appreciated if the authorities could clean up the road and install more lights so people can reach the painted cave more easily.

we couldn t reach there. so sharing the options to reach the park packages expensive self driven cars better than packages and bus from miri to batu niah then taxi to park. its 17 km charges would be rm 50 for one way! it s better to reach there before 2 pm or you won t be able to complete the trek and return back.
accommodation is old and not properly maintained restaurant is not cheap but the place is so fine jungle bird watching early morning and the caves are amazing.

treks were well maintained but the down side is that there s no local guides unless you arrange for one on your own. the caving experience was otherwise quite fun and free as you get to explore the park caves on your own but at your own risk as well. a caving torch is essential for a better experience. the trail and stairs can be quite slippery so make sure to have proper footwear. the whole trail measures approximately 12km roundtrip .
best experience ever... discovering historical place... i will come back again..definitely
nature unspoilt !! many caves in natural state to explore rare insects to see. the evening bat exodus from the deer cave is a fantastic sight to behold.
offers best view at the peak enjoyed 11km of nature.

please aware don t use this locatio please aware don t use this locatio please aware don t use this locatio use this location at goggle map type niah archeology museum use this location at goggle map type niah archeology museum use this location at goggle map type niah archeology museum notice you can only go to the road under the bridge no other way is allowed go to niah cave notice this address niah national park will not take you go to the road under the bridge. only the niah archeology museum will take you got to the road under the bridge go to niah caves.
breathtaking experience huge cave cool inside.. trekking 3 km one way with proper track.. recommend if youre in miri..
better to visit in the morning it needs 4 6 hours to see all corners. boat prices rise after 5 pm after 7 pm it s 20 rm.
amazing scenery. went to mulu national park first and were sceptical in the first place. but it worth the trip!
incredible well worth the effort to get there. take plenty of cold water and sturdy footwear the boardwalk and caves are very slippery. go early so you can hear the rainforest waking up.
unesco heritage site
new impact for niah national park.....having a new belian track and facilities
amazing place! u should come in the morning and walking till evening
love this place. i have been quite a few times and having seen so many other cave systems i start to appreciate how wonderful this place is in terms of history and size.

your team are a disgrace i drove over one hour from miri to visit the niah cave to be rejected because they don t want to work and said i couldn t get in as you close at 3pm your website state 5pm i m extremely disappointed not only by your staff but how i have been treated i flew from sydney and all i wanted was to have a short walk and be on the boat i didn t even want to go all the way to the cave if i couldn t make it on time for the 5pm boat even if you have one after 5pm also for a more expensive price so there is no reason for not letting me in i was prepared to pay more in order to see and have a walk after such a long trip ! be warn if you are coming on your own you have to be there before 3pm what a waste of my time! and they were very disrespectful
bring torchlight before entering the cave make sure go toilet first
please note the park is temporarily closed due to earlier flooding in the national park area.
spactacular caves. well marked trails
it s closed since 9th dec until further notice currently under maintenance.

i read in the internet before i went and it almost cause me to cancel my family trip to this place because the internet keep on telling about how hard the trip and the long walking that you have to go through especially when you bring your kids. turns out it is only just one hour walk one way maybe less if you are adult . i will write in such a way that no one will be hesitate to go anymore. if you are able to go through all the window shopping at the mall you will be able to make this trip. if you bring infant with you bring along baby stroller will ease your journey...but you must have a partner that carry the stroller up the stairs bring a foldable stroller type. bring torch light if you would like to explore deeper in the cave even though enjoying the view from a distance is sufficient imo. gloves is a must if you plan to go deeper into the cave as it get more slippery and you have to hold the rails that are full of birds bats dropping. sandal might work if you are careful but not if you plan to go deeper into the cave sport shoes is better . the scenery along the trails heading to the cave s entrance is so breathtaking so you might want to take your time and stop to take pictures which is why the total journey might take longer than 3 hours back and forth . spare total 4 hours for this journey. i brought so many food and drinks in my bag pack which is unnecessary. you might survive the journey with just a small bottle of mineral water as there are some local sell drinks and tidbits at the cave entrance. it is pricy but i think it would be a good idea to support their business as they keep the place alive. i will not write about the facility as so many people talk about it already...go read other review also.

it s a nice nature walk through the park. the park is provided with walkway and there s no way you ll get lost along the way unless you intentionally leave the developed walkway. the highlight of our trip to this place was exploring the niah cave complex. it s easy to explore however it s not recommended for those claustrophobic or those afraid of the dark as some inner section of the caves have no natural light. bring headlights and flashlight when exploring the caves.

always wanted to come here ever since i saw it in the history textbook during my secondary school. finally i made it to come here. sadly the archelogy museum is closed for renovation. if you love nature here is the place. you will have to walk a lot in order to complete the course. i have walked 10km but still can t finish the exploration. make sure to bring your headlamp raincoat proper attire energy bar to help you fuel up during the tour.

a must visit if you come to miri sarawak. at the site you can enjoy a nice tropical forest hike a look at the old cavemen huts in large open caves while observing the amazing limestone and ancient human stone painting. amidst the fun the highlight of the journey would be the totally dark path to get to the painting cave. you can easily spend one whole meaningful day here. the niah cave is about an hour and a half drive from the town of miri. parking is easy as it has ample of parking space for free. the entrance is charged rm10 per adult and rm3 per child. for foreigners the price is doubled up. the ticketing counter also serves as the entry point. you ll then need to take a boat ride for a very short distance to get across the river that separated the trail from the mainland. you ll be charged rm1 per person for it. once you reach the jetty you ll begin to take a long walk on a well designed trail that is easy to follow but requires good stamina. the whole walking trip could take up at least 4 hours so make sure you bring sufficient water supply. you can find a toilet and a simple stall ran by the locals for rest and refills midway before you reach the cave.
nice experience though quite disappointed as the area was poorly managed there were rubbish everywhere . but overall it was good the staff was friendly.

it was an okay experience. you can visit niah national park if you have the time to cross this place out of your list but i wouldn t put it on the top three things to do in miri bintulu of you only have 48 hours in either city. the scale of the cave in this national park is impressive if you take the time to appreciate the historical findings but otherwise it lacks any adventure or perhaps excitement unless you re someone who s really into anthropology or just history in general . but that makes it very family friendly. it really is just a breezy walk through a cave which some part is completely pitch black the whole family can enjoy if that s your thing . the facilities are well maintained. the wooden track is really something worth mentioning neat and sturdy throughout from a little over 4km track i only saw one part that had one missing board . if you re driving yourself make sure that you picked the right location as your destination. google map directed me via the back entrance! so i had to go through 30 minutes of off road driving in my small city car.

the cave is huge! and to help bring out the inner explorer inside you the caves have a nice wooden walkway laid out throughout its cavities that pass by awe inspiring sites. remember to bring water and a flashlight! it can get pitch black dark at some parts!

i give 5 !!!!!!!! proud to have niah caves. easily reached. drive right to the national park office. register yourself. entery fees adult rm 10.00 each. students rm 3.00. rm1. 00 for a short ride to cross niah river. once across you will start walking. the trail is very very very easy to walk. the beginning of the trail is cemented path. reaching swampy area the pathway is strong wood pier. it will go right to the of the great caves. remember to bring flash light. some places are pitch dark. advice you to bring strong bright torchlight so that you can view the bats and swallow at the high ceilings of the cave! very beyond words feeling!!! don t worry with the pitch dark cave. the trail inside is very safe. you must go right to painted caves go and you will understand!!!! come enjoy the creations of our great god!

first of all the journey from miri or bintulu to cave entrance car park will took around 2 hours due to current trans borneo highway under construction. if you go from bintulu don t take the inner road will be better to take the coastal road which is more safe but longer journey. park at the entrance and walk to the jetty a boat transfer will let you cross the river. the hike path started with concrete walkway then will be a long walk thru wooden platform. if you have kids or old parents coming with you from jetty to cave entrance it will be exhausted walk due to humidity rain up and down hill. they are 2 pondok thru out the walk path journey and the final one is before trader cave with toilet. after walk thru trader cave a short walk will reach main cave entrance. usually it have 2 option either choose the left side uphill slope or choose the right side walk way with stairs. the left side slope is ok but old people might find it difficult. the stair will drain more of your energy. the wooden walkway in the cave is really slippery so you will loss more energy to get the balance and grip. water torchlight head lamp rain coat and hand glove are needed. bring more towel and shirt for exchange most likely you will sweat like hell until your underwear also get wet. wear high socks and spray insect repellent. trust me if you are big size and less excersize you will sure be suffer. do not force yourself to go all the way to painted cave. it will be like 10km walk feel like due to the stairs and slippery walk way. muscle cramp usually happens and along the walkway not much place for you to stop sit and rest. if you bring kids i will suggest to just walk around the main cave if you feel like your kids have little bit tired stop and go back. you need to assume your kids got exhausted and you need to carry him her all the way from main cave to car park trust me it is one way to hell. if you have more than 2 kids and only you and your husband wife i will suggest you think twice before coming. either you stop and return at the right time or bring along one fit adult with you. please start your journey from main cave back to jetty no later than 3pm. the sunlight will getting darker after 3pm. please consider fatigue management if you are the sole driver due to tiredness road accident often happen on pan borneo highway. please consider as well pbh currently under construction and the road is having a lot of lane changes and pot hole. some of the stretch of highway do not have phone signal coverage so if you drive fast and hit something to get help will take ages. my personal experience 20 000 steps 30 floor up countless of near miss fall down due to slippery walkway finish up 2 liter of water shirt wet from top till bottom raining when on the way walk back to jetty please be remind that the main cave walkway is leading you to painted cave. after painted cave you need to return back to main cave entrance follow back the original path. the feeling of walking from main cave entrance all the way to painted cave is like running full marathon with up and down hill.

the niah caves is 75km south of miri airport 1 hour 11 minute car drive and 85 km south of miri town 1 hour 20 minute car drive by coastal road . we rented a 11 seater van for two days and one night from golden system car rental tours at the miri airport and drove to niah caves after lunch at sing chiong restaurant in bekenu town along the way. the rental for the van cost rm350. with insurance and the sales and service tax added in the total cost came to rm390 27 june 2019 . the entrance fee for adults was rm10.00 for malaysians. the boat ride across the small river cost rm1.00 each way. there were four caves traders cave gua dagang . great cave complex gan kira and painted caves. we couldn t visit the last cave the painted caves as it was raining. it took us four and a half hours to get back to the car park after completing the tour of the three caves. parts of the great cave complex was completely dark so we couldn t see anything. we were just concentrating on walking on the plank footpath with our torchlight. we saw several long poles hanging from the ceiling of the cave. bird nest collectors climb up these very long poles to scrape of the nests. a flashlight and good walking shoes are absolutely essential. two members of our group fell because of the slippery planks. luckily no one was injured. a pair of gloves would be useful as the railings were covered with dirt and swiftlet droppings and guano. also take along a bottle of drinking water.

stepping into kwai chai hong this chinese new year is like walking right into a vibrant living folk tale. for 2026 the hidden alleyway in kl s chinatown has transformed into a glowing sanctuary for the guardians of legacy . a walk through the guardian s lane the first thing that hits you is the canopy of lanterns cube shaped and bursting with the playful bold colors of cloth tiger folk art. each one depicts a different zodiac animal with that signature wide eyed protective gaze meant to ward off bad vibes and usher in health and luck. as you cross the iconic red bridge you re greeted by the star of the show the 7 foot tall guardian of triumph. since 2026 is the year of the horse this blue hued masterpiece stands proud with intricate patterns of victory flames and delicate chrysanthemums. if you look closely at the sculptures like the frog perched on the horse s side you ll find hidden auspicious symbols tucked away in the details. why it s the best alley walk in kl it isn t just about the gram worthy backdrops though there are plenty! . this year s collaboration with guardian malaysia feels deeply personal. it s a tribute to the quiet love in our culture the kind that doesn t always say i love you out loud but shows it through protection and the keeping of traditions. traveler s tip the installation is open daily from 9 00 am to midnight but the magic really happens after 7 00 pm. the contrast of the glowing lanterns against the rustic mural covered brick walls creates a nostalgic atmosphere you won t find anywhere else in the city.

kwai chai hong is a beautifully restored hidden alley tucked behind the busy petaling street in kuala lumpur. what used to be a neglected lane has been transformed into a vibrant cultural space filled with murals inspired by 1960s chinatown life. the artwork reflects everyday scenes from children playing in the alleyways to small local vendors giving visitors a glimpse into the area s history. it s compact easy to access and well maintained. if you re exploring chinatown in kl this is a quick but worthwhile stop. it adds a meaningful layer to your walk beyond the usual market experience.

nostalgic vibe tucked away in kl chinatown this restored heritage alleyway brilliantly captures the golden era of the 1960s. the interactive murals and pre war architecture offer an immersive glimpse into old school local life. vibrant art the current installation features twelve sculptural zodiac guardians inspired by the traditional cloth tiger folk art. the centerpiece is a striking 7 foot tall lead guardian making it an elite spot for photography. interactive elements by scanning qr codes next to the murals you can listen to audio stories that bring the heritage scenes to life with sound. perfect ambiance beyond the art the alley is surrounded by trendy cafes and bars such as the famous bunn choon egg tart shop nearby

donkey and i were visiting kl and donkey had planned an itinerary for us to check out kwai chai hong drawn to its rich history and famous artworks. we hoped to experience the art history food and atmosphere all in one place. we visited in the evening around 6 pm which turned out to be the perfect time as we got to experience the alley both in the daylight and as it transitioned into the night. as we were taking pictures it started to rain and we ducked into one of the shops in the back alley to take cover. to our surprise it turned out to be a hidden gem shibuya yakitori shop. so we decided to grab a beer there whilst waiting for the rain to stop. we ordered the conners beer package for 3 at rm 55. since we were quite full we didn t try any of their food or yakitori. i know there are qr codes on the murals but we didn t scan any of them. donkey and i took lots of nice pictures in the area though. overall donkey and i had an enjoyable time taking pictures at kwai chai hong. although the place is quite short and small we still roughly learned about the history of the place based on the artworks the display and the atmosphere within the back alley. google rating fun 4 5 enjoyable time taking pictures and experiencing the art history and atmosphere. atmosphere 4 5 rich history and famous artworks experienced both in daylight and at night. overall 4 5 a worthwhile visit to kwai chai hong for its art history and atmosphere despite being short and small.

after hearing so much about this place i finally got to visit kwai chai hong a hidden heritage alley in kl chinatown also well remembered as the alley of little ghost back in the old glory days. got swept away by this year s cny display themed guardians of legacy . had an amazing time exploring this exciting part of the city. many thanks to my wanderlust buddies for planning this impromptu day trip. abundant blessings love and protection for this cny 2026

i visited this street during chinese new year so there were decorations everywhere which made the place feel extra special but also much more crowded. i went in the middle of the day so it was quite busy. if you visit early in the morning it will probably be less crowded. i really liked the street art and the many small caf s and shops. most of them specialize in just one thing which gives the place a unique vibe.

this spot is consistently bustling with visitors whether they re locals or tourists from abroad and it s easy to see why. during this visit the entire area was beautifully adorned for the mid autumn festival creating a festive and heartwarming atmosphere. colorful traditional lanterns were strung overhead and many of them featured charming rabbit designs adding a playful touch to the celebration. the decorations brought the place to life making it feel both magical and inviting. what makes the experience even more special is the setting itself. the historical building with its vintage charm and old world character offers a glimpse into the past. its architecture and preserved details provide a lovely nostalgic vibe reminding visitors of how things once looked in earlier days. the combination of heritage surroundings and vibrant festival d cor makes this place a delightful spot to explore take photos and enjoy the seasonal festivities at a leisurely pace.

kwai chai hong is a hidden gem in kuala lumpur s chinatown where history and art intertwine to revive the charm of a bygone era. this beautifully restored alleyway showcases intricate murals depicting the daily lives of early chinese settlers offering visitors a glimpse into the city s rich cultural heritage. the fusion of old world architecture and modern artistic expressions creates a captivating atmosphere making it a prime spot for photography and cultural exploration. as night falls the alley transforms with soft lantern lights adding to its nostalgic appeal. visitors can explore the surrounding caf s and artisan shops that complement the heritage theme making the experience even more immersive. whether you re a history enthusiast an art lover or simply looking for a unique spot in the city kwai chai hong stands as a testament to chinatown s enduring legacy.

kwai chai hong is a hidden gem in the heart of kuala lumpur s chinatown offering a unique blend of history art and culture. this beautifully restored lane is a must visit for anyone exploring the area with its charming old shophouses vibrant murals and nostalgic ambiance. walking through kwai chai hong feels like stepping back in time with its carefully preserved architecture and interactive art pieces that tell the story of chinatown s past. the murals depicting scenes of daily life in olden days are incredibly detailed and make for perfect photo opportunities. it s not just a visual treat but also a meaningful connection to the history of the area. the lane is also home to several trendy cafes bars and restaurants where you can enjoy delicious food and drinks while soaking in the artistic atmosphere. it s a great spot to unwind take pictures or simply enjoy the creative vibe. whether you re a history buff an art lover or just looking for a unique instagram worthy location kwai chai hong is highly recommended. a perfect mix of culture creativity and nostalgia in the heart of kuala lumpur!

for travelers with a passion for creativity and a fondness for exceptional coffee look no further than the captivating kwai chai hong art avenue. situated in the heart of kuala lumpur this lively street is a haven where art enthusiasts and coffee aficionados converge. as you amble down this avenue a vivid tapestry of street art portraying everyday life and dazzling installation pieces crafted from glistening plate glass will greet you. each corner of this street transforms into an open air gallery with its own distinctive narrative. but there s more to discover here. kwai chai hong art avenue is also home to a delightful array of cozy coffee shops bars and even a chocolate tasting shop. whether you crave a robust espresso or a velvety latte these inviting cafes have your caffeine desires covered. be prepared for bustling crowds especially as night falls. getting here is a breeze thanks to convenient public transport connections. and when the sun dips below the horizon the street comes to life with a captivating dance of lights promising an unforgettable nocturnal adventure. kwai chai hong art avenue is where imagination and flavor intertwine offering a treasure trove of experiences for every traveler seeking art coffee and the enchantment of the night.

kwai chai hong a hidden gem in kuala lumpur kwai chai hong also known as ghost lane is a charming hidden alleyway in chinatown kuala lumpur. it s a vibrant and colorful street filled with murals street art and traditional chinese architecture. key features instagram worthy the colorful murals and art installations make it a perfect spot for photos. historical charm learn about the area s history through the art pieces. cafes and restaurants enjoy delicious food and drinks in the cozy atmosphere. if you re looking for a unique and authentic experience in kuala lumpur kwai chai hong is a must visit.

update i decided to do another visit on a sat night and the place takes on a whole new identity at night. the entire area including the back alley leading to the entrance is vibrant and crowded thanks to a night market. this is no ordinary night market though. the best way to describe it is a genz night market with everything from craft beer to artisan coffee. been here 2 years ago and decided to give it another go recently. what a big difference that 2 years has been. the place used to be quite hot as there wasn t much shade. now many of the plants have grown to provide a nice cooling shade. some of the murals have faded and it would be good to refresh them a bit. just don t expect too much as it is just a small back alley that has been spruced up with murals and given a 70 s era chinatown vibe. but the place has done a great job of revitalizing the area. my only other complaint is that there is currently a japanese alcohol company sponsorship that has placed japanese elements like lanterns arches and even a big billboard of fujiyama. the vibe at kwai chai hong is the 70 s chinese era and the japanese elements just do not jive with the rest of the place. it s like a bad hollywood movie that can t tell the difference between chinese and japanese. however i do understand that sponsorship is needed to upkeep the place since it is free.

a hidden gem of kuala lumpur.ghost alley also known as kwai chai hong is a small alley off lorong panggung street. its name comes from the cantonese word which means little ghost alley or little demon alley . there are two theories about how the alley got its name. one theory is that the chinese referred to naughty children as and because children liked to play there the alley was named after them. another theory is that the alley was once home to gangsters and was named by the locals. regardless of how it got its name ghost alley is now a popular tourist attraction. there are a number of restaurants cafes and shops here as well as some beautiful street art.

kwai chai hong means little demon alley . it is the latest tourist attraction in the chinatown of kuala lumpur. it is a small alley behind petaling street focused on recreating the culture of the 1960s golden era of the area. the origins of the name are debated. according to one chinese refer to naughty children as kwai chai and this is where the kids liked to play. according to another theory this place was popular amongst gangsters and drinkers and drug addicts and these activities carry negative connotations resulting in such a name. the place today is very popular and frequented by tourists. it is decorated by numerous murals depicting daily life at this place in the old days. the alley has been there for more than a century and has become dilapidated over time but recently lovingly restored. in addition to murals and art it offers local cafes and restaurant experience right inside the alley. come visit and take pictures and visit local stores. no entry fees but tends to be crowded. expect to spend 15 20 minutes if plan to take pictures spend some time in the alley.

kwai chai hong is a lane that locates at the back of petaling street. in the lane have six murals drawn by local artists that record daily life in the 1960s to let tourists know the story of chinatown in kuala lumpur. every mural comes with a qr barcode for you to scan and read the story for the mural. whenever the season of chinese customs comes this lane will be decorated with the theme of the festival. it s a great place for photography lovers must visit.

beautiful street art you can take pictures with wonderful background. because of covid 19 it s not fully open but you can take some pictures at street. also you can found out some chinese restaurant both side of street road. cool place for hangout. you can find out more cool street art neer by other streets so look around and discover yourself stay safe.

hustle and bustle passes this alley by where lots of kung fu has been put on to the wall murals that barely reflect the real struggles of the local community here but just another artistic dystopia of an imaginary pre war shanghai hong kong utopia. our young artists today while talented are no students of history. their efforts are commercially astute lack passion to capture the living memories of the less than idyllic times that have washed down these broken walls in this corner of the world. sure the roving tourist gets his instagram fix in this back alley for opium dens but the blank eyes leaving now tell a different story. no food for the soul in this wandering just a cruel tease of the tummy with outlets more pretentiously shinjuku style rather than the once steaming trolleys at grandfather s morning ritual yook woo hin. alas no whims of my childhood memories here so a slow walk back out to the sunshine side of this town.

took friends visiting malaysia for the first time to petaling street and kwai chai hong and totally regretted it. we were here on a sunday night. beyond some faded murals depicting street life from the past there s nothing malaysian chinese here. just an unsafe uncomfortable tourist trap. constant harassment from vendors dodging traffic watching for pickpockets even people trying to lure us into brothels. an absolute disgrace and embarrassment. this place does malaysia no justice. i d suggest to skip it. there are far better ways to experience the real malaysia.

really enjoyed our visit here everything very well organised into smaller groups with dedicated slots in each place sky bridge top floor etc. so it was not too busy at any point. fantastic views great history and impressive building learnt a lot. cafe at floor 86 is a great stop to grab a drink on the way back down and spend some time admiring the view.

the petronas twin towers are an incredible landmark and they look especially spectacular at night when the lights make them shine against the skyline. even without going inside the exterior view is impressive and provides a fantastic setting for photos. you ll find professional photographers nearby who can capture pictures for you or you can easily take your own with the famous towers in the background. the nearby klcc park also enhances the visit offering a pleasant place to sit back and enjoy the surroundings. keep in mind that afternoons can get quite hot so visiting early in the day or later in the evening is usually more comfortable. it s definitely one of the top places to see in kuala lumpur.

the petronas twin towers are even more impressive in person than in photos. seeing the twin skyscrapers rise above kuala lumpur is striking especially with the sleek steel and glass reflecting light throughout the day. i went around late afternoon and the view was stunning as the towers changed from bright and shiny in the sun to glowing with lights at night. walking around the base the park and fountains add a nice open space to take in the full view and it s a great spot for photos from many angles. if you go up to the sky bridge or observation deck you get wide city views that are unforgettable. it s busy but totally worth it if you want an iconic kuala lumpur experience that mixes modern design city energy and amazing skyline views.

maybe not the tallest buildings in the world regardless of whether the antenna is included or not! what makes the petronas twin towers stand out from the rest is the uniqueness of a pair of interlinked buildings. we visited on a weekday after making an online reservation a few days in advance i would recommend doing this as it gets booked up quickly. the well organised and relatively small group tour approx 20 px takes you to the sky bridge at level 41 then the observation deck at level 86 and finally to the observation deck and cafe at level 83 where you can stay for as long as you wish. most people will be content with 60 90 minutes for the tour. overall a great visit managed by helpful and friendly staff! oh and by the way you ll end up in the gift shop what a surprise

spectacular outside especially at night outdated and disappointing inside especially during the day. sky bridge and top floor are nothing special i was expecting much nicer interior design it feels like in the 90s inside. view from the top is nice during the day perhaps at night it s more outstanding. if you visit the place in the evening to take pictures of the building expect a lot of crowd between 20 00 and 22 00. it gets a bit less crowded between 22 00 and 24 00. at midnight they turn off the building s lighting do no more nice pictures after that there are professional photographers at the kl golden triangle so you can use their services as well.

a treat to eyes! this simply the most loved attraction in kl and my. one of the most iconic landmarks in the world! best time to visit is around sunset and you get great daylight views and night photos. tickets for the skybridge and 86th floor should be booked in advance. klcc park and the fountain show nearby are beautiful and great for photos. even if you don t go up seeing the towers from outside is an unforgettable experience.

the petronas twin towers remain the undisputed crown jewel of kuala lumpur s skyline offering a breathtaking display of modern islamic architecture that is even more impressive in person than in photographs. while i chose to skip the observation deck tour in favor of visiting the nearby klcc tower menara kuala lumpur the experience of viewing the twin towers from the ground and surrounding areas was more than worth the trip. their gleaming stainless steel and glass facades create a mesmerizing geometric pattern that catches the sunlight by day and glows like silver beacons by night. what makes them truly remarkable is their omnipresence no matter where you find yourself in the city these soaring spires serve as a constant majestic north star. walking through the meticulously landscaped klcc park offers the best vantage points for photography allowing you to appreciate the sheer scale and intricate structural details without the need for a ticket. even without stepping inside the towers provide a profound sense of wonder and stand as a testament to world class engineering. they are a must see landmark that defines the identity of malaysia proving that some buildings are just as impactful from the outside as they are from the top.

i absolutely loved visiting the petronas twin towers. the architecture is breathtaking and seeing the towers up close made the experience even more special. the city skyline views are incredible especially around the klcc area and the atmosphere feels lively and memorable. it s truly a must visit spot in kuala lumpur and one of my favorite experiences in the city.

makes you wonder if it s one of the most under marketed landmarks because seeing the petronas twin towers in person is far more impressive than expected. the exterior materials are stunning and the structure s form is architecturally striking. the night view is especially breathtaking. when lit up in white the towers feel majestic and you can even see the beams of light from afar it s a spectacular sight. definitely worth visiting more than once. connected to shopping malls and department stores the area is convenient to explore. just note that tickets for the observation deck sell out quickly so plan ahead.

best attraction in kl and entire my. 88 storey supertall skyscraper. a modern islamic design with visitor skybridge tickets will be available in advance. if not to go still attractive and unforgettable moments. backup side of twins tower a water fountain shows at evening time. don t forget to watch......

standing as a global icon of malaysia s vision and modernization the petronas twin towers remain a breathtaking masterpiece of structural engineering. what makes them truly stand out is the masterful integration of cultural identity with futuristic design. by utilizing the rub el hizb eight pointed star as a geometric foundation the towers don t just reach for the sky they tell a story of islamic heritage and national pride. from a functional and aesthetic perspective the shimmering stainless steel and glass facade creates a dynamic presence that changes with the daylight. the skybridge while a brilliant structural solution for wind resistance has also become one of the world s most sought after viewing platforms offering a unique walk in the sky experience.

ear popping experience you will not regret. buying tickets in advance online recommended. takes less than an hour and the staff are all very helpful and guide you thru every stop to the skybridge and top on the 86th floor observation deck. great gift shops on top floor and ground as you exit. they even let you mail your postcards right there! put this short but memorable stop on your list in kl!

this place is a absolute must visit place in kuala lumpur. the architecture is breathtaking especially when illuminated at night and the surrounding klcc park adds to the overall beauty of the place. the view from the skybridge and observation deck is stunning and offers a unique perspective of the city. the area is clean well organized and perfect for family visits photography and evening walks. with shopping and dining options at suria klcc nearby it s a complete experience that truly represents malaysia s modern identity.

visited the petronas twin towers and it was absolutely stunning. the architecture is breathtaking especially at night when the towers light up. the skybridge experience was smooth and well organized and the view of kuala lumpur from the observation deck is a must see. definitely one of the top landmarks to visit in malaysia!

of course this is a must visit tourist spot if you are in kuala lumpur malaysia it is one of those places where you almost feel like asking did you even go to kuala lumpur if you did not visit the petronas twin towers. i highly recommend visiting at night. it does get more crowded but the weather is cooler the vibes are great and the towers look absolutely stunning when they are lit up during the day it can be very sunny which makes it harder to enjoy the area whereas at night the experience feels much more relaxed and enjoyable. there is also a big cup style night market nearby within walking distance where you can grab food and drinks from food trucks and enjoy live music this makes it a perfect spot to spend the evening soaking in the atmosphere.

an iconic experience that must be done even if you only have one day in kl. a simply stunning building from outside and a thrill to be able to go up and learn about it s construction on the 83rd floor. amazing views and well worth the fairly steep entrance price. a must.

this is one of the most magnificent buildings i have ever witnessed. i was fortunate enough to have access to the rooftop garden and every morning and evening i could simply stand and admire the view it felt almost surreal like ai!. at night the experience was truly indescribable. i highly recommend taking the tour the staff were exceptionally knowledgeable and incredibly friendly which made the experience even more memorable.

petronas twin towers the pride of kuala lumpur malaysia and one of the world s most iconic skylines. height 452 meters 88 floors design a blend of modern architecture and islamic art completed 1998 once the tallest buildings in the world highlight the skybridge on the 41st and 42nd floors connects the towers and is open to visitors uses offices tourism suria klcc shopping mall cultural events at night stunningly illuminated the petronas twin towers are not just architectural marvels they symbolize malaysia s progress and modernity.

the petronas twin towers are impressive from every angle and during my visit they were lit in bright diwali colours an indian celebration marking the triumph of light over darkness good over evil. it made the night view especially striking. the skybridge and observation deck are worth the ticket cost offering clear expansive views of the city. the architecture carries real meaning too the towers are based on an eight pointed islamic geometric pattern reflecting malaysia s muslim heritage and their 1990s construction involved unusually deep foundations and high strength concrete. also worth mentioning the beautiful intergration and coexistence of cultures and ethnicities alongside each other that becomes clear as soon as you enter malaysia. chinese malaysian and indian ethnicities can be observed having very minimal segregation and it s amazing to see just how much they embrace one another s culture and allow time and space for all to celebrate their religious traditions. i m only dropping one star because of the people at the intersection out front who aggressively try to sell photo services. it slightly disrupts an otherwise excellent visit.

the klcc twin towers night view is absolutely stunning. these iconic towers were once the tallest buildings in the world and are connected by the famous skybridge on levels 41 and 42. a must visit spot in kuala lumpur especially at night for photos a relaxing walk music fountain and to truly feel the city s vibrant atmosphere.

amazing experience! i absolutely loved the night view the klcc twin towers look breathtaking when they light up after dark. the atmosphere is calm the surroundings are vibrant and the whole place feels magical. tickets not really recommended you can enjoy most of the beauty from outside. easily accessible via klcc metro station just a short walk away. don t miss the water fountain show it usually happens between 8 pm to 9 pm and it s stunning to watch at night. a must visit spot in kuala lumpur especially at night!

we cannot enter it as it requires buying a ticket. the way in was also confusing as part of it is undergoing renovation. the view from outside was nice though and as it is an iconic landmark it is quite nice for taking a photo there.

visiting the petronas twin towers was truly a lifetime experience for us. the tour lasts around 30 45 minutes and i highly recommend making a reservation in advance. the views are amazing and you can also enjoy the scenery from the caf . definitely a must visit place when you re in kl.

petronas twin towers more commonly known as the twin towers is one of the most iconic landmarks in the whole of malaysia. this architectural masterpiece is truly stunning mesmerizing breath taking. it s extremely beautiful and is massively tall. it almost feels like the top of the tower fades away into the sky. the twin towers used to be the tallest building in malaysia but the less known merdeka 108 is actually taller. regardless no one can deny the twin towers beauty. it is the first place we visited after arriving in malaysia and we truly had a whale of a time here. i ve never seen anything like this in any of rhe other places i ve visited in the past. the inside of the twin towers is paid. but the suria klcc mall in the first few floors in the base of the towers are free. we find visit there the prices are a bit astronomical for my taste. however for the wealthy travellers almost every luxury high end brand we couldn t afford is here! one interesting i must note is the toilets in the ground floor are 2rm rather expensive to use. but if you go to the second floor or first perhaps the toilets are free. so save yourself and your family 2rm and go to the second floor and use the toilet for free. it s not that nice though most other malls had nicer toilets. regardless toilets aside this is truly one of the best views i ve ever seen.

the petronas twin towers are easily one of the most recognizable landmarks in malaysia and seeing them up close is always impressive. even though the towers are primarily office buildings the architecture and scale make them a major tourist attraction. the design is striking both during the day and at night when the entire structure lights up and becomes one of the best photo spots in kuala lumpur. at the base of the towers is suria klcc a large and well maintained shopping mall with plenty of dining options retail brands a cinema and even a small art gallery. it s convenient to explore the mall before or after taking photos outside. the surrounding klcc park also adds to the experience giving you a relaxed space to walk around enjoy the fountains or capture the towers from different angles. if you plan to visit the sky bridge or the observation deck booking tickets in advance is recommended as slots can fill up quickly. the views from the top are worth it if you want a full city panorama. overall the petronas twin towers are a must see landmark. even if you don t go inside the combination of the towers suria klcc and the nearby park makes it an enjoyable stop for both tourists and locals.

visited this place recently and i must say i found this building quite charming compared to other high towers. we went during twilight and could see the sound and light show as well. people were very well organised and there is a lot of place to sit around too. loved the vibes of this place and could be amazing to spend the evening here.

the iconic landmark in kuala lumpur. i ve visited twice with my family and my toddlers always have a great time running around the big open area looking at the tall towers and watching the fountain with the cheeky crows playing around. lots of local and tourist activities to see which always something new for my kids. definitely will visit again when we come back to malaysia.

makes you wonder when you see the petronius tower........the night view is especially breathtaking. when lit up in white the towers feel majestic and you can even see the beams of light from afar it s a spectacular sight. definitely worth visiting more than once.

the petronas twin towers are truly magnificent an architectural marvel that leaves you in awe the moment you lay eyes on them. it s almost unbelievable that such a masterpiece was conceived and constructed nearly four decades ago. the design is both futuristic and timeless perfectly blending modern engineering with malaysian cultural elements. whether you re admiring them from the ground walking the skybridge or standing at the observation deck the petronas towers offer a perspective that reminds you of human creativity and ambition at its finest. a must visit landmark that truly defines kuala lumpur s skyline.

astonishing and beautiful twin towers. these towers are known for being the world s tallest twin towers connecting via a skybridge housing the petronas oil company suria klcc mall and symbolizing malaysia s development. completed in 1998 these 88 story postmodern marvels feature islamic designs offer city views and remain a major tourist attraction very highly recommended to visit.

petronas twin towers name petronas twin towers location kuala lumpur malaysia architect c sar pelli developer owner petroliam nasional berhad petronas construction period 1993 1998 opened 1998 height 451.9 meters 1 483 feet 88 storeys significance tallest buildings in the world from 1998 to 2004 still the tallest twin towers in the world design structure islamic inspired geometric design primarily constructed using reinforced concrete connected by a double deck skybridge on the 41st and 42nd floors at about 170 m high main uses office spaces petronas headquarters and other companies observation deck and visitor attractions key features skybridge not rigidly fixed allows movement during strong winds suria klcc shopping mall at the base klcc park surrounding the towers cultural importance iconic symbol of malaysia s economic growth and modernization one of the most recognized landmarks in southeast asia

the petronas towers are every bit as iconic in real life as they are in photos an absolute must see if you re in kuala lumpur. standing at the base and looking up is genuinely awe inspiring the scale the symmetry and the way they light up at night make them one of the most impressive modern architectural landmarks in the world. the surrounding klcc area is also well worth exploring the park is beautifully maintained and the fountain show in the evening adds a nice touch. the towers themselves house a high end shopping mall with plenty of dining options and the bridge and observation deck experience offers incredible panoramic views of the city. that said the queues for the observation deck can be long and it s best to book in advance. it s also quite a short visit once you re up there so manage expectations accordingly. but even if you don t go inside just seeing the towers up close especially lit up at night is memorable on its own. in short the petronas towers are more than just a photo stop they re a proud symbol of kl and visiting them gives you a real sense of the city s ambition and elegance.

the petronas twin towers are the iconic symbol of kuala lumpur standing tall with stunning architecture and shining steel design. their skybridge night lights and grand presence make them one of the most beautiful and recognisable landmarks in malaysia. a must see for visitors.

first impressions as we approach the city center the twin spires dominate the skyline gleaming silver under the sun by day and sparkling with lights by night. standing at 452 meters 1 483 feet they were once the tallest buildings in the world and remain among the tallest twin structures globally. the islamic inspired design with geometric patterns and sleek steel and glass fa ades immediately conveys elegance and harmony. the surrounding area at the base of the towers lies the suria klcc mall a luxury shopping and dining complex that draws both locals and tourists. outside klcc park provides green space and a scenic lake with fountains perfect for photos and evening strolls. the lake symphony light and water show at night adds a touch of magic. skybridge observation deck one of the highlights for visitors is going up the towers skybridge 41st 42nd floors connecting the two towers it gives a unique vantage point and a thrilling sense of standing between giants. observation deck 86th floor offers panoramic views of kuala lumpur from modern skyscrapers to distant hills. on clear days the view is stunning especially at sunset. our experience we love to taking photos from the park or the fountain area where the full height of the towers fits beautifully in the frame. at night the illuminated towers look especially majestic a must see for photographers and sightseers alike. the audio guided tour inside provides fascinating details about the architecture design and cultural symbolism. nearby attractions after visiting we explore aquaria klcc an oceanarium beneath the towers. avenue k another mall across the street for dining. the nearby bukit bintang area for nightlife and shopping. in short the petronas towers are not just skyscrapers they re an experience that combines awe inspiring architecture cultural symbolism and vibrant urban life at the very heart of kuala lumpur.

the petronas twin towers is truly a masterpiece of modern day kuala lumpur and are a glittering role model upholding the breathtaking skyline of the city. especially during the night the towers glitter and shine becoming a major focal point in the city center. i had a tour to the sky bridge connecting the two towers and it was amazing. amazing views lots to see and experience in the tallest twin towers in the world. i would highly recommend visiting or having a tour to the top an unforgettable memory.

amazing place to experience once in a lifetime petronas twin tower suria klccthe petronas twin towers are a pair of interlinked 88 story skyscrapers in kuala lumpur malaysia designed by c sar pelli that stand at 451.9 meters 1 483 ft . completed in 1996 they were the world s tallest buildings until 2004 and remain the tallest twin towers globally. visitors can access an observation deck on the 86th floor and the skybridge on the 41st and 42nd floors for stunning city views. best time to visit is both day and night to experience the day view and night lights view spent 10 rm to capture a photo by local professional photographers

the petronas twin towers are not just the tallest twin towers in the world they re the glittering heart of modern kl. by day or night they re a jaw dropping sight especially when lit up against the city skyline. start your visit inside suria klcc at the base of the towers a huge and elegant shopping mall with everything from high end luxury brands to local favourites. you ll also find an excellent mix of restaurants food courts and cafes perfect for everything from a quick bite to a fancy dinner. but don t stop there take a stroll through klcc park behind the towers. it s beautifully landscaped with fountains shaded paths and a huge man made lake. you ll get amazing photos from different angles of the towers especially during golden hour or when the towers light up at night. there s even a free light and fountain show every evening! fun fact the towers were once the tallest buildings in the world 1998 2004 and still hold the record for tallest twin towers globally. they stand at 452 metres and are joined by the famous skybridge on levels 41 and 42 which you can actually visit! if you re in kl this place is an absolute must whether for shopping dining photography or just soaking in the vibe of one of southeast asia s most iconic landmarks.

the kuala lumpur city centre klcc is a prominent urban area in the heart of kuala lumpur malaysia known for its iconic skyline modern architecture and vibrant attractions. at the center of klcc stands the petronas twin towers once the tallest buildings in the world rising 452 meters 1 483 feet high. these twin towers are a symbol of malaysia s progress and are connected by a skybridge on the 41st and 42nd floors.

visiting the petronas twin towers is an absolute must if you re in kuala lumpur. standing at 452 metres tall they are still the tallest twin towers in the world and truly the crown jewel of the city s skyline. we went up to the 86th floor observation deck and the view was absolutely breathtaking. you get a sweeping panorama of kl from the bustling city streets to the lush greenery that dots the horizon. the whole experience is very well managed. tickets are timed which helps keep the flow of visitors organised and prevents overcrowding. staff were polite professional and always ready to help. before reaching the top you also get to stop at the skybridge which connects the two towers at the 41st and 42nd floors. walking across it is a surreal experience with glass and steel framing the city far below. what i really appreciated was how clean modern and smooth everything felt from the lift ride to the viewing galleries. it really does feel like a world class landmark. pro tip book your tickets in advance and try to go either early in the morning or near sunset the views are especially magical at those times. overall the petronas twin towers are more than just an iconic photo spot. they represent modern malaysia at its best combining world class architecture with a memorable visitor experience. highly recommended for couples families and anyone looking to see kl from a different perspective.

the petronas twin towers also known as menara berkembar petronas in kuala lumpur remain one of malaysia s most iconic landmarks and honestly one of the most photogenic skyscrapers in the world. as of late 2025 these 451.9 meter tall twin giants still the world s tallest twin towers continue to draw massive crowds blending stunning islamic inspired architecture with modern engineering.

petronas twin towers is a pair of iconic 88 storied building.they were the tallest buildings in the world from 1996 to 2004. it s height is 451.9m. these towers are located within the kuala lumpur city centre klcc area. the towers are connected by a sky bridge the skybridge at the 41st and 42nd floors. the skybridge is a double decker structure that connects the two towers. it offers city views. these towers are designed by architect c sar pelli they blend modern skyscraper design with islamic motifs including an eight pointed star floor plan. it owned by the petronas group the malaysian national oil and gas company. there are guided tours are available.

kuala lumpur city centre commonly known as klcc is one of the most iconic and vibrant places in malaysia. located in the heart of kuala lumpur klcc is best known for the magnificent petronas twin towers and the luxurious suria klcc. it represents modern malaysia s growth ambition and global presence. visiting klcc offers not only a shopping experience but also a cultural and recreational experience. the petronas twin towers are the main highlight of klcc. their modern islamic inspired architecture makes them stand out beautifully against the city skyline. during the day the towers look sleek and powerful reflecting sunlight from their stainless steel and glass surfaces. at night they light up brilliantly creating a breathtaking view that attracts both tourists and locals. many visitors gather outside to take photographs especially near the fountain and park area. suria klcc located at the base of the towers is a large and well designed shopping mall. it houses a wide variety of international and local brands ranging from luxury boutiques to affordable fashion outlets. the mall is spacious clean and fully air conditioned making it a comfortable place to spend time especially during malaysia s hot weather. in addition there are numerous dining options available including fast food local malaysian cuisine and fine dining restaurants. another attractive feature of klcc is klcc park which is located just beside the towers. the park provides a refreshing green space in the middle of the busy city. it includes a jogging track a children s playground and a large lake with a musical fountain. in the evening the fountain show adds a magical touch to the atmosphere as water dances in rhythm with lights and music. this makes klcc not just a commercial area but also a relaxing recreational destination. transportation to klcc is convenient because it is directly connected to public transport such as the lrt. security in the area is well managed which makes visitors feel safe. however the mall can become very crowded during weekends and public holidays and parking fees may be quite expensive. overall klcc is a must visit destination for anyone visiting kuala lumpur. it combines modern architecture shopping dining entertainment and natural scenery in one location. whether someone wants to shop relax dine or simply admire the stunning skyline klcc offers a complete and memorable experience.

a magnificent building and a true architectural marvel a visionary project especially considering it was completed in the early 2000s. the building s true brilliance shines in the evening when the lights are turned on. personally i find the nighttime look of the petronas towers far more captivating than the daytime look. the glow of the white lights cascading over the structure adds an extraordinary charm. although i couldn t secure tickets to go up the tower simply gazing at it from below felt great. i also attended the water show which takes place every evening at 8 pm. however i found myself completely captivated by the towers beauty to the point where the water show felt almost secondary. evenings do get crowded so be prepared and keep an eye on your belongings. after admiring the towers i d recommend taking a walk around the klcc park for a peaceful end to your visit. overall a must see iconic landmark in kuala lumpur.

the petronas twin towers are a must visit if you re in kuala lumpur. standing at 452 meters tall these towers were once the tallest buildings in the world and they re still incredibly impressive today. whether you re seeing them up close or from a distance the architecture is stunning both day and night. the skybridge and observation deck on levels 41 and 86 offer fantastic views of the city skyline. it s a unique experience walking across the bridge that links the two towers! the guided tour is well organized and you get a decent amount of time to enjoy the views and take photos. at the base of the towers is suria klcc a huge shopping mall with everything from luxury brands to local shops plus lots of dining options. right behind the towers is klcc park a great spot to relax especially at sunset. the fountains here light up at night and make for a beautiful photo try to visit around late afternoon so you can catch both daylight and nighttime views. don t miss the light and fountain show at klcc park after dark! overall the petronas towers are more than just tall buildings they re a symbol of modern malaysia and offer a full experience of architecture shopping culture and great views. highly recommended!

a dream come true! the petronas twin towers have fascinated me since childhood and finally seeing them in person was truly emotional. i m deeply grateful for the chance to witness something i had only imagined through photos and dreams. standing beneath these iconic towers was a surreal moment their elegance scale and presence are absolutely captivating. if you ve ever dreamed of visiting them don t hesitate. it s an experience that stays with you.

felt very happy to see this twin towers had been it many times in tv now for the opportunity to see this live. a very good place to visit and must include our itinerary. pre booking of tickets is good. sky was so clear and blue when we had visited. just felt like seeing a picture poster. if possible please take both evening and night light view for best view

petronas twin towers is definitely worth visiting especially if it s your first time in kuala lumpur. the towers look amazing in real life and the view is even better at night when the lights are on. the area around klcc is clean and well maintained with a nice park to walk around and relax. it can get quite crowded but the atmosphere is still enjoyable. plenty of places to eat and shop nearby making it easy to spend a few hours here. overall a great place for sightseeing and photos.

the petronas towers in kuala lumpur are an iconic architectural masterpiece and one of the most recognizable landmarks in the world. rising majestically against the city skyline these twin skyscrapers offer a perfect blend of modern design and cultural symbolism. the sleek steel and glass fa ade reflects both innovation and elegance while the skybridge connecting the towers provides visitors with breathtaking panoramic views of the city. a visit to the petronas towers is a memorable experience from exploring the suria klcc shopping mall at the base to walking across the skybridge and enjoying the observation deck there s plenty to see and do. the surrounding klcc park adds a relaxing green space with walking paths and fountains making it an ideal spot for both sightseeing and leisurely strolls. the towers are not just visually stunning they also symbolize malaysia s progress and ambition. whether you re a tourist or a local the petronas towers are a must visit attraction that leaves a lasting impression. highly recommended for anyone who appreciates architecture city views and cultural landmarks.

the petronas twin towers are a stunning architectural masterpiece that dominates the skyline offering an unforgettable experience through their iconic skybridge and breathtaking panoramic views of kuala lumpur.

what an iconic symbol in kl i ve seen many people come around this area to take a picture on weekend but it still ok to wait a few minutes for you to have one of your own picture with this building.

i really love the views here! i didn t go up to the top floor for a visit but just standing outside made me marvel at this engineering feat.

the petronas towers are undeniably one of the most iconic landmarks in malaysia and their architectural beauty is breathtaking especially when lit up at night. the view from the skybridge is impressive offering a great perspective of kuala lumpur s skyline. however my experience was somewhat mixed. the place is extremely crowded making it difficult to fully enjoy the surroundings. long queues for tickets and security checks can also be frustrating. if you re visiting for the first time it s worth seeing for the architecture and taking some pictures but i wouldn t go out of my way to visit again.

height 452 meters number of floors 88 each tower year of completion 1998 once the world s tallest building 1998 2004 architect c sar pelli argentinian architect an engineering marvel its construction was inspired by islamic geometric patterns unique to malaysian culture. the skybridge connecting the two towers between the 41st and 42nd floors it rises 170 meters above ground. it is 58 meters long. it is not fixed to either tower and can flex with the wind. it houses a giant shopping mall called suria klcc on its lower floors. klcc park located behind it is perfect for walking sitting by the pond and taking photos overlooking the towers.

petronas twin towers used to be the tallest building in the world at one time. had a great time at the park with my kiddos we had lunch at the food court on the third floor saw a movie and we went shopping. it s a great place with many fun activities! you can spend the whole day..
nice place to visit if you like to see the city from high view

the petronas twin towers are an amazing skyscraper structure that was designed to be a lightning conductor since kl has so many thunderstorms. so this building protects others around it. the sky bridge is the first thing people think of with the towers but as i discovered there is also an observation desk on the 86th floor too. amazing views. i went in the afternoon when it was hot. hard to predict best time as weather in april can be cloudy and rainy on and off all day. definitely recommend making advance booking.

the famed petronas twin towers malaysia s or rather kuala lumpur s biggest tourist attraction once the world s tallest building and the only remaining twin towers in the world they define the modern architectural developments in human history. the towers are like the moon they re visible from almost everywhere you go in kuala lumpur and almost all stores selling merchandise signifying them. the towers house a mall an observation deck and most of it is office commercial space. there s also a museum inside for the motorsports fan f1 especially the tickets for which get sold out a day before so book them much in advance. unfortunately i couldn t get the tickets for the museum and that was very disappointing. we visited the towers around 5 pm or so on may 1 labor day and it started getting slightly crowded already. there s a lot of photographers outside the towers offering to take a photograph on your mobile or a camera for as much as 5 myr. it s definitely worth a click as they take a great picture that you could keep as a memorabilia. since it was early in the evening we decided to explore the surya klcc mall inside the towers. the staff and security team were decent if not good but were not much interested to help maybe because it s quite a busy place. after being confused about and looking for some help one staff member finally helped us in understanding where the ticket counter is and how it s already fully booked and closed for the day and no further tourists would be allowed in the museum or the deck. after our visit to the mall we came out of the towers for the thing that most people come here for that is for the towers to be lighted up. by 6 30 pm it was full house people already started picking their spot in the lawn outside the towers waiting for the light and music fountain show with the towers brightening up in all of their glory. the fountain show starts around 7 30 pm and goes on well for about 90 minutes or so the sound show starts a little later around 8 pm or so. keep your children safe as there s no barricade separating the water body here. the symphony of water and the colors during the show is magnanimous with the towers providing a heavenly backdrop it s a visual treat. the music adds soul to the performance. there s a lot of crowd here so be sure to pick a nice spot otherwise your experience might be ruined. the entry is free. the petronas twin towers are definitely the king of the skyscrapers. and i d recommend anyone coming to kuala lumpur to visit them especially in the evenings for a better experience. set aside a couple hours it s definitely worth it. and then maybe explore the night food street and call it a day. super recommended!

we spent the whole day here. we did an early tour up to the sky deck and middle viewing bridge. worth the 246rm for 2 adults and a child. also went to the petrosains interactive science museum. there is a huge 6 story shopping centre in the complex including restaurants. at night time there is a water light show that runs every half an hour.

one of the most impressive skyscrapers in the world. the design is unique and represents malaysian culture beautifully. the view from the skybridge and observation deck is amazing. klcc park nearby is perfect for relaxing and taking photos. highly recommended!
we went on december 25. got the christmas special lighting view. the place is chill and has a festive vibe.
