review petronas twin towers a soaring symbol of kuala lumpur the petronas twin towers aren t just skyscrapers they are an indelible symbol of malaysia s ambition and a captivating marvel of modern architecture. dominating the kuala lumpur skyline these twin giants offer an experience that is both awe inspiring and deeply rooted in the city s identity. from the moment you lay eyes on them their sheer scale is breathtaking. their distinctive postmodern design incorporating islamic art principles makes them instantly recognizable and truly unique. whether viewed from afar gleaming under the tropical sun or dramatically lit against the night sky their presence is undeniably commanding. for visitors the highlight is undoubtedly the skybridge that connects the two towers at the 41st and 42nd floors. stepping onto this double decked bridge offers an unparalleled panoramic view of kuala lumpur a sprawling urban tapestry punctuated by verdant parks and other architectural wonders. looking down from this vantage point provides a dizzying yet exhilarating perspective on the city s vibrancy. further up the observation deck on the 86th floor provides an even more expansive vista. here the city stretches out to the horizon offering a true sense of kl s scale and development. interactive displays and informative exhibits also provide insights into the towers construction and malaysia s journey. beyond the views the petronas twin towers are integrated into the vibrant suria klcc shopping mall offering a convenient and upscale retail experience. the beautifully landscaped klcc park at their base provides a refreshing green oasis amidst the urban bustle perfect for a leisurely stroll or enjoying the spectacular lake symphony light and water show. tips for visitors book tickets in advance especially for the skybridge and observation deck tickets sell out quickly. booking online well ahead of your visit is highly recommended to avoid disappointment. go early or late to avoid peak crowds and potentially enjoy softer light for photography consider visiting right when they open or closer to closing time. explore klcc park don t just rush to the top. take time to enjoy the park s tranquility and the various photo opportunities it offers of the towers from ground level. in conclusion a visit to the petronas twin towers is an absolute must for anyone traveling to kuala lumpur. it s more than just a tourist attraction it s an immersive experience that celebrates architectural prowess offers unparalleled city views and provides a deeper appreciation for malaysia s dynamic spirit. you ll leave with lasting memories and countless stunning photographs of these magnificent towers.

visiting the petronas towers was an unforgettable experience! these iconic twin towers are not only an architectural masterpiece but also a symbol of malaysia s progress and innovation. the views from the skybridge and the observation deck are absolutely breathtaking offering a stunning panorama of kuala lumpur s skyline. the structure itself is beautifully maintained and the surrounding area including the klcc park and shopping mall makes it easy to spend an entire day here. everything from the lighting at night to the hospitality of the staff made the visit exceptional. highly recommend this to anyone traveling to malaysia it s a true highlight of the city..!!!

the petronas twin towers are without a doubt the most iconic landmark of kuala lumpur and a must see attraction in malaysia. standing at 452 meters they were once the tallest buildings in the world and still remain the tallest twin towers globally making them an impressive sight both day and night. the towers are located in the klcc area surrounded by shopping dining and the beautiful klcc park which offers great spots for photos and relaxing walks. at night the towers are beautifully lit creating a stunning backdrop that captures the spirit of the city. visiting the towers is a memorable experience. the skybridge on the 41st and 42nd floors and the observation deck on the 86th floor provide breathtaking views of the kuala lumpur skyline. tickets can sell out quickly so it s highly recommended to book online in advance for a smoother experience. the base of the towers houses suria klcc mall which offers a wide range of international brands restaurants and even an art gallery and aquarium making it a convenient stop for shopping and entertainment. while the towers can get crowded especially on weekends and evenings the visit is worth it for the architectural beauty panoramic views and cultural significance. whether you admire them from the park below or head up to the top the petronas twin towers are an unforgettable highlight of kuala lumpur.

visiting the petronas twin towers was an unforgettable experience. it is truly one of the most beautiful landmarks in malaysia and definitely worth seeing both during the day and at night. the architecture is stunning and the night lighting makes the view even more breathtaking. the skybridge and observation deck offer incredible panoramic views of kuala lumpur. the tour is well organized and the staff are friendly and informative. if you are in kl this iconic landmark is a must visit. a wonderful experience from start to finish.

the petronas twin towers in kuala lumpur are truly stunning and an iconic sight. when i visited the towers looked almost like toy buildings from a distance their sleek design standing out against the night sky. the intense light coming from the towers at night was mesmerizing with beams shooting straight up into the sky from the top creating a spectacular view. however be prepared when planning your visit. the tickets are a bit on the expensive side and the cost can vary depending on the activities you choose such as visiting the skybridge or the observation deck. when i went there was a long line for ticket purchases and unfortunately the tickets for that day were already sold out. people in line were buying tickets for the next day so it s crucial to plan ahead. it s also important to note that while tickets are available online they can sell out just as quickly as they do at the counter. if you re set on visiting the towers i highly recommend booking your tickets well in advance to avoid disappointment. overall the petronas twin towers are a must see especially at night when they re illuminated. but keep in mind the high demand for tickets and plan your visit accordingly to make sure you don t miss out on this incredible experience.

if i could give 10 stars i would but google only allows 5 so i ll just emotionally give the other 5 myself. petronas twin towers is truly one of the few places in the world where you can feel small amazed and slightly broke at the same time. the moment i walked out from the lrt station and looked up i swear my neck cracked louder than my phone s ringtone. these towers are so tall that even my problems felt tiny for a moment which is a miracle because my problems are usually taller than me. the towers look absolutely stunning during the day but at night? different level. the moment the lights turned on i genuinely felt like the towers were telling me your life may be messy but at least we look good. the glow is so bright it almost makes me feel like my future might actually work out. almost. klcc park is amazing too peaceful green and full of people pretending to exercise but actually just scrolling tiktok. the fountain show? 10 10. i stood there like a tourist even though i ve lived in malaysia my whole life. the water went up the lights went on and suddenly i forgot all my stress. if someone proposed to me right there i might accidentally say yes. suria klcc mall is also top tier. i walked in planning to just look around but somehow ended up touching things i definitely cannot afford. even the escalator feels more expensive than my entire salary. the aircond inside is colder than my ex s heart refreshing though. the whole place looks like the kind of mall where you automatically straighten your posture because you don t want to look poor. the skybridge and observation deck were amazing the view is so beautiful it made me question every life decision that kept me on the ground floor. from up there kl looks so peaceful that for a moment i forgot about traffic jams inflation and whether i left the rice cooker on. overall petronas twin towers is a 100 must visit whether you re a tourist a malaysian a bored student or someone pretending to be productive. the towers never get old. every visit feels like the first time.

worth having a look for sure very impressive and stunning. however the 250rm to buy a photo of you that is photoshoped with the tower behind you this is crazy. they print it and if you don t buy it it s probably going in the bins so much waste. so try to avoid the photo at the start if you re not ready to spend 250rm to buy it.
iconic building. very pretty lit up at night. lots of good spots for a pictures. short walk is the saloma bridge which also is pretty at night.

an iconic landmark and definitely a must see when visiting kuala lumpur! the towers are absolutely stunning the architecture symmetry and the night lighting are breathtaking up close. the design and engineering are truly impressive and it s easy to see why this building has become the city s symbol. there was a bit of a wait to get in and i expected the skybridge on the 41st 42nd floors to have a glass floor or transparent section which was a little disappointing since it s not see through. still it s fascinating to stand there between the two towers and look out over the city. the observation deck on the 86th floor offers amazing views of kuala lumpur especially at sunset or after dark when the skyline lights up. however it would have been nice if there was a small caf or bar to enjoy the views a bit longer as the visit feels quite structured and time limited. overall the petronas twin towers are a remarkable feat of design and architecture absolutely worth visiting once. just don t expect a lounge style experience it s more of a quick but memorable sightseeing stop. with love k iam.krisztina

the petronas twin towers soaring 452 meters above kuala lumpur s skyline are a testament to malaysia s ambition and architectural prowess. these 88 story skyscrapers were completed in 1998 and held the title of the world s tallest buildings until 2004. they remain the tallest twin towers globally symbolizing the nation s rapid development and modernization. the towers design incorporates islamic geometric principles reflecting malaysia s cultural heritage. the floor plan is based on a rub el hizb an islamic symbol consisting of two overlapping squares creating an eight pointed star. this design is both aesthetically pleasing and structurally efficient. constructed primarily of reinforced concrete with a steel and glass fa ade the towers exhibit a sleek modern appearance that gleams under the tropical sun. connecting the two towers is a double decker skybridge on the 41st and 42nd floors situated 170 meters above ground. this bridge offers visitors a unique vantage point of the city and serves as an emergency evacuation route between the towers. above the 86th floor observation deck provides panoramic views of kuala lumpur showcasing landmarks like the kuala lumpur tower and the sprawling cityscape. high speed elevators whisk visitors to these heights enhancing the experience. the petronas twin towers are more than just an architectural feat they offer a comprehensive visitor experience. at the base lies suria klcc a premier shopping mall featuring international brands dining options and entertainment venues. adjacent to the towers is the klcc park a beautifully landscaped area with walking paths a fountain with light shows and a children s playground providing a serene escape in the city s heart. located in the kuala lumpur city centre the towers are easily accessible via public transportation including the kelana jaya lrt line. tickets for the skybridge and observation deck can be purchased online or at the ticketing counter though advance booking is recommended due to high demand. visiting hours typically run from morning until evening with the last entry around 6 15 pm. it s advisable to check the official website for the most current information on operating hours and ticket prices. visiting the petronas twin towers is an essential experience when in kuala lumpur. their striking design cultural significance and the breathtaking views they offer make them a highlight of the city. whether you re an architecture enthusiast a photographer or a traveler seeking to understand malaysia s modern identity the towers provide a memorable and enriching experience.

okay so this was one of the most amazing experiences i ve ever had!!! the views were breathtaking and the vibes were so peaceful and quiet. i have a fear of heights and yet i felt totally safe! the splattered informations about the towers were so easy and fun to take a great way for passive learning. looking at how the city and the people looked from 400 meters above the ground was something magical and i genuinely hope everyone can experience this at least once in their lifetime ! i do wish the gift shop was a little less expensive though but still very good moments spent there.

the petronas twin towers are truly a sight to behold and an undeniable highlight of kuala lumpur. towering majestically over the city the structures are not only an architectural marvel but also a symbol of malaysia s modern spirit. our visit there was nothing short of outstanding. the views were spectacular the surroundings immaculately maintained and the overall ambiance made for a memorable outing. the evening light show in the adjacent fountain area was particularly impressive. with vibrant colors synchronized water movements and music blending into the night air it created a magical experience that visitors of all ages can enjoy. families solo travelers children everyone seemed to be caught up in the charm of the moment. that said there was one aspect that left us a little surprised. the restrooms near the towers required a payment of 2 ringgit to access. while we understand that maintenance has its costs this felt unnecessary for such a globally recognized public attraction. what made it worse was witnessing a woman with a small child being denied entry simply because she didn t have the fee. it was a disheartening sight especially in a place that otherwise feels so welcoming. such basic facilities should ideally be free or at least accessible without hassle particularly for families and young children. it s a small thing that can make a big difference to someone s experience. still the grandeur of the petronas towers remains unforgettable. it s a must visit landmark and with a little attention to visitor welfare it can truly offer a flawless experience.

fantastic visit to the tower it was very well managed far better than bhurj khalifa in dubai. hardly any wait time very well managed staff and planning to take us to the top of the tower. overall fantastic experience.

the fee for the walkthrough at the sky bridge is rm35 for malaysians which is acceptable. the view is spectacular. the guide is good. there is also a park which is free. not to mention there is a tiny water park i would say. theres also mall below.

the petronas twin towers are truly a breathtaking masterpiece. i first saw them eight years ago and since then i ve visited the observation deck and skybridge four times. no matter how many times i go the views from these spots never lose their charm. the staff are always welcoming and helpful whether you re a local or an expat. i purchased my ticket online directly from their website for rm80 which is the standard rate for foreigners. however you might find cheaper options through platforms like klook or other third party sites. locals of course enjoy discounted rates. inside the towers suria klcc shopping mall offers a wide variety of stores. one of the highlights of the area is the klcc lake symphony light and sound water fountain show which takes place daily at 8 pm 9 pm and 10 pm. i watched it with my family while enjoying a delicious sandwich at a restaurant right in front of the show. the complex also features an aquarium making it an even more exciting destination for visitors.

you will have an amazing experience when you are on top of this two towers and viewing the city. i was even amazed by just watching on it from my hotel room. they ve said that it s just blink of an eye you will be on too. but we re late as fully booked. malaysia is safe and clean place just be respectful. you need to book in advance as it is fully booked easily a fee will apply.

i first came to the petronas towers in 2005 when i was just 6 years old. i still remember the excitement i felt seeing the towering structure for the first time. the petronas twin towers seemed so majestic and surreal to me back then almost like something out of a dream. at that young age i could never have imagined how many times i would return to this iconic landmark throughout my life. over the years i ve visited the petronas twin towers many times traveling hundreds of kilometers all the way from singapore just to stand in awe beneath them. each visit has been unique with new experiences new memories and more appreciation for the beauty and design of this architectural marvel. whether i was admiring the towers from the observation deck or walking around the surrounding area each trip was special in its own way. as i grew older my connection to the towers deepened. it became more than just a tourist spot it became a symbol of my love for travel discovery and the memories i ve created. today as i reflect on all my visits to the petronas towers i realize how much it means to me. it s a place filled with fond memories and every time i return i find myself more in awe of its magnificence. i will definitely come again for sure. thank you petronas twin towers for being a constant source of wonder and joy in my life. stay beautiful as always and may you continue to shine brightly for generations to come.

recently i had the incredible experience of visiting the iconic petronas twin towers in kuala lumpur. standing tall as the world s tallest twin towers they are an architectural marvel and a symbol of malaysia s modernity. the view from the top was absolutely breathtaking offering a stunning panorama of the city. walking across the skybridge and exploring the observation deck made the visit even more unforgettable. truly a must see destination for anyone visiting kuala lumpur!

a must visit for any kuala lumpur visitor the petronas twin towers boast the most incredible views over the city and beyond. i booked tickets to go up to the viewing platform at the top. the entire experience was very well run but tickets are very limited as group sizes are limited to around 30 people per timeslot. the small tour groups were a big plus however allowing ample space to enjoy the panoramic vista. our first stop the skybridge offered a pleasant view and a cool introduction to the tour. however the truly jaw dropping vistas awaited at the top. we were fortunate to witness a sunset while at the top a truly spectacular sight. gazing down over 1 000 feet below truly conveyed the immense scale of these buildings. there were lots of interesting displays and displays at the top giving you some more in depth details and history of the building. overall it was a solid 5 star experience. the views were awe inspiring and it s got to be one of the most iconic buildings in all of asia. just remember to book your tickets ahead of time!

the concourse level feels more like a high end airport terminal than a mall basement. it is sleek air conditioned and very professional. the check in you ll find the ticketing counters and digital kiosks here. even if you have an e ticket you usually need to show it here to get your physical lanyard. security storage it s very strict. you ll go through a metal detector and large bags backpacks must be left at the free luggage storage counter. the briefing before the elevators you re often shown a cool holographic display about the towers construction. it s a great way to kill the 10 minutes while you wait for your group s color to be called.

the petronas twin towers in kuala lumpur malaysia stand as a testament to modern engineering and architectural excellence. at 451.9 meters tall they were the world s tallest buildings from 1998 to 2004 and remain the tallest twin towers globally. pros architectural marvel designed by argentine architect c sar pelli the towers feature a distinctive islamic inspired design symbolizing unity and progress. skybridge experience visitors can access the skybridge on the 42nd floor connecting the two towers. this bridge offers unique views of the city and is a highlight of the tour. observation deck the 86th floor observation deck provides panoramic views of kuala lumpur s skyline including landmarks like the kl tower and merdeka 118. cons ticket availability due to high demand tickets for the skybridge and observation deck are limited and often sell out quickly. it s advisable to book tickets well in advance to secure your preferred time slot. time constraints the guided tour lasts approximately 45 minutes which some visitors feel is brief given the cost and the experience. crowdedness the popularity of the towers means they can become crowded especially during peak tourist seasons potentially affecting the overall experience. tips for visitors advance booking purchase tickets online ahead of your visit to ensure availability and to choose a convenient time slot. optimal timing for the best views consider visiting during the late afternoon to catch the sunset and see the city transition into night. dress code as a place of business visitors are expected to dress modestly. in summary the petronas twin towers offer a unique opportunity to experience kuala lumpur from a remarkable vantage point. despite some logistical considerations the experience is widely regarded as a highlight of any visit to the city.

the petronas twin towers located in the heart of kuala lumpur malaysia are iconic symbols of modern architecture and engineering. standing at 452 meters they were the tallest buildings in the world from 1998 to 2004 and remain the tallest twin towers globally. the towers are connected by a skybridge on the 41st and 42nd floors offering stunning views of the city skyline. places to visit and attractions around petronas twin towers 1. skybridge and observation deck experience breathtaking views from the skybridge and the observation deck on the 86th floor. 2. suria klcc mall a luxury shopping destination with high end brands restaurants and an art gallery. 3. klcc park a beautifully landscaped park with a jogging track fountains and a playground. 4. petrosains discovery centre an interactive science museum located within suria klcc ideal for families. 5. aquaria klcc a world class oceanarium featuring marine life and interactive exhibits. 6. lake symphony a stunning fountain show with music and lights held in the evening at klcc park. 7. kuala lumpur convention centre klcc hosts various events exhibitions and conferences throughout the year. best time to visit months december to february or may to july for pleasant weather. time visit in the evening for the illuminated towers and lake symphony fountain show. entry fees skybridge and observation deck around rm 80 for adults and rm 30 for children. tickets are limited so booking in advance is recommended. tips for travelers arrive early to secure tickets for the skybridge and observation deck as they sell out quickly. visit the towers in the evening for a spectacular view of the towers lit up against the night sky. allocate time to explore the surrounding attractions like klcc park and aquaria klcc. wear comfortable shoes for walking around the area.

visiting the iconic petronas towers in kuala lumpur malaysia is an experience that combines breathtaking architecture with the vibrant energy of an international tourist hub. the towers themselves are nothing short of magnificent standing tall against the kuala lumpur skyline and serving as a symbol of the city s modernity. whether you re seeing them during the day or lit up beautifully at night the towers are truly a sight to behold. one of the best things about the petronas towers is the diversity of people you ll encounter. it s a place where tourists from all over the world gather making it a truly global destination. as you stand at the base you ll hear languages from different countries see people of various nationalities and experience the mix of cultures that kuala lumpur is famous for. it gives you a sense of how globally recognized this landmark is and how it brings people together. another positive aspect is that the area around the towers is filled with excellent photo opportunities. whether it s from the ground looking up or from various points in the surrounding klcc park you can take stunning photos with the towers in the background. the sleek reflective design of the buildings is perfect for capturing that postcard worthy shot. you ll definitely want to have your camera or smartphone ready as every angle of the towers seems instagram worthy. however there are a few drawbacks to visiting the petronas towers. one of the most noticeable issues is the crowd. given its status as one of the most famous landmarks in malaysia it s not surprising that it s always packed with visitors. while the hustle and bustle do add to the atmosphere in some ways it can also make the experience a bit overwhelming. if you re someone who prefers quieter spots for sightseeing the sheer number of people at the towers might detract from your enjoyment. another disappointment was the unavailability of tickets to go up to the observation deck. while we were excited to see kuala lumpur from one of the towers sky bridges we were told that tickets were sold out for the day. this is something you should be prepared for if you plan to visit as tickets are limited and can sell out quickly. it would be great if there were more availability or at least a better system for reserving tickets in advance as it seems to be a common issue for many tourists. that said even if you don t manage to get tickets to the deck there is still plenty to enjoy on the ground. the klcc park located right next to the towers is a lovely place to stroll and you get excellent views of the towers from the park s lake area. additionally the nearby shopping mall suria klcc offers a range of high end stores restaurants and entertainment options making it easy to spend a whole day in the area. in summary the petronas towers are a must visit for anyone traveling to kuala lumpur. the architecture is stunning and the atmosphere is lively and diverse with tourists from all over the world. however the crowds can be overwhelming and securing a ticket to climb the deck might require some advance planning. even with these small downsides the towers are an essential part of the kuala lumpur experience and it s well worth a visit for the views photos and the overall global vibe.

the petronas twin towers soaring above the vibrant skyline of kuala lumpur malaysia are an iconic symbol of modern architecture and engineering marvel. standing at an impressive height of 451.9 meters these twin skyscrapers were once the tallest buildings in the world and remain a must see destination for tourists. the sleek stainless steel fa ade inspired by islamic art and design reflects both malaysia s rich heritage and its vision for the future. visitors can take an exhilarating trip up to the skybridge which connects the two towers at the 41st and 42nd floors offering panoramic views of the bustling city below and the sprawling landscapes beyond. by night the towers light up the skyline creating a dazzling spectacle against the backdrop of kuala lumpur s energy. whether you re a lover of architecture culture or simply seeking a breathtaking experience the petronas twin towers provide a mesmerizing encounter that captures the heart and soul of malaysia s capital.

visiting the petronas twin towers in kuala lumpur was an absolute highlight of our trip to malaysia. during the day the towers stand tall and majestic offering breathtaking views of the cityscape below. the intricate design and sheer height of the towers are truly awe inspiring. at night the towers transform into a mesmerizing spectacle illuminated by thousands of lights that showcase their architectural brilliance. the sight of the towers against the night sky is simply magical and is not to be missed. beyond their stunning aesthetics the petronas twin towers hold great significance for malaysia. they symbolize the country s economic growth and prosperity serving as a testament to malaysia s modernization and development. additionally the towers house a variety of businesses including offices shopping malls and a concert hall making them a hub of activity and commerce. overall our visit to the petronas twin towers was a memorable experience that exceeded our expectations. whether you re interested in architecture city views or simply want to witness an iconic landmark the towers are a must visit destination in malaysia.

this review is for the kl tower tour which takes you to top. i have mixed feelings for the visit. it was ok on some aspects but not worth the money which was spent. as a non malaysian had to pay rm98 per person. that is for going to the 45th floor where the bridge is and to go to the top most floor which 86th. thats it. on the 45th floor they give you 10 minutes at the conmecting bridges and on 86th floor 15 minutes. you get a view of kl from 86th floor with all the skyscrappers and all but it is not worth what was spent. the windows were not clean so could not take that much of cool photos with landscape of kl. you only get to see 1 tower when you go up obviously. maybe to tick off your bucket list of places and you are not worried about spending then go for it. also souveniors are as usual with all other places. they take your photo at ground level edit it with both towers on background let you select a package of rm150 for soft and a 1 photo album. in my opinion you can spend none and take the same shot outside from klcc park bridge non edited. overall if you are into looking at scyscrappers this is the place for you. or else there is nothing much this attraction offers other than paying to be taken up floors. but if you are to visit then buy tickets online in advance cause it is limited slots per day.

petronas towers iconic marvels of kuala lumpur s skyline standing as gleaming sentinels amidst kuala lumpur s urban landscape the petronas towers command attention with their towering presence and architectural grandeur. my recent visit to these iconic landmarks left me spellbound as i marveled at their sheer magnificence and the unparalleled views they offer of the city below. as i approached the petronas towers i was immediately struck by their sleek modern design characterized by gleaming steel and glass fa ades that reflect the vibrant energy of malaysia s capital. the symmetrical beauty of the twin towers symbolizes the nation s aspirations for progress and prosperity serving as a beacon of pride for malaysians and a testament to their ingenuity and ambition. ascending to the observation deck on the 86th floor i was treated to breathtaking panoramas of kuala lumpur s skyline stretching out before me. from the bustling streets below to the verdant parks and towering skyscrapers the view offered a captivating glimpse into the dynamic spirit of the city. the petronas towers also house a wealth of world class amenities and attractions including luxury boutiques fine dining restaurants and cultural exhibitions. visitors can embark on a shopping spree at suria klcc or immerse themselves in the arts at the petronas art gallery. one of the highlights of my visit was witnessing the towers come to life at night as they are illuminated by a mesmerizing display of lights and colors. against the backdrop of the night sky the petronas towers exude an otherworldly beauty that captivates the imagination and leaves a lasting impression on all who behold them. in conclusion the petronas towers are not just architectural marvels but also cultural icons that embody the spirit of kuala lumpur. whether you re a history buff an architecture enthusiast or simply seeking awe inspiring views a visit to these iconic landmarks is an experience not to be missed.

wow beautiful iconics towers dominate the landscape of kuala lumpur. the petronas towers malay menara berkembar petronas also known as the petronas twin towers and colloquially the klcc twin towers are an interlinked pair of 88 storey supertall skyscrapers in kuala lumpur malaysia standing at 451.9 metres 1 483 feet . from 1996 to 2004 they were the tallest buildings in the world until they were surpassed by the taipei 101 building. the petronas towers remain the world s tallest twin skyscrapers surpassing the original world trade center towers in new york city and were the tallest buildings in malaysia until 2019 when they were surpassed by the exchange 106. the petronas towers are a major landmark of kuala lumpur along with the nearby kuala lumpur tower and merdeka 118 and are visible in many places across the city. they house the malaysian petroleum mega company petronas offices of several companies apartments and a huge shopping mall. located in central klcc the petronas towers are easy to access from anywhere in kl. mrt lrt and buses have stations nearby. free to enter the mall. there is a beautiful park located directly behind these towers. handicap doable and recommended. restrooms located everywhere in the mall. loads of shopping. final take....highly recommended for anyone traveling to kl!!!!!. go and be prepared to be impressed. bring camera or phone.

well ... they look very high when looking from the ground that s a fact. a masterpiece of architecture and construction art congratulations. i do not understand why people pay so high price for the ticket to watch kuala lumpur from those towers. and still often tickets are insufficient to satisfy all who want to visit. in my opinion it is worth to take a walk to see the towers from outside.
its ia really good place to go with family especially to those who loves ootd.

amazing iconic twin towers must visit when you re in kl. beautiful observatory. very clean. the restaurant located here has special options to dress in traditional clothing then eat at a heated table overseeing the sky view. they offer other food that is tasty and can be taken to seats still overlooking the observation view.
the ticket price is expensive but the view is breath taking. i booked via klook and scanned at the entrance. the observation deck time is limited to 15 mins.

petronas twin towers is truly amazing. these iconic twin towers in kuala lumpur are one of the most beautiful buildings in the world i have ever seen in my life. your visit to malaysia is not worth it if you don t visit this building and see it in your own eyes . to satisfy myself my wife and i visited this place twice during our vacation.

finally visited the towers and they re even more impressive in real life! went there in the evening and the lights made everything look magical. took a bunch of photos and just enjoyed the atmosphere. one of those places that really makes you feel like you re in kuala lumpur!
spectacular and honestly a must visit place
the petronas twin towers are truly breathtaking! i was astonished by their towering height and stunning architecture. a must visit landmark in kuala lumpur!

it was an amazing experience! the architecture is stunning and the view from the skybridge is breathtaking. it s a peaceful place to walk around especially at night when the towers are beautifully lit. a great spot to spend time and capture some memorable photos!

very nice twin towers! amazing sky scrapers! it s like 1 huge stainless steel erected at a high altitude. it s amazing really. close to many people hotels and malls. we totally enjoyed this place. recommended to visit if your coming to kl. this is 1 iconic tower! salute kl

it was bustling with visitors and had christmas celebrations going on. the decor was nice with a group choir performing but the lack of christmas discounts didn t make sense. petrosains was full so we couldn t get in making the overall experience quite average. also make sure to get your photos taken by the professionals outside but don t forget to haggle a bit they charge 20 ringgit for one photo and 150 ringgit for all the pictures.

a must see in kuala lumpur. very impressive inside and outside. adjacent to klcc park. visit the park. go to the mall inside the petronas twin towers to escape the heat. high end stores and great options to eat inside and outside. use to be the tallest buildings in the world now just the 3rd tallest in kl. kl loves malls and tall buildings. visit both at petronas twin towers.

the towers are okay but have lost any standing as far as world s tallest towers or buildings are concerned. there are always lengthy white times for getting in so you have to book ahead if you want to go up to the bridge joining the two towers and if you do that at night time it is poorly designed in the sense that there s way too much reflection on the glass so you can t get good photos of views from the bridge. the area surrounding the towers and the shopping mall is well maintained and have kept for tourists and you only need to venture a short way from there to get to see some very slummy parts of kuala lumpur
iconic landmark of malaysia. the towers are stunning especially at night. clean surroundings great photo spots and a must visit if you re in kuala lumpur.
nice place to see all the movies here are nostalgic. cool

i recently visited the petronas twin towers and it was a fantastic experience. we booked our tickets in advance which made the process smooth and hassle free. the entire visit was well polished and exceptionally well run. the views were absolutely amazing and the observation areas were not overcrowded. we had plenty of space to enjoy the sights. we visited both the top of the towers and the sky bridge and the panoramic view of the entire city was breathtaking. we chose to go at 7 pm which was perfect for catching the sunset. the whole experience lasted around 45 minutes giving us ample time to take in the stunning scenery. overall it was a memorable visit and highly recommended for anyone visiting kuala lumpur.
packed with tourists. found out that the best location to take the full length twin tower photo is across the road towards saloma bridge. did not go to the sky lounge

petronas towers is one of the most iconic landmarks in kuala lumpur and it s easy to see why. i had the opportunity to visit the towers during my recent trip to kuala lumpur and it was definitely a highlight of my trip. the tour of the towers takes visitors up to the skybridge a walkway that connects the two towers at the 41st floor but it is charged about 45 ringitts and we did not get a chance to try it. from the top you can take in panoramic views of the city including famous landmarks like the kl tower and the batu caves. everything from the marble floors to the intricate lighting fixtures was perfectly crafted creating an immersive and unforgettable experience. i highly recommend a visit to the petronas towers. whether you re a tourist looking for a unique and unforgettable experience or a local resident looking to appreciate the beauty of your city the towers are a must see attraction.

most amazing place in kl. superb structure and a must visit for all the tourist. it is not only a very tall but also a very huge structure. big mall and shopping centre at the entry point and lots of other attractions in and around. purchase your ticket in advance otherwise you may not get it if you are in kl and planning to visit the place better you go in the morning and purchase the tickets. visit to the structure is just wonderful. view from the top is worth seeing. see the pics in and around petronas tower. kl tower is also very near and observation deck of this tower is superb.

i suggest to go early morning because it becomes crowded after 11 o clock. road side you will find people with cameras setup to click pictures for 10 rm . it was hot and humid and crowds making it more uncomfortable.

it s good if you have been to other towers then no point wasting money to go up you can click pictures from below and enjoy the mall. but if you decide to go up it s nice you should definitely have some tea or coffee at the cafe on 83rd floor
a must visit if you are in kl it s an ikonic stop.
must see place in kl. you can take lots of pictures of the towers just walking around the city center seems like no matter where you are this building will appear.
an iconic landmark of kuala lumpur. the view is stunning especially at night when the towers light up beautifully.

petronas towers! one of the most iconic towers you d come across. while the street leading to them is quite busy you ll surely enjoy the process. i d suggest that you book tickets in advance as during the season we visited malaysia during the chinese new year it s difficult to get tickets. you ll be taken to two different floors from where you can explore the city and the whole experience is mesmerizing. capture every single moment in your mind. it s worth the hype and wait. the staff is extremely passionate and sweet. they ll guide you at every step. once you are done seeing the building you ll find a shop souvenirs chocolates t shirts etc . there s a cafe as well. if you are visiting malaysia make sure to visit petronas at least once. it s beautiful amazingly built and you should try and see it from outside both in the morning as well at night. it s beautifully lit and looks classic. don t forget to read the history of the tallest twin towers!!
absolutely breathtaking structure. i visited on a weekend with my family. the fountain show was great too and free!

amazing place don t miss the sky bridge and top level experience. please be early to ensure ticket availability. we arrived in the middle of pendamic so not many visitors this allow for a comfortable trip. view is amazing advise to do some map study this will make the view at 86th floor more enjoyable.

we brought a visit to the towers around 7.00pm. the group was quite small because of the size of the elevator. we first got up to the skybridge. we got plenty of time to enjoy the view and to take pictures. then we got up to level 83. here we had 15 minutes. it was well organised by coloured stickers and it wasn t too crowded. i m not sure if this is normal. we enjoyed nd got beautiful pictures during sunset!
the place has great views of kuala lumpur both day and night. great spot for photoshoots

petronas twin towers is heart of kuala lumpur city easy to get there. crowdy place both of tourist and local people. it s one of malaysia landmark must to come. malaysian people aslo nice and friendly

the highlight of kl at night which is why kl is also affectionately called the city of lights. a wonder in its own right one can only admire the sheer beauty and elegance of these twin towers as they light up from top to bottom. you cannot simply miss a photo opportunity be it under the towers or in the observation deck right at the pinnacle.

i recommend coming to see it at night. it s incredibly beautiful. there are many people who will offer to take photos for you. it costs 10 malaysian ringgit for one photo. they ll take several shots for you to choose from. once you ve made your decision they ll immediately transfer the photo to you via iphone s airdrop. not sure what happens if you don t have an iphone

this is the iconic petronas twin towers the pride of kuala lumpur and still the tallest twin towers in the world standing as the tallest building in malaysia for many years. the architecture is absolutely stunning. covered with sleek stainless steel and glass the towers shine under the sunlight during the day and glow beautifully at night. the structure is not just tall but bold and powerful with a design that reflects both modern strength and malaysian identity. right below these majestic towers is suria klcc a massive and vibrant shopping mall that s always full of life. whether you re into luxury fashion local brands or just looking to grab a nice meal suria klcc has it all. the mall is well designed spacious and air conditioned making it a perfect place to escape the heat and enjoy some retail therapy. you ll also find a cinema art gallery science center and a beautiful park with a fountain show right outside. the whole area feels like the heartbeat of kl. it s perfect for locals tourists families and anyone who just wants to soak in the city s best. whether you re standing beneath the towers or walking through suria klcc the experience is unforgettable. klcc really gives you that world class city vibe with a unique malaysian touch
a place that makes all malaysians proud. beautiful surroundings jogging tracks for recreation and a shopping mall for shopping. very convenient with direct lrt access.

these towers are amazing in structure. you can easily purchase tickets to the observation deck and get a glimpse of the city from above too. it s connected to a mall so plenty of shopping afterwards too. looks more beautiful specially in night.

beautiful towers to mesmerized during sunset. there was a lot of people trying to take a picture. also a lot of cameramen trying to offer some photography session. recommend to go afternoon so it s not too hot and bright.

very high 87 floors highest twin towers in the world has a bridge in lower floors between the towers to walk through. guided tours for towers very informative. security check on entrance to towers. need to book online or at tower ahead of time as sells out fast. stunning views of the city. has working offices and shops below.
was very lucky to see it on new year s eve all lit up! amazing place and good shops inside too! especially marks and spencer s

recommended it was amazing experience to visit petronas twin towers a beautiful place with amazing things there was no entry tickets and i didn t wait for entering all brands available and many restaurants and juice bar. everything is available here must visit this place

the surrounding greenery and fountains are quite refreshing. spending 85 myr just to go on top and take pictures and see lights does not worth much. i would suggest to avoid buying the ticket and spend the money somewhere else. though the towers looks amazing from outside its an icon in kl.

you will simply be amazed by the grandeur of this building. it looks absolutely amazing specially the night view. it s called by the names of klcc and petronas twin towers. around 8 pm everyday there a water and fountain show happers at the klcc park worth seeing once while enjoying the night view of this grand building
seeing the petronas twin towers in person was impressive. i went at night and the lights made the towers look even more beautiful. the area around klcc felt clean and lively.
have you even visited kuala lumpur if you havent been here?! one of the major tourist spot in kl that you shouldn t miss. best to see at night with the lights. really mesmerizing

one of the tallest twin towers in malaysia. their have shopping centers and you can visit in tower tour. don t bring heavy items they aren t allowed it. please book online tickets from their official web site and take a print outs.

i find it better than burj khalifa only for the reason that such a marvel was built in 1991 when only us was boasting skyscrapers. just don t fall for the scam of getting clicked and then acquiring a photoshopped picture with petronas in the background for 50 ringgits

genting skyworlds theme park is an absolute blast! i visited on a public holiday and barely waited to enter which was fantastic. i d definitely recommend buying tickets in advance for a seamless experience. every ride was thrilling and the atmosphere was vibrant. can t wait to go back!
had an amazing experience at genting skyworlds! the park is beautifully themed clean and full of exciting rides for both thrill seekers and families.

this place is cool. an affordable way to spend a day outdoor. we visited on a sunday and it is not too crowded. we also book our tickets in advance via klook app. the park is general is clean and organize. you could easily find places to eat and use the washrooms. the rides are pretty cool too from thrill rides to kids rides. since we visited on a sunday the queues are not too long. you can even ride the second time if you want to. overall this place is nice to visit with friends and family.

fun theme park only a few thrill rides tho. great theming great location it s on a hill so it won t be hot but prepare for rain and fog. careful on the ice age coaster as it s one of the most rough coaster ive been on. such a shame that the sfx coaster is cancelled and mad ramp is still in progress for some reason beware to buy the tickets online one day before visit because you can t buy it online the same day.

clean nice theme park. not the biggest but its fun. if you have vip tickets there will be a guide bringing you around. food wise not fantastic. you will get 10rm pax food voucher but you can only use at certain outlets you need to download the skypark app. they are cashless so you will need to be make payment by every other methods of cos not including cheque! hahaha felt rhe staff can be more friendly and more customer oriented and more informative... overall a good place to spent a good 4 5 hours depending on which day you are going getting online tickets are highly recommended as its cheaper than walk in and getting at the counter... but you need to get it a day before for online tickets. cos i don know their logic of not being able to get it on the day itself

island best entertainment spot with lots of fun activities and enjoying moments. photo point are very special here to have memory. best part is to get a ride in rope car with bottom glass car. wonderful experience.

honestly i love this theme park because it s not over crowded. even during christmas season there were activities here and there. the overall vibe was on point compared to the normal non festive days which are usually quiet with minimal park music. the rides here are also surprisingly good though the staffs could use a better smile on their faces instead of looking bored.

i visited skyworlds genting on a friday morning and overall it was a really fun experience. there was plenty of paid parking available which made it convenient to find a spot without too much hassle. upon entering i recommend getting a locker to store your belongings it s much easier to enjoy the rides without carrying bags around. some rides require you to wear a raincoat so it s best to either bring your own or buy one on site. it started raining in the evening when i went but the staff handled everything well and most of the attractions resumed once the rain slowed down. despite the weather the overall experience was still enjoyable and lively. the theme park itself is well maintained with a good variety of rides suitable for both thrill seekers and families. i especially liked how organized everything was clear signboards friendly staff and clean facilities throughout. the food options were also decent i tried the hotdog which was surprisingly big and tasty definitely worth trying if you need a quick snack between rides. the atmosphere was cheerful and the park layout made it easy to move from one attraction to another without feeling too crowded. it s a great place for a short getaway especially if you re spending the day in genting. overall despite the rain later in the day i had a great time. skyworlds genting offers a good balance of fun rides photo spots and comfort i d recommend visiting earlier in the morning to make the most of your day and avoid the bigger crowds later on.

we were lucky to get our tickets on the same day without any queue which made for a smooth start. the first couple of rides had very short waits less than 10 minutes so we were off to a great start. however by the time we reached the third ride the waiting time had increased to about an hour and we weren t able to go on all the rides. by the sixth ride it was almost closing time. i feel the park could consider longer hours to accommodate the crowd as crowd control seemed a bit underprepared. that said all the rides we did manage to take were really fun making it an overall enjoyable experience.

the best theme park in malaysia! we visited on a weekday so there was no need to queue for the rides. the park has plenty of food options at affordable prices and there are many family friendly rides suitable for all ages. i purchased the tickets from klook and there was no need to redeem them at the counter we just went straight to the entrance and scanned the tickets. overall it was a smooth and enjoyable experience!

had an amazing experience at genting skyworlds! the park is beautifully themed clean and full of exciting rides for both thrill seekers and families. pro tip 1 buy your tickets in advance. it saves time at the entrance and sometimes you get better deals online. we went straight in without queueing at the counter totally worth it. pro tip 2 do the major thrill rides early! by afternoon queues get much longer. we prioritised independence day defiance planet of the apes terraform tower challenge acorn adventure best decision ever. morning queues were manageable but by 2 3 pm they were significantly longer. pro tip 3 bring a raincoat or poncho. especially if you re riding epic voyage to moonhaven you will get wet it s super fun but be prepared or save it for the end of the day. overall fantastic atmosphere immersive rides and great mountain weather. definitely recommend coming early planning your ride order and making a full day out of it. would absolutely come back again!

visited on dec 23rd 2025. we arrived around 11 00 am which felt a bit late given the holiday crowds. while the park is smaller compared to universal studios or legoland johor the festive holiday atmosphere was fantastic and really set a great mood. key tips for visitors height matters to get your money s worth make sure your kids are at least 110cm. many of the best rides have this height requirement. the app is essential use the genting skyworlds app to reserve your rides vq but keep in mind you can only reserve 3 rides at a time. queue times for popular rides can be very long otherwise. value for money we caught a promo price of rm65 pax which was incredible value. however at the full original price it might feel a bit expensive given the park s compact size. overall a great mix of adult thrills and kid friendly attractions. definitely worth a visit if you can find a good ticket deal

wonderful theme park experience! there are many games and attractions that are great for the whole family and there are lots of beautiful spots for taking pictures. i really recommend the independence day virtual reality show it was very realistic and impressive. we visited in december and the temperature was around 16 c which felt quite cold so wearing sweaters is definitely recommended. overall a fun and memorable place highly recommended!

skyworlds theme park is amazing out of the world. the rides are very exciting and fearful. people have weak heart don t ride. amazing photography place everywhere. one secret tips you will get 50 discounted tickets beside the ticket booking machine. just go there and find for people. otherwise you have to spend 198rm per person. if you buy ticket all the rides are included. so enjoy!

overall it is a great theme park and too bad fox studio didn t manage to get in and end up with own genting branding but hope management keep up and maintain the standard. i believe it is a great theme park in malaysia happening at a cold chill weather. the early advance ticket promotion and also the apps for virtual queue is a great idea and good management if use wisely. there are great thrill rides for the elder one and also some nice easy ride for younger kids. overall it is a great theme park to have a great time. my best 4 thril ride with heart dropping experience 1 terraform tower challenge 2 alpha fighter ride 3 esd global defender 4 rivet town roller keep it up skyworld!!! p s there is parade show and also stage show. do take note on their schedule and check with information counter.

genting highlands theme park absolutely amazing! i had an unforgettable experience at genting highlands theme park! the rides were thrilling the atmosphere was vibrant and everything was clean and well organized. the highlight for me was the cable car ride absolutely breathtaking! the beautiful scenery and cool breeze while travelling above the mountains made the whole journey magical. overall an amazing place to relax have fun and create wonderful memories. highly recommended for anyone visiting genting!

had a great time and a lot of fun. we got a discount for the entry ticket same prices of locals since we stayed at their hotel so show your stay details and get the discount. we enjoyed entire day and recommending to go on weekdays but not in a tuesday since it s close on tuesdays. better you leave all your stuff in a locker and better to dressed a comfortable wear with lite shoes. we enjoyed the same rides repeatedly since we went on a monday. highly recommended.

really fun place to spend the day! the park looks amazing and the mountain views are beautiful. the cool weather makes it super nice to walk around and there s a good mix of rides for both kids and adults. some rides were closed and the queues got long in the afternoon but overall still worth it. staff were friendly and the park was clean and well kept. tip go early and bring a jacket it can get windy later in the day. great spot for families and friends when visiting genting highlands!

very crowded during holiday season especially this new year each ride we made 120mn for q. in one day we can only play 2rides only waste of money . during lunch time there are limit of seats also make along q to order and waiting at least 30mn.

genting highlands is one of the most sought after destinations on everyone s malaysia trip. the best way to enjoy it is to stay overnight so you can truly experience all the entertainment this place has to offer. the outdoor theme park alone deserves several hours so it s best to reach right at opening time. before visiting it helps to check the online map and understand the vr queue system this makes navigating the park and using the app much easier for a smoother experience. we visited on an october weekend which is the off season so the crowd was comparatively less. we bought our tickets directly at the venue instead of online the price was about 300 less per person which was a good saving. however during the peak tourist season from november to june it s advisable to book tickets in advance. overall the park is efficiently managed and offers great value for money at around 3 900 per adult approximately rm 190 .

hilltop amusement park. so many cool rides. multiple food options. a bit costly though. need hurry a bit to cover up all the rides. both outdoor and indoor. book tickets online. there is a 10 myr food voucher for each ticket. don t miss that.

playing in the snow at genting outdoor theme park was such a fun and unique experience. even though it s cold the excitement makes it totally worth it. the area is spacious safe and suitable for all ages. the surrounding mountain view and fresh air add to the experience. it s a great activity to relieve stress and create memorable moments with family or friends. definitely a must try when visiting genting until early jan 2026! notes free entry start 6.30pm snow show will start every 1 hour from 6.30pm until 10.30 in 10 minutes! enjoy

genting skyworlds theme park is an exciting and well designed amusement park with a great variety of fun rides. the attractions are thrilling and suitable for both adults and kids making it a perfect place for families and friends. each themed zone is visually impressive and immersive with creative details everywhere. the rides are well maintained the atmosphere is lively and the cool mountain weather makes the experience even more enjoyable. definitely a must visit theme park for anyone looking for a fun and memorable adventure

it was really fun and exciting!! make sure to purchase your ticket via klook for much cheaper price the food is quite pricey and they offer locker service too bcs you cant bring your bag to most of the games.. they are charging stations if your phone is out of battery. overall its a fun experience for enjoying with friends go at weekdays for much comfortable experience

a thrilling escape to the clouds my review of genting skyworlds theme park malaysia nestled high in the cool misty highlands of genting about an hour s drive from kuala lumpur genting skyworlds theme park is southeast asia s newest adrenaline playground soaring at over 6 000 feet above sea level. opened in february 2022 after a rollercoaster of delays it was originally slated to be a 20th century fox world this outdoor theme park delivers a fresh twist on the classic genting experience. if you re craving a break from malaysia s tropical heat with jaw dropping mountain views and a mix of family friendly fun and heart pounding rides skyworlds is worth the trip. i visited on a crisp november weekend and despite some crowds it felt like stepping into a vibrant immersive storybook think themed worlds inspired by blockbuster movies like ice age rio and planet of the apes. here s my honest breakdown. getting there and tickets easy access with a scenic twist reaching genting highlands is half the adventure. i opted for the awana skyway cable car from the base station a 10 minute gondola ride with panoramic views of lush valleys that instantly sets an exhilarating tone. pro tip avoid peak hours to skip long lines the app helps with real time wait times. tickets start at around rm151 for adults during off peak like early november dropping to rm128 for kids with free entry for tots under 90cm. i snagged mine via klook for a 20 discount bundling in express passes for rm50 extra essential for thrill seekers. the park operates daily from 10 am to 6 pm but closes tuesdays except holidays so plan accordingly. entry includes all rides shows and attractions no hidden fees though lockers and photos add up rm10 20 .

must visit theme park! amazing theming cool highland escape! i recently visited genting skyworlds theme park and had a truly fantastic experience. the park is beautifully designed with 9 uniquely themed zones the detail in areas like rio and the ice age section is truly impressive and makes for great photos. the cool refreshing highland weather is a huge plus making it comfortable to spend the entire day exploring. be sure to bring a light jacket as it can get chilly! key rides highlights for thrill seekers the immersive 4d ride independence day defiance and the drop tower terraform tower challenge are must dos. i also loved the new invasion of the planet of the apes ride it was a real standout! important tips for your visit use the vq system virtual queue this is absolutely essential to maximize your time. download the genting skyworlds mobile app before you go. you get three complimentary vq reservations with your ticket which saves you hours of queuing time for the most popular rides. go cashless the park is cashless so have your credit debit card or e wallet ready for food games and souvenirs. tickets prices vary based on promotions and residency. expect adult tickets non malaysian to be in the rm130 rm198 range but check the official website for the best current deals especially combo packages! overall genting skyworlds is a world class outdoor theme park that offers something for all ages. highly recommended for a fun day out!

not exactly the most thrilling but definitely a family oriented theme park! lots of rides for kids and decent ones for adults too. due to the long queues for most rides we only managed to take 8 rides. not too bad for a sunday! the virtual queue system is definitely a plus! however standard ticket only gives 3 qty i had ? so choose the rides you want to use it for wisely. and select early in case the timings become fully booked. some rides with special shout outs 1. acorn adventure ice age definitely a must to ride! need to store bags in locker but you get to wear your glasses! huge plus for people wearing specs as you get to enjoy the full experience while taking in the fantastic scenery! ride itself was pretty decent best in the theme park. 2. river town roller this was to me a hidden gem! got to control the direction neutral forward backwards of the rolls through your own joystick. decide your own fate! 3. independece day defiance nice relaxing ride witha mega screen flyung theatre show ride. if you want to go for this do use the virtual queue. waiting time is insane and painful. 4. epic voyage to moonhaven u will get wet. do get a poncho else do it as your last ride of the day. my shirt and pants underwear included got pretty damp. ride itself was so so initial drop gets you wet and another bang near the end to makee your wet journey complete. 5. terraform tower challenge did not get to ride this due to insane waiting time 90min... perhaps use your virtual queue for this if you really want to try. looks good! quite a number of rides were either still in renovation or under maintenance so do check before coming. not too bad for a first time here.

i went to genting skyworlds during a merdeka promo and used the vr queue app to book rides. some rides were under maintenance or closed when we arrived which was a letdown since we couldn t ride everything we hoped. on the plus side when rides worked the vibe environment and overall theming felt fun and the mountain weather and scenery made it a nice outing. however as many others on social media say the waits even via virtual queue can be long and it s frustrating when a ride closes after you ve queued.

good experience overall. the rides which were operational were good but some of the high thrill ones were not working . there was long wait time for some of them. my favorite was acorn adventure samba gliders and alpha fighters .

visited skyworlds indoor game park. must try rollercoaster. you ll love this unique style of it and you ll have fun for sure in this. ship ride is one of the best ride for kids as it s roam around the whole theme park from above. buy ticket pass and express pass to skip the queue as every ride have waiting time of 10 20 minutes.

the park is a bit small but with all the queuing even spending the full 8 hours inside is not going to be enough. and this is including the virtual queue system which is also difficult to get timeslots for. thank god is was cold so we are enjoy queuing up quite a few attractions were closed too. we are queuing like 20mins and the attractions closed and our kids really disappointed. not one but 2 3 attractions.it was good to introduce malaysian rates at rm60 which made it more suitable for the value we get out of the park but i need to go 2 times yet can t play all rides and attractions because some they were closed and 1st visit was too foggy and cold food overpriced but the taste was horrible. first visit we had ayam goreng for our lunch but we dont like the taste but nuggets is good. for second time we had mee curry was tasteless nasi ayam rempah but doesn t taste any. so if we visit for 3rd time maybe we just had maggie cup ice cream was good but we really enjoy spending time together. its a good place to visit

genting skyworlds theme park is an absolute blast! nestled in the cool highlands of genting it offers a perfect mix of thrilling rides immersive attractions and stunning views. whether you re zooming down roller coasters exploring themed zones or enjoying family friendly experiences there s something for everyone. the park s vibrant design and top notch facilities make it a great day out for both kids and adults. with its cooler climate and scenic surroundings it s the ideal place to escape the heat and indulge in some high flying fun!

i brought my child here to play. it s fun and exciting filled with fairy tale magic fantasy technology and a sense of the future. there s cheerful music everywhere which really puts you in a good mood.

our kids had a fun time playing the rides and absolute blast with the super friendly photographers! do remember to capture your memories with beautifully taken photos when you are here! theme park photographers gurpreet singh fahad zaman

visiting genting skyworlds was a really enjoyable experience. the park has beautiful themed zones and each area feels lively and well designed. the cool genting highlands weather makes walking around the park comfortable even on a long day. the rides are fun and suitable for all ages from gentle family attractions to more exciting adventures. the epic voyage to moonhaven and independence day defiance rides were especially memorable. staff were friendly helpful and kept the mood positive throughout the park. what i enjoyed most was the overall atmosphere colourful clean and filled with great photo spots. it s a good place to spend time with friends or family and you can easily fill an entire day with activities shows and rides. overall genting skyworlds is a great theme park experience with impressive environments good entertainment value and a refreshing highland vibe. a recommended destination for anyone looking for a fun day out!

it is a lovely spot for a family outing with children we all had a wonderful time! the rides were a blast! and let s not forget the chilly weather in genting! it is definitely a must visit if you are heading to genting highlands with your kids.

it s a great place and an amusement park with a wide variety of rides.it was a lot of fun and perfect for a family trip. the waiting time to enter wasn t long and the price was reasonable. it s also easy to get there by cable car. on weekends it gets very crowded and the queues for rides can be long but you can make reservations in advance.

for only rm65 per adult and rm56 per child the promotional price was well worth it. the skyworlds app was a huge plus as it allowed us to book virtual queue rides and save a lot of time. we truly enjoyed the rides and had a great day.
amazing theme park with world class rides! the attractions are exciting well themed and suitable for all ages. staff were friendly and the park was clean. a must visit when in genting!

an amazing adventurous of the wonderful experiences ever spot in the hill top of genting highlands with huge theme park in the malaysia tourism industry. a must visit spot for the unlimited options of amusement entertainment for each segments from kids to adults. one of my most favourite place to visit during genting highlands trip . lots of things to do list in the single spot for wonderful family trip . definitely kids will enjoy it lots . staying place also available in this place better booking through online to avoid last minute heavy expenses. try to book it virtual queue qr code via genting sky world theme park app in the google play store to avoid hevay queues of waiting time on each rides. the best place to exploring and entertainment for entire day . better to visit normal days than public holidays because crowd is huge

this was my first time visiting skyworlds theme park after the massive renovation. for me as an 80 s baby i personally preferred the old genting outdoor theme park. the new one feels more like an instagrammable park . no doubt there are now more kids friendly games outdoors compared to before but the overall vibe is very different. the old theme park used to have more thrilling and fun rides whereas now it feels toned down. another downside is the waiting time queues can go up to 120 minutes. i really think the theme park should implement a system where if it has already reached full capacity they should stop selling tickets and mark it as sold out. at the current ticket price of rm168 per adult it honestly doesn t feel worth it. you pay a lot but you don t get to enjoy all the rides. even with the virtual queue system you can only reserve up to 3 rides. luckily we bought our tickets during a promo at rm68 per person so missing out on some rides wasn t such a big deal. but if i had to pay rm168 next time i don t think i d come again. honestly the vibe is just not the same compared to the old theme park. back then it was so fun that we would come almost every year.

remember to purchase tickets one day before online otherwise it will be rmb 198 for non malaysians for walk in ticket. our kids age 11 9 6 had whole day of fun. definitely my boy age 6 has not much rides but he loves samba gliders the most. highly recommend to bring older kids so as parents won t feel so exhausted.

you need to have a day long schedule for the park. for indoor you don t need to buy tickets. but for ride you need to buy tickets. i found day long tickets more valuable for us. tickets price varies based of ride type family thrill etc. . 93 myr per ticket was worthy for us

had a chill trip up to genting! weather was super cool and misty such a nice break from the city heat. the cable car ride was awesome and the view felt unreal. skyavenue s packed with food and shops a bit pricey but vibes 10 10 . theme park was fun too though kinda crowded. at night it s all lights and fog super pretty. just bring a jacket trust me perfect weekend escape spot!

visited genting skyworlds theme park with kids and loved it. some rides are real adrenaline pumping and the overall experience is top quality. it rained on and off so a few rides were closed temporarily but even queues of up to 60 minutes were worth it. best to visit on a weekday. vr ape invasion and ice age thin ice were amazing highlights. great fun for the whole family.

we visited here during the weekends bought vip tickets and overall it was a pleasant experience!! our tour guide miss batrisya did such a good job to guide us around the theme park would definitely recommend anyone planning to buy vip tickets during visits

had a great time at genting skyworlds theme park! the park is well designed with a good mix of rides for both thrill seekers and families. the atmosphere is fun and lively and everything feels clean and organized. queues can be a little long during peak hours but overall the experience was smooth. food and drinks are available inside at reasonable prices for a theme park and the staff were friendly and helpful. definitely worth a visit if you re in genting highlands!

this is definitely one of the best theme parks in malaysia other than sunway lagoon. i really enjoyed the cold weather throughout the whole visit which is something you can rarely experience in malaysia. you can download the skyworlds app to easily check the locations and queue times for each ride and facility. some of the best rides i tried were ice age s acorn adventure independence day defiance and rio s samba gliders. the invasion of the planet of the apes ride was the most disappointing for me because the experience felt a bit plain and boring. the 3d effects together with the bumpy ride also made me feel sick afterwards. unfortunately some of the rides were closed even though their locations still appeared on the app.

genting skyworlds theme park amazing theme park experience in the cool genting highlands! the park is beautifully designed with different themed zones and the rides are a great mix of thrilling fun and family friendly. the roller coasters and major attractions are exciting and well maintained and the scenery makes the experience even better. the virtual queue system is very helpful and reduces long waiting times if planned properly

pros the games at genting all look fun plus the view is amazing and the overall atmosphere is great. cons when the weather is uncertain or bad many games are unavailable as shown on the app not just one but often 5 or 6 rides. epic voyage is closed for the whole day and independence day is the same. i ve come here 2 3 times already and it s been very disappointing. sometimes i feel sorry for people who travel far from other states or even from overseas. i understand that it s because of the weather but please plan your holiday so that it s at least worth the money. most of the time the weather in genting is foggy and rainy since it s up in the mountains so only certain games can actually be played.

a very wonderful themepark my wife and i went there as a vip and luckily we were the only vip at that period. we were guided by a professional staff named misha who were very cheerful and helpful during our guide. the way she managed the time and tell us stories about each rides really helps us to explore the team park. sadly we didn t get a formal picture with her. if anyone planning to get the vip treatment. please request for her you won t be disappointed. we managed to play all the available rides due to her management of time and letting us cut lines my wife and i went to the park as a part of our honeymoon. it was fun and one of the unique experience ever. everything is safe and fun. the samba glider is a must. misha the guide even let us rode it twice. we were also provided free ice cream and popcorn during our treatment. and the chocolate drink in the vip room was exquisite. money well spent

genting outdoor theme park is amazing! although the queues can be long it s totally worth it. the rides are thrilling especially the roller coasters once you re on them you forget all about the waiting. the park is clean the staff are friendly and the whole place is well designed. even though the sun is out genting s cool weather makes it comfortable to stay outdoors without feeling hot. truly a 5 star experience i d highly recommend!

a whimsical and thrilling experience for everyone. some of my thoughts and tips 1 please download the skyworld app. buying through the website grants a small rebate and it cost us rm130 for us foreigners after the discounts. 2 plan your vq wisely using it for rides with typically longer waiting times. i recommend using it for terraform independence day and epic voyage 3 bring a water bottle and raincoat. you can refill in the park and also the epic ride can soak you 4 come on a weekday. waiting times on rides were on average 5 mins and up to 40 minutes on the popular rides on a monday afternoon. enjoyed my time here! i hope that the planned rides like mad ramp peak can be expedited because it has good future potential.

we had a wonderful time at genting skyworlds theme park with the vip experience. everything was smooth well organized and truly enjoyable. the vip access made the whole visit so much more comfortable allowing us to enjoy the rides and attractions without any stress. a special thank you to amisha for making our experience exceptional. she was friendly attentive and very helpful throughout the entire tour. her guidance really made our day memorable. highly recommended for anyone looking for a premium and hassle free experience at genting skyworlds!
